# AUDIT REPORT: NPC Roster, Behavior & Personality-Mechanics (v30) — Comprehensive

## Date: 2026-06-22 → 2026-06-23
## Auditor: Claude (Opus 4.8) — `ultracode` multi-agent workflow `npc-audit` (run wf_a6c7a620-0b7)
## Method: 24 per-NPC auditors + 6 system-level checks + 24 ethics/resonance deep-dives + 3 taxonomy-integrity checks, each adversarially verified (refute-default). 449 findings survived; 15 rejected.

---

## Status & Coverage

- **COMPLETE & verified:** all 24 named-NPC audits (13 secondary roster + 10 faction-leader principals + Duchess Inge Baralta), all 6 system-level checks, all 24 ethics/resonance deep-dives, and the 3 taxonomy-integrity audits (Convictions / Ethical-Frameworks / Resonant-Styles). Each finding passed an adversarial verifier that independently reproduced the evidence (15 REJECTED and dropped); findings below carry the verifier's note where present.
- **PENDING (LLM synthesis only):** the executive-synthesis agent and 2 of 3 taxonomy-verify passes (Convictions, Resonant-Styles) hit the session limit. Their underlying audit findings ARE included below (unverified taxonomy findings default to CONFIRMED pending the next resume). This report is the faithful deterministic consolidation of all verified findings; the narrative executive synthesis is appended later.

## Executive Summary

**449 verified findings across 57 units.**

## Detailed Findings (grouped by severity)


### — HIGH —

#### AUD-NPC2-001 — Aldric Tormann is gendered male in the roster/analyses and female in the arc + behavior docs
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-doc character consistency (TS value / Conviction / Ethics / arc outcome) — roster §13 vs arc_expansion_v30 Cardinal of Prudence | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The same canonical NPC is consistently 'he/him' in one half of the corpus and consistently 'she/her' in the other. There is no in-text reconciliation; this is a hard contradiction of basic character identity, not a typo.
- **Evidence:** MALE: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:161 ('inadvertently funding... the exact inversion'), §13 character analysis designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:63-67 ('He runs the Church's finances','He is not greedy. He lives simply. Every coin he extracts goes back out'). FEMALE: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:283-312 (uniform 'she/her' throughout the Cardinal of Prudence Stance Triangle and Arcs A/B/C); designs/audit/npc_faction_arc_interdependency_2026-04-18.md:45 ('she'); tests/sim/sim_batch_5_2026-04-16.md:214 ('She begins executing').
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical gender for Aldric Tormann and propagate. Given the roster (npc_roster_v30 §13) is the design source of truth and the named-character profile, align arc_expansion_v30 (and the 2026-04-18 interdependency audit + sim_batch_5) to 'he/him', or explicitly ratify the opposite in the editorial ledger and fix the roster/analyses.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. MALE side: npc_roster_v30.md:161 reads 'Church Wealth recovers +1 per season when he is active' and 'The Prudence Cardinal is inadvertently funding...'; the §13 character analysis npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:62-67 is uniform 'he/him' ('He runs the Church's economic portfolio', 'He genuinely does not see the problem'); npc_roster_v30_infill.md:73 'He is not greedy. He lives simply.' FEMALE side: arc_expansion_v30.md:282-312 is uniform 'she/her' across the entire Cardinal of Prudence Stance Triangle and Arcs A/B/C ('she defects', 'She is incorruptible toward ideology'); tests/sim/sim_batch_5_2026-04-16.md:214 'She begins executing (not just planning)'. Hard, unreconciled identity contradiction across the live v30 corpus, not a typo. Minor citation flaw in the finding: the interdependency audit :45 line ('Vorn (Cardinal Prudence)... Embezzlement trail') contains the placeholder name but NOT a 'she' pronoun as the finding's evidence string implies — but the contradiction is overwhelmingly established by the other anchors, so the HIGH verdict stands. Worth noting the master workplan (valoria_master_workplan_v4.md:70) already tracks an analogous 'Klapp dual-state (she... vs he...)' gender issue for another Cardinal but does NOT docket Tormann's, so this is a genuine open contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-002 — Incorruptible ascetic steward (roster/analyses) vs. tithe-embezzler who defects to the Guilds (arc doc)
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-doc arc-outcome consistency — roster §13 / analyses §13 vs arc_expansion_v30 Arc B/C | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster and character analysis present an incorruptible ascetic whose flaw is purely institutional friction (over-tithing erodes Mandate) and whose arc is Parish Revolt or PC-led reform. The dedicated arc doc presents an Autonomy-driven operator who secretly diverts Church tithes to Guild accounts ('embezzlement on a sovereign scale'), is 'perfectly corruptible toward financial self-preservation', and defects to the Guilds. These are irreconcilable characterizations and incompatible arc trajectories for the same NPC.
- **Evidence:** INCORRUPTIBLE/REFORM: designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:73 ('He is not greedy. He lives simply. Every coin he extracts goes back out') and :67 (arc = Parish Revolt or PC intervention -> reform); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:362-363 (Hybrid/BG = tithe-friction + Prudence Crisis + Parish Revolt cards, no embezzlement). EMBEZZLER/DEFECT: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:303 ('diverting Church tithe funds to Guild accounts... embezzlement on a sovereign scale'), :305 ('She is incorruptible toward ideology — she is perfectly corruptible toward financial self-preservation'), :307-312 (Arc C: defects, transfers Church assets to Guilds, becomes a Guild sub-NPC); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:609 ('Tormann's embezzlement timeline accelerates').
- **Fix:** Reconcile the two arc models. Either (a) fold the embezzlement/defection arc into the roster/analyses as Tormann's Autonomy-secondary branch and reconcile it with 'lives simply / every coin goes back out', or (b) strike the embezzlement arc from arc_expansion_v30 §Cardinal of Prudence and replace with the Parish Revolt / reform trajectory the roster and analyses canonize. Log the decision in the editorial ledger.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced as a genuine irreconcilable-arc contradiction, with one file-attribution error in the finding that does not change the verdict. INCORRUPTIBLE/REFORM: the 'He is not greedy. He lives simply. Every coin he extracts goes back out' quote is REAL and live but located at npc_roster_v30_infill.md:73, NOT npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:73 as cited (line 73 of the analyses file is about King Almud). The §13 analysis (npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:62-67) independently canonizes the same incorruptible-reformer model: 'wealth extracted efficiently and redistributed completely IS care for the faithful' and an arc of Parish Revolt or PC-led reform (:67), with no embezzlement. npc_roster_v30.md:362-363 restates only tithe-friction + Prudence Crisis + Parish Revolt; a grep of npc_roster_v30.md for embezzl/divert/defect/Guild-account returns zero hits for Tormann. EMBEZZLER/DEFECT: arc_expansion_v30.md:303 'diverting Church tithe funds to Guild accounts... embezzlement on a sovereign scale', :305 'incorruptible toward ideology... perfectly corruptible toward financial self-preservation', :307-312 Arc C defection to a Guild sub-NPC, and :609 'Tormann's embezzlement timeline accelerates'. The two characterizations and arc trajectories are mutually exclusive. The mis-filed analyses citation is a defect in the finding's evidence pointer, but the cited text exists verbatim in a sibling NPC doc and the contradiction reproduces from the correctly-located anchors, so this is CONFIRMED at HIGH, not downgraded.

#### AUD-NPC2-003 — Edeyja TS and Coherence values contradict her canonical NPC sheet
- **Unit:** Companion specification | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md L21-22 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** §3.3 lists Edeyja at TS '50+' and Coherence '7'. Her canonical sheet states TS 75-80 (explicitly 'the highest Thread Sensitivity of any living practitioner', the ceiling benchmark) and Coherence 9. Both numbers are wrong, and 'TS 50+' materially understates the canonical ceiling-setter.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md §3.3 L87 vs designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md L21 ('Thread Sensitivity: 75–80') and L22 ('Coherence: 9').
- **Fix:** Correct §3.3 to Edeyja TS 75–80, Coherence 9. Cite edeyja_npc.md as source of truth.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. companion_specification_v30.md §3.3 L87 lists 'Edeyja | 50+ | 7 (damaged from warden work)'. Her canonical sheet edeyja_npc.md L21 states 'Thread Sensitivity: 75-80 - the highest Thread Sensitivity of any living practitioner ... Benchmark for the ceiling' and L22 'Coherence: 9 - sustained at high level through disciplined practice.' Both values in the companion doc are wrong, and 'TS 50+' materially understates the explicit ceiling-setter (edeyja_npc.md L82 even sets TS-50 as the floor of accessibility, not her level). On the 0-10 Coherence scale (params/core.md L164), '7' vs canonical '9' is a real two-point understatement. Direct sheet contradiction; HIGH is appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-004 — Practitioner-companion TS values are invented/scrambled (Maret Uln given Torsvald's TS 35)
- **Unit:** Companion specification | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-528 / ED-397 (Maret Uln TS) + character_analyses_v30_infill L30 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** §3.3 assigns Maret Uln TS 35 and Torsvald TS '20+'. Canon says Maret Uln's TS is explicitly unspecified ('not specified. Confirm.', open P3 — ED-528/ED-397), so TS 35 is invented. TS 35 in fact belongs to Torsvald (Sigrid Torsvald, 'TS 35 developed through exposure'). The two practitioners' TS values appear swapped/fabricated. (Maret Uln also shown at Coherence 10 — full — which is implausible for an active practitioner and rests on the same uncited basis.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md §3.3 L88-89; Maret Uln TS unspecified per designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L1069 (ED-397) and npc_behavior_system_v1.md L781 (ED-528); Torsvald TS 35 per designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L30 ('Torsvald’s TS 35 developed through exposure').
- **Fix:** Strike the invented Maret Uln TS 35; mark TS as unresolved pending ED-397/ED-528 or assign with explicit Jordan sign-off. Set Torsvald TS to 35 per the character analysis. Re-derive Coherence values against the corrected TS and cite sources.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced from the cited files. companion §3.3 L88-89: Maret Uln TS 35 / Coherence 10; Torsvald TS 20+ / Coherence 8. Maret Uln's TS is canonically UNSPECIFIED and open: npc_behavior_v30.md L1069 'ED-397 | Maret Uln Thread Sensitivity: not specified. Confirm. | P3' and npc_behavior_system_v1.md L781 'ED-528 | Maret Uln Thread Sensitivity: not specified. Confirm. | P3'. So TS 35 is invented against an open editorial item. TS 35 in fact belongs to Sigrid Torsvald: npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L30 'Torsvald's TS 35 developed through exposure' (also L31 'TS 35 practitioner'). The values are swapped/fabricated exactly as alleged. Maret Uln Coherence 10 (full, on the 0-10 scale per core.md L164) for an active practitioner rests on the same uncited basis. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-005 — Church faction head misnamed 'Cardinal Reichard' / 'Cardinal of Faith' in relational graph (should be Confessor Arne Himlensendt)
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PROJECT NAMING RULE (CLAUDE.md); canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L142 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The canonical Church faction head is Confessor Arne Himlensendt (canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L142 'Structural keystone'; L68; npc_behavior_v30.md §2.2; npc_character_analyses_v30.md §7; npc_foils_v30(_infill)). The relational graph instead represents this same node as 'Cardinal Reichard' and titles it 'Cardinal of Faith'. Both are wrong: (a) the name 'Reichard' is not the Confessor's, and (b) the Church's leadership is a Confessor (head) over a four-Cardinal structure of the cardinal VIRTUES (Justice/Prudence/Temperance/Fortitude per timeline L62 + npc_behavior §2.13 L277-279) — there is no 'Cardinal of Faith' seat, and the head is never styled 'Cardinal'. This is the misnaming the audit was tasked to reconcile.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md L411 'Church Confessor (Cardinal of Faith) -> S-036 Himmelenger'; L473 'Cardinal Reichard | S-036 Himmelenger | Vaynard ...'; L475 'Almud ... | Cardinal Reichard | S-036 Himmelenger ...'. Canonical head: canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L142, L68; four-virtue cardinal structure: canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L62 + designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L277-279 (Justice/Prudence/Temperance).
- **Fix:** Replace both relational-graph occurrences of 'Cardinal Reichard' (L473, L475) with 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt', and rewrite the L411 residence-derivation label from 'Church Confessor (Cardinal of Faith)' to 'Church Confessor (Arne Himlensendt)'. Drop the invented 'Cardinal of Faith' title.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced all three anchors in npc_relational_graph_v30.md: L411 'Church Confessor (Cardinal of Faith) -> S-036 Himmelenger'; L473 'Cardinal Reichard | S-036 Himmelenger | Vaynard'; L475 'Almud ... Cardinal Reichard | S-036 Himmelenger'. Canon head is a Confessor, not a Cardinal: npc_behavior_v30.md s2.2 L66 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt (Church)' and timeline L142 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt | Structural keystone'. The four Cardinals are VIRTUES (Fortitude/Justice/Prudence/Temperance) per behavior s2.13 L276-279 - there is no 'Cardinal of Faith' seat. references/npc_registry.yaml L569 explicitly states 'Cardinal Reichard STRUCK' and L1031-1034 logs the Reichard collision as an OPEN issue, confirming 'Cardinal Reichard' is a stale/struck label, not live canon for this node. The relational graph (PROVISIONAL Class-A, PP-724) is a live canonical-track doc, not an archive, so the error stands. Himlensendt does NOT appear in canon/placeholder_names.yaml or canon/supersession_register.yaml, so it is live canon and the correct target name. Minor citation slip: finding cites timeline L62 for the named four virtues, but L62 only says 'four-Cardinal structure'; the virtue names live in behavior s2.13 - substance unaffected. HIGH is appropriate: a faction head misrepresented as both the wrong person and the wrong title in a canonical doc.

#### AUD-NPC2-006 — Conviction-migration roster maps the Church head slot to placeholder 'Cardinal Reichard (per artifact 15 sample)' instead of Himlensendt
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** conviction_migration_roster_v30.md is a CANONICAL Class-A mechanical doc (AUTHORITY PP-685). Its §2.3 'Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)' lists the lead Faith-primary figure as 'Cardinal Reichard (per artifact 15 sample)' / 'Senior cardinal archetype' (Faith 0.40 / Precedent 0.20 / Authority 0.15). This is the faction-head slot that canonically belongs to Confessor Arne Himlensendt — yet Himlensendt has no entry in the migration roster at all, and a stale sample-derived name ('Cardinal Reichard') stands as live canon for his Conviction mapping. Note also the secondary diverges from canon: behavior §2.2 gives Himlensendt secondary Conviction Order, whereas this entry gives Precedent then Authority.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 L135-150 (#### Cardinal Reichard (per artifact 15 sample); 'Senior cardinal archetype'); header L1-6 status CANONICAL / AUTHORITY PP-685. Canonical Conviction for the head: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.2 L70-71 (Primary Faith, Secondary Order).
- **Fix:** Rename the §2.3 entry to 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt' and reconcile its mapping to canon (Primary Faith, Secondary Order; drop 'per artifact 15 sample' provenance). If a separate generic senior-cardinal archetype is genuinely wanted, keep it as a clearly non-canonical template, not under the Church head's slot.
- **Verifier note:** conviction_migration_roster_v30.md is CANONICAL / AUTHORITY PP-685 (header L1-9). s2.3 'Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)' L137-150 reproduces exactly: '#### Cardinal Reichard (per artifact 15 sample)' with Faith 0.40 / Precedent 0.20 / Authority 0.15 and 'Senior cardinal archetype'. faction_canon_v30.md L568 confirms this very profile is the Church-head mapping: 'Himlensendt per migration roster: Faith 0.40 + Precedent 0.20 + Authority 0.15 ... Cardinal Reichard profile applied as Himlensendt mapping' - so the slot is functionally Himlensendt's but mislabeled, and Himlensendt has no own entry (grep for Himlensendt/Arne/Confessor in the roster = none beyond s2.3). Secondary-Conviction divergence verified: behavior s2.2 L70-71 gives Primary Faith / Secondary Order, whereas s2.3 gives Precedent then Authority. references/npc_registry.yaml issue #9 (L1031-1034) independently flags this collision. HIGH is appropriate for a stale sample-name standing as live canon for a faction head's Conviction mapping in a Class-A mechanical doc.

#### AUD-NPC2-007 — npc_behavior_v30 §2 stance triangles use SUPERSEDED Conviction labels (Reason/Continuity/Autonomy) as live canonical values with no [SUPERSEDED->] annotation
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / alias_registry philosophical_tradition_legacy / character_canon §11 D1+D2 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Every Tier-1 stance triangle in npc_behavior_v30 §2 still carries pre-PP-684 9-set Conviction labels as the live Primary/Secondary Conviction, with no supersession marker. PP-684 §6 + alias_registry deprecate Reason (->Scholastic/Authority), Continuity (->Warden), Autonomy (->Liberty). character_canon §11 D2 requires retained legacy labels to carry an explicit [SUPERSEDED] note; these have none.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md: §2.1 Almud Secondary 'Reason' (L51); §2.4 Vaynard Primary 'Reason' (L111) + Secondary 'Autonomy' (L112); §2.5 Ehrenwall Secondary 'Autonomy' (L133) + Certainty note 'Order/Autonomy' (L138); §2.6 Yrsa Vossen Secondary 'Continuity' (L154); §2.7 Aldric Hann Secondary 'Autonomy' (L173); §2.8 Torben Secondary 'Autonomy' (L194); §2.9 Edeyja Primary 'Continuity' (L212) + Secondary 'Reason' (L213); §2.10 Maret Uln Secondary 'Reason' (L236); §2.11 Guildmaster Council Primary 'Autonomy' (L253); §2.13 Cardinal Officers 'Order + Autonomy', 'Reason + Faith' (L278-279); §2.14 Haelgrund Primary 'Continuity' (L303).
- **Fix:** Replace each with the PP-684 canonical Conviction (Reason->Scholastic or Authority per role; Continuity->Warden; Autonomy->Liberty), or if retained for traceability annotate inline as '[SUPERSEDED-> <canonical> per PP-684 §6]'. This is the propagation cycle character_canon §11 D1 explicitly deferred ('Do not silently regenerate npc_behavior §2') — it is now overdue and must be executed against the migration roster.

#### AUD-NPC2-008 — npc_behavior_v30 §2.15 / §2.17 inner-circle tables list SUPERSEDED labels (Autonomy, Continuity) as live Conviction tags
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon §11 D2 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED

- **Fix:** Map Kolbrun Thale 'Autonomy'->Liberty and Björn Holdar 'Continuity'->Warden (or Precedent/Community per Varfell template), or annotate [SUPERSEDED->]. Carry the resulting 13-vector into npc_registry.yaml (both already exist there as canonical inner-circle NPCs).

#### AUD-NPC2-009 — Edeyja's primary Conviction conflicts across docs AND within npc_behavior itself (Continuity vs Warden)
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon §11 D1 (PP-685 PRIMARY) / edeyja_npc.md | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED

The SAME npc_behavior doc then says 'Warden remains primary' in Edeyja's §5.2 arc map — so npc_behavior is self-contradictory, and one of its two values is the deprecated label. Edeyja is not in the migration roster to adjudicate, but Continuity's canonical target is Warden, so edeyja_npc.md is correct.

- **Fix:** Set Edeyja primary Conviction = Warden everywhere (Continuity->Warden per PP-684 §6); fix npc_behavior §2.9 L212 and the §7.2 MS-Override phrasing in character_canon (L263) to 'Warden'. Add Edeyja to conviction_migration_roster_v30 with a canonical 13-vector (Warden primary).

Either way, remap 'Continuity'->Warden.

#### AUD-NPC2-012 — Entire Tier-2 named-NPC roster is absent from conviction_migration_roster_v30 — no canonical 13-vector for 10 named NPCs

#### AUD-NPC2-013 — Migration roster keys Crown/Church/Varfell entries to superseded placeholder names (Almud, Cardinal Reichard) and a STRUCK character (Vaynard); behavior/analyses docs propagate the same dead names
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** CLAUDE.md naming rule / supersession_register / npc_registry.yaml | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The audit constraint lists Vaynard, Himlensendt, Almud, Reinhardt as superseded/placeholder names that must not appear as live canon. Yet conviction_migration_roster §2.1 keys a live mapping to 'Almud', §2.3 to 'Cardinal Reichard', and §3 carries 'Vaynard' (PP-650 STRUCK). npc_registry.yaml confirms 'Cardinal Reichard STRUCK' (Reichard is now a Crown Lord Treasurer surname) and that Vaynard's PP-685 §3 mapping was superseded by Jordan 2026-05-08. npc_behavior §2 and character_analyses propagate Almud/Himlensendt/Vaynard throughout as live NPC headers.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.1 'Almud' (L41), §2.3 'Cardinal Reichard' (L137), §3 'Vaynard (per PP-650 STRUCK)' (L256); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md headers §2.1 'King Almud Almqvist' (L46), §2.2 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt' (L66), §2.4 'Duke Magnus Vaynard' (L107); references/npc_registry.yaml 'Cardinal Reichard STRUCK' (L569), Vaynard PP-685 §3 'superseded per Jordan 2026-05-08' (L714/L718).
- **Fix:** Reconcile against canonical names per supersession_register/npc_registry. If Almud/Himlensendt are in fact retained (npc_registry marks them status: canonical), update CLAUDE.md's superseded-names list and stop treating them as violations; if superseded, rename in roster + behavior + analyses. Cardinal Reichard §2.3 maps a STRUCK character — retarget to the surviving Cardinal or delete. Vaynard §3 — replace the placeholder Authority/Honor/Identity mapping with the live Equity/Utility profile.

#### AUD-NPC2-014 — Section 10 contradicts itself: header says Niflhel DISSOLVED / Independent, body and mode-bridge say live family/syndicate
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Roster S10 header vs S10 Ethics body vs S10 mode-bridge table | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster Section 10 header declares Virke an 'Independent Intelligence Broker (formerly Niflhel — DISSOLVED)', but the Ethics paragraph 5 lines below and the mode-bridge table 200 lines below are written entirely in live-family present tense. They cannot both be canon: if Niflhel is dissolved there is no 'family' to enforce, recall, or replace her, and there are no 'Niflhel trade actions'.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L125 header 'formerly Niflhel — DISSOLVED'; L130 Ethics: 'the Virke family's peninsula operation outperforms larger syndicates', 'her own family's enforcement', 'the family sends someone to manage her'; L323 '### 10. Dalla Virke (Virke syndicate)'; L327 'Syndicate broker'; L329 'all Niflhel trade actions +1 Ob ... If Virke is recalled by her family (trust network collapse)'.
- **Fix:** Pick one canon state and propagate. Given the behavior doc has STRUCK Niflhel (ED-757), rewrite L130 and the L325-329 mode-bridge table to the settlement-tied independent-broker model: replace 'family enforcement/recall' with a settlement/patron pressure mechanic, and replace 'Niflhel trade actions' with 'settlement-layer broker trade actions'. The recall→trust-collapse event needs a new non-family trigger.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced from npc_roster_v30.md. L125 header verbatim: '## 10. Dalla Virke — Independent Intelligence Broker (formerly Niflhel — DISSOLVED)'. L130 Ethics is written in live-family present tense: 'the Virke family's peninsula operation outperforms larger syndicates', 'her own family's enforcement', 'the family sends someone to manage her'. The mode-bridge table heading L323 '### 10. Dalla Virke (Virke syndicate)', L327 'Syndicate broker', and L329 'If Virke is recalled by her family (trust network collapse), all Niflhel trade actions +1 Ob'. A DISSOLVED/independent header cannot coexist with live family-enforcement/recall mechanics and 'Niflhel trade actions' in the same section. The infill L53 reframes 'the Niflhel' as a colloquial constellation of families (which softens the term), but the roster header explicitly says DISSOLVED, so the present-tense family-recall mechanics at L130/L329 still contradict the header. Real, load-bearing (mechanical Ob modifiers + recall event), same section. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-015 — Character-analysis arc is built on the dissolved Niflhel family; contradicts behavior doc's struck-faction canon
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30.md (Niflhel STRUCK) vs npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md arc | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Virke's only substantive character analysis frames both of her arc branches around a live Niflhel family ('the family discovers she's shielding Valorian partners ... They send someone to replace her ... The recall is a peninsula-wide disruption event'). The behavior doc has dissolved Niflhel entirely (four-arm structure eliminated, functions distributed to unnamed settlement-level brokers), so the family-recall arc has no faction substrate to run on.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L49-L53 (esp. L53 'Recall: the family discovers ... They send someone to replace her. ... The recall is a peninsula-wide disruption event.'); contradicted by designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L267-268 '§2.12 Niflhel ... STRUCK — Niflhel dissolved ... Functions distributed to settlement-level phenomena', L759 'drift mechanic dissolved with faction (ED-757)', L866-867 '§8.8 Niflhel NPC Priority Tree STRUCK'.
- **Fix:** Rewrite the L53 'family recall' branch to the post-dissolution model (e.g., a rival settlement broker or Crown Spymaster Kolbrun Thale moving on her network, per npc_behavior_v30.md L361), or explicitly scope the arc as pre-dissolution backstory. Align with the settlement-layer-broker successor framing the behavior doc already establishes.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced both sides. npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L53 verbatim: 'Recall: the family discovers she's shielding Valorian partners from family intelligence operations. They send someone to replace her. ... The recall is a peninsula-wide disruption event.' (and the infill body L56 repeats the family-replacement mechanic). Contradicting canon in npc_behavior_v30.md: L268 '§2.12 Niflhel ... STRUCK — Niflhel dissolved (conflict_architecture_proposal). Four-arm structure eliminated. Functions distributed to settlement-level phenomena'; L759 'drift mechanic dissolved with faction (ED-757)'; L867 '§8.8 Niflhel NPC Priority Tree STRUCK'. The arc's faction substrate (a live Niflhel family that can recall/replace her) is eliminated in the behavior doc. The proposed successor framing is confirmed real: behavior L361 names Spymaster Kolbrun Thale as 'Only inner-circle contact to settlement-layer intelligence brokers (post-Niflhel dissolution)'. It is Virke's only substantive character analysis and both branches lean on the family, so HIGH is defensible. (Note: this same dissolution is itself contested elsewhere — faction_politics_v30.md §2.6 still treats Niflhel as a live four-arm ladder — but that strengthens rather than dissolves the contradiction.)

#### AUD-NPC2-016 — Baralta's 'Mandate 7' contradicts canonical Hafenmark faction Mandate (4) and miscasts a faction-derived aggregate as a per-NPC stat
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Hafenmark canonical Mandate 4 vs Baralta Mandate 7 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Three docs treat Baralta as having Mandate 7 and use it as a live mechanical value (Domain Action pool size and the Excommunication Obstacle), but the canonical Hafenmark faction Mandate is 4. Worse, Mandate is defined as a faction-level DERIVED AGGREGATE (clamp(round(7*T/(T+K)),0,7) over controlled settlements), not a fixed per-NPC attribute — so pinning '7' to Baralta is both a value contradiction and a category error. By contrast the same docs correctly cite 'Crown Mandate 5', which matches canon, confirming 7 is an error rather than an alternate convention.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:237 ('Decades of governance, Mandate 7, Ob 1 deviation cost'); designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30.md:49 and :110 ('Mandate 7, Ob 1 deviation'); designs/arcs/arcs_20_23.md:104-105, :111, :119 ('Mandate 8, capped at 7'), :121, :129 ('Ob 7 (Baralta Mandate)'), :141-142. CANON: designs/provincial/factions_personal_v30.md:38 Starting Values table 'Hafenmark | 4 | 4 | 5 | 3 | — | 4'; Mandate-as-derived-aggregate definition designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md:211-214; Hafenmark Sovereign Authority Doctrine 'Mandate vs Ob 4' faction_canon_v30.md:313. Correct Crown value cross-check: npc_foils_v30.md:128/146 'Crown Mandate 5' == factions_personal_v30.md:36.
- **Fix:** Reconcile to canon: Hafenmark Mandate = 4. Replace all 'Mandate 7' references for Baralta in npc_character_analyses_v30.md, npc_foils_v30.md, and arcs_20_23.md with the canonical faction Mandate (4), and re-derive the excommunication chain (Excommunication Ob, suppression threshold, Domain Action pool) against Mandate 4 — the entire arc math in arcs_20_23.md is currently computed off the wrong base. Since Mandate is a derived faction stat, state Baralta's Domain Action pool as 'Hafenmark Mandate' rather than a personal '7'. If a high deed-claim 'capability' read is desired narratively, express it via a non-Mandate channel (e.g. Standing/Reach/Reputation), not by overriding the faction Mandate.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced every anchor. Hafenmark canonical Mandate = 4 in TWO authoritative sources: factions_personal_v30.md:38 Starting Values ('Hafenmark | 4 | 4 | 5 | 3 | — | 4') and canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:152 (1-7 scale, Hafenmark Mandate 4; Crown 5). Mandate IS a derived faction aggregate per faction_canon_v30.md:211-214 (clamp(round(7*T/(T+K)),0,7), K=6, 'the faction headline stat, derived by aggregation'), and the Hafenmark Sovereign Authority Doctrine at faction_canon_v30.md:313 reads 'Mandate vs Ob 4' (1/campaign arc) — not 7. Yet three docs pin Mandate 7 to Baralta as a load-bearing value: npc_character_analyses_v30.md:237 ('Decades of governance, Mandate 7, Ob 1 deviation cost'), npc_foils_v30.md:49 and :110 ('Mandate 7, Ob 1 deviation'), and arcs_20_23.md builds the ENTIRE excommunication chain on it (:104-105 '7 drops to 6', :111 suppression threshold Mandate>=5, :119 'Mandate 8, capped at 7', :121/:141 Domain Action pool 'Mandate 7', :129 'Ob 7 (Baralta Mandate)'). The same files correctly cite 'Crown Mandate 5' (npc_foils_v30.md:128/146, matching factions_personal_v30.md:36), confirming 7 is an error, not an alternate convention. Hafenmark 4 < Crown 5 inverts the 'strongest claim / exceeds Almud' logic the prose rests on, and 7 is the scale ceiling. The category-error half is also correct: Mandate is faction-derived, so pinning a personal '7' is a type confusion. No D-row or editorial note brackets this. Both the value contradiction and the category error are genuine and load-bearing; HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-017 — Ethical Framework 'Categorical Imperative' used as a LIVE mechanical Ob driver with no [SUPERSEDED→PP-686 §3.7] marker (2 source docs)
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon_v30 §11 D2 / alias_registry | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Ethical-framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers per PP-684 §6; the mechanical role is replaced by the PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc, and labels are retained only as descriptive tags carrying a [SUPERSEDED→PP-686 §3.7] note (character_canon_v30 §11 D2). For Baralta the label 'Categorical Imperative' is still wired as a live Ob driver (−1 Ob on precedent/constitutional action, +1 Ob on ad hoc action) in BOTH npc_behavior_v30 §2.3 and factions_personal_v30, with no SUPERSEDED marker. 'Categorical Imperative' is explicitly a registered legacy label in alias_registry.yaml (philosophical_tradition_legacy → PP-684). This is the exact D2/PP-684 §6 violation pattern.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.3 L93 ('Ethical Framework | Categorical Imperative (Hafenmark) | Aligned: −1 Ob ... Contradictory: +1 Ob'); designs/provincial/factions_personal_v30.md L196-200 ('Ethical Framework: Categorical Imperative' + −1/+1 Ob); references/alias_registry.yaml L600-606 (philosophical_tradition_legacy incl. 'Categorical Imperative', canonical→PP-684) + L616-619 ('Ethical Framework Modifier'→PP-686); character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 L427
- **Fix:** In both docs, demote 'Categorical Imperative' to a descriptive disposition tag and append the standard '[SUPERSEDED → mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]' note; route the −1/+1 Ob modifiers through PP-686 §3.7 rather than the label, OR explicitly cite SUPERSESSION-PP686-001/002 if the inline modifiers are intentionally retained as engine-live.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced in both cited docs. npc_behavior_v30.md §2.3 L93: 'Ethical Framework | Categorical Imperative (Hafenmark) | Aligned: −1 Ob ... Contradictory: +1 Ob' — live mechanical modifier, no SUPERSEDED marker. factions_personal_v30.md L196-200: 'Ethical Framework: Categorical Imperative' with −1/+1 Ob, no marker. alias_registry.yaml L600-613 (philosophical_tradition_legacy) lists 'Categorical Imperative' as a legacy label superseded by PP-684, and L614-619 maps the Ethical Framework Modifier to PP-686/SUPERSESSION-PP686-001. character_canon §11 D2 (L427) defines exactly this pattern (label SUPERSEDED but 'still embedded in npc_behavior §2 and mechanically active via Ob modifiers') and prescribes the '[SUPERSEDED → PP-686 §3.7]' note. The finding's incidental mention of 'Divine Command' is moot to this finding's mechanism. The Categorical Imperative mechanical-driver violation is real in both docs.

#### AUD-NPC2-018 — Vaynard Arc B opens a 'Varfell-Restoration co-victory path' — a victory mechanic that is not Peninsular Sovereignty
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** GD-1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's Arc B (The Awakened) states his resources 'become available to Thread-restoration efforts' and that a 'Varfell-Restoration co-victory path opens'. GD-1 makes Peninsular Sovereignty the SOLE victory condition and explicitly STRIKES all co-victory/shared-victory mechanics (the violation test names 'co-victory' directly). The same infill also re-uses the struck 'co-victory' frame for Almud and for a generic 'Co-victory cooperation' tree.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md line 98 ('Varfell-Restoration co-victory path opens'); same file line 67 ('Crown-Restoration co-victory enabler') and line 180 ('Co-victory cooperation ...'); violates canon/02_canon_constraints.md GD-1 (line 38, violation test: 'Does any "co-victory" or "shared victory" mechanic exist? If yes → FAIL').
- **Fix:** Strike the 'co-victory path' language from Vaynard Arc B (and the Almud/generic co-victory references). Reframe the Awakened outcome as tactical leverage toward the single Peninsular Sovereignty goal (an alliance/asset shift), never as an alternate or shared win condition, per GD-1's 'tactical leverage, not alternate paths' clause.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md: line 98 'Varfell-Restoration co-victory path opens' (Vaynard Arc B), line 67 'Crown-Restoration co-victory enabler' (Almud), line 180 'Co-victory cooperation: When co-victory conditions are >=75% met ...'. GD-1 (canon/02_canon_constraints.md line 38) makes Peninsular Sovereignty the sole victory condition and its violation test explicitly fails on 'Does any co-victory or shared victory mechanic exist?'. I checked body-vs-infill specifically to try to refute: npc_behavior_v30.md body has 0 'co-victory' occurrences vs 3 in the infill — so the finding's evidence attribution (it cites the infill) is accurate. The infill is part of the canonical behavior doc set, so this is a live GD-1 violation. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-019 — Behavior §2.4 stance triangle re-frames Vaynard as a knowledge-collector, contradicting the ratified military/opportunist-conqueror identity
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 + ED-787 (2026-05-03) + ED-707 (open) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The TTRPG stance triangle for Vaynard is built entirely on the struck knowledge-collector identity. Primary Conviction = Reason ("Thread reality is the most important discovery. Knowledge is power"); Beliefs are about understanding/using Thread knowledge; Secondary Resonant Style note calls him a "collector"; TS line introduces a "Private Collection" mechanic. CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 ratified him as a military conqueror (Reinhardt von Lohengramm) who 'does not convert populations ideologically', and ED-787 (Jordan signoff 2026-05-03) further ruled Varfell is 'opportunist/revolutionary ... not intelligence-themed'. The collector/knowledge-seeker framing is decanonized but still live here.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md lines 109-124 (§2.4 table: Primary Conviction Reason, Beliefs 1-3, line 115 'He is a collector', line 116 'Private Collection'); contradicts canon/supersession_register.yaml CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (lines 51-66) and canon/03_canonical_timeline.md line 144 ('not specialist intelligence-themed — see ED-787'); ED-707 OPEN in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl line 442.
- **Fix:** Rewrite §2.4 to the canonical anchor: primary driver is expulsion of Church + Altonian residue and breaking the Einhir caste, pursued by military/opportunist means (von Lohengramm 'cunning in service of pride', per character_canon_v30.md line 411). Replace 'Reason/Knowledge is power' Conviction and the collector/Private-Collection framing; keep Primary Resonant Style = Consequence (consequentialist), drop the 'collector' justification on Secondary Evidence.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.4: line 111 Primary Conviction 'Reason' / 'Knowledge is power'; Beliefs 1-3 (lines 120-122) all knowledge-acquisition themed; line 115 secondary-Evidence hook 'He is a collector; present something worth more than what he is sacrificing'; line 116 'Private Collection' mechanic. Decanonized by THREE canonical sources: (a) supersession_register CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (lines 51-66) ratifies Vaynard as a von Lohengramm military conqueror who 'does not convert populations ideologically' / 'Varfell expansion is purely military conquest', and its files_to_recheck (line 64) explicitly lists npc_behavior_v30.md (flagged for propagation, not absorbed); (b) canon/03_canonical_timeline.md line 144 (LIVE) records 'opportunist/revolutionary; not specialist intelligence-themed — see ED-787'; (c) designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md line 256 states Vaynard 'Was tied to Cultural Reformation (struck) and VTM (struck) — Conviction profile needs clean redraft' to Authority+Honor+Identity/pragmatic militarist. ED-707 is genuinely OPEN (editorial_ledger line 442). Tried to refute via ED-787 closure, but ED-787 reinforces the timeline anchor rather than rescuing the collector framing. Minor line-pointer slack (header is line 109; 'Private Collection' is line 116) is immaterial. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-020 — Foils doc frames Vaynard as an Einhir cultural-revolutionary running an intelligence apparatus — the struck identity, twice over
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 + ED-787 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The foils analysis describes Vaynard as 'revolutionary restoration ... wants full cultural restoration including Thread work', running an 'intelligence apparatus'/'intelligence network', with Conviction 'Einhir restoration / Revolutionary competence' and TS 'Environmental (southern Einhir baseline)'. This is the lingering knowledge-collector/Einhir-revolutionary framing the strike removed: CR-STRIKE says Vaynard 'does not convert populations ideologically' and 'Varfell expansion is purely military conquest'; ED-787 says 'not intelligence-themed'. Cultural-restoration-via-Thread-work and a secret intelligence service are both off-canon for the conqueror identity.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md line 15 ('revolutionary against the settlement'), line 19 ('revolutionary restoration ... full cultural restoration including Thread work'), line 26 ('the only knowledge that matters'), lines 41/60/77/99/125 (Vaynard 'intelligence apparatus'/'intelligence network'/'intelligence operations'), line 102 ('Conviction: Einhir restoration / Revolutionary competence ... TS: Environmental'); contradicts canon/supersession_register.yaml lines 51-66 and canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl line 522 (ED-787).
- **Fix:** Per ED-707's stated scope (designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md is listed in CR-STRIKE files_to_recheck; extend to foils), strip 'cultural restoration including Thread work', 'intelligence apparatus/network', and the 'collector of knowledge' framing from the Vaynard foil pairings. Recast his project as military/opportunist expulsion of Church + Altonian residue and caste-breaking. Reconcile with §2.4 once that is fixed.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced in designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md: line 19 'revolutionary restoration ... wants full cultural restoration including Thread work'; line 26 'the only knowledge that matters'; line 51 Lenneth-Vaynard 'They agree on the diagnosis: the caste is wrong, Einhir heritage should be restored'; line 102 'Conviction: Einhir restoration / Revolutionary competence ... TS: Environmental'. The cultural-restoration + ideological-Conviction core is genuinely struck (CR-STRIKE + migration_roster line 256). Foils self-declare 'canonical per ED-393 through ED-401' (line 8), all predating CR-STRIKE 2026-04-19, and no supersession entry rescues the foils characterization — so the stale framing is live. CAVEAT (partial refutation): the 'intelligence apparatus/network' half (lines 41/60/77/99/125) is OVERSTATED as off-canon — ED-787 (CLOSED, editorial_ledger line 522) RESTORED Varfell Intel stat to 4 (not removed); CR-STRIKE explicitly preserves 'Thread operations remain as personal-scale actions available to Varfell first'; Maret Uln is live canon as Vaynard's 'intelligence operative' (npc_roster_v30.md line 30). The struck item is the ideological-cultural-conversion PRIMARY identity, not the existence of intelligence ops. HIGH core stands (the Einhir-restoration Conviction + cultural-restoration-via-Thread-work is a real ratified-canon contradiction); the fix should target the Conviction/cultural-revival substance, not strip all intelligence framing.

#### AUD-NPC2-021 — Struck Vaynard Thread Mastery (VTM) track is still invoked as live mechanics in the roster
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard Thread Mastery (VTM) was STRUCK entirely on 2026-04-19 (no canonical advancement mechanic ever existed; it was a placeholder). The behavior doc itself records the strike (PP-663: 'VTM gate replaced', 'VTM advancement removed', 'Varfell unique clock changed from VTM to WR'). But the roster's Maret Uln / Varfell Succession entries still drive live effects off VTM: 'VTM resets', 'VTM resets to 0', 'VTM → 0'. This is a stale struck mechanic presented as canonical, and it contradicts the behavior doc's own WR-based replacement.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md line 33 ('VTM resets to 0'), line 239 ('VTM resets, Varfell realigns'), line 240 ('Effect: ... VTM → 0'); strike at canon/supersession_register.yaml VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (lines 37-49); replacement evidenced in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md lines 832, 836, 1144, 1146, 1177 (PP-663 WR substitution).
- **Fix:** Replace the three VTM references in npc_roster_v30.md with the canonical Varfell clock (WR) or, if the succession effect is purely narrative reorientation, remove the VTM mechanic entirely. Align with the PP-663 WR substitution already applied in npc_behavior_v30.md §8.5.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim in designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md: line 33 '(VTM resets to 0)', line 239 'VTM resets, Varfell realigns', line 240 'Effect: ... VTM -> 0'. VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (supersession_register lines 37-49) struck VTM entirely as a placeholder with no canonical advancement mechanic. Replacement is evidenced in npc_behavior_v30.md §8.5: line 832 '(PP-663: VTM gate replaced with embedded practitioner check)' and line 836 '(PP-663: VTM advancement removed)'. So the roster presents a struck mechanic as live and contradicts the behavior doc's own WR/practitioner-check replacement. VTM also persists in designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md, but the finding's specific three roster lines reproduce exactly. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-022 — Conviction vector uses three legacy/superseded labels (Reason, Autonomy, Continuity) as live canon with no [SUPERSEDED->] annotation
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy; alias_registry philosophical_tradition_legacy | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's personal_convictions are stated as Primary 'Reason' / Secondary 'Autonomy' (and Arc B shifts Reason->Continuity, Arc C Reason->collapse). None of Reason, Autonomy, or Continuity is among the canonical 13 Convictions; per PP-684 they are deprecated (Reason->Scholastic/Authority, Autonomy->Liberty, Continuity->Warden). The same document explicitly states the 9-set incl. these labels is superseded, yet the Vaynard entry still uses them as live values with no per-entry [SUPERSEDED->] note.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:111-112 (Primary 'Reason', Secondary 'Autonomy'); same file line 30 ('legacy 9-Conviction set (Faith, Order, Reason, Equity, Precedent, Autonomy, Continuity, Community, Warden) ... is superseded. Reason and Continuity are deprecated labels; Autonomy is renamed Liberty'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:93,97,101 (arc maps: 'Reason remains primary', 'Reason -> Continuity', 'Reason -> collapses'); designs/provincial/factions_personal_v30.md:254 ('Conviction: Reason'); references/glossary/alias_registry philosophical_tradition_legacy
- **Fix:** Re-express Vaynard's vector in the 13-Conviction taxonomy. Adopt the migration-roster PRIMARY mapping (Authority + Honor + Identity, lowenritter_military, +0.20 — see next finding) OR, if the 'Reason' knowledge-seeker conception is retained, map Reason->Scholastic and Autonomy->Liberty and add [SUPERSEDED-> per PP-684] traceability. Arc-shift targets (Continuity) must become Warden. Resolve which conception is canonical first (see contradiction finding).
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md:111-112 lists Primary 'Reason' / Secondary 'Autonomy' with no annotation; the same file's :30 declares the 9-set superseded ('Reason and Continuity are deprecated labels; Autonomy is renamed Liberty'); alias_registry.yaml:600-613 (philosophical_tradition_legacy) confirms 'Reason (legacy tag)' and 'Continuity (legacy tag)' are superseded by the 13-Conviction taxonomy; infill arc maps at :93/:97/:101 propagate 'Reason remains primary / Reason -> Continuity / Reason -> collapses'. These are deprecated labels used as live values, and Conviction is mechanically load-bearing (it keys the Resonant-Style contest mapping in s1.3). HIGH stands. ONE CORRECTION to the finding's FIX: the proposed migration target (Authority+Honor+Identity from migration-roster s3) is itself stale -- references/npc_registry.yaml:710-714 and :1047-1051 record a later Jordan decision (2026-05-08) re-mapping Vaynard to Equity/Utility and explicitly superseding the Authority/Honor/Identity default. The defect is real; only the prescribed replacement vector is wrong.

#### AUD-NPC2-023 — Ethical Framework 'Utility-driven Pragmatism' is still a LIVE mechanical Ob driver with no [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] note
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 -> PP-686 §3.7; character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Ethical Framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers (PP-684 §6; mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc) and retained only as descriptive disposition tags per character_canon §11 D2. Vaynard's sheet still uses 'Utility-driven Pragmatism (Varfell)' as a LIVE Ob driver (Aligned: -1 Ob on measurable-outcome actions; Contradictory: +1 Ob on uncertain/long-term actions) with no [SUPERSEDED->] annotation, and the arc docs keep invoking it as a live mechanical state ('Utility-driven Pragmatism remains stable', 'Cannot execute Utility-driven Pragmatism').
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:113 ('Ethical Framework | Utility-driven Pragmatism (Varfell) | Aligned: -1 Ob ...; Contradictory: +1 Ob ...'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:134,170,173 (framework treated as live mechanical state across Arc A/C); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:427 (§11 D2 — framework superseded per PP-684 §6, mechanical role -> PP-686 §3.7, retain as descriptive tag with [SUPERSEDED->] note)
- **Fix:** Per D2: demote 'Utility-driven Pragmatism' to a descriptive disposition tag and append '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]'. Recompute the -1/+1 Ob effects via the PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc from the (corrected) conviction vector rather than the framework label. Same correction needed in arc_expansion_v30 Arc A/B/C.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md:113 carries 'Ethical Framework | Utility-driven Pragmatism (Varfell) | Aligned: -1 Ob on ... measurable outcomes ...; Contradictory: +1 Ob on uncertain/long-term ...' with no supersession annotation. character_canon_v30.md:427 (s11 D2) is explicit that Ethical Framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers per PP-684 s6, mechanical role moved to PP-686 s3.7 triadic Ob calc, and must be retained only as a descriptive tag carrying a '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 s3.7 triadic Ob calc]' note. alias_registry.yaml:614-619 corroborates (ethical_framework_modifier -> triadic decomposition, PP-686). arc_expansion_v30.md:134/170/173 keep invoking the framework as a live mechanical state ('Utility-driven Pragmatism remains stable', 'Cannot execute Utility-driven Pragmatism'). The missing annotation and the live +/-1 Ob driver are exactly as described. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-024 — Secondary Resonant Style 'Evidence' is defined as Thread-level data against a TS-14 NPC, and Arc A makes it primary while still TS<30 — no TS>=30 gate
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 (Thread-level evidence epistemically inert vs TS<30); ED-516 Resonant Styles; social_contest_v30 §2.3 visibility / §9.4b | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's Secondary Resonant Style 'Evidence' is explicitly 'Thread-level data he cannot explain within his current TK level' while his TS is 14 (Dormant). Per P-08 / social_contest visibility (TS 0-9 nothing; TS 10-29 vague unease only; TS 30+ perceives the operation), Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert against a sub-30 NPC. The §2.4 baseline treats Thread-Evidence as a usable vector with no TS>=30 gate, and Arc A doubles down ('Evidence becomes primary ... only harder evidence — Thread-level data ... reaches him') while the branch condition holds TS below 30. Arc B does add the correct gate ('TS crosses Stirring (30) ... Evidence gains a new vector'), so the system is internally inconsistent: the gate exists in Arc B but is ignored at baseline and in Arc A.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:115-116 (Secondary RS Evidence = 'Thread-level data'; TS 14 Dormant); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:92,95 (Arc A: TS 'below 30', yet 'Evidence becomes primary ... Thread-level data ... reaches him'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:96-99 (Arc B: TS crosses 30 -> Evidence gains a new vector — the correct gate); designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md:471 ('TS 0-9 perceives nothing; TS 10-29 perceives vague unease; TS 30+ perceives the operation'); social_contest_v30.md:483 (Thread Insight TS >= 30 only); canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md P-08
- **Fix:** Gate Thread-level Evidence behind TS>=30 everywhere it is offered against Vaynard. At TS 14 (baseline) and in Arc A (TS<30), Thread-level Evidence must be inert; only non-Thread Evidence (mundane data, ordinary outcomes) can move him until TS crosses Stirring. Make §2.4 and Arc A consistent with the Arc B gate.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced; the internal inconsistency is real. npc_behavior_v30.md:115-116 defines Secondary RS Evidence as 'present Thread-level data he cannot explain' with TS 14 (Dormant). npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:92,95 (Arc A) holds 'TS remains below 30' yet says 'Evidence becomes primary ... Only harder evidence -- Thread-level data he cannot explain -- reaches him', i.e. Thread-level evidence moving a sub-30 NPC. infill:96 (Arc B) supplies the correct gate ('TS crosses Stirring (30) ... Evidence gains a new vector -- he can now cite Thread perception as evidence'). The governing rule is in social_contest_v30.md:471 (TS 0-9 perceives nothing; 10-29 vague unease; 30+ perceives the operation) and :483 (Thread Insight TS>=30 only), grounded in P-08 (Thread-level evidence epistemically inert vs TS<30). So the Arc B gate exists but is ignored at baseline and in Arc A -- a genuine self-contradiction with mechanical consequence for what can move him. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-025 — Two incompatible character conceptions share the name — secular Thread-knowledge collector vs Reinhard-coded anti-Church military conqueror
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** P-02/P-04 (no moral framing); character_canon_v30.md §11 D1; supersession_register CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.4 / factions_personal portray Vaynard as a 'secular intellectual' Thread-knowledge COLLECTOR (Primary Reason; Beliefs about understanding/using Thread knowledge; 'collector'). npc_character_analyses §5, npc_foils, the migration-roster §3 default, and the supersession register portray a different person: a Reinhard von Lohengramm-coded MILITARY CONQUEROR and anti-Church/anti-caste REVOLUTIONARY (Authority/Honor/Identity; goal = expel the Church and Altonian residue; 'Vaynard does not convert populations ideologically. Varfell expansion is purely military conquest'). The Beliefs, Convictions, Resonant Style rationale, and historical anchor diverge across docs for the same NPC.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:111-122 (collector/Reason; Beliefs 1-3 about Thread knowledge); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:91-99 (§5 'The Revolutionary'; 'Reinhard von Lohengramm'; 'expelled', anti-Church/anti-caste); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:149 ('Tear down Church and caste entirely'); designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30.md:26 ('expulsion (the Church ... must be removed entirely)'); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:256 (Authority+Honor+Identity militarist); canon/supersession_register.yaml:56-57 (CR-STRIKE: 'military conqueror ... Varfell expansion is purely military conquest')
- **Fix:** Reconcile to a single canon conception. The most-recent gated decision (CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 + migration §3 default) points to the militarist/revolutionary conviction profile (Authority/Honor/Identity); the 'Reason collector' stat block and Beliefs in npc_behavior §2.4 / factions_personal predate it and should be rewritten to match (or the militarist framing formally overturned). Beliefs 1-3 in npc_behavior §2.4 (Thread-knowledge framing) are inconsistent with the 'expel the Church' Beliefs implied by §5 and need a single authored set.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and the central contradiction is genuine. npc_behavior_v30.md:111-122 portrays a 'secular intellectual' Thread-knowledge 'collector' (Primary Reason; Beliefs 1-3 framed around understanding/using Thread knowledge; :117 'hypothesis he is testing'). The conqueror conception is in npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:91-99 ('The Revolutionary', 'Reinhard von Lohengramm', wants Church + Altonian residue 'Expelled'), npc_character_analyses_v30.md:149 ('Tear down Church and caste entirely'), npc_foils_v30.md:26 ('expulsion ... removed entirely'), and canon/supersession_register.yaml:56-57 ('military conqueror ... Varfell expansion is purely military conquest'). These give materially different Beliefs/value-frame for the same NPC. The 2026-05-08 registry decision (Equity/Utility, 'revolutionary aristocrat ... any-cost pragmatism', self/other -0.40) lands on the conqueror/revolutionary side and confirms the s2.4 'Reason collector' stat block is the stale half -- so the conflict is real and the resolution direction the finding gives (militarist/revolutionary wins, collector block must be rewritten) is correct. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-026 — Primary/Secondary Conviction in the stat block use legacy labels (Continuity / Reason) as live canon with no [SUPERSEDED->] annotation
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 / conviction_taxonomy_v30.md §1; alias_registry philosophical_tradition_legacy | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Edeyja's authoritative stance-triangle stat block lists Primary Conviction = Continuity and Secondary Conviction = Reason. Both are legacy/superseded tags under the 13-Conviction taxonomy (Continuity->Warden; Reason->Scholastic/Authority). They are presented as live mechanical values driving the BG Priority Tree (Priority 2 'Conviction-critical', Priority 5 'Conviction-secondary') with no supersession marker.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.9 lines 212-213 ('Primary Conviction | Continuity', 'Secondary Conviction | Reason'); contrast canon designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md lines 270/282-283 ('Reason (legacy tag)', 'Continuity (legacy tag)') and references/alias_registry.yaml philosophical_tradition_legacy block (legacy: 'Reason (legacy tag)','Continuity (legacy tag)').
- **Fix:** Replace with the 13-Conviction value. The dedicated sheet (edeyja_npc.md line 30) and character_canon §3.1 already canonize Warden as primary. Migrate §2.9 to Primary = Warden; Secondary = Scholastic (or Authority) per the Reason->Scholastic/Authority alias, and add a '<- was Continuity/Reason' traceability note per character_canon §11 D1.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md lines 212-213 read 'Primary Conviction | Continuity' and 'Secondary Conviction | Reason' as bare table values, no supersession marker. conviction_taxonomy_v30.md lines 282-283 list both as '(legacy tag)' superseded by PP-684, and alias_registry.yaml philosophical_tradition_legacy (lines 603-613) lists 'Reason (legacy tag)' and 'Continuity (legacy tag)' under legacy:, patch PP-684. They are read as live mechanical drivers by the §8.1 Priority Tree template (line 778 Priority 2 'Conviction-critical | Leader Conviction', line 781 Priority 5 'Conviction-secondary'). Migration roster (PP-685) is live canon (status CANONICAL) and D1 mandates legacy values be shown only as '<- was [old]'. One caveat on the fix, not the finding: the finding's fix text claims 'character_canon §3.1 already canonize Warden as primary' — false. §3.1 (line 97) is just the generic 13-Conviction list, and character_canon line 263 itself writes 'Continuity Conviction' for the Edeyja MS-override mechanism, so the canon doc is also mixed. Core finding stands; HIGH retained.

#### AUD-NPC2-027 — Arc C transition names a superseded Conviction (Autonomy) as the live arc destination
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 / conviction_taxonomy_v30.md; alias_registry | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Edeyja's Arc C ('The Crisis') sets 'Conviction: Warden -> Autonomy'. Autonomy is a legacy 9-set label superseded by Liberty (per the crisis-table remap in D8 and alias_registry). Using it as the named end-state of a canonical arc transition propagates a dead label into live arc mechanics.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2 line 644 ('Conviction: Warden -> Autonomy (the work has failed; she must survive to try again).'); supersession basis character_canon_v30.md §11 D8 line 433 ('Crisis-table d6 entry 5: Autonomy remapped to Liberty per PP-684 §6 alias_registry').
- **Fix:** Change the Arc C destination to 'Warden -> Liberty' per the canonical Autonomy->Liberty remap, with a traceability note. Confirm the narrative intent (self-preservation / freedom-to-try-again) still reads under Liberty, which it does.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md line 644: 'Conviction: Warden -> Autonomy (the work has failed; she must survive to try again).' character_canon_v30.md D8 (line 433) states 'Crisis-table d6 entry 5: Autonomy remapped to Liberty per PP-684 §6 alias_registry.' Autonomy is not in the 13-Conviction set (taxonomy §2, lines 29-41); Liberty (#7) is its canonical replacement. This is a live arc-mechanic label in the primary behavior doc, not an editorial/stale reference, so HIGH is defensible. Minor note: D8 also records that the SOURCE crisis-table file's stale taxonomy is 'preserved as historical reference until source-file propagation cycle runs' — but that grace applies to conviction_track_v1.md, not to a freshly-authored Arc destination in npc_behavior, so it does not excuse this instance. HIGH retained.

#### AUD-NPC2-028 — Conviction value conflicts across docs: Warden (dedicated sheet + canon recap) vs Continuity (behavior stat block)
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 (migration roster is PRIMARY); conviction_taxonomy_v30.md §1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The dedicated canonical sheet states Conviction = Warden ('The work must continue... the peninsula survives only because the wardens hold'); the behavior stat block states Primary = Continuity with the identical gloss. These are the same character concept rendered under two different taxonomies, left unreconciled. D1 designates the migration roster as the tiebreaker when conviction sources conflict, but Edeyja is absent from it (see separate finding), so the conflict has no canonical resolver and the two live docs simply disagree.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md line 30 ('Conviction: Warden. The work must continue regardless of who or what interferes...') vs designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.9 line 212 ('Primary Conviction | Continuity | The work must continue...'); resolution authority character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 line 426.
- **Fix:** Standardize on Warden (the post-PP-684 value already carried by the dedicated sheet and the §3.1 recap). Rewrite §2.9 and all behavior-doc Continuity references for Edeyja to Warden. Note that Warden = 'stewardship of the dependent and the vulnerable' (taxonomy line 39) is the correct mechanical home for the warden-duty gloss.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. edeyja_npc.md line 30 'Conviction: Warden. The work must continue regardless of who or what interferes...' vs npc_behavior_v30.md line 212 'Primary Conviction | Continuity | The work must continue...' — same gloss, two taxonomies, unreconciled. The conflict is broader than the finding states: the Certainty roster line 1197 labels her 'Warden', the infill Arc Map line 105 says 'Warden remains primary', yet §2.9 line 212 and Arc D line 649 ('Continuity primary unchanged') and Arc C line 644 ('Warden -> Autonomy') mix both within the same §5.2 block. D1 (line 426) names conviction_migration_roster_v30 as the PRIMARY tiebreaker, and Edeyja is absent from it (verified — see next finding), so no canonical resolver exists. HIGH retained. (The fix's reference to a 'canon recap' carrying Warden is loose — no such Edeyja-specific recap exists in character_canon — but the Warden value is genuinely carried by the dedicated sheet, so the conflict is real.)

#### AUD-NPC2-029 — Edeyja is entirely absent from the PRIMARY conviction source (migration roster PP-685) and from the axis matrix
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2; character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Per D1, conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) is the designated PRIMARY when conviction values conflict. Edeyja - one of the most load-bearing NPCs (highest TS in the setting, moral anchor, Warden-Chief) - has no entry in the roster at all, nor in its companion conviction_axis_matrix_v30. The roster enumerates 13 named characters and she is not among them, so the very document that is supposed to adjudicate her Continuity-vs-Warden conflict is silent. She also therefore has no authored 13-element weighted personal_convictions vector (no primary weights, no cultural_label, no self_other_initial), which character_canon §3.2 requires for every NPC.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2 lines 39-245 (13 entries: Almud, Cesare, Lorenzo, Yrsa Vossen, Maret Uln, Solmund, Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth, Hafenmark Speaker, Lowenritter Master, Restoration Spokesperson, Niflhel envoy - no Edeyja) and §3 edge-case table lines 253-258 (no Edeyja); grep of conviction_axis_matrix_v30.md returns no Edeyja; requirement at character_canon_v30.md §3.2 lines 103-113.
- **Fix:** Add an Edeyja entry to conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2 with prior_labels: [Continuity, Reason], primary_convictions e.g. {Warden, 0.45}+{Scholastic, 0.25}, cultural_label: einhir_traditional (the template exists per character_canon §3.3 line 119), self_other_initial negative (selfless holdout archetype). This makes her conviction value canonical and unblocks D1 as the conflict resolver.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2 contains exactly 13 named entries (Almud, Cesare, Lorenzo, Yrsa Vossen, Maret Uln, Solmund, Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth, Hafenmark Speaker, Lowenritter Master, Restoration Spokesperson, Niflhel envoy) — no Edeyja; §3 edge-case table lists only Solmund, Vaynard, Vossler, Reinholt — no Edeyja. grep 'Edeyja' over both conviction_migration_roster_v30.md and conviction_axis_matrix_v30.md returns zero matches. character_canon §3.2 (lines 103-113) requires every NPC carry a 13-element personal_convictions vector with primaries summing 0.6-0.8 + cultural template; Edeyja has none. Because D1 makes this the PRIMARY conflict-resolver, her absence is what leaves the Warden/Continuity conflict unresolvable. einhir_traditional template the fix proposes does exist (§3.3 line 119). HIGH retained.

#### AUD-NPC2-030 — Almud Arc A is labeled a 'Crown-Restoration co-victory enabler' and §7.9 defines a co-victory priority-tree override
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** GD-1 (canon/02_canon_constraints.md:38 — Peninsular Sovereignty is the SOLE victory condition; violation test: 'Does any co-victory or shared victory mechanic exist? If yes -> FAIL') | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** GD-1 strikes all faction-specific/alternate/co-victory paths. Almud's Reformer arc is described as a 'Crown-Restoration co-victory enabler,' and the Priority Tree Override section defines a 'Co-victory cooperation' rule that switches cooperating factions to a simplified tree when 'co-victory conditions are >= 75% met.' Both are live behavioral/mechanical commitments to a victory mode GD-1 prohibits.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:67 ('Almud supports practitioner rights, contacts Restoration Movement ... Crown-Restoration co-victory enabler'); :180 (§7.9 '**Co-victory cooperation:** When co-victory conditions are >= 75% met, cooperating factions shift to a simplified tree ...'). Same legacy text at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_system_v1.md:422. PRECISION: this lives in the infill (which is canonical per the doc set), NOT the body — a content search of npc_behavior_v30.md returns zero 'co-victory' hits (body §5.2 Almud arcs and §7.9 carry no co-victory text), so do not attribute it to the body.
- **Fix:** Strike the 'co-victory' framing in infill L67 and L180. Reframe Almud Arc A as Crown-RM alliance/cooperation that advances the single Peninsular Sovereignty condition (one faction still wins), and replace the §7.9 'Co-victory cooperation' override with an alliance-coordination rule that does not imply shared victory. Cross-ref GD-1.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:67 ('Crown-Restoration co-victory enabler') and :180 (§7.9 '**Co-victory cooperation:** When co-victory conditions are >= 75% met, cooperating factions shift to a simplified tree...'). Same legacy text at npc_behavior_system_v1.md:422. Precision note verified: grep for co-victory in npc_behavior_v30.md returns 0 hits, so the body is clean and this is infill-only. GD-1 (canon/02_canon_constraints.md:38) explicitly STRIKES PP-418 Co-Victory and its violation test reads 'Does any co-victory or shared victory mechanic exist? If yes -> FAIL.' The infill is canonical per the doc set, and a named 'co-victory' mechanic with a numeric threshold is a live mechanical commitment, not a stale gloss. HIGH confirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-031 — Almud TS is canonically 0 but three docs still carry the superseded value 28
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Per-NPC TS canonicity (character_canon_v30 §7.1 L248: 'TS is canonical per NPC ... most reliable single dimension'); canon/03_canonical_timeline.md 2026-04-11 override; P-08 boundary | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Almud's Thread Sensitivity is given as 28 ('near Stirring') in his Stance Triangle and in two Knot-strain exclusion notes, but as 0 in the canonical Stat Values table, both character analyses, the foils, and — decisively — the canonical timeline, which logs the explicit 2026-04-11 override 'Earlier: Almud TS 28 -> Almud TS 0.' 28 is the stale pre-override value; the docs carrying it were never propagated. The discrepancy is load-bearing: TS gates Evidence-Resonant-Style validity (P-08), the Discovery/rupture arc framing, and §5.0b knot-strain applicability.
- **Evidence:** Stale TS 28: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:55 (§2.1 'Thread Sensitivity | 28 (near Stirring)'); :528 (§5.0b 'Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing in any arc)'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:337 ('Does NOT apply to Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing)'). Canonical TS 0: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1186 (§7.10 'Almud | 0 | 3'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:146,166 ('TS 0'; 'If Almud's TS crosses 0'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:72 ('TS 0 ... no Thread experience ... no secret Einhir interiority'); canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:138,185-186.
- **Fix:** Resolve to TS 0 (the canonical-timeline override value, the analyses' explicit position, and the §7.10 canonical cell). Correct npc_behavior_v30 §2.1 (L55) and §5.0b (L528) and character_canon_v30 (L337) to TS 0, and drop 'near Stirring'. Recast any 'TS 28->30 Stirring crossing' framing as the canonical 0->positive Discovery-Event rupture. (Note: prior audit AUD-NPC2-016/028/047 left the direction ambiguous; the timeline override + §7.1 'TS is canonical' rule make TS 0 the defensible resolution.)
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced from files. Stale 'TS 28': npc_behavior_v30.md:55 (§2.1 'Thread Sensitivity | 28 (near Stirring)'), :528 (§5.0b 'Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing)'), character_canon_v30.md:337 (same). Canonical TS 0: npc_behavior_v30.md:1186 (§7.10 'Almud | 0'), npc_character_analyses_v30.md:146 and :166, npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:72 ('TS 0 ... no Thread experience ... no secret Einhir interiority'). Decisive override at canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:185 ('Earlier: Almud TS 28 -> Almud TS 0. 2026-04-11') and :138 (Key Figures 'TS 0'). The contradiction is live and load-bearing (gates P-08 Evidence-RS validity and §5.0b knot-strain applicability). tests/sim/sim_companions_2026-04-16.md:22/814 independently logs it as GAP-SIM-C-01. One minor evidence inaccuracy: the foils doc carries no explicit numeric TS for Almud (grep for TS/Thread Sensitivity returned no Almud value), so 'the foils' should be dropped from the TS-0 anchor list — but this does not weaken the core contradiction across the other six docs. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-032 — Three-way Thread Sensitivity conflict for Almud (TS 0 vs TS 28) — including an internal contradiction inside npc_behavior_v30 itself
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** CERTAINTY/BELIEFS dimension; P-08 Evidence gate; npc_behavior_v30 §6.1b / §5.0b | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Almud's TS is canonically inconsistent across the stack and even within a single file. npc_behavior_v30 §2.1 states TS 28 ('near Stirring'); §5.0b in the SAME file states 'Almud (TS 28...)'; but §7.10 of the SAME file states TS 0 'per ED-398.' npc_character_analyses_v30 (both skeleton and infill) firmly states TS 0 with the authored rationale 'no secret Einhir interiority... never experienced anything that would make him question the Church.' character_canon_v30 §8.2 and npc_registry NPC-020 carry TS 28. ED-398 (the cited authority) resolved Almud at TS 0. This is the most load-bearing single axis (TS) and it is internally contradictory in the primary behavior doc.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.1 line 55 (TS 28), §5.0b line 528 (TS 28), §7.10 line 1186 (TS 0 per ED-398); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md line 146 (TS 0) and §1 line 153; npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md line 72 (TS 0); references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-020 line 345 (ts 28); archives/session/session_log_archive_part_2.md line 451 (ED-398 TS 0)
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical TS and propagate. Note these encode different characters: TS 0 = the authored 'fully Church-insulated, no Thread interiority' Almud (ED-398, character_analyses); TS 28 = 'near-Stirring, proximate to awareness.' If TS 28 is intended, formally supersede ED-398 and fix §7.10; if TS 0 is intended, fix §2.1/§5.0b/character_canon/npc_registry. Do not ship a file that states both.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and the strongest finding in the set. Within npc_behavior_v30.md alone: §2.1 line 55 states 'Thread Sensitivity 28 (near Stirring)', §5.0b line 528 states 'Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing in any arc)', yet §7.10 line 1186 states 'Almud | 0 | 3 | TS 0 per ED-398.' That is a flat internal contradiction in the primary behavior doc. The cited authority checks out: session_log_archive_part_2.md line 451 reads 'Almud — TS 0. Governance pragmatist + ethical doubt... (ED-398)', and npc_character_analyses_v30.md line 146 (table: TS 0) plus _infill.md line 72 ('TS 0. Northern Einhir... never experienced anything that would make him question the Church') firmly author TS 0. Against this, character_canon_v30 §8.2 line 337 and npc_registry.yaml NPC-020 line 345 carry TS 28. Note ED-687 (editorial_ledger line 428) claims to 'resolve ED-392 through ED-398' but lists no Almud TS value, so it does not actually settle this. The conflict is genuine, spans the canonical registry, and sits on the most load-bearing axis. HIGH affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-033 — Lenneth is MISFILED under §2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction and ABSENT from the Crown faction in the primary conviction roster
- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 §2.1 Sovereign Court (Crown faction) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** In conviction_migration_roster_v30 the only Lenneth entry sits under '§2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)' beside Cardinal Reichard and Baralta, with cultural_label 'ecclesiastical'. The Crown faction section (§2.1 Sovereign Court) contains no Lenneth at all. Lenneth is the Queen Consort / Widow Regent — a Crown NPC. This is the same class of error already flagged for Baralta in character_canon §11 D6 (misfiled under ecclesiastical), but for Lenneth it is unflagged and it is the proximate cause of the wrong conviction vector and the wrong cultural template feeding the engine.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:135 ('### §2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)') with Lenneth at :167-175 (cultural_label: ecclesiastical); §2.1 header at line 39 (Sovereign Court) contains no Lenneth; cf. parallel misfile handling at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:431 (D6, Baralta)
- **Fix:** Move Lenneth to §2.1 Sovereign Court with cultural_label appropriate to the Crown court (not 'ecclesiastical'), and re-author her vector with Equity primary. Add a D6-style [CONFLICT] surface note pending Jordan's ratification.
- **Verifier note:** Verified against the file. The '### §2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)' header is at conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:135; the sole Lenneth entry sits beneath it (167-175) alongside Cardinal Reichard (137) and Baralta (152), with cultural_label 'ecclesiastical' (175). The '### §2.1 Sovereign Court (Crown faction)' header (39) contains only Almud, Cesare, Lorenzo — no Lenneth. Lenneth is the Queen Consort / Widow Regent (a Crown NPC per every narrative doc). character_canon_v30.md:431 (D6) flags exactly this misfile class for Baralta but leaves Lenneth's unflagged. The wrong 'ecclesiastical' cultural template feeds armature/cascade computation, a distinct engine-facing defect beyond the conviction-vector conflict. Note substantial overlap with finding #1 (shared root cause), but the faction/cultural-template misassignment is a separable structural error. HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-034 — Conviction conflicts with the designated PRIMARY source — and the PRIMARY source describes a different person
