- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** D1 / PP-685 (conviction_migration_roster_v30 = PRIMARY) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Every narrative doc gives Lenneth Primary Conviction = Equity (Queen Consort, anti-caste reformer). The designated primary source for conviction VALUES on conflict (conviction_migration_roster_v30, PP-685, per character_canon §11 D1) instead gives her Scholastic 0.40 / Faith 0.20 / Precedent 0.20 with cultural_label 'ecclesiastical' and the note 'Scholarly cleric with TS pathway.' Equity does not appear in her vector at all. Per D1 the migration-roster values win on conflict, which would make canonical Lenneth a Scholastic-primary church scholar — directly contradicting the Equity reformer of every other doc. This is not a weight discrepancy; the two sources describe different characters.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:167-179 (Scholastic/Faith/Precedent, 'scholarly cleric'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:172 ('Primary Conviction: Equity'); :182 ('Conviction: Equity becomes primary Crown doctrine'); D1 rule at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426
- **Fix:** Reconcile to one canonical vector. Given the overwhelming narrative weight (analyses, foils, faction_canon, arcs all = Equity reformer), author a correct Crown-faction Lenneth entry with Equity primary in conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1, and either delete or rename the §2.3 'ecclesiastical scholar' entry. Until corrected, tag the analyses entry [CONFLICT-RESOLVE: Equity chosen — basis: narrative supermajority; migration-roster §2.3 entry is a misfile, see ED-727] per the D1 procedure.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:167-179 gives Lenneth primary_convictions Scholastic 0.40 / Faith 0.20 / Precedent 0.20, cultural_label 'ecclesiastical', notes 'Scholarly cleric with TS pathway' — Equity is absent from her vector. npc_character_analyses_v30.md:172 states 'Primary Conviction: Equity' and :182 'Conviction: Equity becomes primary Crown doctrine'; the foils/infill docs corroborate the Equity reformer throughout. character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1) makes migration-roster values primary on conflict, which would canonize a Scholastic-primary church scholar. This is not a weight discrepancy: a Crown anti-caste Equity reformer vs an ecclesiastical Scholastic cleric are materially different characters. The roster note even cites npc_character_analyses as its basis, confirming the author intended the same person but encoded a divergent vector. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-035 — No Resonant Style defined for a named, contest-relevant NPC — live doc still says 'To be determined'
- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (Resonant Style 4-set) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Lenneth has no Resonant Style anywhere in the 4-set (Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity). The only statement is npc_foils_v30_infill 'Lenneth's View — Resonant Style: To be determined.' She is half of 'the campaign's defining political confrontation' (Lenneth<->Baralta) and her entire default arc is persuasion-through-Almud, i.e. a social-contest engagement — yet the argument form that moves her is unspecified, so the Contest system cannot resolve attempts to move her. (Her foil Almud, by contrast, has explicit Resonant Styles in npc_behavior §5.2.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md:79 ('Resonant Style: To be determined'); no Resonant Style for Lenneth in npc_roster_v30 (only a Torsvald cross-ref), npc_behavior_v30 §2 (no Lenneth block), or npc_character_analyses_v30 §2; contrast Almud's styles at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:566 ('Resonant Style shift: Authority only')
- **Fix:** Assign Lenneth a Primary (and optional Secondary) Resonant Style from the 4-set consistent with an Equity-reformer archive scholar (Evidence and/or Consequence are the natural fit). Replace the 'To be determined' placeholder. If Evidence is chosen, note that Thread-level evidence is inert against her until TS>=30 (P-08) — which her bounded pathway never reaches, so her Evidence vector is institutional/archival evidence only.
- **Verifier note:** Verified exhaustively. npc_foils_v30_infill.md:79 ('Lenneth's View — Resonant Style: To be determined') is the only statement, and that infill is header-tagged 'characterization canonical per ED-393 through ED-401', so the placeholder is live in a canonical-tagged doc. npc_behavior_v30 §2 enumerates named stance triangles §2.1-§2.10 (Almud, Himlensendt, Baralta, Vaynard, Ehrenwall, Yrsa, Hann, Torben, Edeyja, Maret Uln) each with explicit Primary+Secondary Resonant Styles — Lenneth has no §2 block at all. Confirmed zero Resonant Style for Lenneth in roster, behavior, analyses, or character_canon (the latter two via grep). She is a Ruler Diamond principal and explicitly 'half of the campaign's defining political confrontation' (foils_infill:42), and her default arc is persuasion-through-Almud — a social contest the engine cannot resolve without her Style. Every peer principal has one. HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-036 — Live foils doc carries an unresolved Conviction placeholder AND a non-taxonomy label ('Family-institutional')
- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §3.1 (13-element weighted vector) / ED-516 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** npc_foils_v30_infill states 'Lenneth's View — Conviction: To be determined / Family-institutional.' Two violations in one line: (a) an unresolved 'To be determined' placeholder for a Tier-1 named NPC's primary conviction, and (b) 'Family-institutional' is not one of the 13 Convictions (Faith/Authority/Order/Scholastic/Utility/Equity/Liberty/Precedent/Community/Identity/Warden/Virtue/Honor) and carries no [SUPERSEDED->] mapping. This also contradicts the analyses doc's firm 'Primary Conviction: Equity.'
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md:79 ('Conviction: To be determined / Family-institutional'); 13-set recap at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:97; contradicting firm value at designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:172
- **Fix:** Replace with the reconciled canonical vector (Equity primary + weighted secondaries). Remove 'Family-institutional' and the placeholder; if a family/institutional disposition is wanted, express it via Community/Authority/Warden weights, not a freeform label.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_foils_v30_infill.md:79 reads 'Conviction: To be determined / Family-institutional'. The 13-Conviction set (character_canon_v30.md:97: Faith/Authority/Order/Scholastic/Utility/Equity/Liberty/Precedent/Community/Identity/Warden/Virtue/Honor) does not contain 'Family-institutional', and no [SUPERSEDED->] mapping exists for it. The infill is tagged canonical characterization (lines 1-8), so both the placeholder and the freeform non-taxonomy label are live for a Tier-1 principal, and both contradict npc_character_analyses_v30.md:172 ('Primary Conviction: Equity'). The conviction-conflict aspect overlaps finding #1, but the non-taxonomy-label-plus-placeholder is a distinct concrete defect in a different doc. HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-037 — Designated-canonical conviction source describes a fundamentally different Maret (Honor/Authority/Identity warlord) than every Maret-facing design doc (Equity/RM-sympathy)
- **Unit:** Maret Uln | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D1 / PP-685 conviction_migration_roster vs npc_behavior §2.10 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** character_canon_v30 §11 D1 explicitly designates conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) as PRIMARY / most-recent canonical for conviction values. That roster characterizes Maret Uln as Honor 0.30 / Authority 0.30 / Identity 0.20, cultural_label 'lowenritter_military', self_other +0.10, notes 'Military-honor archetype with Identity (Varfell categorical loyalty)... pragmatic warlord rather than self-sacrificing'. This is the OPPOSITE of the canonical-status roster, behavior, and analyses docs, which build her entire identity and arc on Equity-driven RM sympathy, a corroding conscience, and a succession that realigns Varfell TOWARD the Restoration Movement. 'Varfell categorical loyalty' directly negates the 'dual loyalty / will defect to RM' premise. Her whole dramatic function rests on a conviction profile the most-recent canonical source contradicts.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:104-117 (Honor/Authority/Identity; lowenritter_military; 'categorical loyalty'; 'pragmatic warlord'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1 names migration roster PRIMARY; 'behavior says Equity/Reason; migration roster says Honor/Authority/Identity'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:235-245 (§2.10 Primary Conviction Equity, Beliefs RM-aligned); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:33 (Compromise: Dual loyalty, RM sympathy); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:18 (Ethics: Rawlsian / equity)
- **Fix:** Jordan-level reconciliation required. Either (a) update conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.2 Maret entry to the Equity/Reason + RM-sympathy profile her arc depends on (and fix cultural_label from lowenritter_military to a Varfell/Einhir-fjord label), or (b) if Honor/Authority/Identity warlord is the intended canon, rewrite the roster/behavior/analyses arc wholesale. Do not author the character_canon Part B sheet until resolved — D1 currently forces the warlord profile to win.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:104-117 gives Maret Honor 0.30 / Authority 0.30 / Identity 0.20, cultural_label 'lowenritter_military', self_other +0.10, notes 'Military-honor archetype with Identity (Varfell categorical loyalty)... pragmatic warlord'. character_canon_v30.md:426 (§11 D1) verbatim designates conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) as PRIMARY/most-recent canonical and explicitly names this exact conflict ('behavior says Equity/Reason; migration roster says Honor/Authority/Identity'). npc_behavior_v30.md:235-245 (§2.10) gives Primary Conviction Equity, Secondary Reason, RM-aligned Beliefs; roster:33 'Dual loyalty, RM sympathy'; roster_infill:18 'Ethics: Rawlsian / equity'. 'Varfell categorical loyalty' (Identity) directly negates the 'will defect to/realign toward RM' premise her entire arc rests on. D1's tie-break rule forces the warlord profile to win in any Part B sheet. The contradiction is live (D1 is current decision-log canon, migration roster is the designated primary). HIGH stands. Minor caveat: the issue's 'intelligence infrastructure' framing of Vaynard is itself loosely worded vs ED-787/timeline:144 ('Vaynard NOT intelligence-themed; opportunist/revolutionary'), but that does not weaken the conviction contradiction, which is the substance of the finding.

#### AUD-NPC2-038 — Maret's entire succession outcome depends on 'VTM resets to 0', but VTM was struck and never replaced
- **Unit:** Maret Uln | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (supersession_register) / PP-486 / ED-308 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Maret's signature mechanic — on Vaynard's elimination she inherits Varfell and 'VTM resets to 0' (loss of Vaynard's intelligence infrastructure) — is built on Vaynard Thread Mastery, which supersession_register VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 struck ENTIRELY as a placeholder with no canonical advancement mechanic. Even the canonical succession spec (PP-486) still carries the struck mechanic with unresolved [TODO] markers. So the succession's central consequence is undefined: there is no live track to 'reset to 0', leaving the BG Event Card effect, the Hybrid Domain Echo, and the analyses arc all referencing a deleted mechanic.
- **Evidence:** canon/supersession_register.yaml:37-49 (VTM STRUCK entirely; files_to_recheck includes npc_priority_trees); params/factions/riskbreakers_identity.md:208-213 (PP-486 canonical succession still says 'VTM resets to 0. [TODO: VTM struck 2026-04-19; revisit.]'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:33,240 ('VTM resets to 0'; 'VTM → 0'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md (§2.10 succession); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:18 ('VTM resets to 0, representing the loss of Vaynard's... intelligence infrastructure')
- **Fix:** Run the deferred victory-paths editorial to replace the VTM-reset consequence with a live mechanic (e.g., Varfell Intel stat penalty, or the embedded-practitioner check already used in npc_behavior §8.5 Priority 2b per PP-663). Then propagate the replacement into PP-486, npc_roster §2.10 + BG card, npc_behavior §2.10, and the analyses arc. Strike all 'VTM' tokens from Maret's docs.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. supersession_register.yaml:37-49 (VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19): 'VTM STRUCK entirely. No canonical advancement mechanic existed — was placeholder.' files_to_recheck includes params/bg/npc_priority_trees.md. riskbreakers_identity.md:208-213 (PP-486 canonical succession, ED-308 resolved) still reads 'Maret Uln becomes faction leader. VTM resets to 0. [TODO: VTM struck 2026-04-19; revisit.]' — the central succession consequence references a struck mechanic with an unresolved TODO. The consequence is propagated unfixed into npc_roster:33 ('VTM resets to 0') and :240 ('VTM → 0'), npc_behavior §2.10, and analyses_infill:18 ('VTM resets to 0, representing the loss of Vaynard's... intelligence infrastructure'). There is no live track to 'reset to 0', so the BG Event Card effect, Hybrid Domain Echo, and analyses arc all dangle on a deleted mechanic. Notably npc_behavior §8.5 line 832 (Priority 2b) already shows the live replacement pattern (PP-663: 'VTM gate replaced with embedded practitioner check'), confirming the fix direction exists but was never propagated to the succession consequence. HIGH stands — a faction-leader-fallback's signature outcome is mechanically undefined.

#### AUD-NPC2-039 — Conviction vector is a three-way cross-doc conflict; the designated PRIMARY source (migration roster) describes a different character than every Maret-facing design doc
- **Unit:** Maret Uln (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (PP-685 = PRIMARY) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Maret's Conviction value is stated three incompatible ways. The DESIGNATED-PRIMARY source for conviction values when docs conflict (conviction_migration_roster_v30 / PP-685, per character_canon_v30 §11 D1) gives Honor 0.30 / Authority 0.30 / Identity 0.20, cultural_label lowenritter_military, notes 'Military-honor archetype with Identity (Varfell categorical loyalty)... pragmatic warlord rather than self-sacrificing.' Every other doc builds her on Equity-driven RM sympathy: npc_behavior §2.10 gives Primary Equity / Secondary Reason ('Aligns Varfell with RM goals'); npc_roster #2 'Dual loyalty... personally sympathetic to the Restoration Movement'; analyses 'Rawlsian — she believes in equity.' 'Varfell categorical loyalty' / 'warlord' directly negates the dual-loyalty/will-defect-to-RM premise that is her entire dramatic function. Because D1 makes the roster win, the live canon is the warlord profile, which guts her arc. (Independently corroborated by the 2026-06-22 audit AUD-NPC2-009, CONFIRMED HIGH.)
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:104-117 (Honor/Authority/Identity; lowenritter_military; 'categorical loyalty'; 'pragmatic warlord'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1 names migration roster PRIMARY and names this exact conflict: 'Maret Uln: behavior says Equity/Reason; migration roster says Honor/Authority/Identity'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:235-236 (Primary Equity / Secondary Reason); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:33; designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:18 ('Ethics: Rawlsian'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:18 ('Ethics: Rawlsian — veil of ignorance')
- **Fix:** Jordan-level reconciliation. Either (a) amend conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.2 Maret entry to the Equity-led RM-sympathy profile her arc depends on (and change cultural_label off lowenritter_military to a Varfell/western-fjord/Einhir-adjacent template, since the roster note's 'categorical loyalty' contradicts the dual-loyalty design), or (b) if Honor/Authority/Identity warlord is intended canon, rewrite the roster/behavior/analyses arc wholesale. Until resolved, do not author the character_canon Part B sheet — D1 currently forces the warlord profile.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced from the files. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:107-116 gives Honor 0.30 / Authority 0.30 / Identity 0.20, cultural_label lowenritter_military, notes 'Military-honor archetype with Identity (Varfell categorical loyalty)... pragmatic warlord rather than self-sacrificing.' That doc is LIVE canon: its header (line 1 '[CANONICAL: 2026-05-01 — PP-685 v1 ... promoted from PROVISIONAL after Stage 10 sim PASS]' and line 6 'Status: CANONICAL', AUTHORITY PP-685) overrides the stale 'PROVISIONAL' cruft on lines 2/9. character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1) names this exact conflict and designates the migration roster PRIMARY ('most-recent canonical'). The opposing profile is in the LIVE, CANONICAL (approved 2026-04-17) npc_behavior_v30.md:235-236 (Primary Equity / Secondary Reason, 'Aligns Varfell with RM goals'), npc_roster_v30.md:33 (dual loyalty / RM sympathy), npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:18 ('Rawlsian — veil of ignorance'). 'Categorical loyalty / warlord' substantively negates the dual-loyalty-defects-to-RM premise. Two live canonical docs genuinely conflict on the character's core, and D1's tie-breaker makes the warlord profile win. The only mitigating nuance — D1 explicitly defers propagation ('Do not silently regenerate npc_behavior §2') and character_canon is a non-committing artifact — does not dissolve the conflict between the two live source docs. Corroborated by AUD-NPC2-009 (audit line 83, CONFIRMED HIGH). HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-040 — The succession that delivers Maret's Conviction realignment is gated on 'VTM resets to 0' — a struck, never-replaced mechanic
- **Unit:** Maret Uln (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (supersession_register) / PP-486 / ED-308 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Maret's personality-mechanics payload (on Vaynard's elimination she inherits Varfell and her Conviction/alignment realigns toward RM) is mechanically delivered by 'VTM resets to 0.' Vaynard Thread Mastery was struck entirely (placeholder, no canonical advancement mechanic) and never replaced; even PP-486 still carries it with an unresolved [TODO]. So the trigger that activates Maret's whole ethics-shift arc references a deleted track with no live consequence. (Corroborated by 2026-06-22 audit AUD-NPC2-010, CONFIRMED HIGH; included here because it is the gate for the personality-mechanics transition under audit.)
- **Evidence:** canon/supersession_register.yaml:37-49 (VTM STRUCK entirely); params/factions/riskbreakers_identity.md:210 (PP-486 'VTM resets to 0. [TODO: VTM struck 2026-04-19; revisit.]'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:33,240 ('VTM resets to 0'; 'VTM -> 0' BG card/Domain Echo); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:18; designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:231 (§2.10 succession trigger)
- **Fix:** Replace the VTM-reset consequence with a live mechanic (e.g., a Varfell Intel/Military stat penalty, or the embedded-practitioner check from npc_behavior §8.5 Priority 2b per PP-663), then propagate into PP-486, npc_roster §2.10 + BG card, npc_behavior §2.10, and the analyses arc. Strike all 'VTM' tokens from Maret's docs.
- **Verifier note:** Fully reproduced. supersession_register.yaml:37-49 (VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19) records 'VTM STRUCK entirely. No canonical advancement mechanic existed — was placeholder' and lists no replacement. The reset still appears as the live succession consequence across multiple LIVE docs: params/factions/riskbreakers_identity.md:210 ('Maret Uln ... VTM resets to 0. [TODO: VTM struck 2026-04-19; revisit.]', under PP-486/ED-308), npc_roster_v30.md:33 ('VTM resets to 0') and :240 (BG Event Card 'VTM -> 0'; Hybrid 'VTM resets'), npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:18 ('VTM resets to 0'), with the trigger at npc_behavior_v30.md:231 ('per PP-486'). So Maret's entire succession/realignment payload is mechanically delivered by a deleted track with no live consequence. Corroborated by AUD-NPC2-010 (audit line 84, CONFIRMED HIGH). HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-041 — "Three monarchs" backstory contradicts canonical timeline ("Two monarchs")
- **Unit:** Peder Almstedt | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** canon/03_canonical_timeline.md (canonical timeline override, 2026-04-11) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Almstedt's roster bio states he "has served under three monarchs." The canonical timeline explicitly overrode this to "Two monarchs (founder + Almud)" on 2026-04-11, because the Almqvist deed-monarchy is only two generations old (first Almqvist = Almud's father). The roster was never propagated, leaving a live contradiction with the source-of-truth timeline.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:45 ("Almstedt has served under three monarchs."); contradicted by canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:191 ("Earlier: Almstedt 'three monarchs' | Two monarchs (founder + Almud). 2026-04-11.") and :188 ("First Almqvist = Almud's father. Two generations to game start.").
- **Fix:** Change "three monarchs" to "two monarchs (the founder and Almud)" in npc_roster_v30_infill.md:45. Optionally rephrase his Byzantine-logothetes "outlived every dynasty" framing to fit a two-generation monarchy, since the parallel implies a much longer institutional tenure than two reigns supports.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. npc_roster_v30_infill.md:45 states verbatim 'Almstedt has served under three monarchs.' canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:191 overrides this: 'Earlier: Almstedt "three monarchs" | Two monarchs (founder + Almud). 2026-04-11.' and :188 'First Almqvist = Almud's father. Two generations to game start.' The timeline is source-of-truth and the override is dated/live, not stale. Corroborated by editorial_ledger_archive_801_1000.yaml:162-163 (ED-404, status resolved): 'Almstedt served under two monarchs (founder + Almud), not three.' The same doc is internally contradictory: roster_infill:111 already says 'Almstedt served under two monarchs — the founder (first Almqvist) and Almud,' so the :45 'three monarchs' line is an un-propagated live error, not a defensible alternate reading. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-042 — Faction assignment contradicts authoritative faction doc: Crown/Ministry vs Hafenmark
- **Unit:** Peder Almstedt | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md §1.2c / §disposition-table vs npc_roster_v30.md / references/npc_registry.yaml | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster and registries place Almstedt in the "Ministry" / "Crown (Ministry)" faction. The authoritative faction document instead places him squarely in Hafenmark: he is a named member of the Hafenmark Inner Circle, the Hafenmark disposition table lists his faction as Hafenmark, and he permanently chairs Hafenmark's Constitutional Review Committee. The arc material reinforces Hafenmark by having the Hafenmark leader (Baralta) directly command him. "Ministry" is defined as a Crown-EXCLUSIVE administrative sub-system (A-09), so Crown-Ministry and Hafenmark are not the same home — this is a genuine cross-doc faction contradiction, not a labeling nuance.
- **Evidence:** Crown/Ministry side: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:102,176,300 ("Ministry"); references/npc_registry.yaml:195 (faction: Crown (Ministry)). Hafenmark side: designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:186 (Hafenmark Inner Circle — Senior Parliamentary Chair), :639 ("Almstedt | Hafenmark | 0"), :880 ("Almstedt chairs permanently (Senior Parliamentary Chair)" of the Constitutional Review Committee); designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md:62 ("Baralta instructs Almstedt to introduce a Parliamentary motion"). "Ministry is Crown-exclusive": designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:28 (A-09).
- **Fix:** Reconcile to a single home faction. Given his Parliamentary function, Hafenmark Inner Circle membership, Constitutional Review Committee chairmanship, and command relationship to Baralta, Hafenmark is the better-supported assignment; update npc_roster_v30.md (#8 heading, summary table, behavior profile) and references/npc_registry.yaml:195 to Hafenmark, and decouple the "Ministry" administrative-apparatus mechanics from the faction label (see related mechanical finding).
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and the anti-contradiction reading fails. npc_registry.yaml:195 sets the faction FIELD to 'Crown (Ministry)'; roster headings/tables say 'Ministry' (npc_roster_v30.md:102,176,300). The authoritative faction doc places him in Hafenmark on three independent anchors: faction_politics_v30.md:186 (named Hafenmark Inner Circle member — Senior Parliamentary Chair), :639 disposition row 'Almstedt | Hafenmark | 0', and :880 (permanently chairs Hafenmark's Constitutional Review Committee). Arc material reinforces it: arcs_10_18.md:62 has Hafenmark leader Baralta instruct Almstedt to introduce a Parliamentary motion. The 'same home' rebuttal is foreclosed by A-09 (faction_politics_v30.md:28): the Ministry system is 'Crown-exclusive,' so Crown-Ministry and Hafenmark are mutually exclusive faction assignments, not a label-vs-apparatus nuance. Genuine cross-doc faction-field contradiction. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-043 — Two competing 'Primary Conviction' rows in §2.8 — live emergence-window mechanic stacked on top of a stale superseding editorial note
- **Unit:** Prince Torben Almqvist | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-618 vs ED-394 (npc_behavior_v30 §2.8 Primary Conviction) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Torben's §2.8 stat table has TWO rows both labeled 'Primary Conviction'. The first (ED-618) is the full emergence-window mechanic (first faction to Disposition >=+2 before S8 sets it; locks after S8; defaults to Order). The second (ED-394) is an older EDITORIAL note that says the opposite framing ('Conviction not specified... At Loyalty 3 (start), he is uncommitted... Proposed: starts with no primary Conviction'). One is meant to supersede the other but both are present in the canonical table, so a reader cannot tell which governs, and the duplicate-key row breaks the sheet schema (a stat table cannot have two 'Primary Conviction' values).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:192 (ED-618 emergence-window row) and :193 (stale ED-394 'Conviction not specified' EDITORIAL row) — same '| Primary Conviction |' key, contradictory content. ED-394 no longer appears in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl (grep ED-394 returns nothing; only ED-618 at :366), indicating it is closed yet its text persists in the table.
- **Fix:** Delete the stale ED-394 row (line 193). Keep the single ED-618 emergence-window row as the canonical Primary Conviction definition.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced directly. npc_behavior_v30.md:192 and :193 BOTH carry the key '| Primary Conviction |' in the §2.8 stat table. :192 is the live ED-618 emergence-window rule (first faction to Disposition >=+2 before S8 sets it; S8 lock; Order default); :193 is the stale '[EDITORIAL: ED-394 ... uncommitted ... Proposed: starts with no primary Conviction]'. ED-394 is the precursor design question ('Torben Conviction: starts blank? Or assigned at creation?') still listed in the in-doc §10 ledger at :1066 and absent from canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl — i.e. superseded by ED-618. Two rows sharing a stat-table key is a genuine schema break and a reader cannot tell which governs. Caveat: the two rows are spiritually aligned (both say the Conviction is emergent/contested), so the contradiction is about which mechanical spec is live, not opposite outcomes — but HIGH stands because a canonical stat sheet with a duplicated key giving two different mechanical definitions of the NPC's single most defining attribute is a real defect.

#### AUD-NPC2-044 — Elske's Conviction is stated four mutually incompatible ways across docs
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-doc Conviction consistency (NPC design) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The same NPC is assigned four different Convictions. The task brief frames her as 'Family vs Self-Determination', the analysis infill fixes her as 'Self-Determination', the victory doc fixes her as 'Order', the systems reference fixes her as 'Autonomy/Precedent', and the primary character analysis says her Conviction is undefined and only emerges post-retrieval. These cannot all be canon; Order vs Self-Determination/Autonomy are near-opposite poles, and a fixed Conviction contradicts the 'emerges later' framing. This drives her behavior, the Loyalty-track 'Conviction violation: -1' trigger, and her Resonant Style, so the ambiguity is load-bearing.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90 ('Self-Determination conviction'); designs/provincial/victory_v30.md:494 ('Elske confirmed ... Conviction: Order'); designs/architecture/complete_systems_reference.md:105 ('Elske (Altonian Proxy): Autonomy/Precedent'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:212 ('her Conviction emerges through Crown-side faction investment in a post-retrieval window'). complete_systems_reference.md:105 also self-flags 'Needs characterization (NPC audit: 24/40)'.
- **Fix:** Adjudicate a single canonical Conviction (primary/secondary) for Elske and propagate it to victory_v30.md:494, complete_systems_reference.md:105, and npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90. If the design intent is genuinely 'emergent post-retrieval' (npc_character_analyses_v30.md:212), strike the fixed-Conviction assertions in victory_v30 and complete_systems_reference and replace with an explicit 'undefined until retrieval window' marker matching Torben's treatment.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced all four divergent statements: npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90 ('Her Self-Determination conviction means she will not act as anyone's instrument'); victory_v30.md:494 ('Conviction: Order'); complete_systems_reference.md:105 ('Elske (Altonian Proxy): Autonomy/Precedent ... Needs characterization (NPC audit: 24/40)'); npc_character_analyses_v30.md:212 ('her Conviction emerges through Crown-side faction investment in a post-retrieval window'). Order vs Self-Determination/Autonomy are genuinely near-opposite poles, and a fixed value contradicts the 'emerges later' framing. It is load-bearing: victory_v30.md:490 applies a 'Conviction violation: -1' Loyalty trigger and infill:90 ties a Resonant Style to it. The systems-reference line self-flags 'Needs characterization.' Four live v30 docs in genuine conflict on a behavior-driving attribute justifies HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-045 — Conviction value is incoherent across docs — FOUR mutually incompatible values for one NPC
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 / conviction_taxonomy_v30 §2; character_canon_v30 §11 D1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Elske is assigned four different, non-reconcilable convictions across live v30 docs: 'Self-Determination' (character analyses infill), 'Autonomy/Precedent' (complete_systems_reference), 'Order' (victory_v30 Elske Loyalty Track), and 'Family' (arcs_20_23). These are not weight-vector variants of one primary; they are four different primaries. There is no authored 13-element weighted personal_convictions vector anywhere.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90 ('Her Self-Determination conviction means she will not act as anyone's instrument'); designs/architecture/complete_systems_reference.md:105 ('Elske (Altonian Proxy): Autonomy/Precedent'); designs/provincial/victory_v30.md:494 ('Conviction: Order. MS: Solidarity. Certainty: 4.'); designs/arcs/arcs_20_23.md:35 ('the Family conviction engages')
- **Fix:** Author a single canonical personal_convictions vector for Elske in the migration roster (primary source) and propagate. Given the consistent 'will not act as anyone's instrument' + cognatic-heir framing, Liberty (canonical #7, glossed 'Self-determination') is the natural primary; reconcile 'Order' and 'Family' as secondary weights or strike them. Replace all four ad-hoc labels with the ratified vector.
- **Verifier note:** All four values reproduced verbatim: 'Self-Determination' (npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90), 'Autonomy/Precedent' (complete_systems_reference.md:105), 'Order' (victory_v30.md:494), 'Family' (arcs_20_23.md:35). No 13-element personal_convictions vector exists for her anywhere (she has no NPC sheet of her own — npc_roster_v30 entry 11 is her spouse Laskaris, not her). One caveat that does not defeat the finding: complete_systems_reference.md is a pre-PP-684 doc that internally uses the legacy 7-Conviction set (line 14: 'Seven Convictions: Faith, Order, Reason, Equity, Precedent, Autonomy, Continuity'), so its 'Autonomy' is a legacy-taxonomy label rather than a freshly invented fifth primary — but that file is still a live canonical source (canonical_sources.yaml:96 lists it as the core_engine design_doc) and is not in the supersession register, so the four-way live conflict stands. Severity HIGH upheld.

#### AUD-NPC2-046 — Resonant Style conflicts across docs: Evidence vs Solidarity
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (4 Resonant Styles); social_contest_v30 / conviction_track_v30 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Elske's argument-form / Mode-of-Suasion is stated two incompatible ways. Character analyses (and arcs_20_23) give Primary Resonant Style = Evidence ('reached through evidence of concrete situations'; 'Evidence resonance; Abstract appeals rejected'). The victory_v30 Elske Loyalty Track instead drives advancement off 'Solidarity MS' and tags her 'MS: Solidarity'. Evidence and Solidarity are different members of the 4-set and gate completely differently in the Contest (Solidarity requires an active Knot; Evidence does not). Both are cited as her resonant style.
- **Evidence:** Evidence: designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90 ('Resonant Style: Evidence'), designs/arcs/arcs_20_23.md:35 ('Evidence resonance; Abstract appeals rejected'). Solidarity: designs/provincial/victory_v30.md:490 ('Personal scene Disposition >= +2 (Solidarity MS) +1') and :494 ('MS: Solidarity')
- **Fix:** Pick one Primary Resonant Style and harmonize. If Evidence is primary, change the Loyalty-Track advancement trigger to an Evidence-keyed scene and retag :494. If Solidarity is intended (it fits the marriage/contact-relationship mechanic), restate the analyses doc and define the required Knot (see Solidarity finding).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. Evidence: npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90 ('Resonant Style: Evidence') and arcs_20_23.md:35 ('Evidence resonance; Abstract appeals rejected'). Solidarity: victory_v30.md:490 ('Disposition >= +2 (Solidarity MS) +1') and :494 ('MS: Solidarity'). I confirmed 'MS' in the victory_v30 block denotes the suasion/resonant-style axis, not Mending Stability — it sits in an NPC stat line beside Conviction/Certainty and keys the advancement trigger. Evidence and Solidarity are distinct members of the ED-516 4-set (npc_behavior_system_v1.md:769) and gate differently (Solidarity requires an active Knot per npc_behavior_v30.md:39; Evidence does not). Genuine contradiction. HIGH upheld.

#### AUD-NPC2-047 — Absent from the designated PRIMARY conviction source (migration roster PP-685)
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (conviction_migration_roster_v30 / PP-685 = PRIMARY source) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** D1 designates conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) as the primary source for conviction values when docs conflict. Elske appears nowhere in that roster, even though she is a named Crown succession candidate with a Certainty stat and a Loyalty track, and peer Crown NPCs (Almud, plus succession alternates Cesare/Lorenzo) and even struck/edge-case characters (Vaynard, Vossler, Reinholt) DO have roster entries. With four conflicting values in the field and no primary-source entry, D1's conflict-resolution rule cannot be applied to her at all.
- **Evidence:** Primary-source rule: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:425 (D1). Roster named entries: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:41-232 + §3 edge cases:255-258 (no Elske; grep for 'Elske' across the file returns zero matches). Peer coverage gap noted vs Almud (roster §2.1:41) and Cesare/Lorenzo (character_canon D7:line)
- **Fix:** Add an Elske entry to conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 (Sovereign Court) with her ratified vector + cultural template (likely altonian_imperial given her acculturation, or valorian_court with Altonian overlay), then treat that as primary and reconcile all downstream docs to it.
- **Verifier note:** Fully reproduced. character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1) designates conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) as primary when sources conflict. grep for 'Elske' across conviction_migration_roster_v30.md returns zero matches. Peer/edge coverage confirmed present: Almud (:41), Cesare (:56), Lorenzo (:71), and even struck/sim-continuity characters Vaynard (:256), Vossler (:257), Reinholt (:258). With four conflicting field values and no primary-source entry, D1's conflict-resolution rule genuinely cannot be applied to her. HIGH upheld.

#### AUD-NPC2-048 — Evidence TS gate is written as 'TS 0 / non-sensitives' but P-08 inertness covers the entire TS<30 band
- **Unit:** Resonant-styles taxonomy & P-08 gating | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** P-08 states Thread-level evidence is inert against TS<30 NPCs (the whole Dormant/pre-Stirring band, not only TS 0). Every place the gate is actually stated restricts it to 'TS 0 NPCs' / 'non-sensitives'. Taken literally this leaves a hole: NPCs with 0<TS<30 (Vaynard TS 14 Dormant, Haelgrund TS 12 Hidden, Cardinal Temperance/Klapp TS 8, King Almud TS 28 near-Stirring) are non-zero, so a literal reading permits Thread-level Evidence against them, violating P-08. The infill is even internally inconsistent: it restricts to 'TS 0' yet its Exception flips only when TS 'crosses Stirring (30+)', implying everything <30 should be gated.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30_infill.md line 113 ('Evidence targeting against TS 0 NPCs...') vs line 114 ('Exception: if the NPC's TS crosses Stirring (30+)...'); C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\character_canon_v30.md line 170 ('TS Gate ... Thread-level evidence is invalid as Evidence targeting vs TS 0 NPCs (P-08 compliance)'); affected NPCs: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.4 line 116 (Vaynard TS 14), §2.14 line 308 (Haelgrund TS 12), §2.1 line 55 (Almud TS 28); arc_expansion_v30.md line 328 (Klapp TS 8)
- **Fix:** Replace 'TS 0' / 'non-sensitives' with 'TS < 30 (below Stirring)' everywhere the gate is stated (npc_behavior_v30_infill §6.1b, character_canon §5 TS Gate). This aligns the restriction boundary with the Exception's Stirring(30) threshold and with P-08.

#### AUD-NPC2-049 — Canonical §6.1b Evidence/TS gate is an empty heading in the source-of-truth file — the P-08 gate exists only in the infill
- **Unit:** Resonant-styles taxonomy & P-08 gating | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 / ED-516 / npc_behavior_v30 §6.1b | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** npc_behavior_v30.md is the canonical NPC doc, and it is the file that carries the live §6.3 Targeting Effects table players act from. But its §6.1b 'Evidence Resonant Style — Thread Sensitivity Gate (AUD-NPC-01 fix)' heading has NO body — the section is hollow (so is §6.1 Appraise Revelation and §6.2 Targeting Declaration intro). The actual P-08 gate text lives only in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md. A GM reading the canonical file sees the §6.3 Evidence row ('Memory + Revealing. Must cite specific, named, verifiable claim. +1D; on win Piety Track +1') with NO Thread-Sensitivity restriction at all. Per P-08, Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert against TS<30 NPCs; the canonical file as written permits Thread-level Evidence targeting against any TS.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md lines 684-688 (empty §6.1b heading; §6.2 intro blank) vs lines 697-702 (§6.3 Targeting Effects Evidence row has no TS gate); gate body present only at C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30_infill.md lines 112-114
- **Fix:** Refill §6.1b body in npc_behavior_v30.md from the infill (lines 113-114), AND add a TS-gate clause directly to the §6.3 Evidence row (e.g. 'Thread-level claims require target TS>=30 (Stirring); ontical evidence only below'), mirroring how the Solidarity row inlines its Knot prerequisite.

#### AUD-NPC2-050 — TS 35 (roster/analyses/registry) vs TS 20+ (behavior §7.10 + arc_expansion_v30)
- **Unit:** Sigrid Torsvald | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS value cross-doc | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Torsvald's Thread Sensitivity is stated as two different canonical values across docs. The roster, character analyses, and npc_registry fix TS 35; the NPC Behavior System integration table and the canonical arc source fix '20+'. The registry already noticed the conflict but only papered over it with an inline comment ('20+' = floor, not ceiling) without reconciling the source docs.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:74 ('TS 35... her TS means she can perceive...'), :173 (summary table 'TS 35'), :271 (TTRPG row 'TS 35'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:30 ('TS 35 developed through exposure'); references/npc_registry.yaml:131 ('ts: 35 # ... behavior_v30 table says 20+'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1196 ('Torsvald | 20+ | 2'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:418 ('the named Riskbreaker, TS 20+'), :440 ('Torsvald's TS 20+ is developing').
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical TS and propagate. If 35 is canon (roster/registry weight), change behavior §7.10 and arc_expansion_v30 from '20+' to 35 and rewrite the developing-TS arc text (see arc-gating finding). If '20+/developing' is the intended design, demote roster/registry/analyses 'TS 35' to a current-or-ceiling value with explicit starting TS stated.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced every anchor. npc_roster_v30.md:74 (TS 35 context, 'her TS means she can perceive'), :173 (summary table 'TS 35 in anti-Thread org'), :271 ('TS 35'); npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:30 ('TS 35 developed through exposure'); npc_registry.yaml:131 (ts: 35 with self-aware inline comment 'behavior_v30 table says 20+ (floor, not ceiling)'); npc_behavior_v30.md:1196 ('Torsvald | 20+ | 2'); arc_expansion_v30.md:418 ('the named Riskbreaker, TS 20+') and :440 ('Torsvald's TS 20+ is developing'). Two different stated values for the same stat live across the source-of-truth docs, and the registry comment papers over it rather than reconciling. The 'floor vs current' reading softens it from a hard contradiction to an under-specification, but the value gates an arc (Finding 2), so HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-051 — Roster says TS 25 and TS 12 are 'above the practitioner threshold' — threshold is 30
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS practitioner threshold = 30 (character_canon_v30 §7.1, line 244) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The canonical practitioner threshold (Stirring) is TS 30. The roster describes Yrsa Vossen (TS 25) as 'just above the practitioner threshold' and Saemund Haelgrund (TS 12) as 'barely above the practitioner threshold.' Both are BELOW 30. This is the (d) 'TS thresholds used inconsistently / practitioner threshold' defect the audit targets: a TS-25 and TS-12 NPC are mechanically sub-practitioner (cannot Thread-Read) but the prose asserts the opposite, which mis-gates every threshold-keyed effect (Thread-Read, Diagnosis arc trigger, §8.10 assessment bands, P-08 Evidence-RS gate).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:52 (Vossen 'TS 25 — just above the practitioner threshold'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:63 (Haelgrund 'TS 12 — barely above the practitioner threshold'). Contradicts designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:244 ('30–49 ... Practitioner threshold') and the band table at :238-246. Note character_analyses §2 (line 174) and §3 (line 38) correctly treat 30 as the threshold and TS 25 as below it.
- **Fix:** Restate Vossen as 'TS 25 — below the practitioner threshold (30), within the Dormant band (20–29)' and Haelgrund as 'TS 12 — Hidden band (10–19), below the practitioner threshold (30); attributes perception to instinct.' Remove both 'above the practitioner threshold' phrasings so the roster agrees with character_canon §7.1.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced directly. character_canon_v30.md:244 sets the practitioner (Stirring) threshold at 30 ('30–49 ... Practitioner threshold; Thread-Read available'). npc_roster_v30.md:52 states Vossen 'TS 25 — just above the practitioner threshold' and :63 states Haelgrund 'TS 12 — barely above the practitioner threshold.' Both are well below 30, so the prose mis-gates Thread-Read / Diagnosis-arc eligibility. The roster:52 sentence is even internally self-contradictory: 'just above the practitioner threshold but below the Forgetting resistance gate (29)' — 25 cannot be above 30 yet below 29. character_analyses_v30.md:38 correctly says TS 25 'is below the Forgetting resistance threshold (29)', and §7.10 (behavior:1198) lists Vossen as a 'Non-practitioner.' Live contradiction; HIGH affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-052 — Maret Uln TS contradiction: 35 vs ~50 across the roster and behavior docs
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS per-NPC canonicity (character_canon_v30 §7.1 line 248: 'TS is canonical per NPC') | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Maret Uln's Thread Sensitivity is authored as two different values. 35 crosses the Active threshold (50) downward (she is an Active practitioner who can Thread-Read but not Deep); ~50 puts her at the Deep band boundary. The two values change which Knot-strain row (§5.0b: Active +1/+1 vs Deep +2/+1) fires and whether her intelligence-op Thread perception is 'Active' or 'Deep' — a live mechanical divergence, not flavor.
- **Evidence:** TS 35: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:240 (§2.10 stat block, 'Crosses Active threshold'), :1194 (§7.10 NPC Stat Values table), designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md:88 (§3.3). TS ~50: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:33, :38 ('TS exposure: TS ~50'), :238 (Mode matrix, 'Practitioner (TS ~50)').
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical value (35 is the majority across behavior §2.10/§7.10/companion §3.3 and is internally reasoned as 'crosses Active threshold'). Update npc_roster_v30 lines 33/38/238 from '~50' to 35.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. TS 35: npc_behavior_v30.md:240 (§2.10 successor block, 'Crosses Active threshold — can perform Thread-Read'), :1194 (§7.10 'Maret Uln | 35 | 2 | Active practitioner'), companion_specification_v30.md:88. TS ~50: npc_roster_v30.md:30, :38 ('TS ~50 means Maret perceives Thread-level phenomena'), and the Mode matrix :238. 35 = Active band (30–49); 50 = Deep band boundary (50–69). Per the §5.0b/§8.2 Knot-strain table (behavior:524–526) these are different rows (Active +1 close/none distant vs Deep +2/+1), so the divergence is mechanically live, not flavor. HIGH affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-053 — Almud TS contradiction: 28 vs 0 (intra-file in npc_behavior_v30), plus wrong ED citation
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS per-NPC canonicity (character_canon_v30 §7.1); §5.0b Knot-strain banding | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Almud's TS is '28 (near Stirring)' in his stat block and in the §5.0b exclusion note, but '0' in the §7.10 NPC Stat Values table. 28 means Evidence attacks are 'partially effective' and he is one threshold-tick from Stirring (a live arc lever); 0 means theologically foreclosed and Evidence-RS is inert (P-08). These cannot both hold. The §7.10 rationale also cites 'ED-398', but ED-398 is Maret Uln's Certainty item (behavior §10 ED list, line 1070) — the citation is stale/incorrect, masking which value is authoritative.
- **Evidence:** TS 28: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:55 (§2.1 'Thread Sensitivity | 28 (near Stirring)'), :528 (§5.0b 'Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing)'); also designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:337. TS 0: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1186 (§7.10 'Almud | 0 | 3 | TS 0 per ED-398') and the Ruler Diamond table designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:147 ('Almud ... 0').
- **Fix:** Reconcile to one value. If 28 (the stat-block + §5.0b + character_canon value) is canonical, change §7.10 line 1186 and the Ruler Diamond table to 28 and fix the rationale to cite the correct Almud-TS editorial item (not ED-398). If 0 is intended, strike '28 (near Stirring)' from §2.1 and the §5.0b note.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. TS 28: npc_behavior_v30.md:55 (§2.1 '28 (near Stirring)'), :528 (§5.0b 'Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing)'), and character_canon_v30.md:337 ('Almud (TS 28, no threshold crossing)'). TS 0: npc_behavior_v30.md:1186 (§7.10 'Almud | 0 | 3 | TS 0 per ED-398') and the Ruler Diamond table in npc_character_analyses_v30.md:146 ('Almud ... 0') — finding cited line 147, but Almud is on 146 (Lenneth on 147); the value is correct, only the line number is off by one. Wrong-ED claim verified: ED-398 (behavior:1070) is 'Maret Uln Certainty', and ED-397 (:1069) is 'Maret Uln Thread Sensitivity' — neither is an Almud item, so the §7.10 rationale cites a stale/incorrect ED. 28 vs 0 changes Evidence-attack efficacy and one-tick-from-Stirring arc leverage vs P-08 foreclosure. HIGH affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-054 — Conviction vector contradicts itself across docs: Faith/Authority vs legacy 'Order/Reason' vs legacy 'Reason'
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy; legacy Reason/Order labels superseded | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Sæmund carries three mutually inconsistent conviction readings. (1) npc_registry.yaml NPC-004 gives a clean PP-684 vector: Faith 0.60 + Authority 0.20, ecclesiastical. (2) valoria_complete_systems_r2 calls him 'Order/Reason (hidden)'. (3) valoria_cross_conversation_review calls his a 'buried Reason conviction'. 'Reason' and 'Order' are not the same as 'Faith/Authority', and 'Reason' is a LEGACY/SUPERSEDED label (→ Scholastic/Authority per PP-684 §6) used here as live canon with no [SUPERSEDED→] annotation. The registry vector (Faith primary) is the only PP-684-conformant one but is contradicted by every prose/systems doc, and a Faith-primary Inquisitor is itself in tension with his Bellarmine characterisation (a man whose Faith in the institution's ontological claims has collapsed — see ETH-1).
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml L110-116 (Faith 0.60/Authority 0.20, ecclesiastical); references/valoria_complete_systems_r2.md L104 ('Haelgrund (Church Internal): Order/Reason (hidden)'); references/valoria_cross_conversation_review.md L88 ('buried Reason conviction'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L56-67 (main stat block lists NO conviction at all).
- **Fix:** Pick one PP-684 vector (the registry's Faith/Authority is defensible, though Authority/Order may better fit 'duty to the institution'), record it in PP-685, and either delete the legacy 'Order/Reason'/'Reason' references in valoria_complete_systems_r2 and valoria_cross_conversation_review or tag them [SUPERSEDED→PP-684]. Reconcile the chosen primary with the Bellarmine arc.
- **Verifier note:** All three readings reproduced verbatim: npc_registry.yaml L110-116 (Faith 0.60 + Authority 0.20, ecclesiastical); valoria_complete_systems_r2.md L104 ('Haelgrund (Church Internal): Order/Reason (hidden)'); valoria_cross_conversation_review.md L88 ('buried Reason conviction'). npc_roster_v30.md L56-67 main stat block lists NO conviction at all (only TS + Arc trigger). conviction_migration_roster_v30 §1 (L17) explicitly states 'Reason' is a legacy tag PP-684 supersedes, and neither systems_r2 nor the review carries a [SUPERSEDED] annotation — so a superseded label is in live use. One nuance the finding slightly overstates: 'Order' IS a live PP-684 conviction (Faith/Authority/Order/Scholastic/... per the taxonomy), so only 'Reason' is strictly superseded; but the substantive three-way inconsistency (Faith-primary vs Order/Reason vs Reason) is real and the registry's Faith-primary vector is the only PP-684-clean one yet is contradicted by every prose/systems doc. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-055 — Sæmund absent from the PP-685 conviction migration roster — the designated authority for conviction VALUES
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (designated PRIMARY conviction source) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** character_canon §11 D1 designates designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md (PP-685) as the PRIMARY source when conviction values conflict. Sæmund Haelgrund — a Full-presence named Church Inquisitor with a defection arc that turns on his belief structure — has NO entry in PP-685. The Ecclesiastical section (§2.3) maps only Cardinal Reichard, Baralta, and Lenneth. With no authoritative migration entry, the conflicting conviction labels scattered across other docs (see CONV-2) have no canonical arbiter, which is precisely the failure mode D1 exists to prevent.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 (lines 135-179: Reichard/Baralta/Lenneth only; no Sæmund); designed-primary status per the audit's CANON block (character_canon_v30 §11 D1). Sæmund is canonical: references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-004 L98-120.
- **Fix:** Add a §2.3 entry for Sæmund Haelgrund to conviction_migration_roster_v30.md with primary convictions + weights + cultural_label + self_other, so the migration roster (not the scattered legacy labels) is the single source of truth for his conviction vector.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. grep for [Hh]aelgrund across designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md returns zero matches; §2.3 Ecclesiastical (L135-179) lists only Cardinal Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth (+Solmund under Varfell). character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (L426) explicitly designates conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) values as PRIMARY in per-NPC sheets when sources conflict ('most-recent canonical'). Sæmund is canonical (npc_registry.yaml NPC-004 L98-120; npc_registry.sql L130). So a Full-presence named NPC with a belief-structure-driven defection arc has no entry in the doc canon designates as the arbiter, exactly the failure mode D1 addresses. The conviction labels genuinely conflict across docs (see finding 2), so the missing arbiter is materially harmful, not cosmetic. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-056 — No Resonant Style authored, yet the TTRPG interface runs a contest that requires one
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (4 Resonant Styles); social_contest_v30 contest interface; interdoc_audit G-3 / valoria_canonical_definitive J-4 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Sæmund's TTRPG stat block explicitly states 'Social scenes with Haelgrund use the contest system — he argues from evidence, not dogma,' and his defection arc resolves through Diagnosis/contest. Running that contest requires a Primary (and ideally Secondary) Resonant Style from the 4-set (Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity). Sæmund has NONE: he is absent from the authoritative stance-triangle table (params/factions/npc_stance_triangles.md), the behavior doc's §2 Resonant-Style roster covers Lennart Haelgrund (Ministry) not Sæmund, and two prior audits explicitly list his stance triangle as missing. The contest interface is therefore unrunnable as written.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L260 ('argues from evidence, not dogma' + contest system); params/factions/npc_stance_triangles.md L28-40 (named-NPC stance table — Sæmund absent); references/valoria_interdoc_audit.md L464 (G-3 'Three missing NPC stance triangles (Lenneth, Elske, Haelgrund)'); references/valoria_canonical_definitive_r2.md L307 (J-4 same).
- **Fix:** Author a Resonant Style block for Sæmund (Primary = Evidence is implied by 'argues from evidence', plus a Secondary, e.g. Authority) and add him to npc_stance_triangles.md so the stated contest interface and the defection arc are mechanically runnable.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced on every cited anchor. npc_roster_v30.md L260 states verbatim 'Social scenes with Haelgrund use the contest system — he argues from evidence, not dogma.' params/factions/npc_stance_triangles.md L28-40 Named-NPC stance table has NO Haelgrund row (Almud, Himlensendt, Baralta, Vaynard, Ehrenwall, Vossen, Hann, Torben, Edeyja, Maret Uln only). npc_behavior_v30 §2 profiles only Lennart Haelgrund (Ministry, §2.14) with Consequence/Evidence styles — grep confirms no Sæmund block exists anywhere in the behavior doc. valoria_interdoc_audit.md L464 (G-3) and valoria_canonical_definitive_r2.md L307 (J-4) both list Haelgrund among NPCs needing a stance triangle. The prior comprehensive audit AUD-NPC2-069 independently CONFIRMED the same missing Behavior block. The contest interface is genuinely unrunnable as written. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-057 — TS hard contradiction: behavior doc says TS 0, every other source says TS 25
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS value; cross-doc canon (P-08 boundary depends on TS tier) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vossen's Thread Sensitivity is stated as TS 0 (non-practitioner) in the canonical behavior doc, but TS 25 everywhere else. This is not cosmetic: TS 0 vs TS 25 determines whether she perceives Thread-touched artefacts at all and whether the entire 'Visible Revolution / self-erasing cultural knowledge' arc is even valid. The behavior doc additionally asserts the contradiction twice internally (the stat block AND the standing-pool table), so it is not a one-off typo.
- **Evidence:** TS 0: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:158 (§2.6 stat block: 'Thread Sensitivity | 0 | Non-practitioner.') and designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1198 (standing-pool table: 'Yrsa Vossen | 0 | 3 | Non-practitioner RM leader'). TS 25: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:52; designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:249; designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:38; designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md (§3, TS 25 cruelty); references/npc_registry.yaml:83,96; references/npc_registry.sql:129; tests/stress/emergent_arc_2026-04-17_batch6.md:276,284,295.
- **Fix:** Pick TS 25 as canonical (it is the supermajority value and the basis of her arc, the npc_registry, and the stress tests) and correct both TS 0 occurrences in npc_behavior_v30.md (§2.6 stat block line 158 and standing-pool table line 1198). Update the '0' Thread-Pool/standing-pool derivation accordingly, and replace 'Non-practitioner' with the canonical framing 'TS 25 — perceives Thread-touched artefacts, below Leap threshold (30) and below Forgetting-resistance gate (29).'
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md:158 ('Thread Sensitivity | 0 | Non-practitioner.') and the standing-pool/certainty table at :1198 ('Yrsa Vossen | 0 | 3 | Non-practitioner RM leader') both assert TS 0 (the '0' is unambiguously the TS column; the '3' is Certainty). Every other source says TS 25: npc_roster_v30.md:52 and :249, npc_character_analyses_v30.md:38, npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:38, references/npc_registry.yaml:83 (ts: 25), references/npc_registry.sql:129 (ts column '25'), and tests/stress/emergent_arc_2026-04-17_batch6.md:276/284/287/295. The value is load-bearing: TS 25 sits above non-practitioner perception but below the Forgetting-resistance gate (29), which is the entire mechanical premise of her self-erasing-cultural-knowledge arc (stress test builds a multi-season arc on exactly this). TS 0 would void that arc and her ability to sense Thread-touched artefacts. Doubly asserted internally, contradicted by the supermajority and by her arc. HIGH stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-058 — Primary Conviction conflict: PP-685 says Honor; every live downstream doc says Equity
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (migration roster is DESIGNATED PRIMARY for Conviction values) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The designated primary source (conviction_migration_roster_v30, PP-685) gives Yrsa Honor 0.35 / Warden 0.25 / Community 0.20. Every downstream live doc instead gives Equity as primary. Per the taxonomy these are different Convictions, not a relabel (conviction_taxonomy_v30 line 54 explicitly distinguishes Warden 'paternal/protective duty' from Equity 'fairness'; Honor line 55 is the pledged-oath code). The behavior priority-tree Conviction-critical (Priority 2) and Conviction-secondary (Priority 5) branches therefore fire on the wrong axis if the engine reads §2/registry rather than PP-685.
- **Evidence:** Honor primary (PRIMARY SOURCE): designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:88-95 (Honor 0.35/Warden 0.25/Community 0.20; prior_labels [Continuity, Military Honor]). Equity primary (downstream): designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:153 (§2.6 'Primary Conviction | Equity'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:471,476,478 ('Equity framework', 'Equity-primary'); references/npc_registry.yaml:88; references/npc_registry.sql:9 ('NPC-003','Equity',0.4,1); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:193 ('3 (equity)'). D1 anchor: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426 ('Yrsa Vossen: behavior says Equity/Continuity; migration roster says Honor/Warden/Community' -> use PP-685 as primary).
- **Fix:** Run the deferred propagation cycle character_canon §11 D1 deliberately deferred: set Yrsa primary Conviction to the PP-685 Honor/Warden/Community vector across npc_behavior_v30 §2.6, arc_expansion_v30, npc_registry (.yaml/.sql), and the analyses — OR, if Equity is the intended retcon, amend PP-685 and record the change so the designated primary source and downstream agree. Do not leave Honor (primary source) and Equity (everywhere else) both live.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:92-95 gives Honor 0.35/Warden 0.25/Community 0.20 with prior_labels [Continuity, Military Honor]; downstream npc_behavior_v30.md:153 ('Primary Conviction | Equity'), arc_expansion_v30.md:471/476/478, npc_registry.yaml:88, npc_registry.sql:9 all give Equity primary; character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1) explicitly names PP-685 the PRIMARY source when behavior conflicts. conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:54-55 confirms Honor (pledged-oath), Warden (paternal duty), Equity (fairness) are distinct Convictions, not relabels, so the conflict is substantive. ONE evidence anchor is wrong, however: 'npc_character_analyses_v30.md:193 (3 (equity))' does not exist — line 193 is royal-assassination arc text and a full-file grep shows the only 'equity' references are Lenneth (172, 182); the Yrsa analyses section (line 34) carries no conviction value. The core contradiction stands on the other six anchors regardless, so severity HIGH is retained.

#### AUD-NPC2-059 — Registry explicitly inverts the primary-source hierarchy ('PP-685 ... superseded by behavior file')
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (PP-685 is the designated PRIMARY when docs conflict) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The npc_registry deliberately reverses canon: it records Equity primary and notes 'PP-685 mapping (Honor/Warden/Community) superseded by behavior file.' This directly contradicts character_canon §11 D1, which designates the PP-685 migration roster as the PRIMARY source precisely when the behavior file conflicts. The registry is the root that propagated Equity downstream. It also carries only TWO convictions (Equity 0.40 + Warden 0.25), silently dropping Honor and Community from the PP-685 vector.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml:88-92 (primary Equity 0.40 + Warden 0.25; conviction_notes:92 'PP-685 mapping (Honor/Warden/Community) superseded by behavior file'); references/npc_registry.sql:9-10 (only Equity 0.4 rank1 + Warden 0.25 rank2 — no Honor, no Community). Contradicts designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1 makes PP-685 primary over the behavior file).
- **Fix:** Correct the registry note: PP-685 is the designated primary, not the superseded source. Restore the full Honor/Warden/Community vector (and the dropped Honor + Community rows) to npc_registry.yaml:88-92 and npc_registry.sql:9-10, or, if Equity is the ratified retcon, fix D1/PP-685 first and have the note cite that ratification rather than asserting the behavior file overrides the migration roster.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim. npc_registry.yaml:92 conviction_notes reads 'PP-685 mapping (Honor/Warden/Community) superseded by behavior file,' which directly inverts character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1, PP-685 = primary). The registry carries only two convictions: npc_registry.yaml:88-89 (Equity 0.40, Warden 0.25) and npc_registry.sql:9-10 (NPC-003 Equity 0.4 rank1, Warden 0.25 rank2) — Honor and Community from the PP-685 vector are silently dropped. All anchors check out.

#### AUD-NPC2-060 — Ethical Framework 'Equity Social Contract' is a LIVE mechanical Ob driver with no [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] note
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 + alias_registry -> PP-686 §3.7 / character_canon_v30 §11 D2 (Ethical Framework labels SUPERSEDED as mechanical Ob drivers) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Ethical-Framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers (mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc) and retained only as descriptive disposition tags. Yrsa's §2.6 stat block uses 'Equity Social Contract (Restoration)' as a live Ob modifier (-1 Ob on actions benefiting the common population; +1 Ob on actions concentrating power) with no [SUPERSEDED] marker — a direct D2/PP-684 §6 violation. The same framework is re-used as a live mechanical generator in arc_expansion ('Her Equity framework generates Presence markers at +1...') and stated as live 'Ethics:' in the roster.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:155 (§2.6 'Ethical Framework | Equity Social Contract (Restoration) | Aligned: -1 Ob on actions benefiting common population. Contradictory: +1 Ob on actions concentrating power.'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:471 ('Her Equity framework generates Presence markers at +1 adjacent territory per season when Stability >= 3'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:49 ('Ethics: Equity (social contract) — consistent with RM ethical framework'). Constraint: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:427 (D2).
- **Fix:** Demote the framework to a descriptive disposition tag with the canonical note: 'Equity Social Contract (Restoration) [SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]'. Re-derive the +/-1 Ob effects via PP-686 §3.7 from her (corrected) conviction vector rather than from the framework label. Apply the same demotion to the arc_expansion 'Equity framework generates...' mechanic and the roster 'Ethics:' line.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md:155 carries 'Ethical Framework | Equity Social Contract (Restoration) | Aligned: -1 Ob on actions benefiting common population. Contradictory: +1 Ob on actions concentrating power' with no [SUPERSEDED] marker; arc_expansion_v30.md:471 re-uses it as a live generator ('Her Equity framework generates Presence markers at +1 adjacent territory per season when Stability >= 3'); npc_roster_v30.md:49 states 'Ethics: Equity (social contract).' character_canon_v30.md:427 (D2) requires the framework be demoted to a descriptive tag with '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]' and warns Ob modifiers stay live in engine. The violation is exactly as described.

#### AUD-NPC2-061 — Legacy Conviction label 'Continuity' used as live Secondary with no supersession annotation
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 alias_registry / npc_behavior_v30 §1.2 line 30 (Continuity is a DEPRECATED label -> Warden) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Yrsa's Secondary Conviction is given as 'Continuity', a deprecated 9-set label that must not appear as live canon without a [SUPERSEDED->Warden] annotation. The very same document declares it dead: npc_behavior_v30 §1.2 states 'Reason and Continuity are deprecated labels; Autonomy is renamed Liberty.' So §2.6 contradicts §1.2 within one file, and arc_expansion re-uses 'Continuity secondary activates' as a live mechanical trigger.
- **Evidence:** Deprecation declared: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:30 (§1.2 'Reason and Continuity are deprecated labels; Autonomy is renamed Liberty'). Live legacy use: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:154 (§2.6 'Secondary Conviction | Continuity'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:478 ('Continuity secondary activates. She is no longer Equity-primary'). Canonical successor: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:94 (Warden 0.25).
- **Fix:** Replace the live 'Continuity' secondary with the canonical successor Warden (per §1.2 + PP-685) in npc_behavior_v30 §2.6 and in arc_expansion_v30, or annotate every occurrence as 'Continuity [SUPERSEDED->Warden]'. Note Aldric Hann (§2.7 line 173; arc_expansion 'Autonomy secondary') carries the identical deprecated-label problem (Autonomy->Liberty) and should be swept in the same pass.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced exactly. npc_behavior_v30.md:30 declares 'Reason and Continuity are deprecated labels; Autonomy is renamed Liberty,' yet npc_behavior_v30.md:154 lists 'Secondary Conviction | Continuity' with no [SUPERSEDED->Warden] note, and arc_expansion_v30.md:478 uses 'Continuity secondary activates. She is no longer Equity-primary' as a live mechanical trigger. Canonical successor Warden 0.25 confirmed at conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:94. Same-file contradiction (sec 1.2 vs sec 2.6) is genuine. The Aldric Hann Autonomy->Liberty parallel is also real (arc_expansion 472 'Hann's Autonomy secondary').


### — MEDIUM —

#### AUD-NPC2-062 — Hann's dedicated schism arc (Arc C-iii) uses explicit 'co-victory' language for Hafenmark+RM and Varfell+RM
- **Unit:** Aldric Hann | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** GD-1 (Peninsular Sovereignty is the SOLE victory condition; 'no co-victory' violation test) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Hann's full arc (the schism branch and his Arc C 'Fractured Operator') frames faction outcomes in terms of co-victory: 'Co-victory Hafenmark+RM requires Vossen's cultural wing specifically (the Solidarity RS pathway). Varfell+RM co-victory can proceed with either wing.' GD-1's violation test is explicit: 'Does any co-victory or shared victory mechanic exist? If yes -> FAIL.' GD-1's own cross-reference list flags conviction_track_v30 §7 (Co-Victory PP-418) for striking, but the same co-victory framing survives here in Hann's arc, gating which RM wing (Vossen's cultural vs Hann's operational) enables which faction's 'co-victory'. Mitigant: GD-1 was canonized 2026-05-17, postdating this arc doc, and strike propagation is explicitly 'Pass 2c, pending' — but the constraint text is canonical immediately and supersedes unannotated implementations.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:486 (C-iii: 'Co-victory Hafenmark+RM requires Vossen's cultural wing specifically ... Varfell+RM co-victory can proceed with either wing'); GD-1 text + violation test at canon/02_canon_constraints.md:38 ('Does any co-victory or shared victory mechanic exist? If yes -> FAIL'; strike list names conviction_track_v30 §7 Co-Victory PP-418).
- **Fix:** Re-skin the C-iii consequence under GD-1: replace 'co-victory' with the faction-leverage framing GD-1 mandates (e.g., 'Vossen's cultural wing unlocks the Solidarity alliance pathway with Hafenmark; Hann's operational wing supports Varfell alignment' — as tactical leverage toward Peninsular Sovereignty, never an alternate/shared win). Add a [SUPERSEDED-BY: GD-1] marker pending Pass 2c.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim. arc_expansion_v30.md:486 (Arc C-iii Schism): 'Co-victory Hafenmark+RM requires Vossen's cultural wing specifically (the Solidarity RS pathway). Varfell+RM co-victory can proceed with either wing.' 'co-victory' appears twice as a live faction-outcome mechanic. canon/02_canon_constraints.md:38 (GD-1) violation test is explicit: 'Does any co-victory or shared victory mechanic exist? If yes -> FAIL', and GD-1's strike list names conviction_track_v30 s7 Co-Victory PP-418 while this arc instance survives unstruck. The finding fairly states its mitigant (GD-1 canonized 2026-05-17 postdates the arc; strike propagation Pass 2c pending), but canon/02_canon_constraints.md:44 says the constraint text 'is canonical immediately and supersedes any unannotated implementation,' and C-iii carries no [SUPERSEDED] marker. A canonical-constraint violation persisting unannotated in a live design doc is a genuine MEDIUM canon finding.

#### AUD-NPC2-063 — Canonical registry record for Hann (NPC-041) is almost entirely null despite source values existing in behavior §2.7 and a full arc in arc_expansion §32
- **Unit:** Aldric Hann | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-041 (declared canonical source of truth for all named characters) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Hann is registered NPC-041 (status: canonical), but the structured record is a wall of [GAP]: ts, coherence, stats, convictions.primary, goals, and arc_trajectory are all null. These values are not actually unknown — behavior §2.7 specifies TS 0, Certainty 3 (Questioning), primary Conviction Equity, and Beliefs; arc_expansion_v30.md §32 gives Hann a full three-stage arc (Infrastructure/Operator/Fractured Operator) with branch conditions and a simplified post-schism Priority Tree. The canonical source-of-truth row was never populated from the design docs that define the character. Notably convictions.primary:null also directly contradicts the same file's reconciliation note (#12) that declares Hann's convictions RESOLVED to Equity/Liberty.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml:652-669 (NPC-041: ts:null, coherence:null, stats:null, convictions.primary:null, goals:null, arc_trajectory:null, all '# [GAP]'); source values at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:172-184 (TS 0, Certainty 3, Equity, Beliefs); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:490-505 (full arc); contradicting note references/npc_registry.yaml:1051.
- **Fix:** Populate NPC-041 from the existing canon: ts: 0, convictions (primary Equity + secondary per the Autonomy/Liberty resolution above), arc_trajectory pointing to arc_expansion_v30 §32, and Certainty 3 / Beliefs. Replace the [GAP] markers that have authored sources; keep [GAP] only on genuinely undefined fields (age, birthplace).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_registry.yaml:652-669 (NPC-041) is status:canonical but ts/coherence/stats/convictions.primary/goals/arc_trajectory all null, each tagged '# [GAP]'. Values are not actually unknown: npc_behavior_v30.md:172-184 gives TS 0, Certainty 3 (Questioning), primary Equity, two Beliefs; arc_expansion_v30.md:490-505 gives a full three-stage arc (Infrastructure/Operator/Fractured Operator) with branch conditions and a simplified Priority Tree (heading at :490 is 'Aldric Hann - Full Arc Expansion', not literally 'S32' - minor citation slip, content anchor correct). Decisively, convictions.primary:null contradicts the SAME file's note #12 at :1047-1051 declaring Hann's convictions RESOLVED to Equity/Liberty. That internal self-contradiction is the load-bearing defect. Mild caveat: registry has a deliberate GAP-deferral policy (character_canon D3), so nulls are partly by design - but a field declared RESOLVED in the same file should not be null, so MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-064 — Hann's stat block is anchored to Vossen ('Shares Vossen's framework' / 'As Vossen'), but Vossen's own conviction/framework anchor is itself non-canonical vs the primary roster
- **Unit:** Aldric Hann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon §11 D1 / conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.2 (Vossen) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Hann is defined by reference: Primary Conviction note 'Shares Vossen's framework but more operationally focused'; Ethical Framework 'As Vossen.' But Vossen's npc_behavior block lists Equity (primary) + Continuity (secondary), whereas the PRIMARY source maps Vossen to Honor 0.35 / Warden 0.25 / Community 0.20 (no Equity primary, and 'Continuity'->Warden). The anchor Hann inherits therefore conflicts with the designated primary source, propagating an unresolved conviction value into Hann by reference.
- **Evidence:** Hann anchor: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:172,174. Vossen behavior block: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:153-155 (Equity + Continuity). Vossen primary-source vector: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:88-102 (Honor/Warden/Community; 'Continuity legacy -> Honor+Warden+Community').
- **Fix:** Reconcile Vossen's npc_behavior conviction/framework to the migration roster first, then re-derive Hann's 'shares Vossen' note from the reconciled vector, or replace the by-reference definition with Hann's own explicit (migrated) values.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced every link. Hann is defined by reference: npc_behavior_v30.md:172 ('Shares Vossen's framework but more operationally focused') and :174 ('As Vossen'). Vossen's own behavior block :153-154 lists Equity (primary) + Continuity (secondary). The designated primary source, conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:91-101, maps Vossen to Honor 0.35 / Warden 0.25 / Community 0.20 with note 'Continuity legacy -> Honor+Warden+Community' -- no Equity primary. So the anchor Hann inherits conflicts with the primary source, propagating an unresolved conviction value into Hann by reference. character_canon §11 D1 names this exact Vossen conflict verbatim ('Yrsa Vossen: behavior says Equity/Continuity; migration roster says Honor/Warden/Community'). The finding is accurate and well-anchored. I hold it at MEDIUM rather than raising it: D1 explicitly classifies it as a surfaced, known conflict with a deferred (not yet executed) reconciliation cycle, so it is a documentation/by-reference contradiction rather than a live engine FAIL. Severity stands at MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-065 — Primary Conviction and Ethical Framework disagree across docs (prosperity/Stewardship vs Order+Autonomy/Faith-institutional)
