- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-doc stat consistency — Conviction / Ethical Framework; roster axis table & analyses vs arc_expansion_v30 & npc_behavior_v30 §2.13 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster axis assignment makes prosperity-maximisation his primary axis and the analyses give Ethics = 'Stewardship'. The arc doc and the behavior-tree both make Order primary, Autonomy secondary, and Ethical Framework = 'Faith (institutional variant)'. A GM reading the roster vs the arc/behavior docs would model a different decision-maker (and different Aligned/Contradictory Ob modifiers).
- **Evidence:** ROSTER/ANALYSES: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:203 ('Prudence Cardinal | 3 (prosperity — maximisation) | 2 (institutional faith) | 5 (order)'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:73 ('Ethics: Stewardship'). ARC/BEHAVIOR: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:282-284 (Primary Conviction Order, Secondary Autonomy, Ethical Framework 'Faith (institutional variant)' with -1/+1 Ob); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:278 (Prudence | Conviction 'Order + Autonomy' | Resonant Style 'Evidence (fiscal data)').
- **Fix:** Set one canonical Conviction stack + Ethical Framework for Tormann and propagate to the roster axis table, the §13 analysis, npc_behavior_v30 §2.13, and arc_expansion_v30. Note Resonant Style = Evidence is consistent across docs and can stand.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced, same single file-attribution slip as Finding 2. ROSTER/ANALYSES: npc_roster_v30.md:203 axis table = 'Prudence Cardinal | 3 (prosperity — maximisation) | 2 (institutional faith) | 5 (order)' (prosperity is Axis 1/primary); 'Ethics: Stewardship' is REAL and live at npc_roster_v30_infill.md:73, NOT npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:73 as cited. ARC/BEHAVIOR: arc_expansion_v30.md:282-284 = Primary Conviction Order, Secondary Autonomy, Ethical Framework 'Faith (institutional variant)' with the -1/+1 Ob modifiers; npc_behavior_v30.md:278 = 'Prudence (Tithes) | Order + Autonomy | Evidence (fiscal data)'. So a GM reading the roster axis (prosperity-primary, Stewardship ethics) vs the arc/behavior docs (Order-primary/Autonomy-secondary, Faith-institutional ethics) models a materially different decision-maker and different Aligned/Contradictory Ob triggers. The finding's note that Resonant Style = Evidence is consistent across docs is correct (confirmed at arc :285 and behavior :278). MEDIUM is appropriate: it is a real cross-doc stat divergence but a tier below the identity- and arc-level HIGH contradictions. CONFIRMED despite the wrong line pointer for the Stewardship quote.

#### AUD-NPC2-066 — Superseded placeholder name 'Vorn' still live for the Prudence Cardinal in multiple docs
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PROJECT NAMING RULE (CLAUDE.md) / ED-758 — superseded Cardinal placeholder surnames | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** ED-758 (2026-04-24) declared the arc_expansion proposed Cardinal surnames Stark/Voss/Vorn/Kald replaced by the canonical Jarnstal/Olafsson/Aldric Tormann/Klapp. 'Vorn' (the Prudence Cardinal's placeholder) still appears as live canon in several design docs, including this NPC's own behavior doc.
- **Evidence:** Superseded by ED-758: canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl:493. Live residue: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:547 ('Cardinal Officers (Stark/Voss/Vorn/Kald)'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:299 ('Vorn can no longer internally manage') and :312 ('players who ally with Vorn'); designs/audit/npc_faction_arc_interdependency_2026-04-18.md:45 ('Vorn (Cardinal Prudence)'). Already on the cleanup docket as the 'Voss/Vorn/Kald alias residue' item (designs/audit/2026-06-11-orchestration/valoria_master_workplan_v4.md:70,153 — J-27/J-28, LA-16).
- **Fix:** Replace 'Vorn' with 'Tormann' (and 'Voss/Stark/Kald' with Olafsson/Jarnstal/Klapp) in npc_behavior_v30.md:547, arc_expansion_v30.md:299/312, and the 2026-04-18 interdependency audit, per ED-758 / LA-16.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. ED-758 (editorial_ledger.jsonl:493, 2026-04-24) declares the arc_expansion proposed names Stark/Voss/Vorn/Kald replaced by canonical Jarnstal/Olafsson/Aldric Tormann/Klapp and states 'Bulk surname replacement applied across arc_expansion' — yet live residue remains: arc_expansion_v30.md:299 'Vorn can no longer internally manage' and :312 'players who ally with Vorn'; npc_behavior_v30.md:547 'Cardinal Officers (Stark/Voss/Vorn/Kald)'; npc_faction_arc_interdependency_2026-04-18.md:45 'Vorn (Cardinal Prudence)'. The ledger's claim that the replacement was applied is therefore contradicted by the surviving instances, so the superseded placeholder is still functioning as live canon in v30 docs. Mitigating context (does not reduce severity, since the names are still live): the issue is already on the cleanup docket as the 'Voss/Vorn/Kald alias residue' item in valoria_master_workplan_v4.md:70 (J-27/J-28) and :153 (LA-16 'Voss/Vorn/Kald alias sweep'). MEDIUM stands per the active CLAUDE.md naming rule / ED-758 supersession.

#### AUD-NPC2-067 — Legacy Conviction label 'Autonomy' used as live canon (Secondary Conviction) with no [SUPERSEDED->Liberty] annotation, in two docs
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon_v30 §1.1 L30, §11 D8 / conviction_taxonomy_v30 §2 (13-set, no "Autonomy") | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Aldric Tormann's Secondary Conviction is given as 'Autonomy' as a live, unannotated value. 'Autonomy' is a legacy 9-Conviction label that PP-684 §6 renames to Liberty; it is not one of the 13 canonical Convictions. It must not appear as live canon without a [SUPERSEDED->Liberty] marker (cf. character_canon §11 D8, which explicitly remaps the crisis-table 'Autonomy' entry to Liberty). Both the arc stat block and the behavior-doc Cardinal table carry the raw legacy label and even build live mechanics on it (Arc B 'Autonomy secondary activates' -> embezzlement; Arc C 'Tormann's Autonomy is real').
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:283 (Secondary Conviction | Autonomy), :303 ('Tormann's Autonomy secondary activates'), :312 ('Tormann's Autonomy is real'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:278 (Prudence | 'Order + Autonomy'). Canon: designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:1-3 + §2 L29-41; designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:30, :433 (D8 remap Autonomy->Liberty).
- **Fix:** Replace 'Autonomy' with 'Liberty' for Tormann's secondary Conviction in arc_expansion_v30.md:283 and npc_behavior_v30.md:278 (and the Arc B/C prose), or annotate as 'Liberty (← was Autonomy, legacy 9-Conv)' per the alias_registry / D8 pattern.
- **Verifier note:** Core claim reproduced: 'Autonomy' is a legacy 9-Conviction label (conviction_track_v1.md §1 L25, the 9-set = Faith/Order/Reason/Equity/Precedent/Autonomy/Continuity/Community/Warden), it is renamed Liberty (conviction_track_v1 L51 'Autonomy is no longer canonical (renamed Liberty per PP-684)' and L58 'Autonomy -> Liberty'; also character_canon D8 L433), and it is NOT among the 13 Convictions (conviction_taxonomy_v30 §2 L29-41; Liberty is #7). arc_expansion_v30:283 ('Secondary Conviction | Autonomy'), :303, :312 and npc_behavior_v30:278 ('Order + Autonomy') do carry the raw label with no inline marker. Two reasons it is overstated as HIGH: (1) the constraint_ref pins the rename to 'PP-684 §6', but I opened conviction_taxonomy §6 (L268-299) and its migration table contains NO 'Autonomy' entry at all (legacy labels listed are Categorical Imperative / Virtue Ethics / Divine Command / Epistemic Reason / Rawlsian / Military Honor / Reason / Continuity); the Autonomy->Liberty mapping lives in conviction_track_v1 and D8, not §6, and 'Autonomy' is absent from alias_registry.yaml and supersession_register.yaml. (2) npc_behavior_v30 is NOT unannotated: §1.2 L30 explicitly states 'Autonomy is renamed Liberty', so that doc carries a document-level supersession note (only the §2.13 table row at :278 lacks an inline tag), and D1 explicitly defers npc_behavior §2 regeneration as a separate propagation cycle ('Do not attempt to silently regenerate npc_behavior §2'). So it is a real, known, deferred-propagation residue in mainly arc_expansion, not a clean two-doc HIGH violation. MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-068 — Tormann is entirely absent from the designated PRIMARY Conviction source (conviction_migration_roster_v30, PP-685)
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (migration_roster PP-685 is PRIMARY source for Conviction values) / §3 reference | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Per character_canon §11 D1, conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) is the designated primary source for Conviction values when docs conflict. Tormann's Conviction vector is contested across docs (see companion finding), so D1's tie-breaker should apply — but Tormann has no entry in the roster at all. §2.3 (Ecclesiastical Faction) lists only Reichard, Baralta, and Lenneth. There is therefore no authoritative weighted 13-element vector for this NPC, and the D1 conflict-resolution rule cannot be executed.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 L135-179 (Ecclesiastical = Reichard/Baralta/Lenneth only; no Tormann/Prudence); §2 L36 ('following 13 named characters'). Primary-source rule: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1).
- **Fix:** Add a conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.3 entry for Aldric Tormann with a weighted primary_convictions vector (1-3 primaries summing 0.6-0.8) + cultural_label + self_other_initial, resolving the Order/Liberty vs prosperity-maximisation conflict, so D1 has a value to point at.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced the absence: conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.3 (L135-179) lists only Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth under Ecclesiastical; no Tormann/Prudence entry. §2 L37 scopes the roster to 'the following 13 named characters' and L37 calls the mappings 'recommendations per integration plan default,' i.e. it was never intended to be exhaustive. character_canon D1 (L426) does name the migration roster as primary 'when sources conflict.' So the finding is factually true but HIGH overstates it: D1 is a value tie-breaker, and the roster lacking a Tormann entry is the same underlying 'no canonical sheet authored yet' gap captured by Finding 7 (roster Stat Blocks deferred, analyses [Name Pending]). It blocks executing D1 mechanically but does not by itself break live play. MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-069 — Ethical Framework 'Faith (institutional variant)' used as a LIVE mechanical Ob driver (-1/+1 Ob) with no [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] note
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 + alias_registry (philosophical_tradition_legacy) + character_canon_v30 §11 D2 (PP-686 §3.7 replaces framework Ob drivers) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The arc stat block uses the framework label 'Faith (institutional variant)' as a live Ob source: 'Aligned: -1 Ob on any action that increases Church wealth/stability; Contradictory: +1 Ob on any action that sacrifices Church resources for theological purity.' Per PP-684 §6 + alias_registry + character_canon §11 D2, framework labels are SUPERSEDED as mechanical drivers — mechanical Ob now comes from the PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc, and any retained label must carry the [SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc] marker. This entry carries none.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:284 (Ethical Framework | Faith (institutional variant) | Aligned -1 Ob / Contradictory +1 Ob). Supersession: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:50, :385, :427 (D2); references/alias_registry.yaml:600-619 (philosophical_tradition_legacy 'Faith'; ethical_framework_modifier -> PP-686).
- **Fix:** Annotate the framework as a descriptive disposition tag with '[SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]' per D2, and either drop the inline -1/+1 Ob numbers or relocate them to the PP-686 §3.7 mission+cascade+expectation decomposition (clamped ±2).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: arc_expansion_v30:284 = 'Ethical Framework | Faith (institutional variant) | Aligned: -1 Ob on any action that increases Church wealth/stability; Contradictory: +1 Ob...' with no [SUPERSEDED] marker. alias_registry.yaml:600-619 lists 'Faith' under philosophical_tradition_legacy and routes ethical_framework_modifier -> PP-686, and character_canon D2 (L427), L50 and L385 confirm framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers and should be tagged. However HIGH overstates the breach: D2 explicitly says 'Mechanical Ob modifiers stay attached to the label since they're live in engine' and to NOT silently re-derive them — so the inline -1/+1 numbers are permitted to remain; what is missing is the descriptive-disposition [SUPERSEDED] annotation. The values are within the PP-686 ±2 clamp (they are ±1), so no mechanical clamp is violated. This is an annotation/traceability gap, not a live mechanical canon violation. MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-070 — Primary Conviction disagrees across docs: prosperity-maximisation/Stewardship (roster+analyses) vs Order primary + Autonomy/Liberty secondary (arc + behavior)
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-doc Conviction consistency — roster axis/analyses vs arc_expansion_v30 & npc_behavior_v30 §2.13 (re: AUD-NPC2-019) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster makes prosperity-maximisation Tormann's primary axis and the character analyses describe an 'OPTIMISER' whose ethic is Stewardship; the arc doc and behavior table instead set Primary Conviction = Order, Secondary = Autonomy(->Liberty). A GM reading the roster vs the arc/behavior docs models a different decision-maker and different Aligned/Contradictory Ob behavior. No reconciliation exists, and the absence of a migration-roster entry means there is no primary source to break the tie.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:161 + axis table L185-189; designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:63-67 (OPTIMISER, no Conviction label); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:282-283 (Order / Autonomy); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:278 (Order + Autonomy). Prior log: designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:361 (AUD-NPC2-019).
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical Conviction vector (recommend authoring it in the migration roster per D1), propagate to roster axis table, analyses, arc_expansion, and behavior §2.13; show legacy as '← was [old]' for traceability.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and corroborated by a prior verified audit. npc_roster_v30:203 puts Tormann on the 9-axis worldbuilding system as '3 (prosperity - maximisation) | 2 (institutional faith) | 5 (order)' (header at L185-189 confirms this is the worldbuilding_integration 9-axis system, not a Conviction vector), and analyses_infill L62-67 paints an 'OPTIMISER'/Stewardship portrait with no Conviction label; arc_expansion_v30:282-283 sets Primary Conviction = Order, Secondary = Autonomy, and npc_behavior_v30:278 = 'Order + Autonomy'. A GM does model a different decision-maker (different Aligned/Contradictory Ob). designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:361 logs this as AUD-NPC2-019 with verifier verdict CONFIRMED at MEDIUM and the same anchors. Note the roster 'prosperity' is a 9-axis position rather than a literal competing Conviction, but the cross-doc decision-modeling divergence is genuine. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-071 — Ethical Framework label inconsistent across docs: 'Stewardship' (roster/analyses) vs 'Faith (institutional variant)' (arc)
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Ethical-framework label consistency across docs for one NPC (taxonomy: frameworks must be consistent + not contradict conviction vector) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Tormann's disposition/framework tag is 'Stewardship' in the roster infill and character analyses, but 'Faith (institutional variant)' in the arc stat block. These are different framework labels for the same NPC, and 'Faith (institutional)' also sits awkwardly against the arc's own Certainty-3 'pragmatic believer who does not experience faith as revealed truth' characterisation. Per the framework-consistency rule a single NPC must not carry conflicting framework labels across docs.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:73 ('Ethics: Stewardship'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:64 (Suger 'wealth as instrument' / stewardship logic); vs designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:284 ('Faith (institutional variant)') with :288 (Certainty 3, 'does not experience it as revealed truth').
- **Fix:** Reconcile to one descriptive disposition tag (both retained only as [SUPERSEDED] tags per D2). 'Stewardship'/'oikonomia' fits the Suger characterisation and the Certainty-3 pragmatist better than 'Faith (institutional)'; whichever is chosen, propagate to all three docs.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced both anchors: npc_roster_v30_infill.md:73 = 'Ethics: Stewardship' (verified directly), analyses_infill:64 = 'The Suger parallel is about wealth as instrument' (stewardship logic), vs arc_expansion_v30:284 'Faith (institutional variant)' which sits against the same doc's Certainty-3 'pragmatic believer ... does not experience it as revealed truth' (:288). This is the framework-label half of the same divergence already logged as AUD-NPC2-019 (which explicitly names 'Stewardship vs Faith-institutional'), so it overlaps Finding 3; it adds the Certainty-3 internal-tension observation, which is fair. Real cross-doc inconsistency for one NPC. MEDIUM is appropriate (a single descriptive tag should be chosen and propagated).

#### AUD-NPC2-072 — Certainty and Beliefs are absent in the source-of-truth roster/analyses; present only in the arc doc under a still-proposed name
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** CANONICAL TAXONOMY — Certainty/Beliefs floor for a named NPC; character_canon_v30 §9.1 Tier (Beliefs + Certainty required) + §2 schema | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** A named NPC should carry a Certainty band + 2-3 Beliefs. The roster (design source of truth) provides none — its Stat Blocks section is explicitly 'Deferred to next session' (no TS/Certainty/Beliefs/Resonant Style), and the character_analyses entry is title-only '[Name Pending]'. The only Certainty (3) and Beliefs (3) live in arc_expansion_v30, and they are attached to a name still written as a proposal ('[Propose: Cardinal Aldric Tormann]'), not yet folded into the canonical character sheet. Net: the canonical roster/analyses for Tormann lack Certainty and Beliefs entirely.
- **Evidence:** No Certainty/Beliefs/RS in roster: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:388-390 ('Stat Blocks … Deferred to next session'); title-only analyses: designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:131 ('[Name Pending]'). Source present only in arc: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:288 (Certainty 3), :291-294 (Beliefs 1-3), :281 ('[Propose: …]').
- **Fix:** Promote Certainty 3 + the three Beliefs from arc_expansion_v30 into the canonical roster/character_canon sheet for Aldric Tormann (and resolve the '[Propose:]' to canonical per ED-758), so the named NPC carries Certainty + Beliefs at the source of truth.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_roster_v30:388-390 'Stat Blocks ... Deferred to next session. Each NPC requires: 10 TTRPG attributes, TS, Coherence, Beliefs (2), Inspirations, Resonant Style...' — so no Certainty/Beliefs/RS in the roster; npc_character_analyses_v30.md:131 is title-only '13. [Name Pending] - Prudence Cardinal' with an empty body. The only Certainty (3, arc:288) and Beliefs (1-3, arc:291-294) live in arc_expansion under '[Propose: Cardinal Aldric Tormann]' (arc:281). Net: the canonical roster/analyses carry neither Certainty nor Beliefs. Real authoring gap for a named NPC. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-073 — Feldhaus 'consequences' arc branch depends on an undefined, formally-open mechanic (Coherence effects on non-sensitive households)
- **Unit:** Annika Feldhaus | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** GAP-ARC-12 / ED-NEW-3 (designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** One of Feldhaus's two arc trajectories ('consequences') is built entirely on Thread-touched goods producing 'low-grade / subtle Coherence effects from prolonged exposure' in non-sensitive households over time ('she is poisoning her customers'). No such mechanic exists. It is explicitly registered as an OPEN design gap (GAP-ARC-12) and an unresolved editorial flag (ED-NEW-3, status FLAGGED). The arc therefore cannot be run as written, and the roster/analyses both self-flag it as 'a design gap.'
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:82 ('subtle Coherence effects from prolonged exposure to Thread-touched luxury goods ... poisoning her customers'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:96 ('potential low-grade Coherence effects on prolonged exposure — a design gap'); designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md:502 (GAP-ARC-12 'Thread-touched goods Coherence effects on non-sensitives not specified'); designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md:515 (ED-NEW-3 FLAGGED). The arc card uses an undefined placeholder 'RS -0.5/season in territory (fractional)' (arcs_10_18.md:202,218) but defines no household/non-sensitive Coherence rule.
- **Fix:** Resolve ED-NEW-3 with an actual mechanic before the 'consequences' branch is playable, or drop that branch and run Feldhaus solely through the 'discovery' branch (which is fully specified). If kept, specify trigger, accrual rate, cap, who is affected, and the stat/track it touches.
- **Verifier note:** All anchors reproduced. npc_character_analyses_v30.md:82 builds the 'consequences' trajectory on 'subtle Coherence effects from prolonged exposure to Thread-touched luxury goods ... poisoning her customers.' npc_roster_v30.md:96 self-flags 'potential low-grade Coherence effects on prolonged exposure - a design gap.' arcs_10_18.md:502 registers GAP-ARC-12 ('Thread-touched goods Coherence effects on non-sensitives not specified ... NPC roster flags as design gap') and :515 registers ED-NEW-3 with status FLAGGED (an open editorial flag, not resolved). The only fractional placeholder in the arc card is 'RS -0.5/season in territory' (arcs_10_18.md:202,218), which is a territory-level Reality-Strain value, NOT a household/non-sensitive Coherence rule - confirming no trigger/accrual/cap/affected-party/track is defined. One of Feldhaus's two trajectories therefore cannot be run as written. MEDIUM is fair: it disables a branch, not the whole NPC (the 'discovery' branch is fully specified at roster:294 / arcs_36_40).

#### AUD-NPC2-074 — Conviction is single-doc only and conflicts with the roster's 'Utility' ethics framing
- **Unit:** Annika Feldhaus (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 13-Conviction / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Feldhaus's only authored Conviction is 'Precedent' in faction_politics_v30.md §1.2c (her secondary Guild-Comptroller / Hafenmark-IC role). The roster and character analyses instead frame her core value-set as utilitarian ('greatest good for the greatest number of guild members', PROFIT-MAXIMISING). Precedent (reverence for ancestral practice) and Utility (instrumental results-maximising) are different Convictions in the 13-set and pull opposite ways mechanically (Precedent rewards the customary; PROFIT-MAXIMISING discards everything non-revenue). She has no entry in npc_behavior §2 stance triangles and no character_canon Part B sheet, so the contradiction is never reconciled. No 13-element weighted vector exists for her in any doc — only a bare single tag.
- **Evidence:** designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:187 (Feldhaus | Precedent | Consequence | 5); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:96 ('Ethics: Utility-oriented — greatest good for the greatest number of guild members'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:37 ('competent, profit-maximising'); npc_behavior_v30.md §2.16 Hafenmark Inner Council (lists only Heljason, Geirson — no Feldhaus row)
- **Fix:** Reconcile to one Conviction vector and propagate. If the design intent is the merchant-utilitarian, primary should be Utility (with Self-Other-positive orientation per taxonomy §2.3/§3 capturing the profit-maximising signature), Precedent as a secondary disposition. Land it in PP-685, then surface in npc_behavior §2 and a character_canon Part B sheet so the triangle is not stranded in the provincial faction doc.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors verify: faction_politics_v30.md:187 (Feldhaus | Precedent | Consequence | 5); npc_roster_v30.md:96 ('Ethics: Utility-oriented'); npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:37 ('profit-maximising'); npc_behavior_v30.md §2.16 (lines 365-370) lists only Heljason + Geirson, no Feldhaus row. So a label divergence (Precedent vs Utility/profit-maximising) genuinely exists across docs. BUT the finding's mechanical premise — that Precedent and 'PROFIT-MAXIMISING' 'pull opposite ways' and contradict — is refuted by the taxonomy itself: conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:63-65 states 'Greed, ambition, and self-aggrandizement are NOT Convictions ... represented via Self-Other orientation,' and a Utility-Conviction NPC 'can be either selfless or self-aggrandizing' depending on the orthogonal Self-Other scalar. Profit-maximising is a Self-Other signature, not a Conviction, so it does not mechanically contradict a 'Precedent' (or any) Conviction tag. The roster's 'Utility-oriented' is informal prose, not a competing Conviction vector. This is the same Conviction-label-divergence class the prior verifier rated MEDIUM (AUD-NPC2-023/027) as tracked propagation debt (character_canon D1: 'separate propagation cycle'). Real divergence, but HIGH overstated and the 'opposite mechanical pull' framing is wrong.

#### AUD-NPC2-075 — Ethical-framework label used as a LIVE mechanical Ob driver over Feldhaus's IC role, with no supersession note
- **Unit:** Annika Feldhaus (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / PP-686 §3.7 / character_canon_v30 §11 D2 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** faction_politics_v30.md §1.2 states the Hafenmark 'Faction ethical framework: Categorical Imperative' and then uses it as a live Ob modifier — 'Actions based on legal precedent, constitutional authority, and established procedure are −1 Ob; ad hoc or precedent-breaking actions are +1 Ob' — with no [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] annotation. Per PP-684 §6 + alias_registry + character_canon §11 D2, framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers; Ob comes from the PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc. Feldhaus sits on this Inner Circle (§1.2c), so this superseded driver governs her. The doc is CANONICAL (PP-660), so this is live canon, not a draft. 'Categorical Imperative' is explicitly in the alias_registry legacy list.
- **Evidence:** designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:157 (§1.2 'Faction ethical framework: Categorical Imperative ... are −1 Ob; ... are +1 Ob'); faction_politics_v30.md:3 (Status: CANONICAL, PP-660); references/alias_registry.yaml:604 (Categorical Imperative under philosophical_tradition_legacy); character_canon_v30.md:427 (D2: mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7)
- **Fix:** Add the [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc] marker to faction_politics §1.2 (and the parallel framework lines in §2.6/§2.7), retaining 'Categorical Imperative' only as a descriptive disposition tag. Route the −1/+1 Ob effect through PP-686 §3.7 (Precedent-weighted mission/cascade/expectation), not the raw label.
- **Verifier note:** Substantively reproduced in part: faction_politics_v30.md:157 (§1.2) presents 'Faction ethical framework: Categorical Imperative ... −1 Ob ... +1 Ob' with no inline supersession marker (grep for SUPERSEDED/PP-686/triadic in the file finds none); the doc is live canon (header line 3 CANONICAL/PP-660; listed with a canonical SHA in references/canonical_sources.yaml:410-413, not in deprecated/). The framework-as-Ob-driver IS superseded by PP-686 §3.7 (faction_behavior_v30.md:337-352 triadic calc; alias_registry.yaml:614-619; character_canon_v30.md:427 D2; supersession_register PP-686-001 lines 105-119). HOWEVER HIGH and 'governs Feldhaus' overstate it: (a) the PP-686-001 supersession scopes the superseded artifact to 'params/bg/core.md §Ethical Framework Modifiers' and its files_to_recheck list is params/* only — faction_politics is NOT enumerated as requiring the inline marker, so this is doc-level propagation lag, not a ratified-constraint FAIL; (b) Feldhaus is touched only via her secondary Hafenmark-IC role, so the §1.2 line governs her only in that capacity; (c) it is the same propagation-debt class the prior audit (AUD-NPC2-027) rated MEDIUM. Real and worth a marker, but not HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-076 — Maret Uln Thread Sensitivity conflict (~50 vs 35) crosses a mechanical threshold band
- **Unit:** Behavior priority-trees | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_roster_v30 §2 vs npc_behavior_v30 §2.10/§7.10 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster states Maret is 'TS ~50' (and the caste/succession prose treats her as a deep practitioner); the behavior doc states TS 35 in both §2.10 and the §7.10 NPC Stat Values table. This is not cosmetic: TS 50 is the Locking/Dissolution/Mending capability floor (alias_registry thread_operations) and the Deep band (+2 Knot-strain/season per §5.0b), whereas TS 35 is Active (Thread-Read only, +1 strain). Her succession arc, Knot-strain propagation, and which Thread ops she can perform all change between the two values.
- **Evidence:** npc_roster_v30.md:30 ('TS ~50'); npc_behavior_v30.md:240 ('Thread Sensitivity 35 ... Crosses Active threshold'); npc_behavior_v30.md:1194 (§7.10 'Maret Uln | 35'); band/capability thresholds: references/alias_registry.yaml:418-432, npc_behavior_v30.md:523-526.
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical TS for Maret and propagate. Behavior is internally consistent at 35; the roster '~50' appears to be the stale value (PP-486 era). Recommend correcting roster to 35 unless Deep-band capability is intended, in which case update §2.10/§7.10 and her Knot-strain row.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and the band-crossing is genuine. npc_roster_v30.md:30 states 'TS ~50' and the prose treats her as a deep practitioner (line 38 'TS ~50 means Maret perceives Thread-level phenomena'); the Mode Interface matrix line 238 also says 'TS ~50'. npc_behavior_v30.md:240 and the §7.10 table line 1194 both say 35. The threshold claim checks out: alias_registry.yaml:418-432 gates Locking/Dissolution/Mending at TS 50+, and the behavior doc's own §5.0b (lines 524-526) puts 30-49 in the Active band (+1 strain) vs 50-69 Deep (+2 strain), with §5.1 line 539 defining the Active threshold at 50. So ~50 vs 35 straddles the Active/Deep line and the Locking-capability floor — a real mechanical, not cosmetic, discrepancy. This one is a genuine intra-corpus stat conflict (not a taxonomy-migration deferral), so MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-077 — Sigrid Torsvald Thread Sensitivity conflict (35 vs '20+')
- **Unit:** Behavior priority-trees | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_roster_v30 §5 vs npc_behavior_v30 §7.10 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster states Torsvald is TS 35 consistently (§5 title, summary table, Mode Interface matrix, infill). The behavior doc's §7.10 NPC Stat Values table lists her at '20+'. TS 35 is Active (Thread-Read capable; +1 Knot-strain band); '20+' read literally spans Dormant (20–29, below the practitioner/Thread-Read threshold) into Active. Her whole 'TS in an anti-Thread org / can perceive Thread collateral' premise depends on being a practitioner, so the band matters.
- **Evidence:** npc_roster_v30.md:173 ('TS 35'), :271 ('TS 35'); npc_roster_v30_infill.md:32 ('TS 35'); npc_behavior_v30.md:1196 (§7.10 'Torsvald | 20+'). Migration-roster band reference: character_canon_v30.md:243-246.
- **Fix:** Set Torsvald to a single canonical TS (roster's 35 is the more-specified value and matches her practitioner premise) and correct §7.10 from '20+' to 35.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. The roster is consistently TS 35: npc_roster_v30.md:173 ('TS 35 in anti-Thread org'), :271 ('TS 35'), and infill :32 ('TS 35. Torsvald's TS developed during prolonged exposure...'). The behavior §7.10 table npc_behavior_v30.md:1196 lists 'Torsvald | 20+'. Read literally, '20+' spans Dormant (20-29, below the Stirring/Thread-Read practitioner floor per §5.1 line 539 and edeyja_npc.md:72) up into Active, whereas her entire premise (perceiving Thread collateral as a covert operative) requires practitioner capability. A single-stat conflict that crosses the practitioner threshold band. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-078 — Edeyja Thread Sensitivity stated as '75–80' in the stance triangle but '50+' in the §7.10 stat table
- **Unit:** Behavior priority-trees | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.9 vs §7.10 (and npc_roster_v30 §1) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Edeyja's stance triangle (§2.9) and the roster both state TS 75–80 (Apex band; the in-fiction highest living practitioner, the basis for her Coherence-9 discipline, Past-Oriented-Pulling capability at 70+, and the +3 Knot-strain band). The behavior doc's own §7.10 NPC Stat Values table lists her at '50+'. 50+ understates her by an entire capability tier (POP requires TS 70+ per alias_registry) and contradicts every other statement of her TS in the corpus.
- **Evidence:** npc_behavior_v30.md:217 (§2.9 'Thread Sensitivity 75–80'); npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 (§7.10 'Edeyja | 50+'); npc_roster_v30.md:20 ('TS 75–80. Coherence 9'); POP threshold references/alias_registry.yaml:414-417.
- **Fix:** Correct §7.10 Edeyja TS from '50+' to '75–80' to match §2.9, the roster, and edeyja_npc.md. ('50+' is the Locking/Dissolution floor and is the wrong band for the setting's apex practitioner.)
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md:217 (§2.9) states 'Thread Sensitivity 75-80'; npc_roster_v30.md:20 states 'TS 75-80. Coherence 9'; edeyja_npc.md:82 references 'TS 75+'. The §7.10 table npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 lists 'Edeyja | 50+'. The capability-tier claim is correct: Past-Oriented Pulling requires TS 70+ (alias_registry.yaml:414-417), while 50+ is only the Locking/Dissolution/Mending floor (alias_registry.yaml:418-432). So '50+' understates the setting's apex practitioner by a full capability tier and contradicts every other statement of her TS. Genuine intra-doc stat conflict. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-079 — Two different 'Haelgrund' NPCs with the same surname, same TS 12, and the same hidden-archive-Thread motif
- **Unit:** Behavior priority-trees | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_roster_v30 §4 vs npc_behavior_v30 §2.14 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster's Church Inquisitor is 'Sæmund Haelgrund' (TS 12, hidden Thread-sensitive, worldview-collapse-on-reveal). The behavior doc's Ministry throughline NPC is 'Registrar Lennart Haelgrund' (§2.14, TS 12 hidden, encountered Thread-adjacent phenomena in deep archives, Belief 'Something is wrong in the deep records'). Same surname, identical TS value, and the same buried-Thread-knowledge-in-archives arc shape, but different given names, factions (Church vs Ministry), and roles. Neither doc acknowledges the other or disambiguates; this reads as an accidental name/identity collision rather than two intended characters.
- **Evidence:** npc_roster_v30.md:56-65 ('Sæmund Haelgrund — Church Inquisitor', TS 12); npc_behavior_v30.md:299-321 ('Registrar Lennart Haelgrund — Ministry', TS 12 Hidden, deep-archive Thread encounter); both confirmed distinct in npc_character_analyses_v30_index.md (Sæmund only) and npc_behavior_v30.md:1195 (§7.10 lists 'Haelgrund | 12').
- **Fix:** Decide whether these are one character or two. If two, rename one (the duplicated surname + identical TS + identical archive-Thread hook is confusing) and have §7.10's bare 'Haelgrund | 12' row specify which. If one, merge the Church-Inquisitor and Ministry-Registrar entries. Either way add a cross-reference so the collision is not silent.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced in full. npc_roster_v30.md:56-65 = 'Sæmund Haelgrund — Church Inquisitor (Cardinal Justice),' TS 12, hidden Thread-sensitive, worldview-collapse-on-reveal (infill lines 23-28 confirm the Bellarmine/Inquisitor framing). npc_behavior_v30.md:299-321 = 'Registrar Lennart Haelgrund — Ministry,' TS 12 (Hidden), 'Encountered Thread-adjacent phenomena in deep archives,' Belief 'Something is wrong in the deep records,' Arc C advancing TS to 20+. Different given names (Sæmund vs Lennart), factions (Church vs Ministry), and roles, but identical surname + identical TS 12 + the same buried-Thread-knowledge-in-archives hook. §7.10 line 1195 lists a bare 'Haelgrund | 12' that disambiguates neither. Two same-surnamed TS-12 hidden-archive characters with no cross-reference is a legitimate naming/identity collision risk. MEDIUM is appropriate (confusing but not a mechanical or canon-law failure).

#### AUD-NPC2-080 — A canonical Crown succession contender (Cesare) has no behavioral spec in the doc that owns behavior
- **Unit:** Cesare | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §2/§5.2/§7.10 + Crown priority tree vs character_canon D7 / conviction_migration_roster §2.1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Cesare is canonical in the migration roster (full Conviction profile) and the relational graph (the 'canonical' rivalry example), and character_canon §11 D7 recommends including him as a succession-alternate stance entry. But he appears nowhere in npc_behavior_v30 §2 (named-NPC stance triangles), §5.2 (arc profiles), §7.10, or the Crown priority tree's succession/dynasty branch — so the Crown succession-competition the graph and roster assert has no behavioral realization. (Independently filed in the 2026-06-22 audit as AUD-NPC2-034, CONFIRMED MEDIUM.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:432 (D7: 'Cesare and Lorenzo ... are not in npc_behavior §2 named-NPC roster but have full conviction profiles in migration roster'; recommend Tier 1/2 succession-alternate entries); designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:103,302,548-558; absence across designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2/§5.2/§7.10; corroboration designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:108,465-469.
- **Fix:** Add Cesare (and Lorenzo) to npc_behavior_v30 §2/§5.2 as succession-alternate stance triangles (status: succession_alternate per sim §1.2, as D7 recommends) and reference them in the Crown priority tree's dynasty/succession branch, OR have the behavior doc explicitly delegate succession-alternate behavior to the migration roster with a cross-reference so the docs aren't silently disjoint.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. character_canon_v30 line 432 (D7) states Cesare/Lorenzo 'are not in npc_behavior §2 named-NPC roster but have full conviction profiles in migration roster' and recommends adding them as succession-alternate entries. The migration roster gives Cesare a full Conviction profile (lines 56-69). The relational graph instantiates the rivalry as canonical (lines 103, 302, 552; edges E-002/E-003 at 548-558). I confirmed the parallel 2026-06-22 audit independently files this as AUD-NPC2-034 with verifier verdict CONFIRMED MEDIUM (table line 108; detail lines 465-470 verifying absence across npc_behavior §2/§5.2/§7.10 and the Crown priority tree). A canonical succession contender with authored relational edges genuinely has no behavioral realization in the doc that owns priority trees and Resonant Style. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-081 — 'Close Knot eligibility' pegged to Disposition +3 contradicts canonical Knot Formation (Disposition +5 + TS>=30)
- **Unit:** Companion specification | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** params/core.md L149 (Knot Formation) + designs/personal/knots_v30.md L25 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** §2.1 'Knot path' offers Companion eligibility to 'Any NPC who forms a Close Knot — relational intimacy equivalent to Companion eligibility' at the doc's +3 threshold. Canonically a Knot is a Thread-substrate binding requiring Disposition +5 AND TS >= 30 (either party); non-sensitive pairs (TS<30 both) cannot form a Knot at all. Treating a 'Close Knot' as reachable at the +3 companion bar, with no TS gate, conflicts with the canonical formation rule and would let TS-0 dyads 'Knot.'
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md §2.1 L49; canon: params/core.md L149 ('Disposition +5 + TS ≥ 30 (either party) ... Non-sensitive (TS < 30 both): Knot impossible') and designs/personal/knots_v30.md L25 (Knot = binding at Disposition +5).
- **Fix:** Either gate the Knot-path companion route behind the real Knot Formation prerequisites (Disposition +5 + TS≥30) and reference knots_v30, or rename this path so it does not claim a 'Close Knot' it has not mechanically formed.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. companion §2.1 L49 'Knot path: Any NPC who forms a Close Knot - relational intimacy equivalent to Companion eligibility', under a section whose threshold is Disposition >=+3 (§1.1, §2.1 L46). Canon: params/core.md L149 'Knot Formation ... Disposition +5 + TS >= 30 (either party) ... Non-sensitive (TS < 30 both): Knot impossible' and knots_v30 §3.1 L81-85 (Disposition +5 AND TS>=30 prerequisites). A 'Close' Knot specifically is the top tier (knots_v30 §3.2-3.3), reachable only via Overwhelming success or upgrade. Phrasing a 'Close Knot' as reachable at the +3 companion bar with no TS gate conflicts with the formation rule. Real mechanical conflict, but the text says 'equivalent to Companion eligibility' (the route confers companion status; it does not redefine the +5/TS gate), so the practical risk is wording/cross-reference rather than a sim that lets TS-0 dyads Knot. MEDIUM is correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-082 — P-08 Evidence/TS gate (§6.1b) is an empty stub in the canonical skeleton — body lives only in the infill
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08; npc_behavior_v30.md §6.1b L684-686 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Himlensendt is TS 0 with Primary Resonant Style Evidence, and his Arc B fires on 'Evidence Resonant Style' defeat / Thread-adjacent findings (behavior §5.2 L598). The mechanic that keeps this P-08-compliant is §6.1b 'Evidence Resonant Style — Thread Sensitivity Gate (AUD-NPC-01 fix)', which restricts Evidence vs TS-0 NPCs to ONTICAL evidence and makes Thread-level evidence inert until TS crosses 30. That gate is fully written in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L112-114 and is correct — but in the canonical skeleton npc_behavior_v30.md the §6.1b heading (and §6.1) has NO body. Per the infill header, the skeleton is canonical and the infill is 'prose extracted from' it; a load-bearing P-08 gate that exists only in the derived infill is a canon-integrity gap. (No P-08 violation in intent — the design is clean — but the gate must live in the canonical doc.)
- **Evidence:** Empty in skeleton: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L684-686 (§6.1b heading then blank; §6.1 L680-682 also blank); index confirms 19-token stub: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_index.md L179. Correct body present only in infill: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L112-114. P-08 text: canon/02_canon_constraints.md L17.
- **Fix:** Backfill the §6.1b (and §6.1) body into npc_behavior_v30.md from the infill L112-114 so the P-08 Evidence/TS gate is canonical in the skeleton, not only in the extracted prose.
- **Verifier note:** Verified directly: npc_behavior_v30.md L678-688 shows '### s6.1 Appraise Revelation' and '### s6.1b Evidence Resonant Style - Thread Sensitivity Gate (AUD-NPC-01 fix)' as headings with blank bodies. The full P-08 gate body is present only in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L112-114 (Evidence vs TS-0 restricted to ontical evidence; Thread-level evidence inert; exception when TS crosses 30 - the mechanical gate for Himlensendt Arc C). The skeleton/infill headers confirm the directionality the finding relies on: skeleton L1-2 'SKELETON - mechanical spec only ... Infill: npc_behavior_v30_infill.md'; infill L1-2 'INFILL - prose/rationale extracted from npc_behavior_v30.md / Skeleton: npc_behavior_v30.md'. A load-bearing P-08 mechanic (canon constraints L17) existing only in the derived/extracted prose, not the canonical mechanical skeleton, is a genuine canon-integrity gap. Consistent with the cited 19-token stub at npc_behavior_v30_index.md L179. MEDIUM is appropriate (no P-08 violation in intent; the gate is correct where written).

#### AUD-NPC2-083 — Himlensendt Arc C ('Confrontation') in the canonical skeleton has only a Risk line — no branch condition or body
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2 (Himlensendt Arc Map) L592-602 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Arc C is the TS-awakening arc the audit was asked to check ('Arc C TS-awakening (P-08 gate)'). In the canonical skeleton, Arc C consists solely of a 'Risk:' line; it has no branch condition, no Effect/Conviction/Behavior text. Arc B likewise lacks its Conviction body. The substantive, P-08-compliant Arc C definition — branch condition 'Direct contact with threadcut being ... OR brought to Locked Zone. Requires physical presence — cannot be achieved through argument alone' (which is exactly what makes the TS-awakening P-08-safe) — exists only in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L79-82. As written in the skeleton, the canonical arc cannot be run and its P-08 safeguard (physical-confrontation-only, not argument) is invisible.
- **Evidence:** Skeleton stub: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L601-602 (Arc C 'Confrontation' = a single 'Risk:' line); Arc B L597-599 missing Conviction body. Full P-08-compliant body only in infill: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L72-82 (Arc C branch 'Requires physical presence — cannot be achieved through argument alone'; 'TS may awaken from 0').
- **Fix:** Restore the Himlensendt Arc B/C bodies into the skeleton from infill L72-82, preserving the explicit 'cannot be achieved through argument alone / requires physical confrontation' branch condition that carries the P-08 compliance.
- **Verifier note:** Verified in npc_behavior_v30.md L592-602: under 'Arne Himlensendt - Arc Map', Arc C 'Confrontation' (L601-602) consists solely of a 'Risk:' line - no branch condition, no Effect/Conviction/Behavior. The substantive, P-08-carrying body is only in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L79-82: branch 'Direct contact with threadcut being ... OR brought to Locked Zone. Requires physical presence - cannot be achieved through argument alone' and 'TS may awaken from 0'. As written in the skeleton, the arc cannot be run and its P-08 safeguard (physical confrontation, not argument) is invisible - matching the finding. One overstatement: the finding also says 'Arc B likewise lacks its Conviction body,' but skeleton Arc B (L597-599) DOES carry a branch condition and a Behavior line (it lacks only an explicit Conviction line), so Arc B is not a bare stub. The headline subject (Arc C) is accurate, so the finding stands at MEDIUM; the Arc B aside is slightly overstated but not load-bearing.

#### AUD-NPC2-084 — 'Reichard' name collision: Church-head placeholder reuses the surname of the canonical Hafenmark Lord Treasurer (Annalie Reichard)
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L360 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** 'Reichard' is already a live canonical surname for a different NPC — Annalie Reichard, Hafenmark Lord Treasurer (a real, distinct inner-council figure). Using 'Cardinal Reichard' for the Church head in the relational graph and conviction-migration roster creates a cross-faction name collision: two unrelated 'Reichard' characters in different factions. Even after the head is renamed to Himlensendt, this should be checked so the rename does not leave a dangling implied relationship between the two.
- **Evidence:** Canonical Hafenmark NPC: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L360 '| Lord Treasurer | Annalie Reichard | Precedent | Evidence | 5 | Distant Feldhaus cousin ...'. Colliding misuse: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md L473, L475; designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md L137.
- **Fix:** After renaming the Church head to Himlensendt (findings above), confirm no remaining doc implies 'Cardinal Reichard' and 'Annalie Reichard' are the same person or related; keep 'Reichard' reserved for the Hafenmark Lord Treasurer only.
- **Verifier note:** The collision is real and registry-acknowledged: Annalie Reichard is a live canonical Lord Treasurer (behavior s2.15 L360; faction_politics_v30.md L146; faction_canon_v30.md L500; npc_registry NPC-037), while 'Cardinal Reichard' is used for the Church head in npc_relational_graph_v30.md L473/L475 and conviction_migration_roster_v30.md L137. npc_registry.yaml L1031-1034 explicitly asks whether these are the same person ('OPEN CARDINAL REICHARD COLLISION'), proving the collision is a tracked concern. HOWEVER the finding's issue text misattributes Annalie Reichard's faction as 'Hafenmark Lord Treasurer' - she is the CROWN (Inner Circle) Lord Treasurer (behavior s2.15 L355-360 'Crown Inner Circle'; npc_registry NPC-037 faction 'Crown (Inner Circle)', with an explicit CORRECTED note 'was listed as Church/Cardinal - actually Crown Lord Treasurer'). The cited evidence anchor (L360) is correct; only the prose label is wrong. The factual slip does not change that a cross-NPC surname collision exists, so the finding holds at MEDIUM, but the 'Hafenmark' descriptor should be read as 'Crown'. Kept at MEDIUM (not downgraded) because the underlying collision is genuine and registry-flagged.

#### AUD-NPC2-085 — Evidence-primary RS on a TS 0 NPC, and the P-08 TS gate (§6.1b) is an EMPTY BODY in the canonical source — the gate text lives only in the _infill overlay
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 / AUD-NPC-01 / npc_behavior_v30 §6.1b (Thread-level Evidence inert vs TS<30; Stirring-30 gate) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Himlensendt is Resonant-Style Evidence (primary) with TS 0 (foreclosed). The protective gate — §6.1b 'Evidence Resonant Style — Thread Sensitivity Gate (AUD-NPC-01 fix)' — has NO body in the canonical npc_behavior_v30.md (header present, lines blank). The actual rule (ontical-only vs TS 0; Thread-level Evidence valid only if TS crosses Stirring 30+, naming Himlensendt Arc C) exists only in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md §6.1b and as a one-line recap in character_canon_v30 §5. A reader of the canonical doc sees an Evidence-primary TS 0 NPC with no operative gate; the §2.2 note even frames Evidence as 'the world is not what revelation says' (Thread-shaped) without restating the gate. Set-membership itself is clean (Evidence + Authority are both within the ED-516 four-style set).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §6.1b L684-687 (empty body) + §2.2 L73-75 (Evidence primary, TS 0); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md §6.1b L112-114 (full gate text + Stirring-30 exception naming Himlensendt Arc C); character_canon_v30.md §5 L170 ('Thread-level evidence is invalid as Evidence targeting vs TS 0 NPCs (P-08)'); editorial_ledger ED-516 L325
- **Fix:** Fold the infill §6.1b gate text into the canonical npc_behavior_v30.md body so the gate is not lost if the infill overlay is dropped. Add a one-line pointer in the §2.2 Evidence/TS rows ('Thread-level Evidence inert until TS>=30 per §6.1b') so the stat block is self-consistent with the P-08 gate.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md §6.1b (L684-687) has the header 'Evidence Resonant Style — Thread Sensitivity Gate (AUD-NPC-01 fix)' followed by blank lines — empty body confirmed. The operative gate text exists only in npc_behavior_v30_infill.md §6.1b (L112-114), including the Stirring-30 exception that explicitly names 'Himlensendt Arc C'. §2.2 confirms Himlensendt is Evidence-primary (L73) with TS 0 (L75), and the §2.2 Evidence note frames the lever as 'the world is not what revelation says' (Thread-shaped) without restating the gate. character_canon §5 L170 carries the one-line P-08 recap ('Thread-level evidence is invalid as Evidence targeting vs TS 0 NPCs') as the finding states, and ED-516 is confirmed resolved in editorial_ledger.jsonl L325 (4-style taxonomy sufficient), so set-membership is clean as claimed. The substantive risk is real: if the infill overlay is dropped, the canonical doc presents an Evidence-primary TS-0 NPC with no operative P-08 gate. MEDIUM is appropriate — the rule is not lost today (it survives in infill + character_canon recap), but the canonical body should not be empty.

#### AUD-NPC2-086 — npc_behavior_v30 §5 arc state-machine and §5.2 arc maps use superseded labels (Reason/Continuity/Autonomy) as live Conviction destinations
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon §11 D4+D8 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The generic transition table and per-NPC arc maps describe Conviction transitions in terms of the superseded 9-set as live values (e.g. 'Collapses to Autonomy', 'Order -> Reason', 'Reason -> Continuity'), so arc behavior is keyed to non-canonical Convictions without annotation. character_canon §8.1 already remapped the equivalent generic trigger ('Collapses to Liberty'), so §5 here is internally inconsistent with the consolidation.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §5.1 'Faction Stability <=1 | Collapses to Autonomy' (L537), 'Faction eliminated | Conviction collapses to Autonomy' (L541); §5.2 Almud 'Order -> Reason' (L565), 'Order -> Autonomy' (L570); Baralta 'Falls to Autonomy'; Vaynard 'Reason remains primary' / 'Reason -> Continuity' / 'Reason -> collapses'; Edeyja 'Reason activates as secondary'. Same content duplicated in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md L66, L89, L93, L97, L101, L105.
- **Fix:** Propagate PP-684 remap through §5/§5.2 and the infill: Autonomy->Liberty, Reason->Scholastic, Continuity->Warden; align with the already-corrected generic trigger in character_canon §8.1.

#### AUD-NPC2-087 — npc_behavior §2 carries Ethical Framework labels as live Ob drivers without the [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] note
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon §11 D2 (framework labels SUPERSEDED per PP-686 §3.7) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Every §2 stance triangle still presents an 'Ethical Framework' row with live -1/+1/+2 Ob modifiers (Virtue, Faith, Categorical Imperative, Utility-driven Pragmatism, Martial Honour, Equity Social Contract, Moral Relativism, Administrative Proceduralism). Per PP-684 §6 + SUPERSESSION-PP686-001/002 these are superseded as mechanical drivers (replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob), retained only as descriptive tags with a [SUPERSEDED] marker (character_canon §11 D2). The markers are absent here, so the labels read as live Ob mechanics.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.1 'Ethical Framework | Virtue (Crown) | -1 Ob...' (L52); §2.2 'Faith (Church)... +2 Ob' (L72); §2.3 'Categorical Imperative (Hafenmark)' (L93); §2.4 'Utility-driven Pragmatism' (L113); §2.5 'Martial Honour' (L134); §2.6/§2.7 'Equity Social Contract' (L155/L174); §2.11 'Moral Relativism' (L255); §2.14 'Administrative Proceduralism' (L305).
- **Fix:** Append '[SUPERSEDED-> mechanical Ob via PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc]' to each Ethical Framework row (retain as descriptive disposition tag only), per character_canon §11 D2.

#### AUD-NPC2-088 — companion_specification_v30 §6.1 triggers a live mechanic on 'Reason' Conviction (superseded)
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 (companion uses superseded label as live mechanic) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The companion departure table keys an Ontological-crisis departure to a companion holding 'Reason or Precedent Conviction'. Reason is a deprecated 9-set label (->Scholastic/Authority per PP-684 §6); using it as a live mechanical trigger means a canonical Conviction value here does not exist in the 13-set.

- **Fix:** Replace 'Reason' with 'Scholastic' (the epistemic-framework heir per PP-684 §6); keep 'Precedent' (canonical). The adjacent §6.1 'Faith, Order, or Equity' trigger (L160) is canonical and clean.

#### AUD-NPC2-089 — Baralta: conviction/framework/template disagree across roster, behavior, analyses (Faith-primary vs Precedent-primary vs Categorical Imperative)

Decide which is canonical and propagate; annotate the framework label [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7].

#### AUD-NPC2-091 — Maret Uln Thread Sensitivity is internally inconsistent (TS 35 vs TS ~50) across the NPC docs

#### AUD-NPC2-092 — Canonical timeline still lists Niflhel as a live faction and records no dissolution event
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** canon/03_canonical_timeline.md vs roster 'DISSOLVED' + behavior 'STRUCK' | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster header asserts Niflhel is DISSOLVED and the behavior doc says STRUCK (ED-757 / conflict_architecture_proposal), but the canonical timeline still carries Niflhel as a live faction with starting stats and contains no dated dissolution event. The dissolution that Virke's entire current identity hinges on is unanchored in the timeline and contradicted by the timeline's own faction table.
- **Evidence:** canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L155 faction starting-values table row 'Niflhel | — | 5 | 4 | — | — | 4'; no match for dissolv/struck/conflict_architecture anywhere in canon/03_canonical_timeline.md; vs designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L125 'DISSOLVED' and designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L268/L867 'STRUCK — Niflhel dissolved'.
- **Fix:** Add a dated Niflhel-dissolution event to canon/03_canonical_timeline.md (citing the conflict_architecture_proposal / ED-757), and either remove the Niflhel faction row at L155 or annotate it as pre-dissolution starting state with a pointer to the dissolution event.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced exactly. canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L155 carries the faction starting-values row 'Niflhel | — | 5 | 4 | — | — | 4' (live faction with stats). Exhaustive case-insensitive grep over the timeline for dissolv/struck/conflict_architecture returns nothing, and a grep for 'niflhel' returns ONLY L155 — there is no dated dissolution event anywhere in the file. This contradicts roster L125 'DISSOLVED' and behavior L268/L867 'STRUCK — Niflhel dissolved'. ED-757 is a real editorial decision (appears in editorial_ledger migration, audit docs, and the behavior doc) but it has not been propagated to the timeline, so the dissolution Virke's current identity hinges on is unanchored in the timeline and contradicted by its own faction table. The fix (add a dated dissolution event citing ED-757/conflict_architecture_proposal and annotate/remove L155) is correct. MEDIUM fair — timeline is canon but the row is a starting-state table rather than a live mechanical lever.

#### AUD-NPC2-093 — Index and infill titles still carry the superseded 'Syndicate Broker (Niflhel)' / 'Virke syndicate' label as canonical
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** Roster header/table 'Independent / DISSOLVED' vs stale index + infill titles | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Every index and the infill heading label Virke with the pre-dissolution faction title, disagreeing with the roster's current 'Independent Intelligence Broker (formerly Niflhel — DISSOLVED)'. This is the title-vs-index disagreement flagged in the task brief; it propagates the dissolved faction as her live affiliation across navigation/lookup surfaces.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_index.md L29 & L75 'Dalla Virke — Syndicate Broker (Niflhel)', L46 & L92 'Dalla Virke (Virke syndicate)'; designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md L52 '## 10. Dalla Virke — Syndicate Broker (Niflhel)'; designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_index.md L29 & L65; vs designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L125 header.
- **Fix:** Regenerate the indexes (doc_index_gen) and update the infill heading to the canonical title 'Dalla Virke — Independent Intelligence Broker (formerly Niflhel — DISSOLVED)' / 'Dalla Virke (settlement-tied independent broker)'. The header L125 is the canonical anchor; conform all titles to it.
- **Verifier note:** Verified verbatim: npc_roster_v30_index.md L29 & L75 'Dalla Virke — Syndicate Broker (Niflhel)', L46 & L92 'Dalla Virke (Virke syndicate)'; npc_roster_v30_infill.md L52 '## 10. Dalla Virke — Syndicate Broker (Niflhel)'; npc_character_analyses_v30_index.md L29 & L65 carry the analysis titles (these are 'The Trust Economy', not the faction label, but the roster indexes do). The canonical anchor roster header L125 says 'Independent Intelligence Broker (formerly Niflhel — DISSOLVED)'. The roster index is provably an auto-generated artifact (file header line 1: '<!-- INDEX — auto-generated by doc_index_gen.py -->', line 2 declares 'canonical: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md', generated 2026-04-19) — so the stale faction label is genuine generation-lag against a later-edited canonical header, exactly as described, and the fix (regenerate via doc_index_gen) is correct. Caveat: the roster's OWN mode-bridge table heading L323 also still says '(Virke syndicate)', so it is not purely an index/infill problem — but the finding's narrower claim is reproduced. Navigation/lookup surface, not live mechanics: MEDIUM is appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-094 — No Conviction vector exists for Virke in any doc — she cannot be run in decision/contest math
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §1/§2 (13-Conviction vector) + character_canon_v30 §3.5 (effective_convictions / PP-686 §3.2 cascade) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Virke is a named, TTRPG-Full roster NPC, but no personal_convictions vector (primary 1-3 of the 13 + weights) is stated anywhere. The decision-derivation and Contest systems consume the 13-element vector; without it she has no Stance Triangle, no Decision Forks, and no effective_convictions cascade. The roster gives only an 'Ethics' prose label. Note the prior 2026-06-22 audit folded this into one LOW omnibus gap (AUD-NPC2-099); decomposed against PP-684 it is a hard runnability gap on the conviction axis.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md — 'Virke' absent entirely (Grep 0 hits); §2.12 is 'Niflhel ... STRUCK' (L267-268); §7.10 stat table L1184-1198 has no Virke row; designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md has no Virke Part B sheet (Part B 'pending Q1 scope', header L2); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L130 gives only 'Ethics: Honour among operators' prose.
- **Fix:** Author personal_convictions for Virke (her trust-network/oath disposition maps to Honor primary + Identity/Community secondary) with weights summing 0.6-0.8 and a cultural template, in the new sheet and in character_canon Part B.
- **Verifier note:** The core claim reproduces: no personal_convictions vector for Virke exists anywhere. Verified Grep 'Virke' = 0 hits in npc_behavior_v30.md; §7.10 stat table (L1184-1198) lists 13 named NPCs, no Virke; character_canon_v30.md = 0 Virke hits and Part B is 'pending Q1 scope decision' (L2, L437); npc_character_analyses_v30.md L105 is a title-only stub with an empty body; npc_roster_v30.md L130 gives only the prose 'Ethics' line. The migration roster (PP-685, CANONICAL) does consume a primary-1-3 + weighted 13-Conviction vector (L21-29), so the system input is real and Virke lacks it. Even the prior 2026-06-22 audit's verifier note (AUD-NPC2-099, L931) concedes 'no Conviction is authored anywhere' as the genuine Virke-specific defect. So the finding is CONFIRMED on fact. I downgrade severity from HIGH to MEDIUM because 'she cannot be run at all' overstates: PART B per-NPC sheets (where ALL Convictions live per L190) are unwritten for every one of the ~37 NPCs — this is a systemic authoring-stage gap, not a Virke-unique breakage — and she does carry a live Behavioral AI flaw (NETWORK PROTECTOR), an Ethics disposition, and full mode coverage that let a GM table-run her. The Conviction axis specifically is missing; that is a real MEDIUM runnability gap on one axis, not a HIGH 'unrunnable NPC.'

#### AUD-NPC2-095 — Virke is absent from the PP-685 migration roster, the designated primary source for Conviction values
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) §2 — DESIGNATED PRIMARY source per character_canon_v30 §11 D1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** D1 makes conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) the PRIMARY source for Conviction values when docs conflict. The roster maps 13 named characters but does not include Dalla Virke. The only Niflhel-faction entry is a generic 'Niflhel envoy (placeholder)' (Scholastic/Utility/Authority) — which cannot stand in for her: Niflhel is dissolved, she is a named TTRPG-Full broker, and that placeholder's intelligence-diplomat profile contradicts her authored Honor/trust-network disposition. So even the primary source has no value to anchor her to.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2 lists 13 named characters (Almud, Cesare, Lorenzo, Yrsa Vossen, Maret Uln, Solmund, Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth, Hafenmark Speaker, Lowenritter Master, Restoration Spokesperson, Niflhel envoy) — no 'Virke'; §2.7 'Niflhel envoy (placeholder)' L232-245; primary-source rule character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 L426.
- **Fix:** Add a Dalla Virke entry to conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2 (replacing the now-dissolved 'Niflhel envoy' placeholder for her) with prior_labels, primary_convictions, cultural_label, and self_other_initial, so the primary source carries her value.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced in full. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md is CANONICAL (header L1/L6, PP-685) and §2 lists exactly 13 named characters (Almud, Cesare, Lorenzo, Yrsa Vossen, Maret Uln, Solmund, Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth, Hafenmark Speaker, Lowenritter Master, Restoration Spokesperson) plus the §2.7 'Niflhel envoy (placeholder)' (L232-245) — Grep 'Virke' = No matches in this file, so she is genuinely absent. character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 (L426) confirms this roster is the PRIMARY source when Conviction values conflict. The §2.7 placeholder is authored as Scholastic 0.30 / Utility 0.30 / Authority 0.10, cultural_label hafenmark_procedural, 'intelligence-diplomatic archetype' — which does contradict her authored Honor/trust-network disposition (roster L130; infill L113 'your word is your network'), so it cannot stand in for her. MEDIUM is appropriate: it is the same authoring gap as F2 viewed from the primary-source doc (real, but a propagation/authoring task, not a canon-rule violation).

#### AUD-NPC2-096 — No Resonant Style assigned to Virke; her implied Solidarity resonance has no defined Knot path
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (4 Resonant Styles) + npc_behavior_v30 §6 / character_canon_v30 §5 (named NPC requires primary+secondary RS) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Named NPCs require a primary (and usually secondary) Resonant Style from the 4-set (Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity) to be targetable in the Contest and Belief/Scar systems; Virke has none anywhere. Her authored material (a decade of personal-trust relationships, the relationship-duration DISCLOSE/CONCEAL decision) points squarely at a Solidarity primary — but Solidarity 'requires an active Knot with the NPC' (character_canon §5 L168; behavior §1.3), and no Knot path to Virke is defined. So even the style her design implies is unusable as written.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §6 has no Virke entry (Grep 0 hits); character_canon_v30.md §5 L161-168 (Solidarity 'requires active Knot'); arc material implying relational resonance: designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md L219 (DISCLOSE/CONCEAL), L227 ('decides based on relationship duration: 5+ year partners = tell'); ED-516 in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl L325.
- **Fix:** Assign Virke a Resonant Style primary+secondary (Solidarity primary fits the trust-network design; Consequence or Evidence secondary). If Solidarity is primary, define the Knot path (how a PC forms a Knot with her — e.g., via the trust-network relationship the roster already describes).
- **Verifier note:** Factual core confirmed: no Resonant Style is assigned to Virke (Grep RS terms in roster S10 = No matches; she is absent from npc_behavior §6, 0 Virke hits), and no Knot path to her is defined anywhere (Grep 'Knot.*Virke|Virke.*Knot' across designs/ returns only two hits, both in 2026-04-29 topographic-analysis corpus JSON — analysis artifacts, not design canon). character_canon §5 L168 does state Solidarity 'requires active Knot with the NPC,' and the arc material (arcs_10_18.md L219 DISCLOSE/CONCEAL, L227 'relationship duration: 5+ year partners = tell') is real. BUT 'her implied Solidarity resonance' is the auditor's own inference, not authored canon — nothing in any doc assigns or even names Solidarity for her. The actual defect is simply 'no RS authored,' a subset of the unwritten-Part-B gap; the Knot-path concern is contingent on a styling choice that hasn't been made. ED-516 (editorial_ledger.jsonl L325) confirms the 4-style taxonomy. Downgraded HIGH->MEDIUM: real missing-axis authoring gap, but the dramatic 'even the style her design implies is unusable as written' rests on an unauthored assumption, not a live contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-097 — Laskaris's interface depends on the Tutoring Demand, whose IP trigger is canonically contradicted (IP 30 vs IP >= 40)
- **Unit:** Doux Alexios Laskaris | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Tutoring Demand trigger (timeline vs params vs arcs) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Laskaris's Hybrid interface lets PCs 'delay the next Tutoring Demand by 1 season', but the Tutoring Demand's trigger value is contradicted across canonical docs: the canonical timeline says IP 30, while params and the arc docs say IP >= 40 (the params doc explicitly corrects 'not 30'). The mechanic Laskaris depends on therefore has no single authoritative trigger.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:339 ('delays the next Tutoring Demand by 1 season'); canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:141 ('Altonia's tutoring demand triggers at IP 30') CONTRADICTS params/bg/npcs_special.md:18 ('Altonian Tutoring Demand triggers at IP >= 40 ... (not 30 ...)') and designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_41_45.md:347,382,421 (all 'IP >= 40').
- **Fix:** Reconcile to the IP >= 40 value (params + arcs, explicit correction) and patch canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:141 from 'IP 30' to 'IP 40' (or log the supersession). Laskaris's delay-by-1-season effect should reference the reconciled trigger.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced the contradiction. canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:141 reads 'Altonia's tutoring demand triggers at IP 30.' params/bg/npcs_special.md:18 reads 'Altonian Tutoring Demand triggers at IP >= 40 ... (not 30 ...)' — an explicit correction. arcs_41_45.md uses IP >= 40 throughout (:348, :383, :419, :421). npc_roster_v30.md:339 has Laskaris 'delays the next Tutoring Demand by 1 season.' The cross-doc trigger-value contradiction (timeline 30 vs params/arcs 40) is live and real, so CONFIRMED at MEDIUM. Caveat noted but not severity-changing: the finding's framing that the contradiction leaves Laskaris's mechanic with 'no single authoritative trigger' is overstated — params already designates IP>=40 as authoritative ('not 30'), and Laskaris's 'delay by 1 season' effect is value-agnostic and functions regardless of the numeric threshold. The defect is a stale timeline line (141 still says 30) needing reconciliation, which is exactly the MEDIUM contradiction the title asserts.

#### AUD-NPC2-098 — Laskaris has a named behavioral AI and a transformation flip but zero presence in the NPC behavior system
- **Unit:** Doux Alexios Laskaris | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30.md (stance triangle / arc profile / priority tree) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster assigns Laskaris a named behavioral AI ('PROTECTIVE') and a state-changing 'flip' (slow-burn -> crisis), which is a behavioral transformation. Yet 'Laskaris'/'Alexios' appear nowhere in npc_behavior_v30.md or its infill/index: he has no Stance Triangle (Section 2), no Arc Profile / state machine (Section 5), no Priority Tree (Section 8), and no defined Beliefs or Conviction value, unlike the named/roster NPCs that are covered there. His decision rule (when to sandbag vs flip) is specified only as prose in the roster.
- **Evidence:** Roster behavioral AI + flip: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:141 and designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:118. Absence: Grep for 'Laskaris'/'Alexios'/'PROTECTIVE' returns no matches in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md or _infill; the Section 2 stance-triangle index (designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_index.md:25-43) enumerates 2.1-2.17 with no Laskaris/Altonian entry.
- **Fix:** Add a behavior-doc entry for Laskaris (at minimum a Conviction value + Beliefs and the PROTECTIVE->flip transition as an arc/priority rule), or add an explicit cross-reference note in npc_behavior_v30.md stating Laskaris's behavior is governed solely by the roster BG card so the omission is intentional and traceable.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. Grep for 'Laskaris|Alexios|PROTECTIVE' in npc_behavior_v30.md and npc_behavior_v30_infill.md returns zero matches (only generic 'Altonian' faction references appear). The Section 2 stance-triangle index enumerates §2.1-§2.17 (npc_behavior_v30_index.md:26-43) with no Laskaris/Altonian-rep entry. Section 5 Arc Maps (index:58-62) cover only Almud, Himlensendt, Baralta, Vaynard, Edeyja — no Laskaris. Section 8 priority trees are faction-level only. Meanwhile the roster assigns him a named behavioral AI 'PROTECTIVE' and a state-changing flip (npc_roster_v30.md:141; npc_character_analyses_v30.md:118 'slow-burn to crisis'). I checked the one plausible refutation — npc_behavior_v30.md:1218 §11 'Named NPC Roster Tracking Capacity' — but it is generic tier/capacity machinery (Active/Passive/Background, ~35 cap) and does NOT name Laskaris or give him Conviction/Beliefs/stance/arc. So the 'zero presence' gap holds: a behaviorally-active, transformation-bearing NPC has no entry in the behavior system. CONFIRMED at MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-099 — Two incompatible primary Convictions: Precedent/Order (behavior+analysis) vs Faith 0.50 'selfless devotional' (migration roster)
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior §2.3 vs conviction_migration_roster §2.3 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Baralta's authored Conviction profile contradicts her migration-roster vector at the primary axis. Behavior §2.3 and the Arc Map make her Primary Conviction Precedent (Secondary Faith, 'privatised, does not govern her political decisions'), and the character analysis frames her as a proceduralist ('Constitutional procedure IS justice'). The migration roster instead gives Primary Faith 0.50, Virtue 0.20, Warden 0.10 with the note 'strong primary Faith... Selfless devotional archetype'. Precedent-primary-with-privatised-Faith and Faith-primary-devotional are mutually exclusive characterizations of the same NPC, and the engine consumes the migration-roster vector for armature/cascade computation.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:91-92 (Primary Conviction Precedent; Secondary Faith 'genuine but privatised... does not govern her political decisions until her Precedent framework fails'), :608-616 Arc Map ('Precedent reinforced'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:231-237; vs designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:154-164 (primary_convictions Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10; notes 'strong primary Faith with Virtue-Warden secondary. Selfless devotional archetype').
- **Fix:** Author one canonical Conviction vector for Baralta and propagate. Per the behavior/analysis canon and ED-400, the intended primary is Precedent with Faith secondary; the migration-roster Faith-primary 'devotional' vector should be rebuilt to match (Precedent-led, Faith subordinate) and re-filed under Hafenmark. Until reconciled, the engine and the prose-writer will read opposite primary Convictions for the same character.
- **Verifier note:** The contradiction is real and reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md:91-92 gives Primary Conviction Precedent / Secondary Faith ('genuine but privatised... does not govern her political decisions until her Precedent framework fails'), Arc Map at :609 reads 'Precedent reinforced', and the analyses frame her as a proceduralist; whereas conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:156-159 assigns Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10 (no Precedent at all) with notes :162-164 'strong primary Faith... Selfless devotional archetype.' These are mutually exclusive primary axes for the same NPC. HOWEVER, severity is overstated at HIGH: character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1) explicitly governs this exact conflict class — 'Use conviction_migration_roster (PP-685) as primary in per-NPC sheets... Do not attempt to silently regenerate npc_behavior §2 from migration roster — that's a separate propagation cycle' — so this is a documented, protocol-bracketed deferred-propagation divergence, not an unmanaged contradiction. The roster header (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:31) further marks entries 'Per-character authoritative until promotion to canonical character sheets' (provisional). It is a real cross-doc inconsistency the engine could read either way, which warrants MEDIUM, but it is bracketed governance state rather than a HIGH unmanaged FAIL.

#### AUD-NPC2-100 — Self-Other orientation -0.40 ('selfless devotional') contradicts the canonical ambitious-claimant characterization
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_migration_roster §2.3 self_other vs character_analyses §6 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The migration roster sets Baralta's Self-Other initial to -0.40 ('Selfless devotional archetype' — primarily benefits the populace), but every authored characterization makes her an active throne-seeker running a decades-long campaign for personal succession. Self-Other tracks for whom the actor instrumentalizes; a -0.40 selfless reading is the opposite of the canonical ambition read and will bias engine drift/behavior the wrong way.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:161 (self_other_initial: -0.40) and :162-164 ('Selfless devotional archetype'); vs designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:231 ('Baralta wants the Crown.
