This is active ambition, not contingency') and :237 ('She is not waiting for it to happen passively... a decades-long campaign for the throne'); self-other semantics designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:135-145.
- **Fix:** Reset Baralta's self_other_initial to reflect the canonical ambitious self-positioning (near-neutral to mildly self-aggrandizing, e.g. ~0.0 to +0.2, matching the Hafenmark procedural template's 0.00 baseline), not -0.40. Update the migration-roster note to drop 'selfless devotional'.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:161 sets self_other_initial: -0.40 with the note (:162-164) 'Selfless devotional archetype', which under the Self-Other semantics in character_canon_v30.md:135-145 means 'mildly altruistic, collective-leaning' (the -0.5..-0.1 band: outcomes attributed primarily to collective benefit). This directly clashes with the canonical portrayal in npc_character_analyses_v30.md:231 ('Baralta wants the Crown. This is active ambition, not contingency') and :237 ('a decades-long campaign for the throne'). The Hafenmark procedural template baseline is 0.00 (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:192, the Hafenmark Speaker), so -0.40 is also off the template the finding proposes she should follow. The semantic clash is genuine. It is part of the same bracketed misfiled-ecclesiastical block D6 surfaces, so it shares the deferred-governance posture, but a strongly-selfless value will bias drift the wrong direction and no doc reconciles the number itself — MEDIUM as filed is appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-101 — Primary Conviction conflict: behavior/arcs say Precedent; designated PRIMARY source (PP-685 roster) says Faith 0.50
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Baralta's primary Conviction is stated as Precedent in npc_behavior_v30 §2.3 and propagated into the arcs layer, but the DESIGNATED PRIMARY source for conviction values on conflict — conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685), per character_canon_v30 §11 D1 — gives her primary_convictions as Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10 (no Precedent at all). The two are mutually incompatible (Faith-primary devotional vs Precedent-primary proceduralist). Per D1, the PP-685 value governs, so the live stance triangle is wrong on its single most load-bearing field. character_canon_v30 itself flags that §2 stance triangles 'conflict' with the roster and were deliberately not reconciled.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.3 L91 ('Primary Conviction | Precedent'); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 L152-165 (Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 L426 + L42 ('though they conflict — see §11 D1'); propagated in designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md L112 ('Baralta's Precedent conviction')
- **Fix:** Reconcile per D1: either (a) update npc_behavior_v30 §2.3 + arc_expansion_v30 to Faith-primary (Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10) matching PP-685, or (b) if the ED-400 Precedent refinement is the intended canon, amend PP-685 as a Class B override and record the reversal of D1 precedence. Do not leave both live. Run the npc_behavior §2 propagation cycle D1 defers.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced the raw conflict: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.3 L91 gives Primary Conviction = Precedent; conviction_migration_roster_v30.md L156-159 gives Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10; arc_expansion_v30.md L112 propagates 'Baralta's Precedent conviction'. baralta_v30.md (the roster's cited source) independently confirms she is Faith-driven ('Faith is the engine of ambition'), so the values are genuinely incompatible. BUT the finding overstates this as 'the live stance triangle is wrong'. Read in full, character_canon §11 D1 (L426) scopes PP-685-as-primary to the *per-NPC sheets* and EXPLICITLY defers npc_behavior §2: 'Do not attempt to silently regenerate npc_behavior §2 from migration roster — that's a separate propagation cycle.' D1 therefore does not make the §2 value 'wrong' or governed-by-PP-685; it consciously holds both pending a deferred cycle. Additionally the §2.3 Precedent value carries an explicit ED-400 editorial note ('User approval required'), so it is flagged-divergent, not silently contradictory. A real, already-surfaced and deliberately-deferred conflict — not a live HIGH FAIL.

#### AUD-NPC2-102 — Certainty stated as 4, 5, AND 0 across three docs — spans the entire band
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** params_core (Certainty) / consistency | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** A named NPC must carry a single consistent Certainty value. Baralta has three different ones: Certainty 4 (Faithful) in npc_behavior_v30 §2.3; Certainty 5 in npc_behavior_v30 §7.10 (same document, internally inconsistent); and Certainty 0 in factions_personal_v30 §T-15a (stated twice). 0 vs 5 is the full width of the 0–7 band — maximally contradictory — and they imply opposite arc vulnerability (Certainty 5 = near-unshakeable; Certainty 0 = already collapsed). arc_expansion_v30 adds a fourth live consumer (Certainty checks on the framework) that cannot resolve which value to read.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.3 L97 ('Certainty | 4 (Faithful)'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §7.10 L1188 ('Baralta | 0 (triple-barrier) | 5'); designs/provincial/factions_personal_v30.md L203 ('Baralta's Certainty 0 (Categorical Imperative worldview, TS 0)'); consumer: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md L597 (Certainty check, failure = Certainty −1)
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical Certainty (TS 0 + 'unshakeable sovereign divine right' per §7.10 rationale points to 5 = Orthodox, not 4 or 0; note 'Certainty 0' in factions_personal appears to conflate Certainty with TS) and propagate to §2.3, §7.10, factions_personal §T-15a, and arc_expansion. Correct the factions_personal 'Certainty 0' wording if TS 0 was meant.
- **Verifier note:** The three values exist but the framing is partly inaccurate. CONFIRMED: npc_behavior §2.3 L97 = 'Certainty | 4 (Faithful)'; npc_behavior §7.10 L1188 column reads TS 0 / Certainty 5; factions_personal §T-15a L203 = 'Certainty 0'. So 4 vs 5 is a genuine inconsistency. HOWEVER two claims fail on inspection: (a) the §7.10 entry is 'Baralta | 0 (triple-barrier) | 5' where 0 is the TS column and 5 the Certainty column — it is NOT an internal 0-vs-5 Certainty contradiction as implied; §7.10 is internally consistent at Certainty 5. (b) factions_personal 'Certainty 0' is stated ONCE on L203, not 'twice' (verified count = 1), and L203 itself parenthetically equates it to 'TS 0' ('Certainty 0 ... TS 0'), i.e. a Certainty/TS conflation, as the finding's own fix concedes. So the true contradiction is 4 (§2.3) vs 5 (§7.10) plus a mislabeled TS-as-Certainty in factions_personal — a real but narrower inconsistency, not a clean 0-vs-5 full-band span. MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-103 — Identity/faction + cultural-template mismatch: PP-685 files Baralta under Ecclesiastical with 'ecclesiastical' template; she is the Hafenmark sovereign claimant
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D6 / conviction_taxonomy §5 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The audited NPC is the Hafenmark sovereign claimant, but the PP-685 migration roster files 'Baralta' under §2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral) with cultural_label 'ecclesiastical' and a selfless-devotional note — the profile of a cleric, not the Hafenmark Duchess. npc_behavior §2.3 places her in Hafenmark with a procedural framework. This compounds Finding 1: even if PP-685 is taken as primary for the conviction value, the entry is mis-filed and uses the wrong cultural background template (ecclesiastical vs hafenmark_procedural), so the resulting effective vector is doubly wrong. character_canon_v30 §11 D6 surfaces this exact misfiling but explicitly leaves it unfixed ('Surface, do not fix … defer to Jordan').
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 L152-165 (Baralta under 'Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)', cultural_label: ecclesiastical); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md §11 D6 L431 ('Baralta (Hafenmark sovereign claimant) misfiled … under Ecclesiastical … Recommend correction to hafenmark_procedural template — defer to Jordan'); template defs designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md §5.1 (ecclesiastical vs hafenmark_procedural)
- **Fix:** Re-file the Baralta PP-685 entry under §2.4 Hafenmark and switch cultural_label from 'ecclesiastical' to 'hafenmark_procedural' (or whichever the resolved conviction identity in Finding 1 requires); ensure she is not conflated with the separate '#Hafenmark Speaker (placeholder)' entry already at §2.4.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced exactly. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md files Baralta under §2.3 'Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)' (L152) with cultural_label: ecclesiastical (L160) and a 'Selfless devotional archetype' note (L164) — a cleric profile. A separate §2.4 Hafenmark Faction entry exists as 'Hafenmark Speaker (placeholder name; specific character in PP-685 finalization)' (L181-191) with cultural_label: hafenmark_procedural, confirming Baralta is not that entry. character_canon §11 D6 (L431) surfaces this precise misfiling and recommends correction to hafenmark_procedural while explicitly leaving it unfixed ('Surface, do not fix ... defer to Jordan'). Templates (ecclesiastical Faith 0.30 vs hafenmark_procedural Order 0.30) exist and differ materially. Minor citation slip: template definitions live in conviction_taxonomy_v30 §4 (Cultural background), not '§5.1' as cited — does not affect substance. Misfiling is real; MEDIUM appropriate as an already-surfaced deferred item.

#### AUD-NPC2-104 — No Belief→Scar (Conviction/Piety Scar) conversion authored for a Tier-1 NPC with rupture-grade arcs
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_track_v1 §2-3 (Scar mechanic) / npc_behavior §5.2 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Baralta is a Tier-1 ruler-diamond NPC with two transformative arcs — Arc B 'The Pragmatist' and Arc C 'Excommunication' (CI +4, Mandate crisis) — yet her Arc Map specifies no Belief→Scar conversion. Arc A merely says 'Precedent reinforced'; Arc B/C describe political consequences but no Conviction/Belief Scar, unlike Almud, Himlensendt, and Edeyja whose arcs carry explicit Conviction shifts/Scars. Excommunication of a Faith-primary figure (per PP-685) is precisely a Belief-rupture event that the Scar mechanic exists to model; leaving it unspecified means her transformation arcs don't engage the personality-mechanics layer. Her stat block also lists only Beliefs 1 and 3 (Belief 2 missing), so there is no clear belief to convert.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2 'Inge Baralta — Arc Map' L606-616 (Arc A 'Precedent reinforced'; Arc B/C no Scar/Conviction shift); Beliefs list §2.3 L99-101 (only #1 and #3 present); contrast Almud/Himlensendt/Edeyja arc maps which specify Conviction shifts; Scar mechanic designs/personal/conviction_track_v1.md §2-3
- **Fix:** Author a Belief→Scar path for Arc C (Excommunication): e.g., on excommunication, convert her Faith/sovereign-divine-right belief to a Conviction Scar per conviction_track_v1 §2, with the resulting Conviction/Resonant-Style effects; add the missing Belief 2 so there is a defined belief to convert.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2 'Inge Baralta — Arc Map' L606-616: Arc A 'Conviction: Precedent reinforced. No arc transformation'; Arc B (The Pragmatist) and Arc C (Excommunication, CI +4, Mandate crisis) describe only political consequences, no Belief→Scar / Conviction-shift line. Contrast confirmed via grep of the same file: Almud L565/L570 ('Conviction shift: Order doubles down', 'Order → Autonomy'), Edeyja L648-649/L656 (explicit Conviction Scar from §3.4), Himlensendt L599 (Scar) — peers do specify Conviction/Scar effects. Scar mechanic is canonical (character_canon L48; npc_behavior §3.3 promoted spec). Beliefs at §2.3 L99-101 list only #1 and #3 (no Belief 2), so there is indeed no clearly-defined belief to convert. A genuine authoring gap for a transformative Faith-rupture arc; MEDIUM as a gap (not a canon FAIL) is correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-105 — Vaynard (TS 14, well below 30) is moved by 'Thread-level data he cannot explain' — Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert against a TS<30 NPC
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard is TS 14 (Dormant). His Secondary Resonant Style is Evidence, operationalized as 'present Thread-level data he cannot explain', and his Arc B/Arc A escalation gates on the same Thread-level Evidence push. P-08 states the epistemological barrier makes Thread-level evidence epistemically inert against TS<30 NPCs — a non-sensitive 'can recite but not act with Thread-level precision'. Using Thread-level data as the lever that shifts a Dormant-TS NPC's Beliefs/arc runs against the inert-knowledge mechanic. (The arc separately notes Edeyja withholds collaboration until TS 30+, showing the barrier is understood — but the Evidence-style lever itself is not gated.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md line 115 (Secondary Evidence = 'Thread-level data he cannot explain', TS 14) and line 116 (TS 14 Dormant); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md line 95 (Arc B: 'Only harder evidence — Thread-level data he cannot explain — reaches him'); constraint canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-08 line 17 ('non-sensitives can recite but not act with Thread-level precision'; brief: 'Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert against TS<30 NPCs').
- **Fix:** Re-scope Vaynard's Evidence lever to non-Thread-level evidence appropriate to a TS<30 actor (e.g., concrete strategic/territorial consequence data, recoverable historical record), or explicitly gate the Thread-level-Evidence lever behind his TS crossing Stirring (30). Pre-30, his persuadability should run through Consequence, not Thread-precision data he structurally cannot render.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.4 line 115 makes Vaynard's Secondary-Evidence lever 'present Thread-level data he cannot explain', TS 14 Dormant (line 116); infill Arc B (line 95) repeats it. The P-08 barrier for TS<30 is canonical and independently confirmed: canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-08 line 17; worldbuilding_v30.md line 239 ('TS 22 ... Cannot bridge epistemological barrier — P-08'); and the SAME behavior doc's §2.14a census tiers (lines 339/344/346) gate Thread recognition at TS>=30, with TS 10-29 'cannot decode' and TS 0-9 nothing. A TS-14 Vaynard structurally cannot render Thread-level data as actionable knowledge, so it is inert as an Evidence lever. Tried to refute via the §6.1b gate (infill lines 112-114), which restricts Thread-level Evidence against 'TS 0 NPCs'/'non-sensitives' and re-activates only at Stirring (30+) — but that leaves the TS 1-29 band un-gated, an internal inconsistency with §6.1b and §2.14a. The 'P-08 violation' label is slightly generous (the live mechanic is under-protective rather than affirmatively allowing study-to-knowledge), but P-08 is immutable and the as-written §2.4 lever runs against it. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-106 — Vaynard's Resonant Style is set to Consequence/Evidence in behavior but left 'To be determined' in foils
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Internal (behavior §2.4 vs foils 'Vaynard's View'); ED-081 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Behavior §2.4 fixes Primary Resonant Style = Consequence and Secondary = Evidence (and the Arc Map, character_canon, and BG trees all assume this). The foils 'Vaynard's View' header instead says 'Resonant Style: To be determined.' One of these is stale. A leader-tier NPC's Resonant Style is load-bearing (it is the PC's social-engagement vector), so an open 'TBD' alongside a fixed value is a contradiction and a residual gap.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md lines 114-115 (Primary Consequence / Secondary Evidence) vs designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md line 102 ('Resonant Style: To be determined'); cf. character_canon_v30.md line 279 (assumes Vaynard Arc C low Spirit) which presupposes a defined style.
- **Fix:** Update the foils 'Vaynard's View' line to Primary Resonant Style = Consequence, Secondary = Evidence, matching the canonical behavior §2.4 (and confirm the Evidence justification is rewritten away from the 'collector' framing per the §2.4 fix).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.4 line 114 Primary Resonant Style 'Consequence' and line 115 Secondary 'Evidence' (fixed values relied on by arc maps); designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md line 102 'Vaynard's View ... Resonant Style: To be determined.' Genuine contradiction between a fixed value and an open TBD for a leader-tier NPC's social-engagement vector. The character_canon cross-reference is slightly imprecise (the 'Vaynard at Arc C low' note is at character_canon_v30.md lines 279 and 430 and concerns Spirit, not Resonant Style) but it is only corroborative; the behavior-vs-foils contradiction stands on its own. Fix is coupled to finding 1 (Evidence justification must be redrafted off the 'collector' framing). MEDIUM appropriate (residual gap, not a canon-constraint breach).

#### AUD-NPC2-107 — Character analysis §5 'The Revolutionary' carries the off-canon 'only leader who understands the crisis / recovering his own heritage' framing
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-787; CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The character analysis casts Vaynard as 'the only faction leader who understands the existential crisis', a southern-Einhir figure 'recovering his own cultural heritage' via Thread investigation and secret intelligence operations, and an ontological heretic 'claiming Solmund was a threadcut being'. While the section correctly cites the von Lohengramm anchor, the surrounding characterization is the knowledge-collector/heritage-recovery/intelligence framing decanonized by ED-787 ('not intelligence-themed') and CR-STRIKE ('does not convert populations ideologically; purely military conquest'). The title 'The Revolutionary' is partially supportable (timeline allows 'opportunist/revolutionary'), but the collector/intelligence substance is not.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md line 92 ('the only faction leader who understands the existential crisis'), line 96 ('recovering his own cultural heritage'), line 97 ('intelligence operations ... builds capability in secret'), line 110 ('claiming Solmund was a threadcut being'); against canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl line 522 (ED-787) and canon/supersession_register.yaml lines 51-66 (CR-STRIKE).
- **Fix:** Keep the von Lohengramm anchor and the anti-Church / anti-Altonian / anti-caste goal; remove the 'intelligence operations / builds capability in secret / recovering heritage / knowledge is the only thing that matters' substance. Re-anchor his method to military/opportunist conquest per CR-STRIKE + ED-787. Verify the 'Solmund was a threadcut being' line is intended as in-world heretical assertion, not authorial canon.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced in designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md §5: line 92 'the only faction leader who understands the existential crisis'; line 96 'recovering his own cultural heritage'; line 97 'his intelligence operations are the consequence ... he builds capability in secret'. The threadcut-claim 'claiming Solmund was a threadcut being' is at line 110, which is the Himlensendt section's 'On Vaynard' — explicitly an in-world heretical classification, not authorial canon; the finding itself flags this should be verified as in-world, which is correct. The heritage-recovery / 'knowledge is the only thing that matters' substance contradicts CR-STRIKE + migration_roster line 256. The finding is appropriately hedged: it concedes the von Lohengramm anchor is correctly cited (line 94) and that 'The Revolutionary' title is partially supportable (timeline allows opportunist/revolutionary). As in finding 2, 'builds capability in secret' intelligence framing is defensible (Intel 4 restored by ED-787), but the cultural-heritage-recovery framing is genuinely off-canon. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-108 — Vaynard Arc A (The Scholar) and Arc B (The Awakened) have empty bodies in the skeleton
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30.md D4; ED-517 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** In npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2, the Vaynard Arc Map lists 'Arc A: The Scholar' and 'Arc B: The Awakened' as headers with no body text in the skeleton; only Arc C has body content. The consolidation audit independently catalogues this as gap item D4. (Some content exists in the infill, but the skeleton arcs are stubs and the infill content is itself off-canon per the collector/co-victory findings, so the arcs are effectively unspecified in canonical, on-identity form.) Arc A's title 'The Scholar' also re-encodes the struck knowledge-collector identity.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md lines 622-626 (Arc A / Arc B headers, no body); flagged in designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md line 343 and line 429 (D4: 'Vaynard Arc A and Arc B in npc_behavior §5.2 have empty bodies (header only)').
- **Fix:** Author Arc A and Arc B bodies (branch conditions, Conviction/Resonant-Style/behavior effects) on the corrected military/opportunist identity. Retitle 'Arc A: The Scholar' to something non-collector. Fold in the corrected (non-co-victory, non-cultural-restoration) Awakened outcome.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2 lines 622-624 show 'Arc A: The Scholar' and 'Arc B: The Awakened' as headers with no body; only Arc C (lines 626-627) has content. Independently catalogued as gap D4 in character_canon_v30.md line 343 ('Vaynard Arc A and Arc B in npc_behavior §5.2 have empty bodies (header only)') and line 429. Hardest refutation attempt: the behavior doc (line 547) points to a canonical source for 'Vaynard Arc A/B full', but that path (designs/systems/arc_expansion_v1_2026-04-16.md) is MISSING from the repo; the content actually lives at designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md (lines 123-176), and (a) the behavior doc's pointer is stale/broken, and (b) that content is itself built on the off-canon Scholar/VTM/Private-Collection/co-victory identity (e.g. line 160 'Varfell+Crown co-victory viable'), so the arcs remain unspecified in canonical, on-identity form as the finding states. ED-517 ('Vaynard 3 arcs') only confirms arc SHAPES, not bodies. Arc A title 'The Scholar' re-encodes the struck collector identity. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-109 — Transformation knot-strain that governs Vaynard Arc C is a single generic 'strain/season' value, not tridimensional
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** P-12 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard Arc C (TS crosses threshold → epistemic seduction) is the trigger for the §5.0b Transformation Knot Strain table, which the heading labels 'P-12 compliance'. But the table emits one generic scalar ('+1 / +2 / +3 strain/season') with no decomposition into the three required dimensions (actuality / intelligibility / temporality). P-12's violation test fails any propagation that is 'a generic strain value with no dimensional structure'. So the very mechanic Vaynard's transformation depends on does not satisfy P-12.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §5.0b lines 518-528 (single 'strain/season' column, applied to 'Vaynard Arc C'); §5.0b infill at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md lines 60-61 (also generic strain); constraint canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-12 line 21 (violation test: 'treat propagation as a generic strain value with no dimensional structure → FAIL').
- **Fix:** Decompose the §5.0b strain output into the three P-12 dimensions (configurational pull / apperceptive degradation / retention-protention disturbance) per the constraint, then reference that tridimensional output from Vaynard Arc C. (System-wide block — also affects Himlensendt Arc C — but materially gates Vaynard's transformation.)
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md §5.0b (lines 518-528) is headed 'AUD-NPC-02 fix, P-12 compliance' yet its table (lines 522-526) emits a single undifferentiated scalar ('+1 / +2 / +3 strain/season' by Close/Distant Knot) with NO decomposition into the three P-12 dimensions; line 528 explicitly names 'Vaynard Arc C (TS crosses threshold -> epistemic seduction)' as an applying case. P-12 (canon/02_canon_constraints.md line 21) requires actuality + intelligibility + temporality and its violation test fails any system that treats 'propagation as a generic strain value with no dimensional structure' — which is exactly what the 'P-12 compliance' header here does. Infill §5.0b (lines 60-61) is likewise generic. System-wide (also Himlensendt Arc C) but materially gates Vaynard's transformation. MEDIUM stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-110 — Certainty value contradicts itself within the same document (3 vs 2)
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** internal consistency; npc_behavior_v30 §2.4 vs §6 TS/Certainty table | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.4 lists Certainty 3 (Questioning), but the §6 TS/Certainty summary table in the same file lists Vaynard Certainty 2. The two values carry different in-fiction readings ('Thread reality is a hypothesis he is testing' vs 'direct Thread perception has destabilised worldview').
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:117 ('Certainty | 3 (Questioning)'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1189 ('Vaynard | 10 (hidden) | 2 | ... Certainty 2 — direct Thread perception has destabilised worldview')
- **Fix:** Pick one starting Certainty and reconcile both tables. The §2.4 rationale (secular intellectual, hypothesis-testing) reads as the intended baseline (Certainty 3); the §6 table's '2 — direct Thread perception has destabilised' appears to describe a post-Arc-B state. Align, or label the §6 value as post-transition.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced inside one file. npc_behavior_v30.md:117 (s2.4) lists 'Certainty | 3 (Questioning)' with a secular-intellectual hypothesis-testing rationale; :1189 (s7.10 NPC Stat Values table, which the finding calls the s6 table) lists Vaynard Certainty 2 with rationale 'direct Thread perception has destabilised worldview'. The two values carry different in-fiction readings (pre- vs post-Thread-encounter), and nothing labels the :1189 value as a post-transition state, so at face value they conflict. (Note: the s7.10 row also shows TS 10 vs the s2.4 TS 14 -- see the TS finding.) MEDIUM is appropriate for a starting-stat contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-111 — Thread Sensitivity starting value is three-way inconsistent (14 vs 10 vs 'environmental/southern baseline'); arc threshold sentence garbled
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** internal consistency; TS starting value across docs | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's starting TS is given as 14 (Dormant) in npc_behavior §2.4, factions_personal, and arc_expansion; as 10 in the npc_behavior §6 table; and only as 'environmental (southern baseline)' (no number) in character_analyses §5. arc_expansion further garbles the threshold: 'Thread Sensitivity 14 (Dormant, hidden) crosses 10 -> Stirring (30)' (14 cannot 'cross 10'; Stirring is 30).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:116 (TS 14 Dormant); designs/provincial/factions_personal_v30.md:254 (TS 14); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:146 ('Thread Sensitivity 14 (Dormant, hidden) crosses 10 -> Stirring (30)'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1189 ('Vaynard | 10 (hidden)'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:149 ('Environmental (southern baseline)')
- **Fix:** Set one canonical starting TS (the weight of docs favors 14 Dormant) and fix the §6 table (10) and character_analyses (add the number). Rewrite the arc_expansion threshold sentence to 'TS 14 (Dormant) advances and crosses Stirring (30)'.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced across docs. TS 14 (Dormant): npc_behavior_v30.md:116, factions_personal_v30.md:254, arc_expansion_v30.md:146, and corroborated by references/arc_register_factions.md:130 ('Vaynard TS 14 (Dormant)'). TS 10: npc_behavior_v30.md:1189 (s7.10 table). No number, only 'Environmental (southern baseline)': npc_character_analyses_v30.md:149 (and analyses_infill:96 frames TS as environmental). references/npc_registry.yaml:705 leaves ts as null [GAP], adding a fourth state of indeterminacy. The garbled threshold is verbatim at arc_expansion_v30.md:146: 'Thread Sensitivity 14 (Dormant, hidden) crosses 10 -> Stirring (30)' -- 14 cannot 'cross 10', and Stirring is 30. Three-way (arguably four-way) inconsistency plus the garbled sentence are exactly as described. MEDIUM holds.

#### AUD-NPC2-112 — Edeyja's Conviction labeled inconsistently across docs: "Warden" (canonical) vs superseded "Continuity"/"Reason"/"Autonomy"
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Conviction value consistency (PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The canonical PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy is Faith/Authority/Order/Scholastic/Utility/Equity/Liberty/Precedent/Community/Identity/Warden/Virtue/Honor. The dedicated doc correctly gives Conviction = "Warden." But npc_behavior_v30 §2.9 gives Primary Conviction = "Continuity" / Secondary = "Reason," the roster_infill says "conviction (Continuity)," and the Arc Map gives "Warden -> Autonomy" and "Continuity primary." Per conviction_migration_roster_v30 §1, "Continuity" and "Reason" are explicitly SUPERSEDED legacy tags (Continuity maps to Warden; Reason maps to Scholastic/Authority), and "Autonomy" is not in the 13-taxonomy (the alias maps Autonomy->Liberty per supersession D8). The result is one NPC carrying both the new canonical label and three stale labels for the same axis.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:97 (13-Conviction list incl. Warden, not Continuity/Reason/Autonomy); designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:30 (Conviction: Warden — correct); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:212-213 (Primary "Continuity", Secondary "Reason"), :644 ("Warden -> Autonomy"), :649 ("Continuity primary unchanged"); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:12 ("conviction (Continuity)"); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:17 ("'Reason' and 'Continuity' ... PP-684 supersedes these")
- **Fix:** Propagate the canonical "Warden" Conviction to npc_behavior_v30 §2.9 (lines 212-213) and the Arc Map (lines 644, 649); remap secondary "Reason" to its PP-684 equivalent (Scholastic or Authority per the migration pattern) and remap "Autonomy" to "Liberty" per alias_registry. Update roster_infill:12 to "Warden."
- **Verifier note:** All cited anchors reproduce exactly: edeyja_npc.md:30 (Conviction: Warden); npc_behavior_v30.md:212-213 (Primary 'Continuity', Secondary 'Reason'), :644 ('Warden -> Autonomy'), :649 ('Continuity primary unchanged'); npc_roster_v30_infill.md:12 ('conviction (Continuity)'). The supersession is real: conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:17 ('PP-684 supersedes' Reason/Continuity); Reason->Scholastic and Autonomy->Liberty confirmed in references/propagation_map.md:61,63 and npc_registry.yaml:472. The 13-taxonomy at character_canon_v30.md:97 contains Warden, not Continuity/Reason/Autonomy. So one NPC does carry the canonical label plus three stale labels — a genuine cross-doc inconsistency. I downgrade from HIGH to MEDIUM because it is contained to legacy prose in two docs and is NOT unresolved at the engine layer: references/npc_registry.yaml (self-declared 'Canonical source of truth for ALL named characters', status ACTIVE) already canonicalizes NPC-001 Edeyja as Warden 0.40 + Precedent 0.20 with migration_notes mapping legacy 'Continuity'->Warden. The single source of truth exists and agrees with the dedicated doc; npc_behavior just has stale descriptive tags.

#### AUD-NPC2-113 — "Does not arc / fixed point / never leaves" contradicts authored Arc B/C/D/E transformation and her leaving the Southernmost
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Arc consistency across dedicated doc / behavior / analyses | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** npc_character_analyses §1 states flatly "Edeyja does not arc. She is a fixed point," and the dedicated doc states "She never leaves the Southernmost." But npc_behavior_v30 §5.2 authors a full multi-branch arc for her: Arc B Collaboration, Arc C Crisis (Conviction shift; "Edeyja may leave the Southernmost for the first time"), Arc D Confrontation, Arc E Wager — and the infill Arc Map explicitly notes Arc C "overrides edeyja_npc.md 'She never leaves the Southernmost.'" The override note acknowledges the conflict but the analyses doc's absolute "does not arc" statement and the dedicated doc's absolute "never leaves" are left standing as canonical, contradicting the behavior doc the same roster treats as her arc source (ED-386).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:12 ("Edeyja does not arc. She is a fixed point."); designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:77 ("She never leaves the Southernmost."); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:631-665 (Arc B/C/D/E with Conviction shifts), :645 ("a walking Calamity echo"); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:107 ("This arc explicitly overrides edeyja_npc.md 'She never leaves the Southernmost'")
- **Fix:** Reconcile: either soften the analyses/dedicated-doc absolutes to "does not arc under default/Arc-A conditions; transformation requires the Arc C crisis branch" (matching the override note's own logic), or demote the behavior-doc arcs. Pick one source of truth and cross-reference it; do not leave an absolute and its override both live.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim. npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:12 states flatly 'Edeyja does not arc. She is a fixed point.' edeyja_npc.md:77 states 'She never leaves the Southernmost.' Yet npc_behavior_v30.md §5.2 (Edeyja Arc Map, lines 631-665) authors Arc A-E with Conviction shifts, Arc C 'Warden -> Autonomy' (:644) and 'walking Calamity echo' (:645), and npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:107 explicitly notes 'This arc explicitly overrides edeyja_npc.md ...She never leaves the Southernmost'. All three docs are CANONICAL/DESIGN-status and live. The infill override note reconciles the dedicated doc, but the analyses doc's absolute 'does not arc' and the dedicated doc's bare 'never leaves' (which has no in-line caveat at :77) are both left standing as canonical alongside the multi-branch arc. This is a genuine unreconciled absolute-vs-override coexistence. MEDIUM is the correct severity — a real cross-doc contradiction, but a characterization/scoping one with an existing (if incompletely propagated) override rationale, not a mechanical canon violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-114 — Thread Sensitivity stated as 50+ in the Certainty roster table, contradicting the canonical 75-80
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** stat consistency (TS); edeyja_npc.md Stats block | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Every primary source gives Edeyja TS 75-80 ('the highest Thread Sensitivity of any living practitioner'). The NPC Certainty roster table lists her TS as '50+'. This understates the setting's deliberately unique ceiling figure and conflicts with the dedicated sheet, the §2.9 stat block, and character_canon's apex band note.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md Certainty table line 1197 ('Edeyja | 50+ | 1') vs designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md line 21 ('Thread Sensitivity: 75-80 - the highest... of any living practitioner'), npc_behavior_v30.md §2.9 line 217 ('Thread Sensitivity | 75-80'), and character_canon_v30.md §x apex-band line 246 ('70+ | Apex | Edeyja band').
- **Fix:** Correct the roster table entry to 75-80 to match canon.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. Certainty roster line 1197 lists 'Edeyja | 50+ | 1', whereas edeyja_npc.md line 21 ('Thread Sensitivity: 75-80 - the highest... of any living practitioner'), npc_behavior_v30.md §2.9 line 217 ('Thread Sensitivity | 75-80'), and character_canon_v30.md line 246 apex band ('70+ | Apex | Edeyja band') all give 75-80/70+. character_canon line 248 states 'TS is canonical per NPC across the texture stack - most reliable single dimension', so an internal TS contradiction is a genuine canon-consistency defect, and it understates a figure the setting deliberately makes unique. MEDIUM retained ('50+' is technically true-but-misleading rather than a flat falsehood, which keeps it below HIGH).

#### AUD-NPC2-115 — Ethical Framework field retained in the stat block without the required [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] marker
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30.md §11 D2; PP-684 §6 -> PP-686 §3.7; alias_registry ethical_framework_modifier | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Edeyja's §2.9 stat block carries an 'Ethical Framework' row ('None (no faction)...'). Per D2, framework labels are superseded as mechanical Ob drivers and must be retained only as descriptive disposition tags carrying the explicit [SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc] note. Edeyja's row has no such marker. Lower severity than the conviction findings because her value is 'None' (no faction), so no live Ob modifier is actually attached - but the field format violates D2 and the doc-wide Priority-Tree template still reads Ethical Framework at Priority 3 as a -1 Ob driver.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.9 line 214 ('Ethical Framework | None (no faction)...') and §8.1 template line 779 (Priority 3 'Framework-aligned opportunity | Ethical Framework | ...-1 Ob'); rule character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 line 427 and §10.1 line 385.
- **Fix:** Append '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc; descriptive tag only]' to the row (or drop the row, since 'None' carries no disposition content). Confirms parity with the consolidation's stated handling for every other NPC.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md line 214 'Ethical Framework | None (no faction). Her decisions are ungoverned by institutional incentive...' — no [SUPERSEDED] marker. character_canon D2 (line 427) and §10.1 (line 385) require the tag '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]'; alias_registry ethical_framework_modifier (lines 614-619) confirms supersession by PP-686. The §8.1 template line 779 still reads 'Ethical Framework' at Priority 3 as a -1 Ob driver doc-wide. The finding already self-limits to MEDIUM and correctly notes the mitigation that Edeyja's value is 'None' so no live Ob modifier is actually attached. MEDIUM retained.

#### AUD-NPC2-116 — Foils use retired ethical-tradition labels (Categorical Imperative, Consequentialism, sovereign divine right) as live conviction across all pairings
- **Unit:** Foils coherence | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** alias_registry.yaml L604; faction_canon_v30 L46; character_canon_v30 §1 supersession | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Beyond the per-character vectors, the foils' entire contrast architecture is built on ethical-tradition labels that PP-684 §6 / PP-686 retired: 'Categorical imperative vs consequentialist pragmatism' (Baralta-Vaynard), 'sovereign divine right', 'universalism', 'reform legitimacy'. These are explicitly retired as mechanical anchors and recorded as aliases; presenting them as the NPCs' operative Convictions propagates stale taxonomy into a doc still marked canonical.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md L58, L90, L95-100, L102, L112; references/alias_registry.yaml L604 ('Categorical Imperative'); designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md L46 (Categorical Imperative / Utility-driven Pragmatism / Equity Social Contract 'all formally retired as mechanical anchors'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md L49 (legacy ethical-tradition labels SUPERSEDED, aliases in alias_registry).
- **Fix:** Convert all ethical-tradition labels in the foils to [SUPERSEDED] descriptive tags with a pointer to the 13-Conviction vector + PP-686 §3.7, mirroring how faction_canon_v30 and character_canon_v30 handle them. Re-derive each pairing's contrast from the canonical convictions (e.g., Baralta Precedent/Authority vs Vaynard Equity/Utility) rather than from retired traditions.
- **Verifier note:** This meta-finding is accurate and reproducible. The foils' contrast architecture is built on retired ethical-tradition labels: 'Categorical imperative vs consequentialist pragmatism' (L58), 'sovereign divine right' (L90, L96, L112), 'universalism' (L36, L84, L112). These are formally retired as mechanical anchors per faction_canon_v30.md L46 ('Categorical Imperative / Utility-driven Pragmatism / Equity Social Contract ... all formally retired as mechanical anchors') and character_canon_v30.md L49, with aliases in alias_registry.yaml L600-613 (L604 'Categorical Imperative'). Both faction_canon (D2 L357) and character_canon (D2 L427) explicitly handle these by retaining them as descriptive disposition tags WITH a [SUPERSEDED] marker; the foils present them as operative Convictions without the marker. Held at MEDIUM (not raised) because canon explicitly PERMITS these as retained descriptive tags — the defect is the missing [SUPERSEDED] pointer in a DESIGN-status doc, i.e. documentation hygiene, not a prohibited use.

#### AUD-NPC2-117 — Baralta's stated Conviction in foils (sovereign divine right / Categorical imperative) contradicts canonical Precedent+Authority
- **Unit:** Foils coherence | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_registry NPC-050 Open Issue #12/#13; vs npc_foils_v30_infill §Baralta's View | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The foils give Baralta's Conviction as 'Constitutional governance / Sovereign divine right' with Ethics 'Categorical imperative + Henry VIII sovereign supremacy' and Resonant Style 'Evidence'. The canonical conviction vector is Precedent 0.50 + Authority 0.30 (hafenmark_procedural). The registry explicitly RESOLVED this drift: 'behavior_v30 authoritative over PP-685... Baralta -> Precedent (was Faith).' 'Sovereign divine right' is neither of her canonical convictions, and 'Categorical Imperative' is a retired ethical-tradition label, not a live conviction.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md L90 ('Conviction: Constitutional governance / Sovereign divine right... Ethics: Categorical imperative + Henry VIII sovereign supremacy'), L22, L45, L84, L96-100, L112; vs references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-050 L684-693 (Precedent 0.50, Authority 0.30; conviction_notes 'PP-685 §2.3 misattributed as Faith/ecclesiastical') and L1047-1052 Open Issue #12; alias_registry.yaml L604 ('Categorical Imperative' = legacy alias); designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md L46 (ethical-framework labels 'formally retired as mechanical anchors').
- **Fix:** Restate Baralta's Conviction as Precedent (primary) + Authority (secondary), hafenmark_procedural template, per npc_registry NPC-050. Demote 'Categorical imperative' / 'sovereign divine right' / 'Henry VIII supremacy' to [SUPERSEDED] descriptive disposition tags. Replace Resonant Style 'Evidence' with the canonical NPC-050 style (her advisor Torvi Heljason NPC-071 carries Evidence; Baralta's own style is unauthored in the registry and must not be asserted as Evidence without source).
- **Verifier note:** Reproducible at the text level: npc_foils_v30_infill.md L90 gives Baralta 'Conviction: Constitutional governance / Sovereign divine right ... Ethics: Categorical imperative + Henry VIII sovereign supremacy ... Resonant Style: Evidence', while npc_registry.yaml NPC-050 L686-687 sets canonical Precedent 0.50 + Authority 0.30. But the finding overstates this. (1) The foils carry header 'Status: DESIGN', dated 2026-04-11 — they PREDATE PP-685 (2026-05-01) and the 2026-05-08 registry correction. The registry itself (Open Issue #12 L1047-1052, #13 L1053-1056) treats the Baralta=Faith reading as known pre-migration drift already resolved, i.e. this is stale-tier content, not a live FAIL. (2) 'Henry VIII sovereign supremacy' is NOT an error: baralta_v30.md L15/L23 (the dedicated NPC source cited in NPC-050 L693) and npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L101 both anchor her on Henry VIII / jurisdictional supremacy as canonical disposition. (3) Confirmed sub-points: 'Categorical Imperative' is a retired label (alias_registry.yaml L604), and asserting Resonant Style 'Evidence' is unsourced — NPC-050 has no resonant_style field; NPC-071 Torvi Heljason L747 carries Evidence. Real but a superseded-doc legacy-label propagation, not a HIGH live contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-118 — Almud's Conviction asserted as 'Order/Reason'; canonical vector is Virtue+Authority
- **Unit:** Foils coherence | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_migration_roster §2.1 + npc_registry NPC-020; vs npc_foils_v30_infill §Almud's View | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The foils give Almud 'Conviction: Order/Reason. Resonant Style: Consequence.' His canonical conviction vector is Virtue 0.45 + Authority 0.30 (valorian_court). 'Order' is not Almud's conviction (Order is the primary for Ehrenwall, Almstedt, Voss, Geirson); 'Reason' is a retired legacy tag. The registry and migration roster both anchor him on Virtue/Authority.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md L65 ('Conviction: Order/Reason. Resonant Style: Consequence.'); vs references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-020 L348-358 (Virtue 0.45, Authority 0.30; prior labels 'Virtue Ethics' + 'Reason'); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md L41-54 (Virtue 0.45, Authority 0.30); faction_canon_v30 L404 ('Almud per migration roster: Virtue 0.45 + Authority 0.30').
- **Fix:** Restate Almud's Conviction as Virtue (primary) + Authority (secondary). Drop 'Order' (wrong conviction) and treat 'Reason' as a [SUPERSEDED] legacy tag. If retaining 'Consequence' as Resonant Style, confirm against a current source (faction_canon Arc B describes 'Reason secondary' legacy text — not a conviction).
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. npc_foils_v30_infill.md L65 gives Almud 'Conviction: Order/Reason. Resonant Style: Consequence.' Canonical vector is Virtue 0.45 + Authority 0.30 (registry NPC-020 L350-351; conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 L45-48). Unlike findings 1-3, 'Order' is not a defensible gloss of any canonical Almud content — the migration roster L44 records his prior labels as 'Virtue Ethics' + 'Reason', never Order; Order is the primary for other NPCs (Almstedt, Voss, Kreutz, Ehrenwall, Geirson). So 'Order' is a genuine misstatement and 'Reason' is a retired tag (alias_registry L610). Capped at MEDIUM rather than HIGH because the foils are DESIGN-status and predate PP-685, the registry already books this as resolved pre-migration drift (Open Issue #12 L1047-1052), and the impact class is documentation hygiene in a superseded-tier doc, not a live mechanical FAIL. Note faction_canon_v30 Arc B L479 'Reason secondary' is legacy arc prose, not a current conviction source, so it does not rescue the foils' values.

#### AUD-NPC2-119 — Entire personality-mechanics substrate is absent from the behavior doc — the layer being audited has no canonical home for Strand
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30.md:20 (npc_behavior §2/§3/§6/§8 = canonical home of stance/Belief/Resonant Style/Priority Trees) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The dimensions audited here (Conviction vector, Resonant Style, Certainty, Beliefs, stance) live canonically in npc_behavior_v30 §2/§3/§6 per character_canon §11. Strand has zero presence in npc_behavior_v30.md, its _infill, its _index, and npc_relational_graph_v30.md. §2.15 (Crown Inner Circle) lists Voss/Reichard/Thale/Linder/Kreutz — not Strand. The consequence specific to this audit: his live, always-active BG modifier (+1D Crown admin) and the 'Strand Turned' card / Influence Debt arc are driven by an OVERPERFORMER + flattery profile that has no Conviction / Resonant Style / Certainty / Belief backing to trigger from. The personality-mechanics layer is asserted in prose (roster/analyses) and operationalized in arcs, but never instantiated in the doc that is supposed to hold it.
- **Evidence:** grep 'Strand'/'Gerik' in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md, npc_behavior_v30_infill.md, npc_behavior_v30_index.md, npc_relational_graph_v30.md = 0 matches; designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:355-363 (§2.15 Crown Inner Circle, no Strand); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:20 (behavior doc = canonical home of stance triangles/Belief/Resonant Style/Priority Trees). (Overlaps prior audit AUD-NPC2-049, CONFIRMED — restated here because it is the root cause of the missing ethics/resonance layer.)
- **Fix:** Create a Strand entry in npc_behavior_v30 §2.15 (Role / Conviction vector / Certainty / Beliefs / Resonant Style) and a §8 Priority Tree, and add him to npc_relational_graph (Crown edge to Almud and to Reichard given the Steward/Treasurer overlap), so the conviction/RS/Certainty/Belief fields recommended in the findings above have a canonical home and the 'Strand Turned' card has an AI substrate.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced: Strand/Gerik = 0 matches in npc_behavior_v30.md, npc_behavior_v30_infill.md, npc_behavior_v30_index.md, and npc_relational_graph_v30.md; npc_behavior_v30.md:355-363 (§2.15 Crown Inner Circle) lists Voss/Reichard/Thale/Linder/Kreutz, not Strand. Meanwhile he carries a live always-active BG modifier and a 'Strand Turned' Event Card (npc_roster_v30.md:316-317) plus the Influence-Debt arc machinery (arcs_10_18.md:452-468), and character_canon_v30.md:20 names npc_behavior as the canonical home of stance/Belief/RS/Priority Trees. This matches AUD-NPC2-049 in the 2026-06-22 audit (verdict CONFIRMED, MEDIUM), and the finding flags the overlap. I hold MEDIUM, with the rationale narrowed: behavior §2 is the TIER 1 stance-triangle roster, and Strand is Tier 2, so 'absent from §2' is partly by-design (Tier 2 lives in the roster). The genuine, bounded gap is that his live BG card/modifier lacks a §8 Priority Tree or any relational-graph edge for the AI to trigger from — a real operational hole, appropriately MEDIUM, not the HIGH-adjacent 'entire substrate missing' framing.

#### AUD-NPC2-120 — Ehrenwall's Convictions (Order / Autonomy) contradict the designated-canonical migration mapping (Honor / Authority / Identity)
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.5) vs npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.5 gives Ehrenwall Primary Conviction = Order, Secondary = Autonomy. The canonical PP-684 migration roster maps the Löwenritter Master to a completely different primary set: Honor 0.35 / Authority 0.25 / Identity 0.20, with prior legacy labels recorded as 'Military Honor / Continuity' (not Order/Autonomy). This is the same class of defect the 2026-06-22 audit confirmed HIGH for Edeyja (AUD-NPC2-007) and Maret (AUD-NPC2-009), but Ehrenwall was never covered. Order does not appear in her canonical conviction vector at all, and the legacy secondary was Continuity, not Autonomy.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:132-133 (Primary Order, Secondary Autonomy); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:198-212 (§2.5 'Lowenritter Master' → Honor 0.35 / Authority 0.25 / Identity 0.20; prior_labels ['Military Honor','Continuity']); designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:25-41 (canonical 13-Conviction set).
- **Fix:** Reconcile §2.5 to the PP-684 migration mapping: restate Ehrenwall's Convictions as Honor (primary) with Authority + Identity, OR get an editorial decision (logged) that overrides the migration roster for this character. Whichever wins, make the behavior doc, roster, and migration roster agree.
- **Verifier note:** The two-doc divergence is real and reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md:132-133 gives Primary Order / Secondary Autonomy, while conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:200-212 maps the Lowenritter Master to Honor 0.35 / Authority 0.25 / Identity 0.20 with prior_labels ['Military Honor','Continuity']. But this is NOT the Maret-class HIGH (AUD-NPC2-009). Three differences sink the HIGH: (1) the roster entry is titled 'Lowenritter Master (placeholder name)' (roster:200), NOT a named 'Ehrenwall' — compare the NAMED 'Maret Uln (Varfell; per PP-665 not renamed)' at roster:104. So the mapping to Ehrenwall is inferential. (2) character_canon_v30.md:426 (§11 D1) explicitly designates the migration roster PRIMARY and names the exact conflicts for 'Maret Uln' and 'Yrsa Vossen' — Ehrenwall is NOT named, so no canonical rule forces the roster to win over her authored §2.5 sheet. (3) Most decisive: character_canon_v30.md:42 and D1 explicitly acknowledge npc_behavior §2 conflicts with the migration roster and DEFER reconciliation ('Do not attempt to silently regenerate npc_behavior §2 from migration roster — that's a separate propagation cycle'). This is an acknowledged, tracked, deferred conflict, not an uncaught defect. The Maret HIGH turned on the roster-PRIMARY designation that is absent here. Real divergence, but MEDIUM not HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-121 — Ehrenwall Certainty is internally contradictory: 4 in §2.5 vs 5 in the §7.10 canonical stat table
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 vs §7.10 (internal, same doc) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** §2.5 lists Certainty = 4 ('Faithful — conventional piety, martial background ... ED-393 resolved'), stated as a resolved value. But the §7.10 table, explicitly labeled 'Canonical values for NPC Behavior System integration,' lists Ehrenwall Certainty = 5 ('Deed-logic worldview is self-reinforcing'). Two different 'canonical' Certainty values for the same NPC in the same document. This is the integration-facing value the sim would read, so the disagreement is load-bearing.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:138 (§2.5 Certainty 4, 'ED-393 resolved'); :1180-1190 (§7.10 'Canonical values...' table — Ehrenwall | 0 | 5 | 'Deed-logic worldview is self-reinforcing').
- **Fix:** Pick one Certainty value and propagate. The §7.10 'Deed-logic self-reinforcing → 5' rationale and her TS 0 non-practitioner profile read as the more deliberate value; if 5 wins, correct §2.5 (and the now-stale 'ED-393 resolved = 4' citation).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced exactly in the same document: npc_behavior_v30.md:138 (§2.5) gives Certainty 4 ('ED-393 resolved'); npc_behavior_v30.md:1190 (§7.10, header line 1182 'Canonical values for NPC Behavior System integration') gives Ehrenwall Certainty 5 ('Deed-logic worldview is self-reinforcing'). Two 'canonical' values for one NPC, with the integration-facing table holding the 5. The split is repo-wide and load-bearing: the actual archived editorial resolution editorial_ledger_archive_1001_1200.yaml:1017 ('Ehrenwall TS=0/Cert=4 ... Resolves ED-392 through ED-398') supports 4, while arc_expansion_v30.md:369 and canon_flatten_examination.md:128 cite ED-393 for 5. Either way the §2.5-vs-§7.10 internal contradiction is real and unresolved. MEDIUM holds.

#### AUD-NPC2-122 — Deprecated Conviction label 'Autonomy' used as live canon throughout Ehrenwall's design (taxonomy renamed it 'Liberty')
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30.md §1.2 line 30 ('Autonomy is renamed Liberty'); PP-684; alias_registry.yaml:613 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The behavior doc's own §1.2 deprecation note states plainly: 'Autonomy is renamed Liberty.' The canonical 13-Conviction set and alias registry contain Liberty, not Autonomy. Yet Ehrenwall's sheet uses 'Autonomy' as her Secondary Conviction, in her Certainty justification ('Consistent with Order/Autonomy Conviction'), and as her crisis/collapse Conviction in the Almud arc map (Arc C: 'Order → Autonomy'). Parallel to confirmed AUD-NPC2-059 (Torsvald) / AUD-NPC2-108 (foils), but Ehrenwall-specific and uncovered.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:30 (deprecation: 'Autonomy is renamed Liberty'); :133 (Secondary Conviction Autonomy); :138 ('Order/Autonomy Conviction'); :570 (Arc C 'Order → Autonomy'); references/alias_registry.yaml:613 (canonical set lists Liberty, no Autonomy).
- **Fix:** Replace 'Autonomy' with 'Liberty' everywhere it labels a Conviction for Ehrenwall (§2.5 Secondary Conviction, the Certainty note, §5.2 Arc C collapse), consistent with §1.2's own rename.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced for the Ehrenwall-specific anchors: npc_behavior_v30.md:30 declares 'Autonomy is renamed Liberty'; conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:35 and alias_registry.yaml:613 list Liberty (no Autonomy) in the canonical 13; yet §2.5 uses 'Autonomy' as Ehrenwall's Secondary Conviction (line 133) and in her Certainty note ('Consistent with Order/Autonomy Conviction', line 138). The direct precedent AUD-NPC2-059 (Torsvald, same deprecated 'Autonomy' label) was CONFIRMED MEDIUM, so MEDIUM is correctly calibrated. ONE evidence anchor is wrong and should be discounted: line 570 ('Arc C: Order -> Autonomy (survival). Almud becomes an exile') is in ALMUD's §5.2 arc profile, not Ehrenwall's — the finding misattributes Almud's Conviction collapse to Ehrenwall's '§5.2 Arc C collapse.' The two §2.5 instances are genuinely Ehrenwall's and carry the verdict; the line-570 anchor is a misattribution but does not negate the finding.

#### AUD-NPC2-123 — Secondary Conviction 'Autonomy' is a superseded legacy label used as live canon (2 docs)
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 / conviction_taxonomy_v30 §legacy-map; alias_registry ethical_disposition | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Ehrenwall's Secondary Conviction is recorded as 'Autonomy' with no [SUPERSEDED->Liberty] annotation. 'Autonomy' is a deprecated 9-Conviction-set label; PP-684 renamed it Liberty. The label is presented as a live canonical Conviction value, not a traceability alias. The rationale text even uses it in its legacy 'martial self-determination' sense, which is exactly the Liberty semantics.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 line 133 ('Secondary Conviction | Autonomy | ...falls back on martial self-determination'); designs/architecture/complete_systems_reference.md §1.8 line 79 ('Convictions: Order (national survival)/Autonomy'). Canon mandate: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §1.2 line 30 ('Autonomy is renamed Liberty'); designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md line 282-283 + references/alias_registry.yaml line 611-613; character_canon_v30.md line 217/433 (crisis-table 'Autonomy' explicitly remapped to Liberty).
- **Fix:** Replace 'Autonomy' with 'Liberty' as the live secondary Conviction in npc_behavior_v30 §2.5 and complete_systems_reference §1.8 (or annotate 'Liberty [legacy: Autonomy]'). Apply consistently to the Beliefs/Certainty rationale lines that read 'Order/Autonomy'.
- **Verifier note:** Core fact reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 line 133 records secondary Conviction 'Autonomy' and complete_systems_reference §1.8 line 79 'Order (national survival)/Autonomy', with no [SUPERSEDED->Liberty] marker; the Autonomy->Liberty rename under PP-684 is genuine (npc_behavior line 30, character_canon_v30 lines 217/433, conviction_track_v1 line 58). But the HIGH framing as an Ehrenwall-specific violation is overstated: this is a uniform doc-wide state of ALL §2 stance triangles (Almud secondary 'Reason' line 51, Vaynard 'Reason'/'Autonomy', Vossen/Edeyja 'Continuity'), which canon explicitly acknowledges and DEFERS — character_canon_v30 line 42 and D1 (line 426): 'Do not attempt to silently regenerate npc_behavior §2 from migration roster — that's a separate propagation cycle.' The prior verified comprehensive audit (designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md lines 418-421) rated this exact issue MEDIUM as 'a known, tracked deferred-propagation debt.' Two cited anchors are also misattributed: conviction_taxonomy_v30 lines 282-283 and alias_registry lines 611-613 reference 'Reason'/'Continuity' legacy tags, NOT 'Autonomy.' Real but tracked/deferred and systemic, not an isolated HIGH FAIL.

#### AUD-NPC2-124 — Ethical Framework 'Martial Honour' still used as a LIVE mechanical Ob driver with no supersession note
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D2; PP-684 §6 -> PP-686 §3.7; SUPERSESSION-PP686-001 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Ehrenwall's Ethical Framework is 'Martial Honour (Löwenritter)' carrying live Ob modifiers (Aligned: -1 Ob on orders protecting sovereignty; Contradictory: +2 Ob advancing personal/factional gain). This is the exact pattern §11 D2 flags as a violation: framework labels are superseded as mechanical drivers (mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc) and require a [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] marker; framework-label Ob modifiers in params/bg/core were superseded 2026-05-01. Her sheet has no such marker and presents the -1/+2 as live engine modifiers.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 line 134 ('Ethical Framework | Martial Honour (Löwenritter) | Aligned: -1 Ob ... Contradictory: +2 Ob'). Canon: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md line 427 (D2) + line 49-50 + line 385; params/bg/core.md line 235 ('Ethical Framework Modifiers — SUPERSEDED 2026-05-01 (PP-686 v2)'); references/alias_registry.yaml line 614-615 (ethical_framework_modifier canonical = PP-686 §3.7).
- **Fix:** Append '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]' to the Ethical Framework row and demote 'Martial Honour' to a descriptive disposition tag; route the -1/+2 Ob through PP-686 §3.7 (mission/cascade/expectation alignment) rather than the label. Apply per D2 to the §8.7/BG priority-tree 'Framework-aligned opportunity' references that key off the label.
- **Verifier note:** Fact reproduced: npc_behavior_v30 §2.5 line 134 carries 'Martial Honour (Loewenritter)' with live -1/+2 Ob and no [SUPERSEDED] marker. But the claim that this is a flagged violation is contradicted by the canon it cites: character_canon_v30 D2 (line 427) states the CURRENT canonical decision is 'Mechanical Ob modifiers stay attached to the label since they're live in engine' and the [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] marker is prescribed for the future Part B per-NPC sheets, NOT for §2; §10.1 (line 385) lists Ethical Framework as 'CONFLICTED — labels SUPERSEDED but mechanically active,' with the marker+PP-686 routing as what the consolidation WILL do. The params/bg/core.md line 235 supersession struck the FACTION-level modifier table (Crown/Church/.../Loewenritter Honor -1/+2), and alias_registry line 614-619 governs the generic 'ethical_framework_modifier' — neither is the §2.5 NPC-sheet row. Every §2 NPC carries this same live-label pattern (Almud 'Virtue (Crown)' line 52). Same deferred-propagation debt; demoting to a per-NPC HIGH overstates. MEDIUM consistent with how the framework dimension is canonically treated.

#### AUD-NPC2-125 — Absent by name from the PRIMARY conviction source; placeholder roster entry contradicts authored vector
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (migration roster = conflict-resolution PRIMARY source); PP-685 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Per character_canon_v30 §11 D1, conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) is the designated authority when Conviction values conflict, and §11 explicitly states the npc_behavior §2 stance triangles CONFLICT with the migration roster. Ehrenwall does not appear by name in the roster at all. The only Löwenritter entry is a placeholder 'Lowenritter Master' whose primary_convictions are Honor 0.35 / Authority 0.25 / Identity 0.20 — which does not match Ehrenwall's authored Order(primary)/Autonomy(->Liberty) vector. There is no reconciliation note, so the authoritative source and the live stat block disagree on her primary Conviction.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.5 lines 198-212 (placeholder 'Lowenritter Master', Honor/Authority/Identity; no 'Ehrenwall'/'Lisbeth' anywhere in file); vs designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 lines 132-133 (Order primary / Autonomy secondary). Conflict acknowledged: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md line 42 + line 386 (D1) + line 426.
- **Fix:** Add a named Ehrenwall entry to conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.5 with the authored vector (Order primary + Liberty + cultural lowenritter_military background), superseding/explicitly linking the 'Lowenritter Master' placeholder, OR add a [CONFLICT: ...] note on the §2.5 stat block per the §11 D1 surfacing convention. Resolve whether her primary is Order (behavior doc) or Honor (roster placeholder).
- **Verifier note:** Premises reproduced: grep for 'Ehrenwall'/'Lisbeth' in conviction_migration_roster_v30.md returns zero hits — she is absent by name. The only Lowenritter entry is the placeholder 'Lowenritter Master' (§2.5 lines 200-212, Honor 0.35/Authority 0.25/Identity 0.20), differing from §2.5's Order-primary. character_canon_v30 D1 (line 426) does name the migration roster PRIMARY on conflict. However HIGH overstates impact relative to the structurally identical Maret Uln case the comprehensive audit rated HIGH (lines 289-292): there the named roster profile directly NEGATES the character's defection arc (load-bearing). Here the roster entry is generic and UNNAMED (not asserted to be Ehrenwall), no Ehrenwall arc structurally depends on Order-vs-Honor, and D1 explicitly defers reconciliation to the unwritten Part B per-NPC sheets (character_canon line 439, 'PART B ... pending Q1 scope'). A real absence + placeholder mismatch, but lower load-bearing impact and a known Part-B-deferred reconciliation — MEDIUM, not HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-126 — No live arc map / Belief-revision track for Ehrenwall — Coup Counter arc superseded, replacement not authored
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §5.2 (Named NPC Arc Profiles); arc_expansion_v30 supersession block; Belief->Scar mechanic (§3.3/§3.4) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Ehrenwall carries 3 Beliefs but has no Arc Map in §5.2 (only Almud, Arne, Inge, Magnus, Edeyja are mapped), so there is no defined Belief->Scar / Belief-revision trajectory for her. The arc she did have (Coup-Counter-driven Arc A/B/C) is explicitly marked SUPERSEDED by the graduated Löwenritter autonomy clock (Loyal->Restless->Autonomous->Split), and 'Ehrenwall Arc A/B/C (entire track)' is listed among the affected/dead sections — but no replacement arc profile keyed to the autonomy clock has been authored. Her Beliefs (esp. 'Almud is faltering — I must be ready to act') and her stated Consequence vulnerability ('keeping count of the Crown's failures') therefore have no mechanical revision/Scar pathway.
- **Evidence:** Beliefs: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 lines 140-143; §5.2 arc maps present only for Almud/Arne/Inge/Magnus/Edeyja (npc_behavior_v30.md lines 532-668; no Ehrenwall map). Supersession: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md lines 20-23 ('Coup Counter ... -> Löwenritter graduated autonomy'; 'Affected sections ... Ehrenwall Arc A/B/C (entire track)'). Belief->Scar mechanic exists at npc_behavior_v30.md §3.3/§3.4.
- **Fix:** Author an Ehrenwall arc profile in §5.2 keyed to the graduated autonomy clock (Loyal->Restless->Autonomous->Split), mapping Belief 2 ('Almud is faltering') revision and any Belief->Scar conversion to clock-stage transitions, replacing the struck Coup-Counter Arc A/B/C.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. arc_expansion_v30.md lines 20-23 supersede the Coup Counter ('-> Loewenritter graduated autonomy (Loyal -> Restless -> Autonomous -> Split)') and line 23 explicitly lists 'Ehrenwall Arc A/B/C (entire track)' among affected sections. npc_behavior_v30 §5.2 Arc Maps exist only for Almud, Arne, Inge, Magnus, Edeyja (headers at lines 554/592/606/620/631) — no Ehrenwall map. She carries 3 Beliefs (§2.5 lines 141-143) and a stated Consequence vulnerability, with no authored §5.2 Belief->Scar/revision trajectory keyed to the new clock; the _infill §11 texture still references the superseded 'Coup Counter' (lines 117-119). Minor caveat: the supersession block offers a 'rough mapping for reader translation' (CC 0-3 ~ clock stages) and says structure 'remains valid as illustration,' so it is translation-guided rather than a total void — but no authored replacement profile exists. Gap is real; MEDIUM appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-127 — No consolidated per-NPC sheet for Ehrenwall in character_canon_v30; character_analyses §11 body empty
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 (consolidation doc); §11 line 43 [GAP] flag | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** character_canon_v30 is the consolidation that 'supersedes piecemeal lookup' for per-NPC texture, yet it contains zero Ehrenwall/Löwenritter per-NPC entry (the only hit is the §11 D-table). Her npc_character_analyses_v30 §11 ('Grandmaster Ehrenwall — The Ledger That Never Forgives') is an empty heading with no body in the skeleton file (texture lives only in the _infill). character_canon §11 itself flags the 'Ehrenwall character_analyses entries' as empty-body/[GAP]. The net effect: for a major named faction leader, the only authoritative personality-mechanics stat block is the conflicted npc_behavior §2.5 (plus the divergent complete_systems_reference §1.8) — there is no consolidated, reconciled sheet.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md — no Ehrenwall/Löwenritter per-NPC sheet (only the §11 reference); line 43 ('Does not invent texture for empty-body sections (... Ehrenwall character_analyses entries ...). Flags [GAP]'). Empty heading: designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md §11 lines 273-276 (heading only; body present only in _infill lines 117-119).
- **Fix:** Add an Ehrenwall per-NPC sheet to character_canon_v30 Part B carrying the reconciled 13-Conviction vector (Order + Liberty), Certainty 4 (Faithful), Resonant Styles (Consequence/Solidarity), TS 0, and Beliefs, so the consolidation actually covers the Löwenritter Grandmaster and resolves the §2.5-vs-roster conflict in one authoritative place.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. character_canon_v30.md contains exactly one Ehrenwall reference — the [GAP] flag at line 43 ('Does not invent texture for empty-body sections (... Ehrenwall character_analyses entries ...)') — and no per-NPC Ehrenwall/Loewenritter sheet (Part B is unwritten; line 439 'PART B — NPC SHEETS pending Q1 scope decision'). npc_character_analyses_v30.md §11 lines 273-276 is a heading only ('Grandmaster Ehrenwall — The Ledger That Never Forgives'); the body lives only in the _infill (lines 117-119). The net effect the finding states holds: the only authoritative personality-mechanics block is the conflicted §2.5 plus the divergent complete_systems_reference §1.8. Partly by-design (Part B deferred), but the consolidation gap is genuine and correctly flagged as a gap rather than a fabrication. MEDIUM as filed.

#### AUD-NPC2-128 — Secondary Resonant Style recorded as 'Loyalty' (outside the 4-set) and inconsistent across docs
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (4 Resonant Styles: Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** complete_systems_reference §1.8 lists her Pressure Point (Resonant Style) as 'Consequence/Loyalty'. 'Loyalty' is not one of the four canonical Resonant Styles (Evidence, Consequence, Authority, Solidarity per ED-516). The canonical behavior stat block instead gives her secondary style as 'Solidarity'. So one doc uses a non-existent style label and the two docs disagree on her secondary vector.
- **Evidence:** designs/architecture/complete_systems_reference.md §1.8 line 79 ('Pressure Point: Consequence/Loyalty') vs designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 line 136 ('Secondary Resonant Style | Solidarity'); 4-set fixed in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl line 325 (ED-516) and npc_behavior_v30.md §1.3 lines 34-39.
- **Fix:** Change complete_systems_reference §1.8 'Loyalty' to 'Solidarity' to match the canonical 4-set and the behavior stat block. (Primary 'Consequence' is in-set and consistent; no change needed there.)
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. complete_systems_reference §1.8 line 79 reads 'Pressure Point: Consequence/Loyalty.' 'Loyalty' is not among the four canonical Resonant Styles fixed by ED-516 (editorial_ledger.jsonl line 325: 'Evidence, Consequence, Authority, Solidarity') and listed in npc_behavior_v30 §1.3 lines 36-39. The canonical behavior stat block npc_behavior_v30 §2.5 line 136 gives the secondary style as 'Solidarity.' So one doc uses an out-of-set label and the two docs disagree on her secondary vector. Primary 'Consequence' is in-set and consistent. Genuine error and cross-doc contradiction; MEDIUM as filed.

#### AUD-NPC2-129 — The Grandmaster Brandt succeeds is named inconsistently (Sigrid vs Lisbeth Ehrenwall), colliding with Riskbreaker Sigrid Torsvald
- **Unit:** Halvar Brandt | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L145 vs npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5/§7.10 + proper_noun_registry.yaml | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Brandt's arc, his BG 'Brandt Succession' card, and his TTRPG framing all hinge on succeeding 'Ehrenwall.' But the canonical timeline names the Loewenritter Grandmaster 'Sigrid Ehrenwall,' while every v30 NPC doc and the proper-noun registry name her 'Lisbeth Ehrenwall.' Worse, 'Sigrid' is the canonical first name of the Riskbreaker Torsvald in the same roster — so the timeline's 'Sigrid Ehrenwall' collides Brandt's predecessor's identity with another Loewenritter NPC. The figure at the center of Brandt's only arc is canonically ambiguous.
- **Evidence:** canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L145 ('Grandmaster Sigrid Ehrenwall'); archives/session/session_log_archive_part_4.md L138/L246 ('Grandmaster Sigrid Ehrenwall NPC created'). Contradicted by designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L128 (§2.5 'Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall') + L1190 (§7.10 'Ehrenwall'); references/proper_noun_registry.yaml — canonical Grandmaster is Lisbeth Ehrenwall, while L180 canonical 'Sigrid Torsvald'. Brandt succession framing: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L283-284; designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md L71.
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical first name for the Grandmaster (the v30 NPC corpus standard is 'Lisbeth Ehrenwall') and correct canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L145 from 'Sigrid Ehrenwall' to 'Lisbeth Ehrenwall' so 'Sigrid' is reserved for Torsvald. Re-check Brandt's succession references resolve to the corrected name.
- **Verifier note:** The contradiction reproduces: canon/03_canonical_timeline.md L145 reads 'Grandmaster Sigrid Ehrenwall' while the live NPC corpus uses 'Lisbeth Ehrenwall' (npc_behavior_v30.md §2.5 L128, index L30, plus arc_expansion_v30, complete_systems_reference, etc.). 'Sigrid' is canonically Torsvald (proper_noun_registry.yaml L179-180 'Sigrid Torsvald'; npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L29 '5. Sigrid Torsvald'), so the timeline double-uses 'Sigrid.' 'Sigrid Ehrenwall' is confined to the timeline + an archive + audit/sim artifacts, so it is the outlier vs corpus-wide 'Lisbeth.' BUT a cited anchor is misdescribed: the finding claims 'the proper-noun registry name her Lisbeth Ehrenwall / canonical Grandmaster is Lisbeth Ehrenwall' — FALSE. proper_noun_registry.yaml L103-105 gives canonical 'Grandmaster Ehrenwall' with alias ['Ehrenwall'] and NO first name; npc_registry.yaml (last_name Ehrenwall) likewise carries no first name. So the registry endorses neither 'Sigrid' nor 'Lisbeth.' HIGH is overstated because the surname 'Ehrenwall' is consistent everywhere, and every Brandt succession reference (roster L283-284, analyses L71, infill, §7.10 L1190) uses surname-only 'Ehrenwall' — Brandt's arc resolves correctly regardless of the Grandmaster's first name. This is a one-line first-name inconsistency in the timeline, not a mechanical/philosophical FAIL, and 'the figure at the center of Brandt's arc is canonically ambiguous' overstates the impact since Brandt never invokes a first name. Real contradiction, MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-130 — Brandt's succession arc + BG card depend on the superseded binary Coup Counter
- **Unit:** Halvar Brandt | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** clock_registry/Coup Counter supersession; canonical_registry.md L121; campaign_architecture_v30.md L179 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Brandt's entire BG/Hybrid consequence is built on the Coup Counter with a numeric threshold ('threshold is 2 not 3 — he acts sooner'). But canon has REPLACED the binary Coup Counter with the Graduated Autonomy track (Loyal -> Restless -> Autonomous -> Split). The mechanic Brandt depends on no longer exists in its cited form, and its ceiling is itself contradictory across canon (0-4 vs 0-3). The Loewenritter BG priority tree (behavior_v30 §7.5) already uses the new framing ('activates on Crown elimination or Split'), so Brandt's docs are out of sync with the rest of the system.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L87 + L284 ('Coup Counter mechanic continues but Brandt's threshold is 2 not 3'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md L71 ('Brandt's threshold is 2, not 3'). Superseded by: designs/architecture/campaign_architecture_v30.md L179 ('Graduated Autonomy track ... replacing the binary Coup Counter per worldbuilding §3 + clock_registry §3'). Ceiling conflict: designs/architecture/canonical_registry.md L121 (Coup Counter 0-4) vs designs/world/worldbuilding_v30_infill.md L31 (Coup Counter 0-3). New framing used at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L1152 §7.5.
- **Fix:** Rewrite Brandt's succession trigger and 'acts sooner' mechanic onto the Graduated Autonomy track (e.g., Brandt advances the Autonomy state one step earlier / fires Split at Restless rather than Autonomous), and drop the bare 'threshold 2 not 3' Coup Counter reference. Confirm the single canonical range.
- **Verifier note:** The factual core reproduces. Brandt's docs do cite the Coup Counter with 'threshold is 2 not 3' (npc_roster_v30.md L284 + L87/L283; npc_character_analyses_v30.md L71).
