Intel action vs Ob 3... Schoenland central recalls Solberg'). So the trigger both fires on events Solberg has no causal link to and omits the documented bias-exposure trigger. Grep confirms 'Supply Chain Exposure' and 'Parish Revolt' appear nowhere else in npc_behavior_v30.md except this line. This is a live cross-subsystem wiring error in a CANONICAL-status doc, not stale/editorial. Held at MEDIUM (not higher): it is one BG-mode NPC-faction automation line and breaches no immutable P-xx/GD-xx constraint, but it is a genuine reproducible contradiction that would misfire in play.

#### AUD-NPC2-163 — Conviction vector exists ONLY in the registry YAML; Solberg is absent from the designated PRIMARY source (conviction_migration_roster PP-685) and from character_canon
- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (designated PRIMARY source for Conviction values) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Solberg's personal_convictions (Utility 0.30 + Order 0.30) are stated only in references/npc_registry.yaml (NPC-012). He does not appear in designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md (PP-685) — the source character_canon_v30 §11 D1 designates as authoritative when docs conflict — nor anywhere in character_canon_v30.md. The roster maps exactly 13 named legacy characters (§2.1-§2.7) and lists 4 edge cases (§3); Solberg is in neither. The registry's migration_notes claim the vector was derived 'Per PP-685 §4', but §4 step 5 requires 'Append entry to this roster as Class B extension' — which was never done. The labels themselves are clean (Utility and Order are both in the 13-Conviction set, conviction_taxonomy_v30 §lines 29-41; no legacy Continuity/Reason/Autonomy/9-set labels are used), and the value has no cross-doc conflict because no other doc states it — but its sole home is a registry that points back to a primary source not containing him.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml lines 285-306 (NPC-012: Utility 0.30 + Order 0.30; migration_notes 'Per PP-685 §4'). Grep for 'Solberg|Schoenland' in designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md -> No matches found. Grep for same in designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md -> No matches found. Roster mapping scope: conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2 (line 37 '13 named characters'), §3 edge cases (lines 253-258), §4 process (lines 264-282). Authority for D1: character_canon_v30.md §11 line 426 + §10 line 386.
- **Fix:** Append a Solberg (NPC-012) entry to conviction_migration_roster_v30.md as a Class B / §4 extension (he has 'No prior legacy label' per the registry, so use the §4 no-prior-tags path) so the PRIMARY conviction source actually contains the value the registry already cites. Keep Utility+Order; record self_other_initial 0.00. Then have the registry reference the specific roster sub-section rather than the generic §4 process.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced every anchor. references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-012 (lines 297-303) is the sole home of Utility 0.30 + Order 0.30, with migration_notes 'Per PP-685 §4'. Grep for 'Solberg|Schoenland' in designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md = No matches, and in designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md = No matches. The roster maps exactly 13 named characters (§2 line 37) and 4 edge cases in §3 (Solmund, Vaynard, Vossler, Reinholt — lines 255-258); Solberg is in neither. §4 step 5 (line 272) does require 'Append entry to this roster as Class B extension', never done. character_canon §11 D1 (line 426) explicitly designates conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) values as PRIMARY when sources conflict. Labels are clean: Utility (#5) and Order (#3) are both in the 13-Conviction set (conviction_taxonomy_v30 lines 29-41); no legacy labels used. The finding's framing — value lives only in a registry that points back to a primary source not containing him — is exactly accurate. MEDIUM is appropriate: it is a provenance/traceability defect in a mechanical field, not a P-xx/GD-xx violation, and the auditor correctly notes there is no cross-doc value conflict.

#### AUD-NPC2-164 — No Resonant Style (primary/secondary) defined for Solberg in any doc
- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (4-style taxonomy) / character_canon_v30 §sheet-schema line 73 / npc_behavior §1.3 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Solberg has no Resonant Style anywhere. The canonical NPC sheet schema requires a primary (often secondary) Resonant Style drawn from the 4-set (Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity, ED-516). npc_behavior_v30 §2 (lines 46-378) carries no stance triangle / Resonant Style for Solberg (he surfaces only as the §7.7 'Recall Solberg' trigger and is absent from the §7.10 stat table). This is acute for Solberg specifically because his entire arc is a social/persuasion play: PCs must move Schoenland central / offer him a real recall path, and he 'detects' false offers — yet no argument form is defined as the one that reaches him. No Knot-path question arises (he is not assigned Solidarity), and no P-08 Thread-Evidence-vs-TS<30 violation arises (no Evidence style is assigned and no Thread-level evidence is routed at him).
- **Evidence:** Absence across designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2 (lines 46-378) and §1.3; designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md §12 (lines 63-68); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md §12 (lines 122-127) + _infill (lines 58-61). Schema requirement: character_canon_v30.md sheet schema line 73 ('Resonant Style — primary + secondary'). 4-style canon: canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl ED-516 (line 325). Prior audit corroboration: 2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md AUD-NPC2-033 verifier note (line 463) lists Solberg among roster NPCs with no Resonant Style in the behavior doc.
- **Fix:** Assign Solberg a primary (and ideally secondary) Resonant Style from the 4-set, consistent with his STABILITY-SEEKING / pragmatic-factor profile and his Utility+Order vector — most naturally Consequence (he responds to 'this outcome harms/helps the goal') with secondary Authority (Schoenland-central directives), and explicitly NOT Evidence (avoids any temptation to route Thread-precision argument at a TS-null NPC). Record it on a character_canon sheet and/or the behavior doc.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. The canonical sheet schema requires Resonant Style primary+secondary (character_canon_v30 line 73), drawn from the 4-set per ED-516 (editorial_ledger.jsonl line 325: Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity, status resolved). Grep for 'Solberg' across npc_behavior_v30.md returns a single hit — line 1167, the §7.7 'Recall Solberg' trigger; he has no §2 stance triangle / Resonant Style and no §7.10 row. Absence also confirmed in npc_roster_v30_infill §12 (lines 63-68), npc_character_analyses_v30 §12 (122-127) and _infill (58-61). Prior audit AUD-NPC2-033 verifier note (line 463) independently lists Solberg among roster NPCs with no Resonant Style in the behavior doc. The acuteness rationale (his arc is a persuasion play in which he 'detects false offers') is supported by analyses line 127 and roster_infill line 68. The P-08 self-protection caveat (assigning NOT-Evidence to avoid routing Thread-precision at a TS-null NPC) is sound. MEDIUM holds: a schema-required field is entirely absent for an arc-bearing NPC.

#### AUD-NPC2-165 — Certainty value missing for Solberg — omitted from the §7.10 Certainty table that lists his roster peers
- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §sheet-schema line 60 (Certainty) + npc_behavior §7.10 NPC Stat Values table | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** A named, recurring, arc-bearing NPC should carry a Certainty value (0-7 band + label) per the sheet schema header. Solberg has none. Most conspicuously, the canonical npc_behavior_v30 §7.10 'NPC Stat Values (Thread Sensitivity and Certainty)' table lists 13 NPCs (Almud ... Yrsa Vossen) but omits Solberg — even though Solberg is referenced in the SAME document at §7.7 (the 'Recall Solberg' trigger). The doc thus mechanically invokes him without ever giving him the Certainty stat the table exists to record.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §7.10 table lines 1184-1198 (13 NPCs, no Solberg row) vs §7.7 line 1167 ('Recall Solberg' in the same doc). Schema header requiring Certainty: character_canon_v30.md line 60. No Certainty value for Solberg appears in npc_roster_v30_infill §12 (lines 63-68), npc_character_analyses_v30 §12 (122-127)/_infill (58-61), or references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-012 (where ts/coherence/stats are all explicitly null, lines 294-296, and no certainty key exists).
- **Fix:** Add a Solberg row to the §7.10 table with a Certainty value + rationale consistent with his profile (e.g. Certainty ~3 'Questioning' — a competent factor whose professional judgment is quietly compromised by an unconscious homesickness bias he 'cannot fully see', roster_infill line 67), and TS 0/null (non-practitioner). Mirror the value onto any character_canon sheet.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced directly. The §7.10 'NPC Stat Values (Thread Sensitivity and Certainty)' table (npc_behavior_v30.md lines 1184-1198) lists exactly 13 NPCs (Almud, Lenneth, Baralta, Vaynard, Ehrenwall, Himlensendt, Torben, Elske, Maret Uln, Haelgrund, Torsvald, Edeyja, Yrsa Vossen) with no Solberg row — while the SAME document invokes him at §7.7 line 1167. Schema header lists Certainty (character_canon_v30 line 60). No Certainty appears in roster_infill §12 (63-68), analyses §12 (122-127)/_infill (58-61), or registry NPC-012 (ts/coherence/stats null, lines 294-296; no certainty key). The doc mechanically invokes him without the stat the table exists to record. MEDIUM holds.

#### AUD-NPC2-166 — Arc C Coherence degradation omits the §5.0b tridimensional Knot-strain propagation its own template (Vaynard Arc C) carries
- **Unit:** Sigrid Torsvald | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** P-12 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Torsvald's Arc C explicitly states she experiences 'what Vaynard experiences in Arc C' as a TS 30+ NPC in active Coherence crisis, and the arc contemplates a Knot with the PC (player intervention via Knot at Close). The general rule §5.0b mandates that a TS 30+ NPC in active crisis propagates +1 strain/season to all Knotted NPCs. Vaynard's parallel Arc C includes this propagation explicitly; Torsvald's Arc C describes only her own generic 'Coherence begins degrading' with no knot-strain propagation, so the tridimensional drift-through-knots requirement is dropped for this transformation arc.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:451-452 (Torsvald Arc C: '...Coherence begins degrading... The Coherence degradation shows in operational choices' — no §5.0b reference), :454 (Arc C contemplates Knot at Close with player); the parallel template at :174 ('Knot strain propagation (§5.0b): TS 30+ in active crisis → Close Knot +1 strain/season to all Knotted NPCs').
- **Fix:** Add the §5.0b knot-strain propagation clause to Torsvald Arc C (TS 30+ in crisis → +1 strain/season to Knotted NPCs, incl. the PC if Knotted at Close), matching Vaynard Arc C, so transformation drift propagates through knots rather than staying a single self-contained Coherence value.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced and actually stronger than framed. Torsvald Arc C (arc_expansion_v30.md:451-452) describes only self-contained 'Coherence begins degrading' with no §5.0b reference, and :454 contemplates a Knot at Close with the PC. Vaynard Arc C carries the clause explicitly at :174 ('Knot strain propagation (§5.0b): TS 30+ in active crisis -> Close Knot +1 strain/season to all Knotted NPCs'). Decisively, the rule itself — npc_behavior_v30.md §5.0b:528 — enumerates scope: 'This applies to: Vaynard Arc C ..., Himlensendt Arc C ...; Does NOT apply to: Almud ..., Edeyja ...'. Torsvald is a TS 30+ NPC in active Coherence crisis but is in neither list, so the gap exists in both the arc text and the rule's own applicability table. Held at MEDIUM not HIGH: §5.0b's general clause ('a transforming NPC's thread-shift exerts force on everyone knotted to them') would still functionally cover a Knotted Torsvald at the table, so this is incompleteness (missing explicit entry + un-restated clause), not a contradiction with P-12. (Aside: §5.0b expresses a single '+N strain/season' scalar, itself only loosely P-12-tridimensional, but that is beyond this finding's scope.)

#### AUD-NPC2-167 — Torsvald is 'she/her' in roster, analyses, and registry but 'he/him' throughout the canonical arc doc
- **Unit:** Sigrid Torsvald | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Gender / pronoun consistency | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster, character analyses, and the registry (incl. the foil NPC's description) consistently render Sigrid Torsvald as female. The canonical arc source renders the same character male throughout its arc profiles. A GM cross-referencing the canonical arc doc against the roster gets contradictory characterization for the same NPC.
- **Evidence:** Female: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:74 ('her TS means she can perceive'),:271 ('she may abort mid-mission'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:31 ('The person she is becoming'); references/npc_registry.yaml:142 ('TS lets her perceive'),:829 (NPC-080 'Completes missions Sigrid aborts'). Male: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:425 ('he is constrained, captured'),:426 ('He has a line'),:439/:448/:452/:453/:454 ('He compartmentalises. He operates.').
- **Fix:** Normalize pronouns in designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md §'Torsvald (Riskbreaker) — Full Arc Profiles' to she/her to match the roster/registry canon.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim. Female: npc_roster_v30.md:74 ('her TS means she can perceive'), :271 ('she may abort mid-mission'); npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:31 ('The person she is becoming'); npc_registry.yaml:142 ('TS lets her perceive'), :829 (NPC-080 'Completes missions Sigrid aborts'). Male: arc_expansion_v30.md:425 ('he is constrained, captured'), :426 ('He has a line'), :439/:446/:448/:452 ('He compartmentalises. He operates.'). Same NPC, directly contradictory gendering across docs a GM is told to cross-reference. The arc doc is flagged editorial/'translate mechanics,' but pronouns are not a mechanic to translate, so the inconsistency stands on its face. MEDIUM is right for a pervasive characterization-consistency defect (authoring error, not a canon-constraint violation).

#### AUD-NPC2-168 — Arc A→B trigger 'TS crosses 30' is pre-satisfied if starting TS is 35
- **Unit:** Sigrid Torsvald | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** arc_expansion_v30 §Torsvald Arc A/B | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The transformation arc gates Arc B ('The Operative Who Sees') on TS crossing/reaching 30, and Arc A's passive conditioner advances TS toward that threshold (+1 per Southernmost-adjacent op). But the roster/registry canonical starting TS is 35 — already past the gate. If 35 is the start, Arc A's TS-development loop and the 'TS crosses 30' trigger are dead on arrival; Torsvald begins the campaign already in Arc B's perceptual state, which contradicts the 'undercover/cover intact' premise of Arc A.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:440 ('Arc A passive conditioner: Torsvald's TS 20+ is developing... At TS 30 (Stirring): Arc B conditions are met'), :441 ('Trigger: TS crosses 30...'), :444 ('Arc B branch condition: TS ≥ 30'); contradicted by references/npc_registry.yaml:131 (ts: 35) and designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:271 ('TS 35').
- **Fix:** Reconcile the start value with the arc gate. Either set Torsvald's starting TS below 30 (e.g., 20–28) so the developmental arc functions, and treat '35' as a near-ceiling/current-late-arc value; or, if 35 is the true start, rewrite the Arc A/B gate so the trigger is a Discovery/exposure event rather than a TS threshold she has already passed.
- **Verifier note:** Mechanics are real: arc_expansion_v30.md:440 advances TS toward 30 via Spirit checks and :441/:444 gate Arc B on 'TS crosses 30' / 'TS >= 30'; if start TS = 35 the loop and trigger are inert. BUT this is a cross-doc reconciliation issue, not an internal break: within arc_expansion_v30 Torsvald's value is stated consistently as 'TS 20+' (:418, :429, :440), so the gate functions on the arc doc's own number. The defect only materialises if the roster's 35 is imported as the start. The 'TS 35' docs also frame it as developed-and-still-developing (analyses_infill:30 'developed through exposure', :32 'risks accelerating Torsvald's own TS development past the point where she can conceal it'), which supports the auditor's own reconciliation (35 = current/late, 20+ = start) rather than a dead-on-arrival contradiction. Each doc is internally coherent on its own number; the break only appears when two docs are merged. That is a propagation/consistency defect (MEDIUM), not a hard mechanical contradiction; 'dead on arrival' overstates it. Downgraded HIGH -> MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-169 — Live canonical arc doc assigns Sigrid two LEGACY 9-set Convictions (Autonomy, Continuity) with no [SUPERSEDED->] annotation
- **Unit:** Sigrid Torsvald (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 conviction taxonomy / npc_behavior_v30 §1.2 (legacy-set deprecation) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Sigrid's only full Stance Triangle anywhere lives in arc_expansion_v30.md (Status: CANONICAL, approved 2026-04-17). Its table sets Primary Conviction = Autonomy and Secondary Conviction = Continuity. Both are deprecated legacy 9-set labels under PP-684: Autonomy -> Liberty, Continuity -> Warden. They are used as live values with no supersession note. The doc's POST-SESSION-B/C override banner strikes only Niflhel content and does not exempt these labels from the 2026-05-01 PP-684 migration, so they stand as uncorrected live canon. This also directly contradicts the registry's Utility/Honor vector for the same character.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md lines 424-425 ('Primary Conviction | Autonomy', 'Secondary Conviction | Continuity'); status header line 3 (CANONICAL); override banner lines 11-30 (strikes Niflhel only). Taxonomy: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §1.2 lines 26-30 (Reason/Continuity deprecated, Autonomy renamed Liberty). Conflict: references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-005 lines 134-140 (Utility/Honor). NOTE: prior audit AUD-NPC2-059 (2026-06-22 file line 134) flags the deprecated-label half; this finding additionally pins the direct contradiction with the registry vector and the missing supersession annotation.
- **Fix:** In arc_expansion_v30.md migrate Autonomy->Liberty and Continuity->Warden (or rebuild the vector to match the ratified registry/migration-roster entry once authored), with a [SUPERSEDED-> PP-684] note. Reconcile to a single conviction vector across arc doc + registry + (new) migration-roster entry.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors verified: arc_expansion_v30.md line 424 'Primary Conviction | Autonomy', line 425 'Secondary Conviction | Continuity'; npc_behavior_v30 §1.2 line 30 confirms Continuity deprecated and Autonomy renamed Liberty; no [SUPERSEDED->] note present. The deprecated-label half is real. BUT the 'live canonical' premise is the load-bearing error: the doc's batch header says CANONICAL (line 3), yet THIS specific arc is gated behind ED-596, logged at line 745 as 'proposed here. Requires user approval. P2' with NO resolution entry anywhere in canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl (grep returns nothing). So the Autonomy/Continuity vector is PROPOSED content, not ratified live canon. The 'direct contradiction with the registry Utility/Honor vector' is the documented, expected gap between a proposed legacy arc and the reconciled registry — character_canon §11 D1 (line 426) explicitly says do NOT treat npc_behavior/arc legacy values and roster/registry values as a live contradiction; they coexist by design pending a propagation cycle. The finding itself concedes prior audit AUD-NPC2-059 already flagged the deprecated-label half — and that audit (2026-06-22 file line 133) rated it MEDIUM, not HIGH. Real but pre-existing and over-severitied; MEDIUM at most, and it lives in unratified content.

#### AUD-NPC2-170 — Vaynard TS contradiction (10 vs 14) and wrong band label ('Dormant' for 14)
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS band labels (character_canon_v30 §7.1 table, lines 238-246) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's TS is '14 (Dormant)' in his stat block but '10 (hidden)' in §7.10. Separately, 14 is mis-banded: the canonical band table puts 10–19 in 'Hidden' and 20–29 in 'Dormant', so TS 14 is Hidden, not Dormant. Compounding it, his Private-Collection Discovery-Event trigger is written as 'TS 14+', so at the §7.10 starting value (10) he sits below his own trigger.
- **Evidence:** TS 14 (Dormant): designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:116 (§2.4 'Thread Sensitivity | 14 (Dormant)'); trigger 'TS 14+' at :116 and infill designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:100. TS 10 (hidden): designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1189 (§7.10). Band table: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:241-243.
- **Fix:** Set one canonical starting TS for Vaynard and align the Discovery-Event trigger to it. Relabel to the correct band (14 → 'Hidden'; or if 'Dormant' is intended, set TS to 20–29). Make §2.4 and §7.10 agree.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30.md:116 (§2.4) shows 'Thread Sensitivity | 14 (Dormant)' with note 'At TS 14+, Private Collection use triggers Spirit check for Discovery Event'; the infill trigger is restated at npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:100 ('Private Collection use at TS 14+'). §7.10 (:1189) lists 'Vaynard | 10 (hidden)'. Band mislabel verified against character_canon_v30.md:242–243: 10–19 = Hidden, 20–29 = Dormant, so TS 14 is Hidden, not Dormant. And at the §7.10 starting value 10 he sits below his own 'TS 14+' trigger. Both sub-claims hold; MEDIUM affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-171 — Edeyja TS (75–80 vs 50+) and Certainty (0 vs 1) contradictions
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS per-NPC canonicity + Certainty range 0–5 (character_canon_v30 §7.1, §7.4) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Edeyja's TS is 75–80 (Apex) almost everywhere, but '50+' in two places, and her Certainty is 0 (Accepted) in her stat block but 1 in §7.10. 75–80 vs 50+ straddles the Apex/Deep boundary and the 70+ Knot-strain row; Certainty 0 vs 1 changes whether she is 'full Thread acceptance' (Solmund as rendering) or merely 'Transitional.'
- **Evidence:** TS 75–80: designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:21, designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:217 (§2.9), designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:19, designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:337. TS 50+: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 (§7.10), designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md:88 (§3.3). Certainty 0: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:218 (§2.9 '0 (Accepted)'). Certainty 1: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 (§7.10).
- **Fix:** Standardize Edeyja to TS 75–80 and Certainty 0 (the supermajority, and the values in her dedicated edeyja_npc.md). Correct §7.10 (TS 50+ → 75–80; Certainty 1 → 0) and companion §3.3 (50+ → 75–80).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. TS 75–80: edeyja_npc.md:21, npc_behavior_v30.md:217 (§2.9), npc_roster_v30.md:20, character_canon_v30.md:337. TS 50+: npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 (§7.10), companion_specification_v30.md:87. Certainty 0: npc_behavior_v30.md:218 ('0 (Accepted) | Full Thread acceptance'). Certainty 1: npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 (§7.10 'Edeyja | 50+ | 1'). 75–80 vs 50+ straddles the Apex (70+) Knot-strain row vs Deep; Certainty 0 vs 1 flips 'full acceptance' vs 'Transitional.' MEDIUM affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-172 — Edeyja Coherence stat contradiction: 9 (canon) vs 7 (companion spec)
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Coherence range 0–10 / per-NPC baseline (character_canon_v30 §7.2, line 265 'Edeyja: 9') | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Edeyja's Coherence is authored as 9 ('Stable', sustained through disciplined practice) in her dedicated doc, the behavior stat block, the roster, the character analyses, and character_canon's per-NPC note — but as '7 (damaged from warden work)' in the companion practitioner table. Both are in-range (0–10) so this is not an out-of-scale defect, but it is a hard stat contradiction: 9 vs 7 changes her position relative to the ED-665 decision thresholds and the prose framing ('near-perfect rendering stability' vs 'damaged').
- **Evidence:** Coherence 9: designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:22, designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:219 (§2.9), designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:19, designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:16, designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:265. Coherence 7: designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md:88 (§3.3 practitioner table, '7 (damaged from warden work)').
- **Fix:** Set Edeyja's companion-spec Coherence to 9 to match all five other sources, or, if a 'damaged' companion-mode value is intended, state it explicitly as a deviation from the canonical 9 with a rationale so the prose-writer and ED-665 thresholds are not mis-fed.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. Coherence 9 in five sources: edeyja_npc.md:22, npc_behavior_v30.md:219 ('Coherence | 9 | Sustained through disciplined practice'), npc_roster_v30.md:20, npc_character_analyses_v30.md:16 ('Coherence 9 (near-perfect rendering stability)'), character_canon_v30.md:265 ('Edeyja: 9'). Coherence 7 in companion_specification_v30.md:87 ('7 (damaged from warden work)'). Both in-range (0–10) so not an out-of-scale defect, but a hard one-source-vs-five contradiction that shifts her relative to the ED-665 Coherence decision bands (character_canon:254–261, where 7 and 9 are both 'Stable 10–6' — wait, 7 and 9 are both in the 10–6 Stable band, so the band class is actually identical). The prose framing ('near-perfect stability' vs 'damaged') and any finer ED-665 threshold still diverge, but since both values fall in the same Stable decision band the mechanical impact is narrower than a band-crossing case. MEDIUM (at the low end) affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-173 — Torsvald TS contradiction (35 vs 20+) and unpinned arc concealment-break threshold
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** TS per-NPC canonicity (character_canon_v30 §7.1) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Sigrid Torsvald is 'TS 35' in the roster (summary table, Mode matrix, infill) but 'TS 20+' in §7.10 and the companion table. 20+ vs 35 straddles the practitioner threshold (30): at 20+ she may be sub-practitioner, at 35 she is an Active practitioner who can Thread-Read. Her arc text separately floats the concealment-break point as 'TS 40? 45?' (un-numbered GM discretion), compounding the imprecision on a track that drives her abort-rate arc.
- **Evidence:** TS 35: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:173 (summary), :271 (Mode matrix), designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:32. TS 20+: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1196 (§7.10), designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md:90 (§3.3). Unpinned threshold 'TS 40? 45?': designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:61.
- **Fix:** Fix Torsvald to a single TS (35 is the most-cited and is internally coherent with 'Active practitioner'). Update §7.10 and companion §3.3 from '20+' to 35, and replace 'TS 40? 45?' with a single numeric concealment-break threshold.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. TS 35: npc_roster_v30.md:173 (summary table), :271 (Mode matrix 'Undercover operative. TS 35'), npc_roster_v30_infill.md:32. TS 20+: npc_behavior_v30.md:1196 (§7.10 'Torsvald | 20+'), companion_specification_v30.md:89. Unpinned concealment-break 'GM discretion — TS 40? 45?': npc_character_analyses_v30.md:61. 20+ vs 35 straddles the 30 practitioner threshold (sub-practitioner vs Active Thread-Reader), and the arc-driving concealment point is left un-numbered. MEDIUM affirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-174 — Sæmund lacks a Behavior System block despite a contest-based social interface and a defection arc
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Behavior System block (Conviction/Beliefs/Resonant Style) missing | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Sæmund is a Full-presence NPC whose TTRPG interface explicitly states 'Social scenes with Haelgrund use the contest system — he argues from evidence, not dogma.' Running that contest and the defection arc requires Conviction, Beliefs, and Resonant Styles. Sæmund has only Ethics (Deontological), axis positions (5/9/2), a PROCEDURALIST AI tag, and the ambiguous §7.10 TS/Certainty row. He has no Primary/Secondary Conviction, no Beliefs list, and no Resonant Style — the full block that the comparable Lennart Haelgrund gets in §2.14 and that the contest/Resonant-Style system depends on.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L260 (contest-system interface); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L194 (axis positions only); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md L27 (Ethics only); contrast designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L301-317 (Lennart full block: Conviction/Beliefs/Resonant Style/Deviation Ob)
- **Fix:** Author a Behavior System block for Sæmund: Primary/Secondary Conviction (e.g., Order/Institutional Faith per his axes), 2-3 Beliefs, and a Primary Resonant Style (Evidence fits — 'he argues from evidence'), plus Leadership Deviation Ob, so the stated contest interface is runnable.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. Roster L260 states 'Social scenes with Haelgrund use the contest system — he argues from evidence, not dogma.' Sæmund's only authored attributes are: axis positions 5/9/2 (roster L194), Ethics Deontological (roster_infill L27), PROCEDURALIST AI tag (roster_infill L28), and the TS/Certainty row (§7.10 L1195). Grepping npc_roster_v30_infill.md for Conviction/Resonant Style/Belief returns no matches; the only Haelgrund with a full block (Primary/Secondary Conviction, Beliefs, Resonant Styles, Leadership Deviation Ob) is Lennart in §2.14 L301-317. Sæmund/Haelgrund does not appear in character_canon_v30.md, so no per-NPC sheet supplies it either. The contest system and Resonant-Style targeting depend on Conviction/Resonant Style, so the stated interface is not runnable as written. Real gap; MEDIUM is fair.

#### AUD-NPC2-175 — Implied Evidence Resonant Style + TS<30 with no TS≥30 gate — Thread-level evidence is P-08-inert against Sæmund
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 (Thread-level evidence epistemically inert against TS<30; gate re-activates at Stirring 30+); social_contest_v30 L451/L467 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The interface frames Sæmund as Evidence-responsive ('he argues from evidence'), and his entire arc hinges on evidence that Thread Sensitivity is natural. But Sæmund is TS 12 (well below the Stirring/30 threshold), so per P-08 Thread-level evidence is epistemically INERT against him — he can recite it but cannot act on it with Thread-level precision. No doc gates his Evidence vector to the ontical (non-Thread) tier or notes the TS≥30 re-activation, so as written Thread-level evidence is treated as a valid Evidence attack/driver against a TS<30 NPC. social_contest_v30 and npc_stance_triangles both encode the correct gate (TS≥30 / 'Thread-level evidence invalid vs TS 0 NPCs, ontical only'), so Sæmund's missing gate is a P-08 violation by omission. (The arc partly leans on this — his self-discovery is the gate crossing — but the contest-level Evidence vector is ungated.)
- **Evidence:** P-08 (immutable); designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md L451 (TS≥30 adds bonus dice) + L467 (TS≥30 to use Thread technique as evidence); params/factions/npc_stance_triangles.md L26 ('Thread-level evidence invalid as Evidence targeting vs TS 0 NPCs (P-08 compliance). Ontical evidence only.'); Sæmund TS 12 per designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L63. Note the prior comprehensive audit flagged the same TS 1-29-band hole in social_contest §6.1b (2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md L268-271).
- **Fix:** When authoring Sæmund's Evidence Resonant Style, explicitly restrict the Evidence vector to ontical (non-Thread) evidence while TS<30, and state the TS≥30 re-activation (which the defection arc itself supplies). This makes the contest P-08-compliant and lets the arc's gate-crossing be the mechanically meaningful moment.
- **Verifier note:** The P-08 substance is sound (Sæmund is TS 12/15, both <30; Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert against him; social_contest L451 keys weaving bonus dice to TS≥30 and L467 lets only TS≥30 adjudicators treat technique as evidence). But the finding's framing has two problems. (1) It claims npc_stance_triangles 'encode the correct gate (TS≥30)' — false: L26 actually reads 'Thread-level evidence invalid as Evidence targeting vs TS 0 NPCs', i.e. a TS-0-keyed gate, which the prior audit AUD-NPC2-006 itself flagged as a P-08 HOLE for the entire TS 1-29 band, not a correct TS≥30 gate. So the cited 'correct' anchor doesn't say what the finding says. (2) The 'violation by omission' is entirely contingent on an Evidence Resonant Style that finding 4 establishes does NOT exist for Sæmund — there is no authored mechanical artifact that currently treats Thread-level evidence as a valid attack against him, so this is a forward-looking authoring caution, not a live P-08 breach in any existing doc. Real and worth heeding when his style is authored, but it duplicates the already-confirmed §6.1b/TS-0 hole and is not an independent live violation. Downgraded to MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-176 — 'Deontological' ethics is the only live ethics datum, lacks the [SUPERSEDED→PP-686 §3.7] note, and contradicts the Faith-primary conviction
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / PP-686 §3.7 (Ethical Frameworks superseded as live Ob drivers); framework-vs-conviction consistency | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The infill and analyses give Sæmund a single ethics tag — Deontological ('duty to the institution') — with NO [SUPERSEDED→PP-686 §3.7] annotation, so as written it still reads as his governing mechanical driver rather than a descriptive disposition tag (PP-684 §6 / character_canon §11 D2 demote framework labels to descriptive only; the triadic Ob calc PP-686 §3.7 is the mechanical role now). It is also internally inconsistent with the conviction vector: 'Deontological / duty to the institution' is an Authority/Order disposition, but the registry assigns Faith as primary. A Faith-primary actor whose stated ethics is institutional-duty deontology is a framework-vs-conviction mismatch.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md L27 ('Ethics: Deontological — duty to the institution'); echoed as his only ethics datum in designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L24-28; conviction primary Faith per references/npc_registry.yaml L112.
- **Fix:** Mark the Deontological label as a descriptive disposition tag with [SUPERSEDED→PP-686 §3.7] so it cannot be read as a live Ob driver, and reconcile it with the conviction vector (if his frame is institutional duty, make Authority/Order primary; if Faith is primary, recast the disposition as Faith-deontology, not institutional-deontology).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_roster_v30_infill.md L27 ('Ethics: Deontological — duty to the institution') and npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L27 carry the same single ethics tag with no [SUPERSEDED→PP-686 §3.7] annotation. character_canon §11 D2 (L427) demotes Ethical-Framework labels to descriptive disposition tags with exactly that [SUPERSEDED → mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc] note. registry primary is Faith (L112). The omission of the supersession tag is a genuine consistency defect against D2. The 'framework-vs-conviction mismatch' second prong is partly interpretive — 'institutional-duty deontology' reading as Authority/Order rather than Faith is a reasonable but not airtight inference — but the missing-annotation defect alone supports MEDIUM. Not elevated because no immutable P-xx is breached; D2 is a consolidation decision, not a canon constraint.

#### AUD-NPC2-177 — Sæmund's Certainty is ambiguous and conflicting: §7.10 'Haelgrund 4' vs Lennart's §2.14 'Certainty 3 (Questioning)'
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Certainty value consistency (named NPC); behavior §7.10 L1195 vs §2.14 L309 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The §7.10 NPC stat table lists a bare-surname 'Haelgrund | 12 (hidden) | Certainty 4' whose rationale ('procedural certainty masking buried knowledge') maps to Sæmund's PROCEDURALIST flaw and whose neighbours are roster NPCs — so it is intended as Sæmund. But that row sits inside npc_behavior_v30, whose only profiled Haelgrund is Lennart (Ministry), who is given Certainty 3 (Questioning) in §2.14. The shared surname makes the 4-vs-3 split undetectable to a reader: Sæmund has no unambiguous, single-source Certainty, and the value collides with Lennart's. (No Certainty band-LABEL is given for Sæmund either; the canon expects '0-7 band w/ label e.g. Questioning'.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L1195 ('Haelgrund | 12 (hidden) | 4 | procedural certainty masking buried knowledge'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L309 (Lennart 'Certainty | 3 (Questioning)'); collision corroborated AUD-NPC2-068 (2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md L703-708).
- **Fix:** Put a single canonical Certainty value+label for Sæmund in his own stat block (roster §4 or a new behavior block) and relabel the §7.10 row explicitly 'Sæmund Haelgrund (Church)' so it cannot be confused with Lennart's Certainty 3.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim: npc_behavior_v30.md L1195 ('Haelgrund | 12 (hidden) | 4 | procedural certainty masking buried knowledge') and L309 (Lennart 'Certainty | 3 (Questioning)'). The L1195 rationale ('procedural certainty masking buried knowledge') maps to Sæmund's PROCEDURALIST flaw (roster_infill L28) and its table neighbours (Maret Uln, Torsvald, Edeyja, Vossen) are roster NPCs, so the row is intended as Sæmund — yet it sits in the doc whose only narratively-profiled Haelgrund is Lennart at Certainty 3, making the 4-vs-3 split undetectable to a reader. The prior audit AUD-NPC2-068 independently CONFIRMED this at MEDIUM. Also accurate that no Certainty band-LABEL is given for Sæmund (Lennart gets 'Questioning'). MEDIUM appropriate — value collision / disambiguation defect, no canon-constraint violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-178 — Cardinal-of-Justice vs Field-Inquisitor and Savonarola vs Bellarmine — conflicting characterisations that drive opposite conviction reads
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Role/title consistency (drives conviction read); faction_canon vs registry/character_canon | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Sæmund's institutional rank and archetype — both of which determine his conviction/ethics frame — are stated two incompatible ways. (a) Role: registry NPC-004 and character_canon say 'Field Inquisitor, NOT a Cardinal; Cardinal Justice = Arnlod Olafsson (NPC-038)', but faction_canon §Church lists Sæmund himself AS 'Cardinal of Justice', and the roster title is 'Cardinal Justice / Inquisitor'. (b) Archetype: registry tags him 'Savonarola archetype' (zealous purifier — reads as Faith-fervour primary), while roster_infill and analyses build him entirely as 'Robert Bellarmine' (institutional duty over private doubt — reads as Authority/Order with collapsing Faith). Savonarola and Bellarmine imply opposite conviction vectors, so the unresolved archetype is the root cause of the Faith-vs-Order/Reason conviction conflict (CONV-2).
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml L116/L120 ('Field Inquisitor, NOT Cardinal… Savonarola archetype'); designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md L542 ('Cardinal of Justice — Sæmund Haelgrund (Inquisitor; TS 12 unrecognized)'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md L25 + designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L26 (Bellarmine).
- **Fix:** Decide rank (Field Inquisitor under Cardinal Olafsson is the registry/character_canon position and is the more coherent one) and fix faction_canon L542; pick ONE archetype (Bellarmine is the one the prose actually develops) and delete the Savonarola tag from the registry, then derive the conviction vector from that single archetype.
- **Verifier note:** Both prongs reproduced. (a) Role: npc_registry.yaml L116/L120 and L102 say 'Field Inquisitor, NOT a Cardinal. Cardinal Justice = Arnlod Olafsson (NPC-038)'; npc_registry.sql L130 repeats 'Field Inquisitor, NOT Cardinal'; roster_infill L24 'Reports to Cardinal Olafsson (Justice)'. But faction_canon_v30.md L542 lists 'Cardinal of Justice — Sæmund Haelgrund (Inquisitor; TS 12 unrecognized)', and roster/infill section headers title him 'Church Inquisitor (Cardinal Justice)'. Direct role contradiction. (b) Archetype: registry L116/L120 and SQL L130 tag 'Savonarola archetype'; roster_infill L25 and character_analyses_infill L26 build him entirely as 'Robert Bellarmine'. Savonarola (zealous purifier) and Bellarmine (institutional duty over private doubt) imply opposite conviction vectors, plausibly the root of finding 2's Faith-vs-Order conflict. MEDIUM appropriate — real cross-doc contradiction, no immutable-canon breach. The faction_canon 'Cardinal' line directly conflicts with the registry's emphatic 'NOT a Cardinal', so the finding is not overstated.

#### AUD-NPC2-179 — No Beliefs authored for Sæmund, though his defection arc is a textbook Belief→Scar conversion
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Named-NPC Beliefs requirement (2-3 Beliefs); Belief→Scar transformation mechanic | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The personality-mechanics canon expects a named NPC to carry 2-3 Beliefs, and transformation arcs convert a Belief to a Scar (Piety/Conviction Scar). Sæmund's arc is exactly such a transformation — his belief that 'the Church is right about the nature of reality / TS is heretical corruption' must crack into a Scar on the defection/suppression branch. Yet no Beliefs list exists for him anywhere (the comparable Lennart Haelgrund gets 3 explicit Beliefs in §2.14; Sæmund gets none). Without an authored Belief, the Belief→Scar trigger (decisive contest + Resonant Style + engaged Belief, per npc_stance_triangles §Belief Revision) has nothing to operate on, and the Coherence-degradation 'suppress' branch (analyses branch C) has no Belief/Scar anchor.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L28 (branches B/C describe the worldview collapse but author no Belief); contrast designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L312-315 (Lennart's 3 Beliefs); Belief→Scar trigger in params/factions/npc_stance_triangles.md L65-68.
- **Fix:** Author 2-3 Beliefs for Sæmund (e.g. 'The Church's account of reality is true; thread-claims are heresy'; 'Procedure protects the innocent from zealotry'; 'My instincts are trained skill, nothing more') and specify which Belief converts to a Conviction/Piety Scar on the defection/suppress branch.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. No Beliefs list for Sæmund exists in any doc: npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L28 describes branches B/C (worldview collapse / Coherence degradation) in prose but authors no Belief; grep for Sæmund/Haelgrund in npc_behavior_v30.md surfaces only the Lennart §2.14 block and the L1195 row, no Sæmund Beliefs. Contrast Lennart's 3 explicit Beliefs at npc_behavior_v30.md L312-315. The Belief Revision (Belief→Scar) trigger is genuinely at npc_stance_triangles.md L65-68 ('Old Belief → Scar (permanent)') and requires an engaged Belief to operate on. character_canon §11 D3 recommends a texture floor of ≥1 Belief per Tier-3 NPC. The transformation-arc dependency is real. MEDIUM appropriate (texture/authoring gap, not a canon-constraint breach).

#### AUD-NPC2-180 — Bare-surname 'Haelgrund' alias resolves to Sæmund but is used for Lennart (Ministry) — collision absent from the collision DB
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** proper_noun_registry.yaml alias mapping; throughline_registry §T4; name_collision_database.yaml | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Two distinct canonical NPCs share the surname Haelgrund (Sæmund, Church Inquisitor; Lennart, Ministry Registrar), both hidden TS 12. proper_noun_registry.yaml registers the bare alias 'Haelgrund' as resolving to Sæmund ONLY, yet npc_behavior §2.14 and throughline_registry §T4 use bare 'Haelgrund' to mean Lennart (the institutional-governance throughline). So the alias registry mis-resolves the surname relative to the throughline/behavior usage, and the collision is NOT recorded in name_collision_database.yaml. Because the two characters carry different conviction labels (Sæmund Faith/Authority vs Lennart Continuity/Precedent) and different Certainty values, any tool resolving 'Haelgrund' will pull the wrong personality-mechanics block.
- **Evidence:** references/proper_noun_registry.yaml L196-204 (alias 'Haelgrund' → Sæmund, Church only); references/throughline_registry.md L60-63 (§T4 'Haelgrund is the throughline character for institutional governance… The Ministry…' citing npc_behavior §2.14 = Lennart); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L299 (Lennart); name_collision_database.yaml (no Haelgrund entry). Collision also logged AUD-NPC2-035/066/067.
- **Fix:** Add a Haelgrund entry to name_collision_database.yaml; in proper_noun_registry.yaml split the surname so bare 'Haelgrund' is flagged ambiguous (or rename one NPC, as prior audits recommend); require full given names in throughline_registry §T4 and all stat tables.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced on every anchor. proper_noun_registry.yaml L196-204 registers alias 'Haelgrund' → Sæmund Haelgrund (Church Inquisitor) ONLY. throughline_registry.md §T4 (L60-63) reads 'Haelgrund is the throughline character for institutional governance… The Ministry…' and cites npc_behavior §2.14, which is Lennart Haelgrund (Ministry Registrar, L299). So bare 'Haelgrund' in the throughline/behavior usage means Lennart while the alias registry resolves it to Sæmund — a genuine mis-resolution. name_collision_database.yaml has NO Haelgrund entry (grep returned no matches). The two carry different convictions (Sæmund Faith/Authority vs Lennart Continuity/Precedent) and different Certainty, so a tool resolving the surname pulls the wrong block. Prior audit AUD-NPC2-067 CONFIRMED the same collision and noted no canon uniqueness rule is breached, hence MEDIUM (documentation/disambiguation defect).

#### AUD-NPC2-181 — Belief set is split three ways across docs with conflicting #1/#2 text
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §3.1 named-NPC Belief floor (2-3 Beliefs, single canonical set) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** No single doc holds Yrsa's full, consistent Belief set, and the three sources disagree on content (not just ordering). npc_behavior §2.6 prints only an item numbered '3' (Violence), with #1/#2 absent under a numbering that implies three. npc_behavior_infill supplies a #1 (recover buried history) and #2 (power without accountability). arc_expansion supplies a DIFFERENT 4-Belief set whose #1 ('Power is not taken — it is grown') and #2 ('the Church made Thread practice heresy') do not match the infill's #1/#2. A reader of the canonical stat block sees one Belief; the two 'background' docs disagree with each other.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:161-162 (only '3. Violence is the tool of the powerful...'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:29-30 (#1 'deserve to know their own history', #2 'Power without accountability is tyranny'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:464-468 (#1 'Power is not taken — it is grown', #2 'The Church has made Thread practice heresy', #3 Violence, #4 Hidden Hann choice).
- **Fix:** Establish one canonical Belief set (the arc_expansion 4-item set is the most complete, incl. the Hidden Belief #4) and write it into npc_behavior_v30 §2.6 so the canonical stat block satisfies the 2-3 Belief floor with complete numbering; reconcile the divergent infill #1/#2 wording into that set or strike the infill duplicates.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced exactly. npc_behavior_v30.md:161-162 prints only an item numbered '3' (Violence) with no #1/#2; npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:29-30 supplies #1 'deserve to know their own history' and #2 'Power without accountability is tyranny'; arc_expansion_v30.md:464-468 supplies a different 4-item set whose #1 'Power is not taken — it is grown' and #2 'The Church has made Thread practice heresy' do not match the infill's #1/#2. Content (not just ordering) diverges across three docs; the canonical stat block shows one Belief. Severity MEDIUM appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-182 — Arc B Belief->Scar transformation described narratively but never wired to the Scar mechanic
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** Belief->Scar / Conviction Scar mechanic (conviction_track_v1 §2, retained per npc_behavior §1.2; signalled via social_contest_v30 §290) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The taxonomy mandate requires transformation arcs to convert a Belief to a Scar. Yrsa's Arc B is a textbook case: her signature Belief #3 ('Violence is the tool of the powerful — the movement wins through community, not force') is directly contradicted when the arc has her 'shift toward allowing targeted defensive violence' and flips her Conviction primary. The arc describes this as a Conviction shift ('Continuity secondary activates ... no longer Equity-primary') and an RS crisis, but never invokes the canonical Belief->Scar / Conviction Scar conversion the mechanic exists to model.
- **Evidence:** Belief at risk: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:162 ('3. Violence is the tool of the powerful...'). Arc transformation without Scar wiring: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:476-478 (Conviction 'being tested' / 'shifts toward defensive violence' / 'Continuity secondary activates') and :484 (C-i 'Solidarity RS crisis'). Scar mechanic exists: designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md:290 (Conviction Scar via Resonant-Style targeting, ref npc_behavior_v30 §3.3); conviction_track_v1 §2 retained per designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:30.
- **Fix:** Wire Arc B's 'shift toward defensive violence' outcome to the canonical Belief->Scar conversion: on the shift, convert Belief #3 to a Conviction Scar (per social_contest §290 / npc_behavior §3.3) rather than only narrating a Conviction relabel, so the transformation produces the mechanical Scar the system expects.


### — SOFT —

#### AUD-NPC2-183 — 'what the Church needs to win via Conviction Yield' phrasing risks implying a Church-specific victory path
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** GD-1 (Peninsular Sovereignty is the SOLE victory condition) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster and analysis frame Tormann as undermining 'the cultural authority the Church needs to win via/through Conviction Yield.' Read literally this suggests a Church-specific win condition. Conviction Yield is in fact a CI-generation mechanic (per-territory CV -> CI/season, PP-412) that advances toward the universal goal, not a victory trigger — so this is phrasing risk rather than a hard GD-1 violation, but the wording should not survive as if CI/Conviction Yield were itself a victory path.
- **Evidence:** Phrasing: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:161 ('the exact inversion of what the Church needs to win via Conviction Yield'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:65 ('the cultural authority the Church needs to win through Conviction Yield'). Mechanic identity (CI generation, not victory): sim/peninsular/ci_track.py:43,112 + conviction_track_v30 §3 PP-412. Sole-victory rule: canon/02_canon_constraints.md:38 (GD-1).
- **Fix:** Reword to 'the CI/Conviction Yield the Church relies on to advance toward Peninsular Sovereignty' (or similar) so no faction-specific victory is implied. No mechanical change needed.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and correctly scoped as phrasing risk rather than a hard violation. The phrasing exists at npc_roster_v30.md:161 ('the exact inversion of what the Church needs to win via Conviction Yield') and npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:65 ('the cultural authority the Church needs to win through Conviction Yield'). Conviction Yield is in fact a CI-generation mechanic, confirmed at sim/peninsular/ci_track.py:39-49 (PP-412 Step 1 institutional momentum CI +1; Step 2 CI_YIELD_BY_PT table mapping per-territory PT to CI), not a victory trigger. GD-1 (canon/02_canon_constraints.md:38) makes Peninsular Sovereignty the sole victory condition and explicitly STRIKES all faction-specific alternates. Because Conviction Yield only advances CI toward the universal goal, the wording is a soft phrasing risk (a literal reader could infer a Church win-path) and not a GD-1 FAIL. SOFT is the right call; no mechanical change needed, only a reword.

#### AUD-NPC2-184 — Primary RS = Evidence on a TS-0 NPC: ordinary-evidence reading is P-08-clean, but the docs never state the TS>=30 gate that bars Thread-level evidence as a vector
- **Unit:** Aldric Tormann (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 (Thread-level Evidence epistemically inert vs TS<30) + npc_behavior_v30 §1.3 Resonant Style table (Evidence has no TS gate) + social_contest_v30 (TS30 Stirring gate) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Tormann is TS 0 with Primary Resonant Style = Evidence (fiscal data / loyalty data). Per the audit method, when Primary is Evidence and the NPC is TS<30, Thread-level evidence must not be treated as a valid vector without a TS>=30 (Stirring) gate (P-08: Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert against TS<30 NPCs). Here the authored Evidence vector is ordinary fiscal/loyalty data (ledgers, RM-recruitment patterns), which is P-08-clean — but none of the three docs that assign him Evidence state the boundary that Thread-level evidence is inert against him, so a GM could wrongly try to move him with Thread-Diagnosis findings. The canonical §1.3 Evidence row also carries no TS gate of its own.
- **Evidence:** TS 0: designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md:287. Evidence RS: arc_expansion_v30.md:285; designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:278; designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:67 ('his Resonant Style is evidence-responsive'). Gate basis: canon/02_canon_constraints.md (P-08); designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md:471 (TS 0-9 perceives nothing), :451 (TS>=30 bonus). §1.3 table (no gate): npc_behavior_v30.md:36.
- **Fix:** Add a one-line note to Tormann's Evidence RS entries that the moving vector is ordinary/fiscal evidence and that Thread-level evidence is inert against him at TS 0 (P-08; gate at Stirring 30+), mirroring the AUD-NPC2-128 fix pattern.
- **Verifier note:** Verified the facts: TS 0 (arc:287), Primary RS Evidence (arc:285; behavior:278 'Evidence (fiscal data)'; analyses_infill:67 'evidence-responsive'), the §1.3 Evidence row (npc_behavior_v30:36) carries no TS gate, and social_contest_v30:471 ('TS 0-9 perceives nothing') / :451 (practitioner TS>=30 bonus) supply the boundary basis. The finding itself concedes the authored Evidence vector is ordinary fiscal/loyalty data and therefore P-08-clean; it asks only for a one-line clarifying note so a GM does not try to move him with Thread-Diagnosis findings. This is a documentation-hardening suggestion, not a violation. SOFT is correct as filed.

#### AUD-NPC2-185 — The non-sensitive-exposure mechanism, as narrated, imports a drift vector with no canonical grounding (objects -> Coherence drift / sensitisation in TS-0 people)
- **Unit:** Annika Feldhaus | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** P-12 (drift propagation tridimensional through knots) / Foundations §16.1, §16.3, §14 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The framing 'Thread-touched goods produce Coherence effects in non-sensitive households' / 'poisoning her customers' treats inert artefacts as a source of Coherence drift radiating into non-sensitive owners. Canon grounds Coherence-drift propagation in knots to a *drifting practitioner* (§16.3 / P-12), and grounds TS development in felt *confrontation* that exceeds rendering, not mere proximity to objects (§14). There is no canonical 'static Thread-touched object -> Coherence drift in a TS-0 owner' pathway. This is the constraint ED-NEW-3 must satisfy; the canon-respecting lever against a TS-0 actor is ONTICAL (the goods are heretical Einhir-ruin contraband), which the sibling arc already models correctly via Haelgrund pursuing provenance 'regardless of the Thread dimension.'
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:82; canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md:351-357 (§16.3 propagation via knots to a drifting practitioner) and :301 (§14 TS via felt confrontation, 'not intellectual study'); canon/02_canon_constraints.md:21 (P-12 violation test); contrast designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_36_40.md:118 (Haelgrund, TS 0, acts on 'heretical provenance ... regardless of the Thread dimension' — the clean ontical lever).
- **Fix:** When filling ED-NEW-3, route Feldhaus's exposure consequence through the ontical channel (heretical-provenance scandal / Church + Ministry reaction / market collapse) rather than a Coherence/ontological-harm-to-non-sensitives mechanism, OR ground any household effect in an explicit, canon-compatible vector. Soften the 'poisoning her customers' prose so it does not assert a non-canonical drift mechanism.
- **Verifier note:** Verified and not overstated; the finding self-rates SOFT and frames itself as a fill-direction caution, not a live violation. canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md:351-355 (§16.3) grounds Coherence-drift propagation in the knots of a *drifting practitioner* ('a practitioner's drift is not a private matter ... knotted threads carry that alteration'); :301 (§14) grounds TS development in 'felt encounter that exceeds the rendering's capacity ... not intellectual study of reports' - neither supports a static inert-object -> TS-0-owner Coherence pathway. The analyses:82 prose ('poisoning her customers' via object exposure) does assert such a vector. The contrast is accurate: arcs_36_40.md:118 has Haelgrund (TS 0) acting on 'heretical provenance ... regardless of the Thread dimension' - a clean ontical lever. Crucially this is unratified prose tied to an open gap (ED-NEW-3 FLAGGED), so there is no live mechanic to test against P-12's FAIL criterion (canon/02_canon_constraints.md:21). Correctly a SOFT advisory on how ED-NEW-3 should be filled, not a canon violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-186 — Project-rule lists Almud/Vaynard/Himlensendt as superseded placeholders, but they are live across CANONICAL canon (timeline, migration roster, character_canon) — needs adjudication, not a doc edit
- **Unit:** Behavior priority-trees | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** CLAUDE.md / PROJECT NAMING RULE (Solmund; superseded placeholders) vs live corpus usage | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The audit's PROJECT NAMING RULE (from CLAUDE.md) states Vaynard, Himlensendt, Almud are 'superseded/placeholder' names that 'must NOT appear as live canon in v30 docs,' tracked in supersession_register.yaml / placeholder_names.yaml. In fact neither registry lists these character names (placeholder_names.yaml covers only mechanic module names; supersession_register's CR-STRIKE explicitly affirms Vaynard's Reinhardt-coded military-conqueror identity as the canonical anchor). Meanwhile they are used as live canon in the CANONICAL timeline (03_canonical_timeline 'Almud Almqvist ascends', 'marries Lenneth'), the CANONICAL migration roster (PP-685 'Almud', 'Vaynard'), and character_canon_v30. Only the Solmund/Galbados rule is corpus-consistent. So the behavior doc's use of Almud/Vaynard/Himlensendt is consistent with the rest of canon and inconsistent only with the literal CLAUDE.md prohibition.
- **Evidence:** Constraint text (CLAUDE.md naming rule); canon/placeholder_names.yaml:24-195 (mechanic names only, no character names); canon/supersession_register.yaml:51-66 (CR-STRIKE affirms Vaynard as canonical anchor); canon/03_canonical_timeline.md (Almud, Lenneth, Vaynard, Himlensendt as live); conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:41,256 (Almud, Vaynard). Solmund rule honored: npc_behavior_v30 uses 'Solmund' throughout (e.g. :70,:81).
- **Fix:** Escalate to Jordan for adjudication rather than editing the behavior doc unilaterally: either (a) the CLAUDE.md placeholder list is stale and should drop Almud/Vaynard/Himlensendt (corpus already treats them as canonical), or (b) a genuine rename is pending and the registries + timeline + migration roster must all be updated together. The behavior doc should not be 'fixed' in isolation while the canonical timeline and PP-685 still use the same names.
- **Verifier note:** Verified. placeholder_names.yaml is a registry of MECHANIC-MODULE placeholder names (header lines 1-22 describe 'generic-named modules'; entries like VARFELL-MANDATE-ACTION-001); a grep for Vaynard/Almud/Himlensendt/Reinhardt returns only the rename_reason prose of the 'vaynards_hall' module (lines 30-47), which AFFIRMS Vaynard's Reinhardt-coded military-conqueror identity as canonical — no character name is listed as a placeholder to retire. supersession_register.yaml:51-66 (CR-STRIKE) likewise affirms Vaynard as the canonical identity anchor. Meanwhile these names are used as live canon in the (PROVISIONAL-but-treated-canonical) migration roster (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:41 'Almud', :256 'Vaynard') and the behavior doc uses Solmund correctly (e.g. :70, :81). So the behavior doc's usage is corpus-consistent and conflicts only with the audit harness's literal reading of the CLAUDE.md naming rule. Correctly framed as an escalation/adjudication item, not a doc edit. SOFT stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-187 — P-10 'no moral framing of drift' is substantially respected; only minor arc-prose phrasing to watch
- **Unit:** Behavior priority-trees | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-10 (no moral framing of drift) / P-02 / P-04 vs npc_behavior_v30 §5 + §7.1 + §12 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Coherence-drift and Framework-Drift handling is P-10-clean: §4.3 Coherence thresholds and Coherence-0 'Conversion' are mechanical/TS-gated (P-15 aligned), §7.1 Framework Drift is pure stat movement with no moral valence, and §12 affirms 'No NPC is evil ... Arc emergence produces transformation, not corruption.' The only items worth a light touch are evocative arc-prose terms in §5.2 — Vaynard Arc C 'epistemic seduction'/'Consumed', Almud Fortress 'personally hollowed', Himlensendt Arc C 'may become a reformer, a madman, or a saint.' These read as character-state/Coherence descriptions (consistent with the Lacanian-Real framing), not as drift-as-sin or alignment, so they do not rise to a FAIL — but 'seduction'/'madman' sit closest to the moral register and could be hardened to configurational language.
- **Evidence:** npc_behavior_v30.md:497-510 (§4.3 Coherence rule, Conversion at 0); :748-761 (§7.1 drift table, stat-only); :1101 (§12 'transformation, not corruption'); evocative prose at :100-102 (Vaynard 'epistemic seduction'/collapse), :627 (Vaynard Arc C 'Consumed'), behavior_infill :69 ('personally hollowed'), :82 ('reformer, a madman, or a saint'). P-10 / P-02 source canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md:235 ('does not resolve into he was bad all along').
- **Fix:** No required change for compliance. Optional polish: rephrase 'epistemic seduction' / 'madman' toward configurational/rendering language (e.g. 'epistemic destabilisation', 'unintegrated') so the drift register stays unambiguously ontological per P-10/P-02.
- **Verifier note:** Verified. §12 compliance table npc_behavior_v30.md:1101 explicitly states 'No NPC is evil... Arc emergence produces transformation, not corruption.' The flagged evocative terms exist but are defensible: 'epistemic seduction' is a canonical Foundations §16.1 term used mechanically in the §5.0b strain table (npc_behavior_v30.md:520-528), not loose moralizing; 'Consumed'/'Coherence may degrade' (Arc C, :626-627) is configurational/Coherence language; 'personally hollowed' (infill :69) and 'reformer, a madman, or a saint' (infill :82) are character-state descriptions. None frame drift as sin/alignment; npc_character_analyses_v30.md:30 even uses 'epistemic seduction' for seduction-toward-empathy, underscoring it is morally neutral in this corpus. So P-10/P-02/P-04 are not violated; only 'seduction'/'madman' sit near the moral register as optional polish. SOFT (no required change) is right.

#### AUD-NPC2-188 — No TS / Coherence / Spirit authored for Cesare (latent P-08 readiness gap)
- **Unit:** Cesare | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 / character_canon §10.1 (TS axis) — Thread Sensitivity readiness | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Cesare has no Thread Sensitivity, Coherence, or Spirit value anywhere — the migration roster is conviction-only and he is absent from the registry's optional ts/coherence fields and from any NPC sheet. This is not a live violation: no arc, Thread mechanic, or Thread-level evidence is attributed to Cesare, so there is nothing for P-08 to bite on today. But if Cesare is ever promoted from succession_alternate to authored-active, a TS value must be set before any Thread-evidence interaction (P-08 inert-against-TS<30) can be adjudicated, and the timeline establishes Crown figures at TS 0 (Almud, Torben) as the default expectation.
- **Evidence:** Repo-wide grep for 'cesare' intersected with TS/sensitivity/coherence returns only designs/audit/* sim-eval prose, no canon value; references/npc_registry.yaml:12 lists ts/coherence as optional and Cesare has no entry; character_canon_v30.md:393 ('TS reliable; Coherence partial') treats TS as a per-NPC axis; default precedent canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:138,141 (Crown figures TS 0).
- **Fix:** No change required while Cesare remains succession_alternate / not authored-active. On any promotion to authored-active, author TS (default Crown TS 0 unless a deed-soldier exposure rationale is given), Coherence, and Spirit before attaching any Thread-layer interaction.
- **Verifier note:** Accurate and correctly self-limited to latent (not a live violation). My repo grep confirms no TS/Coherence/Spirit value for Cesare anywhere outside designs/audit prose; the migration roster entry (lines 56-69) is conviction-only; npc_registry lists ts/coherence as optional (line 13) and Cesare has no entry at all. character_canon_v30 line 393 treats TS/Coherence/Spirit as per-NPC axes. The finding explicitly concedes nothing for P-08 to bite on today since no Thread arc or Thread-level evidence attaches to Cesare — correct. One minor citation imprecision: the finding cites 'canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:138,141 (Crown figures TS 0)' but only line 138 (Almud 'TS 0') actually carries a TS value; line 141 is Prince Torben with no TS stated. The default-Crown-TS-0 precedent (Almud line 138) still holds, so the substance survives. Latent readiness gap, no current violation. SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-189 — Cesare's basis for competing with the sitting Almqvist king is unauthored (deed-logic permits it in principle)
- **Unit:** Cesare | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** canon/03_canonical_timeline.md (Almqvist bloodline + deed-monarchy succession) vs 'Crown succession alternate' framing | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Cesare is framed as a 'Crown succession alternate' in a rivalry with the reigning King Almud, but the canonical timeline establishes Crown succession through the Almqvist bloodline (Almud, heirs Elske and Torben), and the canonical Key Figures list contains no Cesare/Lorenzo. The deed-monarchy's own logic does leave room for a non-bloodline capability claimant — succession is 'rebuttable' (Coup Counter / Motion of No Confidence) and 'cognatic senior ... capability over gender' — so this is not a hard contradiction. But Cesare's specific claim, his relation (if any) to the Almqvist line, and how he is a 'succession' contender against a sitting king with living heirs are entirely unauthored; the only basis given is 'per sim §1.2' continuity.
- **Evidence:** Framing: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:103,302,552 ('Crown succession competition'); upstream only as sim alternates designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:56 ('Crown succession alternate per sim §1.2'). Canonical succession structure: canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:119 (heirs Elske, Torben), :136-145 (Key Figures list — no Cesare/Lorenzo); deed-logic that permits it: canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:92 ('deed-presumption is rebuttable ... Cognatic senior succession ... capability over gender').
- **Fix:** When Cesare is authored (or promoted), record his succession basis explicitly: deed-capability claimant vs Almqvist cadet/bloodline relation, and the trigger by which he contends (Coup Counter / Motion of No Confidence), so the 'succession alternate' label is grounded in the deed-monarchy rules rather than only in sim continuity.
- **Verifier note:** Accurate and correctly framed as not-a-hard-contradiction. The canonical timeline establishes Almqvist-bloodline succession with heirs Elske and Torben (canon/03_canonical_timeline.md lines 119, 140-141) and a Key Figures list (lines 136-145) that contains no Cesare or Lorenzo; my grep of canon/ confirms zero Cesare/Lorenzo occurrences anywhere in canon. The deed-logic that leaves room for a non-bloodline capability claimant is real: line 92 'the deed-presumption is rebuttable: the Coup Counter and Motion of No Confidence ... Cognatic senior succession ... capability over gender.' Upstream, Cesare's only basis is 'Crown succession alternate per sim §1.2' (migration roster line 56). So his specific claim, any Almqvist relation, and his trigger against a sitting king with living heirs are genuinely unauthored, while deed-logic permits the archetype in principle. Latent authoring gap, not a live violation. SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-190 — Cesare has NO Resonant Style (primary or secondary) authored in any document
- **Unit:** Cesare (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §3 schema line 73 (Resonant Style = required primary+secondary for every named NPC); npc_behavior_v30 §1.3 / ED-516 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The per-NPC schema makes Resonant Style (primary + secondary) a mandatory field for 'Each named NPC' (character_canon §3, line 73). Cesare carries none: he is absent from npc_behavior_v30(+infill), npc_character_analyses_v30(+infill), npc_roster_v30(+infill), npc_foils, and social_contest_v30 (zero hits in all). His only profile (migration roster) authors convictions + self_other but no Resonant Style. Result: the Contest/social engine has no defined argument-form that can reach Cesare, so a Crown-succession-alternate cannot be correctly resolved in a social contest. This is the ethics/resonance-layer specialization of the already-confirmed AUD-NPC2-034 (Cesare absent from behavior roster).
- **Evidence:** Required by designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:73 + §5 line 161 ('Each NPC has a primary and secondary'); ABSENT: grep 'Cesare' returns 0 hits in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md, _infill, npc_character_analyses_v30.md, _infill, npc_roster_v30.md, _infill, npc_foils_v30(_infill), designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md; only profile is designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:56-69 (no Resonant Style field)
- **Fix:** Author Cesare's Resonant Style from the 4-set. Given Authority-primary + Utility-secondary + high Self-Other, the conviction-consistent mapping is Primary=Consequence (Utility 'claims to produce good results' — character_canon §5:166) with Secondary=Authority (defers to recognised binding authority — §5:167). Add to npc_behavior §2 / §5.2 as the succession_alternate stance triangle per character_canon D7.
- **Verifier note:** The factual absence is real: schema line 73 ('Resonant Style — primary + secondary') and §5 line 161 ('Each NPC has a primary and secondary') require it; grep returns 0 Cesare hits in npc_behavior_v30(+infill), npc_character_analyses_v30(+infill), npc_roster_v30(+infill), npc_foils_v30(+infill), and social_contest_v30; the migration block (56-69) has no Resonant Style field. The finding's claim that Cesare appears in NO design doc is INACCURATE — he is substantively authored in npc_relational_graph_v30.md (node NPC-Cesare, rivalry edges E-002/E-003, settlement S-001 Valorsplatz, Cesare<->Almud and Lorenzo<->Cesare canonical rivalries) — though that doc supplies relationships, not a Resonant Style (the combined grep confirmed none of the 17 graph hits carry Resonant Style/Certainty/Belief). Severity overstated: character_canon §11 D7 (line 432) EXPLICITLY records that Cesare 'is not in npc_behavior §2 named-NPC roster' and is to be 'Include[d] as Tier 1/2 succession-alternate entries... Profiles drawn entirely from migration roster + sim continuity', and D1 defers propagation to a separate cycle. The empty sheet field is a documented, intentionally-pending authoring state for a succession_alternate, not an unflagged schema violation. The §5:166/167 fix-mapping (Consequence=Utility, Authority=binding-authority) is sound. Downgraded to SOFT (known/tracked work item).

#### AUD-NPC2-191 — Cesare has NO Certainty value (and no TS), leaving load-bearing arc/crisis mechanics undefined
- **Unit:** Cesare (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §3 schema line 82 (Certainty 0-5 required for every named NPC) + §7.4 + §8 line 321-322 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Certainty (0-5 band, §7.4) is a schema-required field that per-NPC sheets 'always show' so the prose-writer can calibrate without inference (character_canon §7.4 line 305). Cesare has none. This is load-bearing: §8 line 321 ties Certainty movement to 'TS crosses Stirring 30', and Cesare also has no authored Thread Sensitivity anywhere (the migration-roster schema omits TS, and he is absent from every TS-carrying doc) — so neither the TS>=30 Certainty trigger nor a Belief->Scar/Certainty conversion can ever fire. A degenerate interaction also exists: §8 line 322 ('Certainty reaches 0 -> Primary becomes Authority') is a no-op for Cesare because his primary already IS Authority; this should be checked when his Certainty/arc is authored.
- **Evidence:** Required: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:82 (schema), :305 ('always show all four values'), :281-293 (§7.4 band), :321-322 (Certainty triggers); ABSENT for Cesare: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:56-69 (no Certainty, no TS); zero Cesare hits in any TS-bearing doc (npc_behavior/roster/analyses)
- **Fix:** Author a Certainty band (label per §7.4, e.g. Skeptic/Questioning for an instrumentalist courtier) and a TS value when promoting Cesare to a Tier-1/2 succession-alternate sheet. Mark [PROVISIONAL] per §3. Note: confirm the §8 Certainty-0 collapse rule has a sensible effect for an already-Authority-primary NPC.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors verified: character_canon §7.4 line 281 'Certainty (0-5)'; line 305 'Per-NPC sheets in Part B always show all four values (TS / Coherence / Spirit / Certainty)'; §8 line 321 ties Certainty movement to 'TS crosses threshold (Stirring 30 / Active 50)'; line 322 'Certainty reaches 0 -> Primary becomes Authority'. Cesare's migration block (56-69) carries no Certainty and no TS, and the roster schema (21-29) omits both fields entirely. The degenerate-interaction note is correct and well-spotted: line 322's collapse-to-Authority is a no-op because Cesare's primary already IS Authority (roster:61). However, the same D7 context applies: line 432 marks Cesare as a not-yet-promoted succession_alternate whose sheet is pending, and §3 schema is scoped to 'Each named NPC in Part B' — Cesare is not yet a Part B authored sheet. Missing TS/Certainty is the expected pre-authoring state, not a live violation. Downgraded to SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-192 — Cesare has NO Beliefs authored, so no Belief->Scar arc conversion is possible and the prose-writer has no voice anchors
- **Unit:** Cesare (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §3 schema line 66 + §6.1 (1-3 first-person Beliefs required for every named NPC) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The schema requires 1-3 first-person Belief quotes per named NPC; §6.1 calls these the voice anchors the prose-writer consumes and the strings revealed via the Contest Appraise step. Cesare has zero Beliefs anywhere. Consequently the Belief-revision -> permanent Scar -> Conviction-crisis arc machinery (§6.2: revision requires the winning argument to use the NPC's primary/secondary Resonant Style and engage a specific Belief) is wholly inoperable for him — there is no Belief to scar and (per the prior finding) no Resonant Style to target it with.
- **Evidence:** Required: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:66 (schema 'Beliefs 1-3 first-person voice quotes'), :184-200 (§6.1/§6.2 Belief revision -> Scar requires Resonant Style + Belief engagement); ABSENT for Cesare in all docs (designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:56-69 has no Beliefs; 0 hits elsewhere)
- **Fix:** Author 2-3 first-person Beliefs consistent with Authority/Utility + self-aggrandizing self_other (e.g. an instrumental-legitimacy / ends-justify-means statement) when the sheet is promoted, so the Appraise reveal and the Belief->Scar arc path become usable.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors verified: schema line 66 ('Beliefs — 1-3 first-person voice quotes'); §6.1 line 184/190 (Beliefs are the 'voice anchors the prose-writer skill consumes', revealed via Contest Appraise); §6.2 lines 194-200 (Belief revision requires the winning argument to use the NPC's primary/secondary Resonant Style AND engage a specific Belief, producing a permanent Scar). Cesare has zero Beliefs (none in migration block 56-69; 0 hits elsewhere), so the Belief->Scar path is indeed inoperable for him, compounded by the absent Resonant Style. The mechanical reasoning is correct. But this is the same single underlying condition as findings 2 and 3 — Cesare is an unpromoted succession_alternate per D7 (line 432), and the schema's Belief requirement applies to authored Part B sheets. Documented-pending, not an unflagged defect. Downgraded to SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-193 — Secondary Resonant Style (Authority) is labeled 'practically unreachable' in the stat block, while the canonical reachable path lives un-cross-referenced in arc_expansion
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.2 secondary RS vs arc_expansion_v30 Authority trigger path | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The §2.2 note calls Authority 'practically unreachable - but a Cardinal defection or inner-tradition document ... could fire this,' implying it is near-dead.
