But arc_expansion_v30 defines a concrete, canonical Authority-RS path to Himlensendt's Arc B (Cardinal of Temperance / Klapp presents the Thread-Sensitivity-prevalence dataset = 'an authority he must hear'), which is described as 'the canonical path ... that does not require the player to defeat him in direct contest.' The stat block undersells a path the arc layer treats as primary, and the two are not cross-linked. Set-membership is fine (Authority is one of the four ED-516 styles).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.2 L74 ('practically unreachable'); designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md L339, L345 ('Authority RS trigger - the Cardinal of Temperance IS an authority he must hear'; 'canonical path to Himlensendt's Arc B'); npc_behavior_v30_infill.md Arc B L78 (Authority activates as secondary)
- **Fix:** Add a cross-reference from §2.2 Secondary RS row to arc_expansion_v30 (Klapp/Temperance dataset as the canonical Authority trigger) and soften 'practically unreachable' to reflect that an in-Church scholarly authority is the designed route to Arc B.
- **Verifier note:** Both ends reproduced. npc_behavior_v30 §2.2 L74 calls the Authority secondary RS 'Practically unreachable — but a Cardinal defection or inner-tradition document ... could fire this.' arc_expansion_v30.md L339 defines the Klapp/Cardinal-of-Temperance Thread-Sensitivity dataset as the Authority RS trigger ('the Cardinal of Temperance IS an authority he must hear') and L345 explicitly calls the Klapp-treatise route 'the canonical path to Himlensendt's Arc B that does not require the player to defeat him in direct contest.' infill Arc B L78 corroborates ('Authority activates as secondary'). The two are indeed not cross-linked, and the stat block's 'practically unreachable' does undersell a path the arc layer treats as the designed (canonical, non-contest) route to Arc B. This is accurately a SOFT cross-reference/wording improvement, not a contradiction (the stat-block caveat 'a Cardinal defection or inner-tradition document' is in fact gesturing at exactly the Klapp path, so they are reconcilable).

#### AUD-NPC2-194 — Virke's designated canonical sheet file does not exist — every personality-mechanics dimension is orphaned
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/npcs/npcs_special.md (canonical-home pointer) vs arc_expansion_v30.md L15/L20/L566 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** All three Niflhel-strike redirects route Dalla Virke's authored sheet to 'designs/npcs/npcs_special.md'. That file does not exist. The only npcs_special.md in the repo is params/bg/npcs_special.md (a board-game params file) which contains zero Virke/Niflhel content. With no sheet file, she has no Conviction vector, no Resonant Style, no Certainty, and no Beliefs anywhere — this single broken pointer is the root cause of the four dimension-level gaps below.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md L15 ('Dalla Virke -> independent intelligence broker (designs/npcs/npcs_special.md)'), L20, L566 ('Dalla Virke became the canonical independent broker (designs/npcs/npcs_special.md)'); Glob 'designs/npcs/npcs_special*' and 'designs/**/*special*.md' -> only params/bg/npcs_special.md, which Grep 'Virke|Niflhel' returns No matches; designs/npcs/ listing has no npcs_special.md.
- **Fix:** Create designs/npcs/npcs_special.md (or fold Virke into character_canon_v30 Part B) and author her full personality-mechanics sheet, then correct the three arc_expansion_v30.md pointers if the home changes. Until the file exists the redirect is a dangling canonical reference and she cannot be run.
- **Verifier note:** Two facts check out: designs/npcs/npcs_special.md does not exist (Glob found only params/bg/npcs_special.md), and that params file has zero Virke/Niflhel content (Grep: No matches). But the finding's load-bearing premise — that this is Virke's 'designated canonical home' via live 'Niflhel-strike redirects' — is false. BOTH references are in DEAD canon: arc_expansion_v30.md L15 sits inside the '<!-- POST-SESSION-B/C CANONICAL OVERRIDE -->' HTML comment, explicitly under the heading 'DEAD (non-canonical, do not implement)'; L566 sits under a '**STRUCK**' header ('Niflhel dissolved ... dead-on-arrival'). Neither is a live pointer; they are dead-comment prose mentioning a file that was never created. The finding also miscounts: it cites 'L15/L20/L566 ... all three redirects,' but I read L20 — it is the Coup Counter supersession note and contains no npcs_special.md reference. A repo-wide Grep for 'npcs_special' confirms ONLY these two dead references point at designs/npcs/; every other hit points at params/bg/npcs_special.md. Virke's actual live home is npc_roster_v30.md S10 (L125-130), which exists. So 'her sheet file does not exist, therefore every dimension is orphaned, root cause = one broken pointer' collapses: the missing-sheet/missing-dimensions reality is genuine but is exactly what F2/F5/F6 capture; there is no broken live pointer to fix. Downgraded to SOFT — a dangling reference inside already-struck dead canon, not a HIGH structural gap.

#### AUD-NPC2-195 — Roster 'Ethics' label for Virke is a bare disposition tag, not reconciled to a conviction vector and missing the D2 [SUPERSEDED] annotation
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D2 (Ethical Framework labels SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7) + PP-684 §6 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The roster presents 'Ethics: Honour among operators — your word is your network' as a framework-style header. Per D2/PP-684 §6, ethical-framework labels survive only as descriptive disposition tags with a [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] note; this label has no such annotation and is never reconciled to a 13-Conviction vector (it maps cleanly to Honor + Identity/Community). It is not wired to a live Ob modifier, so this is not a hard mechanical-driver violation (hence SOFT) — but it is the only 'ethics' datum she has and it floats free of the taxonomy. It is at least internally consistent across docs (the caste annotation repeats 'your word is your network').
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L130 ('Ethics: Honour among operators — your word is your network'); consistent echo at npc_roster_v30_infill.md L113 ('Her ethics ("your word is your network") apply regardless of caste'); D2 rule character_canon_v30.md §11 L427; alias_registry.yaml registers Honor/Reason/Continuity legacy tags L609-611.
- **Fix:** When authoring her sheet, convert the 'Ethics' line to a descriptive Ethical-Disposition tag with the [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] marker and pair it with the explicit Honor-primary conviction vector it implies.
- **Verifier note:** Verified accurately and the SOFT self-rating is correct. npc_roster_v30.md L130 presents 'Ethics: Honour among operators — your word is your network' as a framework-style header with no [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] marker; the same bare pattern holds for every roster NPC (L49 Vossen, L74 Torsvald, L96 Feldhaus), so it is not reconciled to a 13-Conviction vector. character_canon §11 D2 (L427) is the rule prescribing the descriptive-disposition-tag + [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] treatment, and alias_registry.yaml L600-613 registers Honor (current PP-684 conviction) alongside 'Reason (legacy tag)' and 'Continuity (legacy tag)' as superseded philosophical-tradition labels. Correctly SOFT: D2's annotation is prescribed for the Part B sheets (which don't exist yet), the label drives no live Ob modifier, and it is internally consistent across docs (infill L113 echoes 'your word is your network'). Real, accurate, low-stakes — confirmed at SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-196 — Baralta referred to as 'Hafenmark Duke' in the relational graph; canonical title is Duchess
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** Duchess vs Duke (canonical title) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** One isolated reference titles her 'Hafenmark Duke (Baralta)', whereas she is canonically 'Duchess Inge Baralta' everywhere else. Minor, but a title slip in the headquarters-residency table.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:410 ('Hafenmark Duke (Baralta) -> S-018 Gransol'); canonical title e.g. designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_index.md:28 / designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:229 ('Duchess Inge Baralta').
- **Fix:** Change 'Hafenmark Duke (Baralta)' to 'Hafenmark Duchess (Baralta)' in npc_relational_graph_v30.md:410.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim. npc_relational_graph_v30.md:410 reads 'Hafenmark Duke (Baralta) → S-018 Gransol' in the faction-leader-headquarters derivation list (sandwiched between 'Crown leader (Almud...)' and 'Church Confessor...'). The canonical title is Duchess everywhere else: npc_behavior_v30_index.md:28 ('§2.3 Duchess Inge Baralta'), npc_character_analyses_v30.md:229 ('Duchess Inge Baralta — The Protestant Monarch'), and canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:143 ('Duchess Inge Baralta'). An isolated cosmetic title slip in one residency-rule example line; no mechanical impact. SOFT is correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-197 — Edeyja absent by name from npc_relational_graph_v30; only an abstract "Warden contact" geographic edge exists
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Relational graph coverage | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The relational graph contains no "Edeyja" node; the only Warden-side representation is an impersonal Stillhelm-Watch -> Askeheim-Ruins sub-feature edge. Given she is the named Warden-Chief and the human face of the WC track (per roster Mode Interface), the absence of any named relational edges (e.g., to Vaynard at TK5, to Restoration-affiliated practitioners she has "permitted limited contact," to PC expedition) is a coverage gap — the relationships are described in prose elsewhere but not modeled in the graph.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md (no "Edeyja" match anywhere; only :476 "Stillhelm Watch (Warden contact)" / Askeheim Ruins edge); contrast relationships asserted in designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:54,60 (Dahl Erikssen / Vaynard) and roster Mode bridge designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:230
- **Fix:** Either add Edeyja named relational edges (Vaynard conditional-on-TK5; PC-expedition WC edge; Restoration limited-contact edge) or add an explicit note in the relational graph that Warden relationships are intentionally modeled only as the abstract thread-witnessed sub-feature edge and why.
- **Verifier note:** The factual core is confirmed: grep for 'Edeyja' in npc_relational_graph_v30.md returns no node; the only Warden-side representation is the impersonal Stillhelm-Watch -> Askeheim-Ruins edge at :476 (and the related :415 note). The relationships the finding lists (Vaynard conditional-on-TK5, Dahl Erikssen, PC expedition) do exist as prose in edeyja_npc.md:54,60 and roster :230. But the finding is overstated because the relational graph ALREADY supplies exactly the explicit modeling rationale the finding's own 'fix' offers as an acceptable alternative: line 476 states the Warden tie is 'now correctly modeled as a thread-witnessed sub-feature relationship, not a settlement-edge in the canonical graph' per PP-726, and :415 reiterates 'Wardens lead -> no settlement.' So the omission is a documented, deliberate modeling decision (Wardens have no settlement node; ties are thread-witnessed sub-feature edges), not an unexplained coverage hole. Given the graph is explicitly settlement/geography-scoped and self-documents why Edeyja has no personal node, this is a soft enhancement suggestion at most, not a LOW defect.

#### AUD-NPC2-198 — npc_behavior_v30 §2 wires Ethical-Framework labels as the LIVE mechanical Ob driver with zero [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] annotation (13 NPCs)
- **Unit:** Ethical-frameworks supersession | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D2; PP-684 §6; PP-686 §3.7; SUPERSESSION-PP686-001 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Every named-NPC stance triangle in the CANONICAL npc_behavior_v30.md (header line 16: 'Status: CANONICAL — approved 2026-04-17') carries an 'Ethical Framework' row that ASSIGNS LIVE Ob modifiers (e.g. '-1 Ob on doctrine-aligned action ... +2 Ob') keyed off the framework label. Per character_canon_v30 §11 D2 these labels are SUPERSEDED as mechanical drivers and must be retained only as descriptive tags carrying the explicit note '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]'. No §2 entry carries that note, or any PP-686 reference, or any [SUPERSEDED] marker. The framework labels are presented as the operative Ob source, exactly the violation the audit defines under (a).
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md lines 52 (Almud — Virtue/Crown), 72 (Himlensendt — Faith/Church), 93 (Baralta — Categorical Imperative), 113 (Vaynard — Utility-driven Pragmatism), 134 (Ehrenwall — Martial Honour), 155 (Vossen — Equity Social Contract), 174 (Hann), 237 (Maret Uln hybrid), 255 (Guild Council — Moral Relativism), 305 (Haelgrund — Administrative Proceduralism), plus §2.8 line 195. Confirmed absence: Grep for 'PP-686|\[SUPERSEDED' over the file returns only §1.2 line 30 (the CONVICTION-taxonomy supersession, not the framework one). Controlling decision: C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\character_canon_v30.md line 427 (§11 D2) prescribes the exact annotation string. Supersession C:\Github\ttrpg\canon\supersession_register.yaml lines 105-119 (PP-686-001) lists files_to_recheck = params/* ONLY; npc_behavior_v30.md was never flagged for the framework-Ob recheck (contrast lines 64/102 where it IS flagged for PP-687), so these live modifiers were never reconciled.
- **Fix:** Append '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc]' to each Ethical Framework row in npc_behavior_v30 §2.1-§2.17, demoting the label to a descriptive disposition tag and removing/flagging the inline Ob numbers as non-authoritative (mechanical Ob now = mission + cascade + expectation triad, clamped ±2). Add npc_behavior_v30.md to the PP-686-001 files_to_recheck list in supersession_register.yaml.
- **Verifier note:** The raw facts reproduce: npc_behavior_v30.md (line 6 'CANONICAL — approved 2026-04-17') carries 'Ethical Framework' rows with inline Ob modifiers at lines 52/72/93/113/134/155/174/195/237/255/305, and a case-sensitive Grep for '[SUPERSEDED' over the file returns nothing (the only 'superseded' is the lowercase 9-Conviction note at line 30). BUT the claim that this is a HIGH canon VIOLATION fails. The marker requirement the finding cites is NOT a live constraint binding npc_behavior. character_canon_v30 §11 D2 (line 427), §10.1 (line 385), and §1 (line 49) all scope the '[SUPERSEDED]' marker to 'Per-NPC sheets in PART B' of character_canon ITSELF — and that Part B does not exist ('pending Q1 scope decision', lines 2/439). character_canon is PROVISIONAL ('Does not commit. Lands as artifact for Jordan review', lines 2/6/44). Decisively, D1 and D2 both say 'Do NOT silently regenerate npc_behavior §2' / 'that's a separate propagation cycle' — the source doc is deliberately left unedited. §10.1 line 385 column 'Status pre-consolidation' explicitly DIAGNOSES the existing npc_behavior state as 'labels SUPERSEDED but mechanically active' — a surfaced, tracked migration-lag, not an adjudicated FAIL. The underlying PP-686 framework-Ob supersession is real (alias_registry lines 614-619; supersession_register PP-686-001 lines 105-119), but PP-686-001 scopes files_to_recheck to params/* by design, and the inline §2 Ob numbers are known tracked debt. Real latent debt, correctly characterized as 'CONFLICTED' in canon — not a HIGH violation. Downgraded to SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-199 — npc_behavior_v30 §4.1 / §7.1 / §8 keep Ethical Framework as the operative Ob/decision input with no supersession note
- **Unit:** Ethical-frameworks supersession | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D2; PP-686 §3.7 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Beyond the §2 sheets, the same CANONICAL doc treats 'Ethical Framework' as a live engine input in three more places without the [SUPERSEDED->PP-686] note: the decision procedure ('What does the faction's Ethical Framework favour? Apply Ob modifiers'), the Framework Drift table (per-framework passive mechanical effects keyed to the label), and the BG priority trees ('Framework-aligned opportunity ... -1 Ob'). These propagate the superseded label as the live mechanical driver into decision derivation and faction drift, compounding the §2 violation.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md line 478 (§4.1 Step 1 Institutional Filter — 'Apply Ob modifiers'); lines 750-760 (§7.1 Framework Drift table — Faith/Virtue/Categorical Imperative/Utility-driven Pragmatism/Equity Social Contract/Moral Relativism/Martial Honour each with a mechanical drift effect); line 779 (§8.4 Priority 3 'Ethical Framework ... -1 Ob') and line 858 (§8.5 '-1 Ob (Martial Honour alignment)').
- **Fix:** Add a single normative banner at §4.1 and §7.1 stating framework labels are descriptive only and that Ob/decision modifiers derive from PP-686 §3.7 (triadic, ±2), with the [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] tag; or re-key these rules to the triadic alignment modifiers directly. Keep the labels as flavour.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors reproduce exactly: §4.1 Step 1 'What does the faction's Ethical Framework favour? Apply Ob modifiers' (line 478); §7.1 Framework Drift table keyed to framework labels (Faith/Virtue/Categorical Imperative/Utility-driven Pragmatism/Equity Social Contract/Moral Relativism/Martial Honour, lines 750-760); §8.1 Priority 3 'Framework-aligned opportunity ... Ethical Framework ... -1 Ob' (line 779) and §8.7 line 858 '-1 Ob (Martial Honour alignment)' (the finding cites §8.4/§8.5 but the actual Martial Honour line is the Löwenritter tree §8.7 line 858; minor mislabel). However this rides on the same defective premise as Finding 1: no live constraint requires the [SUPERSEDED->PP-686] note in npc_behavior, and character_canon D2 explicitly defers re-keying to 'a separate cycle.' Note also that §7.1 Framework Drift effects (CI passive, Influence, TK accrual, marker-spread) are NOT the ±2 Ob-modifier mechanic that PP-686 superseded — they are passive faction-drift effects, so 'propagates the superseded Ob driver' overstates §7.1's content. Real tracked debt, not a MEDIUM violation. Downgraded to SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-200 — Vaynard: conviction labels diverge three ways across docs, undercutting any framework-vs-vector check
- **Unit:** Ethical-frameworks supersession | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Audit criterion (b)/(c); conviction_migration_roster_v30 §3 (Vaynard STRUCK); PP-684 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's conviction is stated as 'Reason' (legacy, deprecated) in npc_behavior §2.4, as 'Einhir restoration / Revolutionary competence' (not a 13-Conviction term at all) in npc_foils_v30_infill, and is flagged STRUCK with a clean-redraft default of 'Authority + Honor + Identity' in the migration roster §3. Three incompatible conviction descriptions for one NPC means his 'Consequentialism/Utility-driven Pragmatism' framework tag cannot be validated against a stable vector, and the legacy 'Reason' label appears as live canon without the required [SUPERSEDED->] annotation (it is a deprecated tag per §1.2 / alias_registry).
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md line 111 ('Reason'); C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_foils_v30_infill.md line 102 ('Einhir restoration / Revolutionary competence'); C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\personal\conviction_migration_roster_v30.md lines 255-256 (§3 edge case: 'Vaynard ... STRUCK ... Conviction profile needs clean redraft -> Authority + Honor + Identity'). Deprecated-label rule: npc_behavior_v30.md line 30; references\alias_registry.yaml lines 600-613.
- **Fix:** Land the PP-685 §3 clean redraft (Authority/Honor/Identity) into npc_behavior §2.4 and npc_foils, replacing the legacy 'Reason' and the free-text 'Einhir restoration' with 13-Conviction terms; then revalidate the framework tag against that vector. Flag for Jordan (edge-case review per roster §3).
- **Verifier note:** The three legacy strings reproduce (npc_behavior §2.4 line 111 'Reason'; npc_foils_infill line 102 'Einhir restoration / Revolutionary competence'; migration roster §3 line 256 STRUCK -> 'Authority + Honor + Identity'). But the finding's framing — that this 'undercuts any framework-vs-vector check' and that the clean-redraft target is Authority/Honor/Identity — is superseded. references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-052 (lines 708-714, ACTIVE 2026-05-08) ratifies Vaynard as Equity 0.35 + Utility 0.30 and states verbatim 'PP-685 §3 mapping (Authority/Honor/Identity) superseded per Jordan 2026-05-08.' So the migration-roster default the finding wants propagated is itself dead canon, and the registry value RECONCILES the divergence: Utility = the 'Consequentialism/Utility-driven Pragmatism' framework, Equity = 'Einhir revival idealism' (the 'Einhir restoration' free-text). The 'Reason' legacy tag at behavior line 111 is flagged deprecated at the doc level (line 30) and in alias_registry (line 610); the missing inline marker is the same non-binding issue as Finding 1. Residual real issue: behavior §2.4 still shows stale legacy 'Reason' rather than the ratified Equity/Utility vector — a genuine propagation-lag worth fixing, but the conviction is RESOLVED in canon, not three-way-open. Downgraded to SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-201 — Magnus Vaynard: ethical-framework label inconsistent across docs ('Utility-driven Pragmatism' vs 'Consequentialism')
- **Unit:** Ethical-frameworks supersession | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** Audit criterion (c); PP-684 §6 framework-label consistency | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** One NPC carries two different framework labels in two design docs: npc_behavior §2.4 names his Ethical Framework 'Utility-driven Pragmatism (Varfell)', while npc_foils_v30_infill labels his Ethics 'Consequentialism'. These are not stated as aliases and neither carries a supersession/cross-ref note, so a reader gets divergent framework canon for Vaynard depending on which doc they open (criterion c). The drift rides on top of an already-unstable conviction profile (see related note), increasing the reconciliation cost.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md line 113 ('Utility-driven Pragmatism (Varfell)') vs C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_foils_v30_infill.md line 102 ('Ethics: Consequentialism').
- **Fix:** Pick one canonical descriptive tag for Vaynard (Utility-driven Pragmatism and Consequentialism are near-synonyms — collapse to one) and apply it in both docs with the [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7] marker, or register them as aliases in references/alias_registry.yaml.
- **Verifier note:** Both labels reproduce: npc_behavior_v30 §2.4 line 113 'Utility-driven Pragmatism (Varfell)' and npc_foils_v30_infill line 102 'Ethics: Consequentialism'. They are near-synonyms (the finding concedes this) and both map to the ratified Utility conviction (npc_registry NPC-052 line 711: Utility 0.30). Two mitigations the finding ignores: (1) npc_foils_v30_infill is Status: DESIGN (line 6), and the 'Consequentialism' label sits in PART TWO 'SUBJECTIVE PERSPECTIVES' which line 63 explicitly frames as in-character views 'not objective assessments' — it is descriptive prose, not a mechanical framework-assignment field colliding with §2.4. (2) The PART ONE foil text (line 58) already glosses Vaynard as 'consequentialist pragmatism', tying the two terms together in the same doc. Minor descriptive-label drift worth a tidy-up, not a MEDIUM naming violation. Downgraded to SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-202 — 'Meritocratic' ethics label is non-canonical and contradicts the registry's 'No prior legacy label'
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 13-Conviction taxonomy + npc_registry NPC-009 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The roster gives Strand the ethical label 'Meritocratic.' This is not one of the canonical 13 Convictions (PP-684) and not a recognized legacy ethical-tradition label; it appears nowhere else in the repo. It also directly contradicts npc_registry NPC-009, whose conviction migration_notes state 'No prior legacy label' and assign Authority+Utility. So Strand carries a stray, untyped conviction label in the roster that the registry explicitly denies exists.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51 ('Ethics: Meritocratic'); grep 'Meritocratic' across repo = only this file; designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:29-41 (13 Convictions; no 'Meritocratic'); references/npc_registry.yaml:233 (NPC-009 migration_notes: 'No prior legacy label. Inferred from role... Authority + Utility')
- **Fix:** Either drop 'Meritocratic' as a label and replace with the registry's canonical conviction wording (Utility-led, with the meritocratic conviction expressed as prose), or, if 'Meritocratic' is intended as a descriptive disposition tag, mark it [SUPERSEDED]/descriptive per character_canon §1 and reconcile the registry's 'No prior legacy label' note.
- **Verifier note:** Verified: 'Meritocratic' is not among the 13 canonical Convictions (conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:29-41) and grep shows it appears as a label only in npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51 (plus derivative audit docs); line 112 uses 'meritocratic' as ordinary prose. So the 'non-canonical label' observation is real. But the contradiction framing is wrong. character_canon_v30.md:49 explicitly states the framework RETAINS non-canonical ethical-tradition labels as 'descriptive disposition tags' with [SUPERSEDED] markers — i.e., a prose ethics descriptor is permitted, it just should carry the marker. And the registry's 'No prior legacy label' (npc_registry.yaml:233) is a migration-traceability note meaning Strand (a New NPC) had no entry in the legacy 7-/9-Conviction source to migrate from; it is in a different register from a prose disposition descriptor and does not 'explicitly deny [the label] exists.' The two statements do not collide. What remains is a minor housekeeping item: the roster's 'Ethics: Meritocratic' lacks the [SUPERSEDED]/descriptive marker the framework prescribes. That is SOFT, not a LOW contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-203 — Registry assigns Authority 0.30 co-primary, in tension with Strand's anti-hereditary-authority psychology
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-009 + PP-684 taxonomy | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The registry derives Strand's convictions as Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30. Utility fits (effectiveness/results). Authority ('deference to and assertion of legitimate command; auctoritas/imperium') sits awkwardly with his canonical interiority: he resents 'the principle that birth outweighs ability' and is 'more loyal to the founding logic than the founding family' — i.e., his frame is competence-over-command-legitimacy, closer to Liberty/Equity than Authority. The primary weights also sum to exactly 0.60, the floor of the 0.6-0.8 band.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml:227-233 (Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30; sum 0.60); designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:30 (Authority def), :35 (Liberty), :34 (Equity), :133 (primary band 0.6-0.8); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51,112 (anti-hereditary-authority meritocratic conviction)
- **Fix:** Re-examine whether the co-primary should be Liberty or Equity rather than Authority (Utility likely remains the dominant frame), and consider raising the primary weight sum off the 0.60 floor. Coordinate with whoever ratifies the migration roster.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_registry.yaml:229-230 sets primary Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30 (sum 0.60), which is exactly the floor of the 0.6-0.8 primary band stated at conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:133. Strand's canonical interiority (npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51,112) is competence-over-lineage and resentment of 'the principle that birth outweighs ability' / 'more loyal to the founding logic than the founding family' — which sits awkwardly with Authority defined as 'deference to and assertion of legitimate command' (taxonomy:30), and arguably fits Liberty (:35) or Equity (:34) better. This is a legitimate, appropriately hedged design-judgment observation, not a hard canon violation — and the registry's own migration_notes flag the assignment as 'Inferred,' so it is explicitly provisional and ratification-gated. SOFT is the correct severity; confirmed at that level.

#### AUD-NPC2-204 — Conviction vector exists only in the registry, not the roster — split-brain primary source
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 §4 (migration process) — roster vs npc_registry.yaml NPC-009 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-009 assigns Strand a conviction vector (Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30, valorian_court, self_other -0.10) with migration_notes 'No prior legacy label... Per PP-685 §4.' But PP-685 §4 step 5 explicitly instructs 'Append entry to this roster as Class B extension.' The registry performed steps 1-4 (chose convictions) but skipped step 5 (never appended to the roster). The result: the operative conviction vector lives in a reference YAML while the designated-primary canonical doc (the roster) denies he has one. Any consumer following D1 (read the roster first) finds nothing; any consumer reading the registry gets Authority/Utility. The two canonical conviction stores disagree on whether Strand even has a vector.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml:227-233 (NPC-009 convictions: Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30; migration_notes 'Per PP-685 §4'); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:264-282 (§4 process; step 5 = 'Append entry to this roster as Class B extension'); :39-84 (no Strand entry).
- **Fix:** Make the registry vector and the roster agree: either promote NPC-009's Authority+Utility vector into roster §2.1 as the Class B entry the §4 process requires, or, if the roster is meant to be the only conviction store, move the vector there and have the registry reference it. Do not leave a derived vector in the registry that the primary doc contradicts.
- **Verifier note:** Confirmed the two anchors: npc_registry.yaml:227-233 holds Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30 with migration_notes 'Per PP-685 §4'; conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:272 step 5 = 'Append entry to this roster as Class B extension,' and the Crown §2.1 block (lines 39-84) has no Strand entry. So §4 step 5 was indeed not performed — that part is real. But the framing 'the two canonical conviction stores disagree on whether Strand even has a vector' is false: the roster nowhere asserts Strand lacks a vector; its 13-entry table is scoped to legacy-tagged characters (§2 line 37) and §4 explicitly routes new characters elsewhere. A D1 consumer reads the roster 'when sources conflict' for VALUES — there is no conflicting value to adjudicate, because only the registry carries one. This is a process-completeness nit (an un-run append step), not a contradiction between canonical stores. SOFT, not MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-205 — 'Meritocratic' ethics label and the registry's 'No prior legacy label' / Authority-Utility vector contradict each other
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / D2 — framework label must not contradict the conviction vector | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Two canonical statements about Strand's ethics conflict head-on. roster_infill asserts an ethics label ('Meritocratic'); registry NPC-009 asserts 'No prior legacy label' and derives Authority 0.30 + Utility 0.30. A framework label that the conviction source says does not exist is a D2 violation (framework label contradicting the conviction vector). Additionally the chosen Authority co-primary sits in tension with the very psychology the label encodes: Strand's drama (roster_infill:51, :112) is resentment of hereditary AUTHORITY and the principle that birth outweighs ability — i.e. anti-Authority — yet Authority is made a co-primary conviction. Liberty or Equity would track 'ability over lineage / freedom from imposed hereditary authority' far better than Authority.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51,112 (anti-hereditary-authority meritocratic conviction) vs references/npc_registry.yaml:229-233 (Authority 0.30 co-primary; 'No prior legacy label'); taxonomy candidates designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:35 (Liberty = freedom from imposed authority), :34 (Equity = fairness across station), :33 (Utility). (Prior audit AUD-NPC2-075, CONFIRMED.)
- **Fix:** Reconcile in one pass: drop or [SUPERSEDED]-annotate 'Meritocratic', and re-examine the co-primary — Utility likely stays dominant, but the second primary should probably be Liberty or Equity (ability-over-lineage), not Authority. Whatever is chosen must be stated identically in the roster entry (per the gap above) and registry NPC-009.
- **Verifier note:** This is AUD-NPC2-075 from the 2026-06-22 audit, where it was filed as category 'note,' CONFIRMED as SOFT with explicit verifier finding 'no canon FAIL' (audit lines 755-760). The finding here re-frames it up to MEDIUM and asserts a 'D2 violation (framework label contradicting the conviction vector).' I reject the contradiction/violation framing: npc_registry.yaml:233 'No prior legacy label' refers to migrated legacy ethical-tradition tags (the thing PP-685 migrates), not to the infill prose descriptor — so a prose word 'Meritocratic' and the note 'no prior legacy tag to migrate' are about different layers and do not collide. The substantive half — that Authority (taxonomy:30 'deference to/assertion of legitimate command') sits awkwardly with Strand's anti-hereditary-authority interiority (npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51,112), and that Liberty (:35) or Equity (:34) would track better — is a real but SOFT judgment call about a deferred/migratable vector (weights sum to 0.60, the band floor per taxonomy:133, which is valid). No D2 violation; SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-206 — Docs disagree on WHAT Strand's conviction is (caste-transgressive Einhir conviction vs meritocratic/Utility) and on his caste/background
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-doc conviction/caste consistency — faction_politics vs analyses/registry | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Two incompatible characterizations of Strand's conviction coexist as canon. (a) faction_politics_v30 frames his conviction as caste-transgressive: 'Strand is Northern Einhir by background but adopts a Conviction that aligns with Southern Einhir or RM politics,' driving §3.3 Scar accumulation; arcs_10_18 extends this (RM Influence Debt → he 'imports southern Einhir administrative practices'). (b) npc_character_analyses / roster_infill / registry frame his conviction as Meritocratic / Utility-Authority with NO caste valence at all — and roster_infill calls him a 'Minor lord of Feldmark (T5),' not Northern Einhir. So one branch makes his conviction an Einhir caste-transgression; the other makes it caste-neutral meritocracy, and the background facts (Northern Einhir vs Feldmark minor lord) are themselves unreconciled. A named conviction never appears in either branch to settle it.
- **Evidence:** Caste-transgressive framing: designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:683 ('Northern Einhir by background but adopts a Conviction that aligns with Southern Einhir or RM'); designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md:459 ('imports southern Einhir administrative practices'). Caste-neutral/meritocratic framing: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:48 ('Minor lord of Feldmark (T5)'), :51 ('Meritocratic'); references/npc_registry.yaml:222-224 (birthplace null, territory null, ts null — no caste/Einhir coding).
- **Fix:** Decide and state Strand's caste/background once (Feldmark minor lord vs Northern Einhir) and whether his conviction is a caste-transgressive Einhir-aligned conviction or caste-neutral meritocracy, then make faction_politics §3.3/§3.6.2, the arcs Einhir-import beat, the analyses/roster prose, and registry NPC-009 (birthplace/territory) all agree. The named conviction chosen in the roster/registry fix above should be the thing both branches reference.
- **Verifier note:** Verified the two framings: faction_politics_v30.md:683 ('Northern Einhir by background but adopts a Conviction that aligns with Southern Einhir or RM') + arcs_10_18.md:459 ('imports southern Einhir administrative practices') vs npc_roster_v30_infill.md:48 ('Minor lord of Feldmark (T5)') / :51 'Meritocratic' / registry birthplace+territory null. But the 'background facts unreconciled' half largely dissolves on context: Feldmark is T5, the Crown-heartland breadbasket in the NORTH (arcs_41_45.md:203 'T5 Feldmark, the breadbasket… the populated north'; :209 'Strand… mobilises the reserve from T5 Feldmark'; session_log_archive_part_2.md:154 'Gerik Strand… vassal landowner from Feldmark'). So 'Feldmark minor lord' and 'Northern by background' agree on the north — they are not contradictory facts. The genuine residue is a prose-framing nuance about whether his conviction reads as caste-transgressive (Einhir-aligned) or caste-neutral meritocracy; these can coexist as the same anti-hereditary/founding-deed drive (npc_roster_v30.md:112 'more loyal to the founding logic than the founding family'), and the registry vector is an explicit deferred default. Real Tier-2 cross-doc tidy-up, not a hard contradiction. SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-207 — 'Meritocratic' used as a bare live ethics label — non-canonical and missing the [SUPERSEDED] annotation D2 requires
- **Unit:** Gerik Strand (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 / alias_registry — Ethical Framework supersession | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_roster_v30_infill labels Strand 'Ethics: Meritocratic.' Per character_canon §11 D2, ethical-framework labels are SUPERSEDED as mechanical drivers (mechanical role → PP-686 §3.7) and may be retained ONLY as descriptive disposition tags carrying a [SUPERSEDED → PP-686 §3.7] note. 'Meritocratic' carries no such annotation. Worse, 'Meritocratic' is neither one of the 13 Convictions nor a recognized legacy ethical-tradition label in alias_registry — it appears as a live ethics label only in this file (plus a downstream arc-analysis echo). It reads as a live disposition driver, not an annotated descriptive tag.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51 ('Ethics: Meritocratic — competence should determine standing'); :112 ('Strand's meritocratic conviction'); echo in designs/arcs/gm_ref/arc_narrative_analysis.md:234. Constraint: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:427 (D2), :49 (legacy ethical labels retained as descriptive tags WITH [SUPERSEDED] markers); designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:29-41 (13 Convictions — no 'Meritocratic'). (Prior audit AUD-NPC2-074, DOWNGRADED.)
- **Fix:** Re-render as 'Disposition (descriptive): meritocratic [SUPERSEDED → mechanical role = PP-686 §3.7; conviction vector = Utility/Authority per NPC-009]', or drop the label and express the meritocratic drive as prose only. Either way add the [SUPERSEDED] marker and stop presenting 'Meritocratic' as a live ethics field.
- **Verifier note:** Independently verified: 'Meritocratic' is not among the 13 Convictions (conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:29-41) and not in alias_registry.yaml's philosophical_tradition_legacy list (lines 600-613: Categorical Imperative/Virtue/Faith/Scholastic/Equity/Honor/Reason/Continuity) — so it is genuinely non-canonical as a label, and it appears as a live 'Ethics:' line only at npc_roster_v30_infill.md:51 (with a prose echo at :112 and an arc-analysis echo at arc_narrative_analysis.md:234). HOWEVER this exact item was already adjudicated by the 2026-06-22 audit as AUD-NPC2-074 and DOWNGRADED to SOFT (the finding itself cites that). I confirm that reasoning: every roster NPC carries a free-text 'Ethics:' descriptor drawn from outside the 13 (npc_roster_v30_infill.md:46 Almstedt 'Procedural', :61 Laskaris 'Oikonomia', :67 Solberg 'Institutional loyalty', :73 Tormann 'Stewardship') — these are prose dispositions, not conviction-vector entries. D2 (character_canon_v30.md:427, :49) requires the [SUPERSEDED] marker on RECOGNIZED legacy ethical-tradition labels; 'Meritocratic' is not one, so D2's marker rule does not cleanly bind it. Real cleanliness nit, no violation. SOFT, matching the prior adjudication; the MEDIUM here is inflated.

#### AUD-NPC2-208 — Ehrenwall's character-analysis body is an empty stub in the main doc; all texture lives only in the infill
- **Unit:** Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_character_analyses_v30.md §11 vs _infill §11; character_canon_v30.md line 43 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_character_analyses_v30.md §11 ('Grandmaster Ehrenwall — The Ledger That Never Forgives') is a header with an empty body. character_canon_v30 itself flags this ('Does not invent texture for empty-body sections (... Ehrenwall character_analyses entries ...)'). The full characterization exists only in npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md §11. Same skeleton/infill split the prior audit flagged for Solberg (AUD-NPC2-080); fine if the infill convention is intended, but the main doc reads as a gap and a reader consulting only the canonical (non-infill) analyses doc gets nothing.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:273-276 (§11 header, empty body); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:117-119 (§11 full body present); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:43 (notes Ehrenwall character_analyses entry as empty-body [GAP]).
- **Fix:** Either merge the infill §11 body into the main analyses doc, or add an explicit pointer banner at §11 directing to the infill, consistent with whatever the project's skeleton/infill convention requires.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_character_analyses_v30.md:273 ('11. Grandmaster Ehrenwall — The Ledger That Never Forgives') is a header with an empty body (lines 274-276 blank); the full body is present only at npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:117-119; and character_canon_v30.md:43 explicitly lists the Ehrenwall character_analyses entry among empty-body sections it surfaces as [GAP]. The defect is real but it is the documented skeleton/infill convention, explicitly acknowledged and tracked (character_canon D4 + line 43; also flagged in 2026-05-08-character-generation-audit.md:45). The directly parallel precedent AUD-NPC2-080 (Solberg, identical main-empty/infill-full split) was CONFIRMED at SOFT, not LOW. Aligning to that precedent: SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-209 — 'Protective consequentialism' framework label carries no [SUPERSEDED] marker
- **Unit:** Halvar Brandt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 (legacy ethical-framework labels retained ONLY as descriptive tags WITH [SUPERSEDED] markers; PP-684 §6 -> PP-686 §3.7) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Brandt's roster 'Ethics:' line reads 'Protective consequentialism' — a legacy ethical-framework label. Per character_canon §11 D2, such labels are superseded as mechanical drivers and may be retained only as descriptive disposition tags WITH a [SUPERSEDED] marker. The roster_infill carries the label with no marker (the entire file has zero [SUPERSEDED]/PP-686 annotations). In this doc the 'Ethics:' field is descriptive prose (the live mechanical layer here is the 'Behavioral AI' flaw, not an Ob calc), so this is a marker-hygiene defect, not a live-driver violation — hence SOFT. (The genuinely live framework-driver violation in the corpus is Almud's 'Ethical Framework | Virtue (Crown)' Ob row in npc_behavior §2.1, but that is a different NPC and out of scope for this unit.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md §6 L38 ('**Ethics:** Protective consequentialism — the peninsula must survive...'). Marker requirement: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 L49 ('Legacy ethical-tradition labels... SUPERSEDED by PP-684 §6... Per-NPC sheets retain legacy labels as descriptive disposition tags with [SUPERSEDED] markers'). Ob-driver supersession: character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 L50; references/alias_registry.yaml ethical_framework_modifier L614-619. No markers in file: grep 'SUPERSEDED|PP-686' on npc_roster_v30_infill.md returns none.
- **Fix:** Annotate the line as 'Protective consequentialism [SUPERSEDED->PP-686 §3.7; descriptive disposition tag only]' (and apply the same marker convention to the other unmarked 'Ethics:' lines in this file).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced verbatim: npc_roster_v30_infill.md §6 L38 '**Ethics:** Protective consequentialism — the peninsula must survive...'. Marker requirement confirmed at character_canon_v30 §11 D2 (L427) and §1 supersession note L49 ('Per-NPC sheets retain legacy labels as descriptive disposition tags with [SUPERSEDED] markers'). No [SUPERSEDED]/PP-686 annotation appears on the line (or anywhere in the infill file). The auditor's own scoping is correct and honest: in this doc the 'Ethics:' field is descriptive prose and the live mechanical layer is the EXTERNAL THREAT FIXATED Behavioral AI (roster_infill L87, roster L87), not an Ob calc — so this is marker-hygiene, correctly SOFT, and explicitly distinguished from the genuine live-driver violation (Almud's Virtue Ob row in npc_behavior §2.1 L52), which is out of scope. The defect is a whole-file convention gap (Edeyja L14, Maret L18, Haelgrund L27 etc. all unmarked) rather than Brandt-specific, but the finding acknowledges this and the severity stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-210 — Almud's conviction text is acknowledged in-ledger as an 'archetype placeholder' — provenance flag, not yet reconciled to the 13-set
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-082 (editorial_ledger); CONVICTION provenance | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** ED-082 ('Almud BG Conviction text') resolved with 'Amendment2 text confirmed as archetype placeholder. Mechanical function unchanged.' This corroborates that the Order/Reason stat-block text predates the PP-684/PP-685 migration and was knowingly left as placeholder. It explains the divergence from the roster but means the live §2.1 conviction text should not be treated as settled canon — it is flagged placeholder yet is the value the engine-facing stance triangle still exposes.
- **Evidence:** canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl ED-082 line 82; live placeholder text at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.1 lines 50-51
- **Fix:** When reconciling the conviction vector (see HIGH conviction finding), explicitly close ED-082 by replacing the placeholder Order/Reason text with the PP-685 13-Conviction value, and note ED-082 as resolved-by-migration in the stat block.
- **Verifier note:** Verified verbatim: editorial_ledger.jsonl line 82 — {'id': 'ED-082', 'status': 'resolved', 'description': 'Almud BG Conviction text', 'decision': 'Amendment2 text confirmed as archetype placeholder. Mechanical function unchanged.'}. This corroborates that the §2.1 Order/Reason conviction text is a knowingly-retained archetype placeholder predating the PP-684/PP-685 migration, which is exactly why it diverges from the migrated roster/registry value. Accurate, correctly scoped as a SOFT provenance note rather than a violation. Confirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-211 — Two different authored inspirations for Almud across docs (Manuel I Komnenos vs Wars-of-the-Roses Lancastrian)
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** CONVICTION/INSPIRATION consistency (character_canon §10.3) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** character_canon_v30 §10.3 lists Almud's inspiration as 'Wars of the Roses Lancastrian deed-claim under structural assault,' while npc_character_analyses_v30_infill gives the historical anchor 'Manuel I Komnenos' (the six-boards-at-once Byzantine emperor). These are different analytic anchors feeding the prose-writer's inspiration field. Not a mechanical violation, but the inspiration dimension is inconsistent for one NPC, which the constraint flags under cross-doc consistency for prose-feedstock fields.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md §10.3 line 408 (Lancastrian); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md line 77 (Manuel I Komnenos)
- **Fix:** Pick one primary historical_parallel (or explicitly record both as layered anchors with distinct facets) so the prose-writer inspiration field is single-valued per character_canon §10.3 pattern.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. character_canon_v30 §10.3 line 406 gives Almud's historical_parallel as 'Wars of the Roses Lancastrian deed-claim under structural assault'; npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md line 77 gives 'Historical anchor: Manuel I Komnenos' (the six-boards-at-once Byzantine emperor). Two distinct analytic anchors feed the prose-writer inspiration field for one NPC. Not a mechanical violation; correctly filed SOFT as a cross-doc prose-feedstock inconsistency. Confirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-212 — Role-frame mismatch: the PRIMARY conviction source files Almud as 'Crown Captain', not as the reigning King
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1; role labeling | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The designated primary source (PP-685) heads Almud's entry 'Almud (Crown Captain, currently Crown leader per sim continuity)' with the rationale 'public-spirited Captain archetype,' and npc_registry/ faction_canon echo 'canonical Crown Captain per sim continuity §1.2.' The audited identity, however, is the reigning monarch ('King Almud Almqvist', Crown sovereign). The Virtue/Authority weighting and self_other −0.10 were authored for a Captain archetype, not a 27-year deed-monarch. This is a provenance/role-frame mismatch that may mean the primary conviction value needs re-validation against the King role rather than blind adoption.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.1 line 41 + notes lines 50-54 ('Captain archetype'); contrast King role at designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md line 379 and designs/territory/valoria_political_hierarchy_v30.md line 33
- **Fix:** During reconciliation, confirm the Virtue 0.45/Authority 0.30 + self_other −0.10 values are intended for the King (sovereign) and not a stale Captain-era profile; if the role was retconned Captain→King, re-validate the weights and self_other against the monarch archetype and update the roster note.
- **Verifier note:** Verified. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.1 line 41 heads the entry 'Almud (Crown Captain, currently Crown leader per sim continuity)' and line 52 gives the rationale 'public-spirited Captain archetype' for the self_other −0.10; npc_registry.yaml NPC-020 line 354/356 and faction_canon_v30.md line 379 echo 'canonical Crown Captain per sim continuity §1.2' (faction_canon even renders it 'King Almud Almqvist (canonical Crown Captain...)'). The audited identity is the 27-year reigning monarch (valoria_political_hierarchy_v30.md line 33). So the Virtue/Authority weighting + self_other −0.10 were authored against a Captain archetype, a provenance/role-frame mismatch worth re-validating against the King role. Correctly SOFT. Confirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-213 — Crown succession contender Lorenzo has a full conviction profile but no behavioral specification (stance triangle / Resonant Style / arc / priority-tree presence)
- **Unit:** Lorenzo | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §2 / §5.2 / Crown priority tree (vs conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 + npc_relational_graph_v30); character_canon_v30 §11 D7 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Lorenzo is treated as a canonical Crown succession claimant (relational graph names a 'Lorenzo <-> Cesare alternative-succession-pair') and carries a full conviction profile, yet he has no stance triangle, Resonant Style, arc map, or any presence in the Crown priority tree in npc_behavior_v30 — the doc that owns behavior. character_canon §11 D7 explicitly recommends including him as a succession-alternate entry, but that authoring has not landed. A canonical succession contender therefore cannot be run by the engine. This overlaps the standing audit's AUD-NPC2-034 (CONFIRMED MEDIUM); cited here so it is not double-counted but recorded as a live Lorenzo gap.
- **Evidence:** Profile exists: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84. Recommended-but-unauthored: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:432 (D7) + Part B 'pending Q1 scope decision' (lines 2, 439). Absence in behavior doc: no Lorenzo entry in designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2/§5.2/§7.10/§8.3 (confirmed by standing audit designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:465-470, AUD-NPC2-034).
- **Fix:** Per D7, author a Tier-2 succession-alternate stance block for Lorenzo in npc_behavior §2/§5.2 (primary Convictions Virtue/Scholastic/Authority, Resonant Style primary+secondary, status succession_alternate per sim §1.2) and reference him in the Crown dynasty-management branch of the priority tree.
- **Verifier note:** Factually reproduced but double-counted by the finding's own admission, which caps its weight. CONFIRMED facts: conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84 gives Lorenzo a full conviction profile; grep of designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md returns zero hits for lorenzo/cesare, so he has no stance triangle / Resonant Style / arc / Crown-priority-tree presence in the doc that owns behavior; character_canon_v30.md:432 (D7) recommends authoring him and line 439 marks Part B 'pending Q1 scope decision.' BUT the finding itself states this 'overlaps the standing audit AUD-NPC2-034 (CONFIRMED MEDIUM); cited here so it is not double-counted.' I verified AUD-NPC2-034 at designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:465-470 — same gap (Cesare AND Lorenzo absent from behavior §2/§5.2/§7.10 and the Crown tree), already adjudicated CONFIRMED MEDIUM. Re-filing an already-confirmed audit item against the same evidence is not an independent defect and should not be re-scored at full weight; hence SOFT (advisory/non-additive). It is also a deferred-authoring state on a PROVISIONAL profile, not a canon violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-214 — Forward-looking P-08 gate: if Lorenzo's eventual sheet sets Primary = Evidence, it must carry the TS>=30 Thread-evidence gate and an authored TS
- **Unit:** Lorenzo (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 / npc_behavior_v30 §5.3 / social_contest_v30 §C2-0 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** His Scholastic-weighted vector makes Evidence the natural Primary Resonant Style. P-08 requires that Thread-level evidence is epistemically INERT against a TS<30 NPC unless TS crosses Stirring (30+). Lorenzo currently has NO authored TS anywhere, so this is not yet a live violation (no Evidence vector is asserted) — but it becomes one the moment a sheet lists Evidence as a valid vector without also stating his TS and gating Thread-level evidence behind TS>=30. Flagging now so the gate is built in at authoring time rather than retrofitted.
- **Evidence:** P-08 (Thread-level evidence epistemically inert vs TS<30); the existing gate pattern for Evidence-Primary NPCs at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:339 (TS>=30 deep-archive recognition) and designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md:467 (C2-0: adjudicator with TS>=30 may treat Thread technique as evidence); no TS value for Lorenzo in any grepped file (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84 has no TS field).
- **Fix:** When authoring the sheet: (a) state Lorenzo's TS explicitly (a court-scholar succession alternate with no Thread pathway noted plausibly sits TS<30), and (b) if Primary = Evidence, scope it to mundane/documentary evidence and gate Thread-level evidence behind TS>=30 per P-08 / social_contest §C2-0. Do not present Thread-level evidence as a live vector against him while TS<30.
- **Verifier note:** Correctly scoped as forward-looking and explicitly NOT a live violation ('no Evidence vector is asserted'). The P-08 TS>=30 Thread-evidence gate pattern is verified: npc_behavior_v30.md:339 (TS>=30 deep-archive recognition of Thread data; TS 10-29 cannot decode; TS 0-9 sees nothing) and social_contest_v30.md:467 (C2-0: adjudicator with TS>=30 may treat Thread technique as relevant evidence). No TS value exists for Lorenzo in any grepped file (roster entry 71-84 has no TS field). One minor characterization point: line 339 is the deep-archive interpretation mechanic rather than an NPC-stance 'Evidence-Primary gate' per se, but it is a legitimate TS>=30 Thread-evidence gate and the finding cites it generically as the gate pattern. As a SOFT design-time reminder to build the gate in at authoring rather than retrofit, it is accurate and appropriately low-weight.

#### AUD-NPC2-215 — Stale pre-PP-686 phrasing of the succession trigger ('Loyalty 0 + Mandate 0') vs canonical 'Loyalty 0 + L 0 + PS 0'
- **Unit:** Maret Uln | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-486 (riskbreakers_identity) vs npc_roster §2 / npc_behavior §2.10 elimination condition | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Roster and behavior state the Varfell-succession trigger as 'Vaynard eliminated (Loyalty 0 + Mandate 0)'; the canonical PP-486 spec states 'Loyalty 0 + L 0 + PS 0 (full institutional + populist collapse)'. Under PP-686 v2, Mandate = round(0.5*Legitimacy + 0.5*Popular_Support), so Mandate 0 implies both L 0 and PS 0 — mechanically near-equivalent, not a behavioral contradiction. But the 'Mandate' shorthand is the pre-PP-686 derived-stat phrasing and should be updated for consistency with the decomposed L+PS canon.
- **Evidence:** params/factions/riskbreakers_identity.md:210 ('Loyalty 0 + L 0 + PS 0 — full institutional + populist collapse'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:240 ('Vaynard eliminated (Loyalty 0 + Mandate 0)'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:231 ('Loyalty 0 + Mandate 0, per PP-486'); params/bg/core.md:160 (Mandate = round(0.5*Legitimacy + 0.5*Popular_Support))
- **Fix:** Update roster §2/Mode-Interface and behavior §2.10 to 'Loyalty 0 + L 0 + PS 0' to match PP-486 and the PP-686 L/PS decomposition.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced and the finding's own mechanical-equivalence reasoning checks out. riskbreakers_identity.md:210 (PP-486) states the trigger as 'Loyalty 0 + L 0 + PS 0 — full institutional + populist collapse'. npc_roster_v30.md:240 uses 'Vaynard eliminated (Loyalty 0 + Mandate 0)' and npc_behavior_v30.md:231 uses 'Loyalty 0 + Mandate 0, per PP-486'. core.md:160 confirms 'Mandate = round(0.5 × Legitimacy + 0.5 × Popular_Support)' under PP-686 v2, so Mandate 0 implies L 0 and PS 0 — mechanically near-equivalent, not a behavioral contradiction. This is correctly a consistency/terminology nit (pre-PP-686 derived-stat shorthand), and SOFT is the right severity. Confirmed as a SOFT editorial-phrasing item.

#### AUD-NPC2-216 — Maret's Primary = Evidence intersects the confirmed §6.1b gate defect; her own pre-Active states would be unguarded under the as-written gate
- **Unit:** Maret Uln (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 (Thread-level evidence inert vs TS<30) / §6.1b Evidence-style gate | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Maret's Primary Resonant Style is Evidence. The Evidence-style TS gate (§6.1b) is, as written, keyed to 'TS 0 NPCs' / 'non-sensitives' with re-activation only at Stirring 30+, leaving the TS 1-29 band unprotected against Thread-level Evidence (AUD-NPC2-006, CONFIRMED HIGH, P-08). At her stated 35/50 Maret is above the gate so Thread-evidence is currently a valid vector against her; but because her TS is itself contradictory (finding 5) and any below-30 reading (or a pre-awakening arc beat) would fall in the unguarded band, the gate defect is load-bearing for this NPC. Flagging as SOFT here since the root gate fix is owned by AUD-NPC2-006; the Maret-specific exposure is the dependency.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:238 (Primary Resonant Style Evidence); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:112-114 (§6.1b gate keyed to 'TS 0' / 'non-sensitives', exception at 'Stirring (30+)'); canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md §10.1 (P-08 inertness); designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:266-271 (AUD-NPC2-006 CONFIRMED)
- **Fix:** Apply the AUD-NPC2-006 fix (restate §6.1b prohibition as 'against any NPC with TS < 30 (Stirring)'), then re-confirm Maret's Evidence-vector status against her single reconciled TS value.
- **Verifier note:** Verified and honestly scoped by its author. npc_behavior_v30.md:238 sets Primary Resonant Style = Evidence; npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:112-114 keys the §6.1b gate to 'TS 0 NPCs' / 'non-sensitives' with re-activation only at 'Stirring (30+)', leaving TS 1-29 unguarded; canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md:249-251 (§10.1) states P-08 inertness ('propositionally available ... epistemically inert'); the root defect AUD-NPC2-006 is CONFIRMED HIGH in the audit (lines 80, 266-271). The Maret-specific exposure is correctly limited: at her stated 35/50 she is ABOVE the gate, so the defect does not currently expose her — the unguarded-band risk is hypothetical (a sub-30 reading or pre-awakening beat). The finding flags itself SOFT because the fix is owned by AUD-NPC2-006 and the Maret angle is only a dependency. That self-assessment is accurate. Confirmed at SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-217 — Adjacent (non-naming) stale-mechanic residue: 'VTM' survives in npc_roster_v30.md after the Vaynard-Thread-Mastery track was struck
- **Unit:** Naming contamination | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** canon/supersession_register.yaml VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (struck mechanic, not a name) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Out of strict naming scope but worth flagging for a follow-up cycle: supersession_register.yaml struck the VTM (Vaynard Thread Mastery) advancement track ENTIRELY on 2026-04-19 (no canonical advancement mechanic existed; it was a placeholder). npc_roster_v30.md still references VTM as a live mechanic in Maret Uln's succession card. This is a struck MECHANIC, not a superseded NAME — the 'Vaynard' inside 'VTM' is canonical — so it is not naming contamination, but it is stale-mechanic propagation in an NPC doc.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:239 ('VTM resets, Varfell realigns'), :240 ('VTM -> 0. Varfell cannot target RM'). Struck by canon/supersession_register.yaml:37-49 (VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19: 'VTM STRUCK entirely ... was placeholder'; files_to_recheck lists params/bg, not npc_roster). character_canon_v30.md notes Vaynard arcs are themselves partly empty (§8.3, D4).
- **Fix:** In a non-naming propagation pass, replace the two VTM references in npc_roster_v30.md:239-240 with the post-strike Varfell-reorientation mechanic (or a neutral 'Varfell realigns' phrasing) and add npc_roster_v30.md to the VTM-STRIKE files_to_recheck list. No name change required.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced both residue sites: npc_roster_v30.md:239 ('Maret succession fires as a Domain Echo — VTM resets, Varfell realigns') and :240 ('VTM -> 0. Varfell cannot target RM. Varfell Stability -1'). canon/supersession_register.yaml VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (line 37) struck the VTM track entirely ('VTM STRUCK entirely. No canonical advancement mechanic existed — was placeholder', lines 42-43). Critically, its files_to_recheck (lines 44-48) lists victory_v30.md, varfell_path_b_v30.md, params/bg/core.md, params/bg/npc_priority_trees.md — and does NOT include npc_roster_v30.md, confirming this residue is untracked. The finding correctly scopes this as a struck MECHANIC (the 'Vaynard' token inside 'VTM' is canonical, not a name violation), so it is out of strict naming scope but live stale-mechanic propagation. SOFT severity is appropriate for an adjacent, governed, low-impact cleanup item; confirmed as flagged.

#### AUD-NPC2-218 — Torben's defining mechanic (emergence window) is still editorial status 'provisional/PROPOSED' yet rendered as locked canon
- **Unit:** Prince Torben Almqvist | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-618 status (canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The ED-618 emergence-window text in §2.8 is written as settled canonical rules (precise tiebreaks, S8 lock, Order default), but the editorial ledger still carries ED-618 as status 'provisional' / '[PROPOSED: ...]'. The single most defining attribute of this NPC — what Conviction he ends up with — therefore rests on an unratified proposal presented to the GM as final. Surfacing for ratification tracking; no mechanical error in the rule itself.
- **Evidence:** canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl:366 ('id': 'ED-618' ... 'description': '[PROPOSED: 16.2a Torben Emergence Window ...]', 'status': 'provisional'); rendered as canon at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:192 with no provisional marker.
- **Fix:** Either ratify ED-618 (flip to resolved) or add an in-line provisional flag to the §2.8 emergence-window row so its draft status is visible to the GM.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced exactly. canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl:366: id ED-618, status 'provisional', description '[PROPOSED: 16.2a Torben Emergence Window (ED-618) First faction to reach Disposition >= +2 before Season 8 sets his primary Conviction]'. It is rendered as settled canonical rules at npc_behavior_v30.md:192 (precise tiebreaks, S8 lock, Order default) with no provisional/draft marker. The NPC's single most defining attribute therefore rests on an unratified proposal presented to the GM as final. Ratification-tracking surfacing, not a mechanical error in the rule. SOFT appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-219 — 'Yrsa ↔ Maret Vossen kinship' is fabricated — wrong surname, wrong faction, no canonical kin tie
- **Unit:** Relational-graph integrity | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_roster_v30 §2,#3 / npc_behavior_v30 §2.6,§2.10 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** §0 lists 'Yrsa ↔ Maret Vossen kinship (PP-665 rename context)' as a canonical motivating tie the framework instantiates. This is false on three counts: (1) Maret's canonical surname is Uln, not Vossen (the graph invents a shared surname); (2) Maret Uln is a Varfell intelligence operative, not RM and not Yrsa's kin; (3) no kinship between Yrsa Vossen and Maret Uln appears in roster, behavior, or timeline. Yrsa's canonical RM lieutenant is Hann (npc_behavior §2.7), not Maret. The '(PP-665 rename context)' citation is unverifiable and does not establish a kin edge. The tie is also never actually instantiated as an edge in §9.1 — it exists only as this false provenance claim.
- **Evidence:** Graph: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:16. Canon: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:28 (#2 Maret Uln — Varfell), :42 (#3 Yrsa Vossen — RM); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:149 (§2.6 Yrsa Vossen, RM), :229 (§2.10 Maret Uln — Varfell), :172-181 (Yrsa's lieutenant is Hann).
- **Fix:** Strike the 'Yrsa ↔ Maret Vossen kinship' claim. If a Varfell↔RM tie between Maret Uln and the RM is intended, model it as Maret's authored RM-sympathy (roster §2 / §14) — an affinity, not kinship — and use correct surnames (Uln, Vossen). Verify or drop the PP-665 citation.
- **Verifier note:** The tie is genuinely non-canonical, but the finding's three-count rationale is substantially WRONG. 'Maret Vossen' is NOT Maret Uln — it is Yrsa Vossen's OWN pre-rename name. PP-665 renamed 'Maret Vossen' INTO 'Yrsa Vossen' to resolve a forename collision with Maret Uln (canon/patch_register_index.md:172 'Maret Vossen → Yrsa Vossen forename rename'; conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.2:88 'Yrsa Vossen (RM leader; per PP-665 rename from Maret Vossen)' and :104 'Maret Uln (Varfell; per PP-665 not renamed)'). So the finding's claims that 'Maret's surname is Uln not Vossen (graph invents a shared surname)' and that this is about 'Maret Uln, a Varfell intelligence operative' are misreadings — the surname Vossen WAS correct for the pre-rename Maret, and the §0 line refers to Yrsa's superseded self, not Maret Uln. The actual defect (matching in-repo AUD-NPC2-079, rated SOFT/CONFIRMED) is a phantom dyad pairing a person with her own former name. It lives only in a §0 Purpose illustrative sentence, is explicitly tagged '(PP-665 rename context)' showing author awareness, and is never instantiated as an edge (absent from §6/§9.1, which contain only E-001/E-002/E-003). The PP-665 citation IS verifiable (contrary to the finding). A prose-example error to strike, not a HIGH load-bearing fabricated-kinship defect — SOFT.

#### AUD-NPC2-220 — E-001 sworn-bond (Almud→Halvar Brandt) mis-specifies the counterpart and overstates its provenance
- **Unit:** Relational-graph integrity | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_roster_v30 §6 / worldbuilding_v30 / npc_behavior_v30 §2.5 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Reciprocity/faction check: E-001 stores a strength-2 sworn-bond from NPC-Almud (Crown monarch) to NPC-Halvar-Brandt and claims it is 'Canonical; surfaced in npc_roster §6.' Two problems. (1) Counterpart mismatch: the graph's own §5.1 composition exemplar (line 357) describes this bond as 'Halvar↔Lions'-Table-leader' — i.e., to the order's leader (canonically Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall), not to Almud. Almud is Crown; the Lions' Table is a Löwenritter order. The two statements disagree on who Halvar is bonded to. (2) Provenance overstated: roster §6 places Brandt under Löwenritter/Ehrenwall with an EXTERNAL-THREAT-FIXATED flaw and a succession arc to replace Ehrenwall; it says nothing about a sworn-bond to Almud. (3) Reciprocity: unlike the rivalry (E-002/E-003 stored both ways), E-001 has no reciprocal/partner representation, so Halvar's side of the symmetric sworn-bond is absent.
- **Evidence:** Graph: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:537-545 (E-001 Almud→Halvar, 'surfaced in npc_roster §6'), :357 (§5.1 'Halvar↔Lions'-Table-leader exemplar'). Canon: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:81-87,174 (Brandt = Löwenritter, under Ehrenwall); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:128 (§2.5 Grandmaster Lisbeth Ehrenwall = Löwenritter leader); designs/world/worldbuilding_v30.md:109,292 (Lions' Table = Löwenritter order).
- **Fix:** Decide the canonical counterpart and make it consistent: if the bond is Brandt↔Ehrenwall (Löwenritter), fix E-001's `from` and the §5.1 exemplar to match and correct the provenance note; if a Crown↔Brandt tie is genuinely intended, cite the actual source (roster §6 does not support it). Either way, store the symmetric counterpart per §9.1's own 'store both directions' rule.
- **Verifier note:** Real but overstated, matching in-repo AUD-NPC2-078 (DOWNGRADED). The central 'counterpart mismatch / wrong superior' claim does not hold: E-001 is type SWORN-BOND — symmetric PEER solidarity (§9.1:537-545, 'formed_by: Lion's Table service shared in Altonian border patrol'), NOT a liege-vassal edge — so it makes no chain-of-command claim that Almud is Brandt's liege. Brandt's liege being Grandmaster Ehrenwall (npc_roster_v30_infill.md §6:35 'Second-in-command to Ehrenwall'; canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:145) is uncontradicted by a symmetric peer bond. The §5.1:357 exemplar 'Halvar↔Lions'-Table-leader' is a SEPARATE liege-vassal composition example with an unnamed leader; it does not bind Halvar to Almud, so the 'two statements disagree on counterpart' claim conflates a sworn-bond instance with a liege-vassal exemplar. The implied 'Lion's Table' collision is also not a defect: §6.1:409 placing Lion's Table HQ at S-001 Valorsplatz is CANON (valoria_geography_v30.yaml:251; faction_politics_v30 establishes the Lions' Table as Löwenritter coordination under the Crown umbrella). Residual genuine issues are minor: the illustrative detail that Almud personally shared border patrol with Brandt is unverified B2-deferred flourish (provenance overstatement vs roster §6), the formed_by season window (-3..0) conflicts with the '12-years-ago' backstory (AUD-NPC2-111, LOW), and no Brandt↔Ehrenwall edge exists that his succession arc presupposes. SOFT, not MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-221 — Per-NPC Solidarity entries (Ingrid Stenskald, Cardinal Jarnstal) state Solidarity with no Knot-path cross-reference
- **Unit:** Resonant-styles taxonomy & P-08 gating | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §6.3 (Solidarity) / ED-680 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The Solidarity Knot path is well-defined at the SYSTEM level (§6.3 + fieldwork_v30 §5.6a per ED-680: Disposition +5 + PC-or-NPC TS>=30 + Knot capacity). But several per-NPC Solidarity assignments give no Knot-path cross-reference, so a reader could treat Solidarity as freely targetable. Skald-Chief Ingrid Stenskald (§2.17) is listed RS=Solidarity in a bare council table with no Knot/Disposition/TS note; Cardinal of Fortitude (Jarnstal) Secondary=Solidarity and the negotiation note (line 227) invites 'Solidarity RS — personal appeal' without stating the active-Knot prerequisite. Because these sub-NPCs are presumably TS 0, Solidarity is only reachable if the PC is TS>=30 — an unstated, easily-missed constraint.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md line 377 (Ingrid Stenskald | Community | Solidarity | 3 — no Knot path); C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\arcs\arc_expansion_v30.md line 201 (Jarnstal Secondary RS Solidarity) + line 227 ('Solidarity RS — personal appeal' with no Knot prereq). Contrast the correct pattern at arc_expansion_v30 line 555 (Warden Orm: 'Solidarity RS (requires Knot at Close with Orm)')
- **Fix:** Append a Knot-path cross-reference to Solidarity-bearing sub-NPC entries (e.g. '(Solidarity requires active Knot — §6.3 / fieldwork §5.6a; PC must be TS>=30 to form a Knot with this TS-0 NPC)'), matching the Warden Orm pattern.

#### AUD-NPC2-222 — Inner-circle/council Resonant-Style columns are mislabeled 'MS' (Mending Stability)
- **Unit:** Resonant-styles taxonomy & P-08 gating | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.15-§2.17 (schema) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The §2.15 Crown Inner Circle, §2.16 Hafenmark Inner Council, and §2.17 Varfell Jarl Council tables have a column header 'MS' whose cells contain Resonant Style values (Authority, Evidence, Consequence, Solidarity). 'MS' canonically denotes Mending Stability (a 0-100 territory/world stat), not a Resonant Style. The mislabel records these sub-NPCs' Resonant Styles under the wrong field name and is parseable as a stat error by tooling/engine ingestion.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md line 357-363 (Crown IC header '| Conviction | MS | Certainty |' with cells Authority/Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Authority), lines 367-370 (Hafenmark IC: Evidence/Consequence), lines 374-377 (Varfell: Consequence/Solidarity)
- **Fix:** Rename the 'MS' column header to 'Resonant Style' in §2.15, §2.16, and §2.17.

#### AUD-NPC2-223 — Conviction-vs-Resonant-Style category error: Order/Reason labeled as Almud's 'Resonant Style primary' (and deprecated 'Reason')
- **Unit:** Resonant-styles taxonomy & P-08 gating | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** CONVICTIONS taxonomy (PP-684) / ED-516 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** arc_expansion_v30 Almud Arc A says 'Almud's Resonant Style primary shifts: Order fades, Reason emerges.' Order and Reason are CONVICTIONS, not Resonant Styles (the RS 4-set is Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity). The sentence conflates the Conviction axis with the Resonant Style axis. It compounds the error by using the deprecated label 'Reason' (superseded -> Scholastic/Authority per PP-684) as live canon. Separately, the Arc B exit note justifies an Almud-targeting Consequence argument as working 'because it comes through Authority' — but Almud's styles are Consequence/Solidarity (§2.1); Authority is not his RS, so the stated access-vector rationale is off (the argument used, Consequence, IS his primary, so the mechanism survives — only the justification is wrong).
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\arcs\arc_expansion_v30.md line 76 ('Almud's Resonant Style primary shifts: Order fades, Reason emerges. Evidence RS becomes effective again (Reason Conviction = evidence-responsive)') and line 95 ('the only RS attack Almud can hear ... because it comes through Authority'); Almud's canonical RS at C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md lines 53-54 (Consequence / Solidarity)
- **Fix:** Rewrite line 76 to separate the axes: 'Almud's primary CONVICTION shifts (Order -> Scholastic/Authority [was Reason]); his Evidence Resonant Style becomes effective for ontical evidence as his worldview turns evidence-responsive.' On line 95, drop 'because it comes through Authority' (Authority is not his RS) — the lever is Ehrenwall delivering his actual primary, Consequence.

#### AUD-NPC2-224 — Main roster body for #12 is empty; all canonical content lives only in the infill file
- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_roster_v30 §12 (line 146-153) vs Infill convention | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Section 12's body in the CANONICAL-status npc_roster_v30.md is completely empty (header + horizontal rule only), whereas neighboring entries #11 Laskaris and #13 Tormann carry full 'Behavioral AI' bodies in the same main file. This is permitted by the documented SKELETON + '<!-- Infill: ... -->' atomization pattern (the infill is rich and complete), so it is not a content gap, but the inconsistency with adjacent populated entries in a file marked Status: CANONICAL is worth surfacing: a reader of the main roster alone gets nothing for Solberg while getting full bodies for his neighbors.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:146-153 (empty body); contrast designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:141 (Laskaris PROTECTIVE body present) and :161 (Tormann OPTIMISER body present); canonical content present in designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:63-68; file header marks Status: CANONICAL (npc_roster_v30_index.md:16).
- **Fix:** No mechanical fix required if the infill is the single source of truth. Optional: either backfill the #12 main-roster body to match the population level of #11/#13, or apply the empty-body convention uniformly so the main roster is consistently skeleton-only across all entries (currently it is mixed: #2/#3 are populated, #1/#4/#12 are stubs).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced and not overstated. npc_roster_v30.md:146-153 is header + blank lines + horizontal rule with no body. Adjacent entries carry full Behavioral AI bodies in the same file: Laskaris PROTECTIVE at :141, Tormann OPTIMISER at :161. The canonical Solberg content does exist, only in npc_roster_v30_infill.md:63-68 (full STABILITY-SEEKING profile). Index marks the file Status: CANONICAL (npc_roster_v30_index.md:16). The finding explicitly concedes this is permitted by the documented SKELETON + infill atomization pattern and is 'not a content gap' with 'No mechanical fix required', flagging only the population inconsistency.
