The restructure from Conviction 0.5/0.5 to 0.40/0.30 is documented in the note, but the self_other drop from +0.8 to +0.40 is not explained and conflicts with the note's own 'high' claim.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:64 (self_other_initial: +0.40) and :67 ('High Self-Other captures the Cesare Borgia signature — instrumental Authority for self-aggrandizement'); band table designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:137-143 (+0.1..+0.5 'Mildly self-interested'; +0.5..+1.0 'Self-aggrandizing'); sim baseline designs/audit/2026-04-30-architecture-session/sims/pp686_sim_v2.py:382,558 (Cesare orient 0.8, 'self-aggrandizing').
- **Fix:** Raise Cesare's self_other_initial to >= +0.5 (e.g. +0.6 to +0.8, matching the sim baseline) so the value lands in the 'Self-aggrandizing' band the note claims; OR, if +0.40 is intentional, correct the note to 'mildly self-interested' and drop the 'high / self-aggrandizement' wording. Reconcile against pp686_sim_v2.py.
- **Verifier note:** The central evidentiary claim is wrong. The finding asserts 'the cited source (sim §1.2 / pp686_sim_v2.py) actually models Cesare at self_other +0.8.' I traced the scenario→section mapping: the eval doc's §1.2 'Succession Dynamics' is scenario_2 in pp686_sim_v2.py (def at line 376), and scenario_2 sets self_other_orientation=0.3 for the successor (line 395), NOT 0.8. The +0.8 appears only in scenario_6 'Successful tyrant' (line 501) and scenario_8 'Self-Other attribution' (lines 552,558) — neither is §1.2. The finding's own cite 'pp686_sim_v2.py:382,558' proves this: line 382 is the conviction baseline (Authority/Utility 0.5/0.5, no self_other) and line 558 is scenario_8, not §1.2. So +0.40 is actually CLOSER to the §1.2 source value (0.3) than the +0.8 the auditor claims, and the 'contradicts the sim baseline (+0.8)' framing is refuted. The only surviving, reproducible prong is a minor internal note-vs-band mismatch: roster line 64 (+0.40) with line 67 prose 'High Self-Other ... self-aggrandizement' vs the band table at character_canon_v30 line 142 (+0.1..+0.5 = 'Mildly self-interested'; +0.5..+1.0 = 'Self-aggrandizing'). +0.40 does sit just below the +0.5 self-aggrandizing threshold, so the word 'High' is loose. Real but cosmetic prose/threshold quibble on a provisional doc; not a sim contradiction. LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-256 — Cesare absent from the enforced canonical name registry; no full name canonized, mononym unratified
- **Unit:** Cesare | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** references/npc_registry.yaml (enforced canonical name source) vs live use in 3 v30 docs | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** references/npc_registry.yaml is declared the 'Canonical source of truth for ALL named characters' with the enforcement rule 'No character name may appear in design docs without an entry here', and its schema requires first_name + last_name. Cesare (and Lorenzo) have NO entry, yet 'Cesare' appears as a live named NPC in three v30 canonical docs and is instantiated as relational node NPC-Cesare with authored edges. So no full name is canonized for Cesare, and unlike Edeyja (registry entry: last_name: null '# mononym confirmed final per Jordan') his mononym status is not ratified anywhere — he is simply unregistered.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml:1-13 (banner 'Canonical source of truth for ALL named characters'; 'ENFORCEMENT: No character name may appear in design docs without an entry here'; schema required:[id,first_name,last_name,faction,role,status]); registry grep for 'cesare|lorenzo' returns no match; Edeyja mononym precedent at references/npc_registry.yaml:21-23 (last_name: null # mononym confirmed final). Live named uses: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:56-69; designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:410,432; designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:103,302,548-558 (NPC-Cesare edges E-002/E-003).
- **Fix:** Add a registry entry for Cesare (and Lorenzo): id, faction Crown, role 'Crown succession alternate', status succession_alternate (per sim §1.2). Decide and record the name shape — either canonize a surname or set last_name: null with an explicit mononym-confirmed note as was done for Edeyja. Until then the name is unratified despite live use.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors verified. references/npc_registry.yaml lines 2 and 5 carry the 'Canonical source of truth for ALL named characters' banner and the 'No character name may appear in design docs without an entry here' enforcement rule; line 12 schema requires [id, first_name, last_name, faction, role, status]; Edeyja mononym precedent confirmed at line 23. My grep of the whole registry and of canon/ returns zero Cesare/Lorenzo matches, and Cesare appears live in three v30 docs (conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 lines 56-69; character_canon_v30 lines 410 inspiration table and 432 D7; npc_relational_graph_v30 lines 103/302/365/548-558 with authored edges E-002/E-003). So the literal enforcement-rule breach is real and reproduced. BUT severity is overstated at MEDIUM: every upstream source flags these as not-yet-authored — the migration roster is explicitly PROVISIONAL and 'Per-character authoritative until promotion to canonical character sheets' (lines 2,9,31), and character_canon D7 (line 432) deliberately logs Cesare/Lorenzo as 'succession_alternate (per sim §1.2) rather than authored-active.' The registry is a known-incomplete surface (dozens of [GAP] markers, open-issues list). The 'mononym unratified' sub-claim is synthetic — no doc asserts Cesare is a mononym; it is simply unregistered pending authoring, not a contested name shape. This is registry-hygiene debt on provisional sim-continuity entities, not a live canon violation. LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-257 — No companion-drift mechanic; Edeyja's Coherence framed as deficit ('damaged'), edging toward moral/condemnation register
- **Unit:** Companion specification | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** P-10 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Companion Coherence is tracked (§1.1) and three practitioner companions can drift, but the doc gives drift no mechanical treatment and no tridimensional structure (P-10 requires actuality/intelligibility/temporality, never one-dimensional, never moral). Worse, §3.3 frames Edeyja's Coherence as '7 (damaged from warden work)' — a deficit/condemnation-adjacent framing. P-10 bars treating Coherence as moral standing or character condemnation; canon explicitly frames Edeyja as 'sustained at high level through disciplined practice' and 'not defined by this injury.'
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md §3.3 L87 ('Edeyja | 50+ | 7 (damaged from warden work)'); contradicted by designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md L22 ('Coherence: 9 — sustained at high level through disciplined practice') and L16 ('not defined by this injury'); constraint canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-10 + canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md §16.1.
- **Fix:** Remove the 'damaged' deficit framing. State Companion drift handling explicitly: a practitioner Companion whose Coherence falls produces tridimensional effects (actuality/intelligibility/temporality) per P-10, phrased structurally (commensurability with human-mode being), never as moral fall. Cross-reference the canonical drift treatment in foundations §16.1-16.4.
- **Verifier note:** Two claims, both weaker than HIGH. (a) 'No companion-drift mechanic / no tridimensional treatment': same delegation point as finding 1 — companion §3.3 L91 routes Thread behavior to threadwork_v30 and Knot drift propagation lives in knots_v30/foundations §16. Absence of a restated drift mechanic in a personal-scale relational spec is not a P-10 FAIL; P-10's FAIL test (canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-10) targets Coherence-DRIFT MECHANICS, and this doc defines none — it only tracks a Coherence value. (b) The 'damaged from warden work' phrasing in §3.3 L87 is real, but P-10 bars MORAL/sin/condemnation framing of drift; 'damaged from warden work' is a somatic/occupational-injury descriptor (consistent with edeyja_npc.md L14 'excess exposure damaged her chest and shoulder'), not a moral-fall/condemnation register. It is a clumsy editorial gloss, not a P-10 valence violation. The stronger, verifiable defect is simply that the number is wrong (see next finding). Downgrade to LOW editorial.

#### AUD-NPC2-258 — Companion knot-strain to the PC is never propagated; only PC->companion direction is modeled
- **Unit:** Companion specification | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** P-12 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Practitioner companions (Edeyja, Maret Uln, Torsvald in §3.3) travel persistently with the PC, hold a Knot to the PC (§2.1 'Knot path', §7.2 'Ob 1 if Knot present'), and carry a persistently-tracked Coherence stat (§1.1, §3.3). Per P-12, a drifting practitioner's configuration must propagate tridimensionally (actuality + intelligibility + temporality) through every knot to the connected entity — here, the PC. The doc contains no companion-drift-to-PC propagation at all. The ONLY Coherence interaction in §6.1 runs the wrong way: 'PC Coherence reaches 0 (Severance) while companion is present' (companion reacts to the PC's drift). A practitioner companion whose own Coherence drops imposes nothing on the PC.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md §1.1 L19 (Coherence persistently tracked), §2.1 L49 (Close Knot path), §3.3 L85-91 (three Coherence-bearing practitioner companions), §6.1 L161 (only PC-Coherence-0 trigger; no companion-Coherence trigger); canonical P-12 home: designs/personal/knots_v30.md L16 ('§A P-12 (relational contagion)') and canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-12.
- **Fix:** Add a companion-drift propagation rule: when a practitioner Companion's Coherence drops below a threshold while Knotted to the PC, apply tridimensional knot-strain to the PC — actuality (configurational pull / strain on the PC's Knot), intelligibility (apperceptive degradation in the PC-companion relation), temporality (retention/protention disturbance re shared scenes). Route through the canonical Knot strain system (knots_v30 §5/§8) rather than inventing a parallel value, and ensure the three dimensions are individuated, not a single generic 'strain'.
- **Verifier note:** Overstated on two counts I checked directly. (1) Wrong target: P-12's FAIL test (canon/02_canon_constraints.md P-12) asks whether 'the system' propagates drift through knots one-dimensionally or as 'a single generic strain value' — the obligation lands on the Knot system, not on a personal-scale companion spec that delegates Thread ops ('Practitioner companions perform Thread operations per threadwork_v30', companion §3.3 L91). The canonical propagation channel already exists and is agent-NEUTRAL/bidirectional: knots_v30 §8.1 L246-256 imposes Knot strain whenever ANY Knotted practitioner's Coherence drops (not PC-only), and foundations §16.3 states propagation flows from whichever party drifts. So a Knotted practitioner-companion whose Coherence falls already triggers strain on that Knot via existing canon. (2) Premise overstated: a Companion is acquired at Disposition >=+3 (companion §1.1, §2.1), but a Knot requires Disposition +5 + TS>=30 (params/core.md L149; knots_v30 §3.1). 'Knot path' is only one of three eligibility routes (§2.1 L49) and §7.2 says 'Ob 1 if Knot present' (conditional) — most companions are NOT Knotted, so P-12 has no channel for them. The companion doc not restating Knot-propagation is a minor cross-reference gap, not a HIGH canon FAIL. The genuine P-12 tridimensionality defect (single generic 'strain') lives in knots_v30 §5/§8, which the auditor itself flags as the canonical home.

#### AUD-NPC2-259 — Bare 'Vossen' should be 'Yrsa Vossen'; surname is a TS-0 non-practitioner (stat citation uncorroborated)
- **Unit:** Companion specification | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-665 (Yrsa Vossen) + CLAUDE.md naming rule | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** §3.2 cites 'Vossen: Charisma 5, Agility 3' as an example of established characterisation. The canonical name is Yrsa Vossen (forename canonicalized by PP-665, renamed from the colliding 'Maret Vossen'). Bare-surname use is a minor naming-hygiene issue; more notably the stat snippet is uncorroborated against her sheet, and Yrsa Vossen is a TS-0 non-practitioner RM leader — fine as a Social-Path example, but the offered stats should be sourced.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md §3.2 L81; canon: canon/patch_register_index.md L172 (PP-665 Maret Vossen -> Yrsa Vossen) and designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L1198 ('Yrsa Vossen | 0 | 3 | Non-practitioner').
- **Fix:** Use full canonical name 'Yrsa Vossen' on first reference and cite her sheet for any stat values used as examples.
- **Verifier note:** Verified and already LOW. companion §3.2 L81 uses bare 'Vossen: Charisma 5, Agility 3'. Canonical forename is Yrsa Vossen: canon/patch_register_index.md L172 'PP-665 ... Maret Vossen -> Yrsa Vossen forename rename'; npc_behavior_v30.md §2.6 L149 'Yrsa Vossen (Restoration Movement)' and L1198 'Yrsa Vossen | 0 | 3 | Non-practitioner RM leader' (confirming TS 0). The bare-surname use is a minor naming-hygiene point and the example stat snippet is uncorroborated against her sheet, exactly as stated. Severity LOW is right.

#### AUD-NPC2-260 — Confessor Arne Himlensendt has no entry in the canonical NPC roster
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Audit method step 1; npc_roster_v30.md | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_roster_v30.md (Status: CANONICAL) is the roster of named NPCs and the doc the audit method starts from. It contains the Church's SUBORDINATE officers — Saemund Haelgrund (#4, Cardinal Justice) and Aldric Tormann (#13, Cardinal Prudence) — but the Church FACTION HEAD, Confessor Arne Himlensendt, the 'structural keystone' of the entire post-war settlement, is entirely absent (no heading, no summary-table row, no axis row). Every other faction head (Almud, Baralta, Vaynard, Ehrenwall, Vossen, Edeyja) is rostered. The Confessor exists in behavior, character_analyses, foils, and the timeline, but not the roster.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_index.md L13-55 (heading index: no Confessor/Himlensendt; Church entries are #4 Haelgrund and #13 Tormann); grep of designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md(+_infill) for 'Himlensendt|Arne|Confessor' = no matches; Summary Table designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L168-181 (no head row).
- **Fix:** Add a Confessor Arne Himlensendt roster entry (Church faction head) mirroring the other faction-head entries: faction, compromise, AI flaw, key consequence, axis positions, and Mode Interface row — sourced from npc_behavior_v30 §2.2 and npc_character_analyses §7.
- **Verifier note:** The bare fact is confirmed: grep of npc_roster_v30.md and _infill for 'Himlensendt|Arne|Confessor' returns zero matches, and the heading index (npc_roster_v30_index.md L13-49) plus Summary Table (L168-181) list only 13 NPCs with Church entries #4 Saemund Haelgrund (Cardinal Justice) and #13 Aldric Tormann (Cardinal Prudence). BUT the finding's load-bearing justification is false: it claims 'Every other faction head (Almud, Baralta, Vaynard, Ehrenwall, Vossen, Edeyja) is rostered.' I verified the opposite - Almud (Crown), Baralta (Hafenmark), Vaynard (Varfell), and Ehrenwall (Lowenritter Grandmaster) have NO roster entries (grep + Summary Table L168-181; Ehrenwall appears only as a background referent for Torsvald/Brandt at L273/L283). Only Edeyja and Vossen are rostered, and they are Warden/RM leaders. So npc_roster_v30.md is a secondary/supporting-NPC roster; the absence of the Church HEAD mirrors the absence of all four other great-faction heads - it is the rule, not a unique gap. Himlensendt is fully specified elsewhere (behavior s2.2, character_analyses s7, foils, timeline L142). This is at most a LOW completeness nit, not a HIGH gap; the premise that drove HIGH does not survive contact with the file.

#### AUD-NPC2-261 — Conviction vector conflicts across docs AND the designated PRIMARY source (PP-685) has no Himlensendt entry — faction_canon fabricates one by borrowing Cardinal Reichard's profile
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 (migration roster = designated PRIMARY for conviction values) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The Tier-1 stat block gives Faith (primary) / Order (secondary). faction_canon_v30 instead asserts a weighted vector 'Faith 0.40 + Precedent 0.20 + Authority 0.15' and labels it 'per migration roster.' But Himlensendt does NOT appear in conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) at all — §2.3 Ecclesiastical lists only Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth. faction_canon's own line states it is the 'Cardinal Reichard profile applied as Himlensendt mapping,' i.e. another NPC's vector copied over. Net: (a) the D1-designated PRIMARY source is empty for this NPC (absence-from-roster gap); (b) the secondary conviction conflicts between sources (Order vs Precedent+Authority); (c) a doc cites a non-existent roster entry as authority.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.2 L70-71 (Faith/Order); designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md L568 ('Faith 0.40 + Precedent 0.20 + Authority 0.15 ... Cardinal Reichard profile applied as Himlensendt mapping'); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.3 L135-179 (only Reichard/Baralta/Lenneth; no Himlensendt — grep confirms 0 hits); character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 L426
- **Fix:** Author a real Himlensendt entry in conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) as a Class B amendment with his OWN vector, then make §2.2 and faction_canon_v30 cite it consistently. Decide the canonical secondary (Order per §2.2/infill, vs Precedent/Authority per the copied Reichard profile) — recommend Faith primary + Order secondary to match the stat block and infill arc logic ('If Faith wavers, Order sustains'). Remove the false 'per migration roster' citation from faction_canon_v30 L568 until the roster entry exists; stop reusing Reichard's weights.
- **Verifier note:** The factual anchors are real: I confirmed 0 hits for 'Himlensendt' in conviction_migration_roster_v30.md (grep returns nothing; §2.3 Ecclesiastical L135-179 lists only Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth), and faction_canon_v30.md L568 literally reads 'Faith 0.40 + Precedent 0.20 + Authority 0.15 ... Cardinal Reichard profile applied as Himlensendt mapping' with weights byte-identical to Reichard's roster entry (L142-144). So (a) the roster has no Himlensendt entry and (b) faction_canon's 'per migration roster' citation is false/borrowed — that sub-claim is CONFIRMED and worth a fix. BUT the headline 'conviction vector conflicts across docs' (Order vs Precedent+Authority) is overstated: npc_behavior §1.2 L28 defines the canonical model as 1-3 weighted primaries (0.6-0.8) PLUS distributed cultural background (0.2-0.4). The faction_canon weighted vector and the §2.2 'Primary Faith / Secondary Order' label are two representational layers of the same construct, not contradictory assertions — Faith is primary in BOTH, and the infill Arc Map (L77) independently confirms 'Secondary (Order)'. character_canon §11 D1 (L426) already surfaces source-conflict on convictions as a known item explicitly deferred to 'a separate propagation cycle'. Moreover character_canon is PROVISIONAL/unratified (L2, L6) and PART B is unwritten, so D1 cannot be the binding PRIMARY-source constraint the finding leans on. Net: a real but minor citation/bookkeeping defect (false 'per migration roster' tag + borrowed Reichard weights), not a HIGH cross-doc canon contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-262 — Ethical Framework label 'Faith (Church)' is still used as a LIVE mechanical Ob driver with no [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] note
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 -> PP-686 §3.7 / character_canon_v30 §11 D2 (Ethical Framework SUPERSEDED as mechanical Ob driver) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The stat block carries 'Ethical Framework | Faith (Church) | Aligned: -1 Ob on doctrine-aligned action. Contradictory: +1 Ob against doctrine. Thread truth: +2 Ob.' This is exactly the construct PP-684 §6 + PP-686 superseded: framework labels are retained only as descriptive disposition tags; mechanical Ob now comes from PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc (mission/cascade/expectation alignment, clamped +/-2). The label here drives concrete Ob modifiers (incl. a +2 'Thread truth' term) with no [SUPERSEDED] marker required by D2 and character_canon §10.1.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.2 L72 (full Ob-modifier line on 'Faith (Church)'); character_canon_v30.md §11 D2 L427 and §10.1 L385 (retain label as disposition tag WITH [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] marker; do not leave as live driver); supersession_register.yaml PP-686-001 L105-119 (Ethical Framework Modifiers superseded, cap +/-3 -> +/-2)
- **Fix:** Annotate the framework row '[SUPERSEDED -> mechanical Ob via PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc]' and demote 'Faith (Church)' to a descriptive disposition tag. Move the live numbers (-1/+1/+2 Thread-truth) into the PP-686 §3.7 triadic computation or explicitly mark them as the legacy values pending re-derivation. Note: this defect is shared by every named NPC in §2 (Almud 'Virtue', Baralta 'Categorical Imperative', Vaynard 'Utility-driven Pragmatism') — a doc-wide propagation cycle, but it is live for THIS unit.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors verified: npc_behavior_v30.md L72 carries 'Ethical Framework | Faith (Church) | Aligned -1 Ob ... Thread truth: +2 Ob' with no [SUPERSEDED] marker (the only SUPERSEDED notes in the file are L26/L30, about the Conviction taxonomy, not the framework rows), and supersession_register PP-686-001 (L105-119) confirms Ethical Framework Modifiers superseded with cap +/-3 -> +/-2. HOWEVER the finding misreads the authority chain. character_canon §11 D2 (L427) is NOT a constraint that npc_behavior violates — it is forward-looking guidance for character_canon's OWN consolidated Part B sheets (which are 'pending Q1 scope decision', i.e. unwritten), and that doc is PROVISIONAL/unratified (L2, L6). Crucially D2 says the OPPOSITE of a live violation: 'Mechanical Ob modifiers stay attached to the label since they're live in engine' and 'Do not silently re-derive Ob modifiers from conviction vector — that's a separate cycle.' §10.1 L385 likewise targets 'Mechanical Ob via PP-686 §3.7' as the consolidation's goal, not a defect already actionable in the source. So the live -1/+1/+2 numbers are the PRESCRIBED interim state, not a canon FAIL. The only genuine defect is a missing annotation on a source doc whose migration is explicitly deferred — and the finding itself concedes it is 'shared by every named NPC in §2' (Almud L52, Baralta L93, Vaynard L113, etc.), a doc-wide propagation backlog, not a Himlensendt-specific HIGH violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-263 — Belief 3 wording diverges between the canonical stat block and the systems-reference summary (voice-anchor drift); Belief->Scar conversion is otherwise handled
- **Unit:** Confessor Arne Himlensendt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** Belief / Scar voice-anchor consistency (character_canon_v30 §6.1; Tier-1 texture floor) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Belief 3 reads differently across docs: npc_behavior_v30 §2.2 = 'Thread practitioners are deluded or deceived - compassion demands I save them from themselves'; valoria_complete_systems_r2 §1.5 = 'Thread practitioners are dangerous.' These are not the same anchor (pastoral-pity vs threat-framing) and the prose-writer consumes Beliefs verbatim as voice anchors. Certainty (5/Orthodox) is consistent everywhere, and Belief->Scar conversion IS specified (infill Arc B: Belief 1 'Solmund's word is the only truth' -> Scar; new Belief forms), so this is a minor texture-consistency issue, not a gap.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.2 L79-81 (Beliefs 1-3); references/valoria_complete_systems_r2.md §1.5 L54 (Belief 3 = 'dangerous'); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md Arc B L76 (Belief 1 -> Scar conversion); Certainty 5 consistent across §2.2 L76 + r2 §1.5 L52
- **Fix:** Pick the canonical Belief 3 wording (recommend the §2.2 pastoral-pity version — it is load-bearing for his 'compassion and suppression are the same act' characterization in character_analyses §7) and align the r2 summary to it.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced directly. npc_behavior_v30 §2.2 L81 Belief 3 = 'Thread practitioners are deluded or deceived — compassion demands I save them from themselves'; valoria_complete_systems_r2.md §1.5 L54 Belief 3 = 'Thread practitioners are dangerous.' These are genuinely different voice anchors (pastoral-pity vs threat-framing). Certainty 5 (Orthodox) is consistent across §2.2 L76 and r2 L52, and r2 L52 also agrees on convictions Faith/Order and secondary RS Authority, so the divergence is isolated to Belief 3 wording as the finding scopes it. Belief->Scar conversion is specified in infill Arc B (L76: Belief 1 'Solmund's word is the only truth' -> Scar, new Belief forms), confirming the finding's caveat that this is texture-consistency, not a gap. LOW is correct — r2 is a 2026-04-15 summary doc, so this is plausibly a pre-refresh stale summary rather than an authored contradiction, but the verbatim mismatch is real and the prose-writer does consume Beliefs as anchors. The fix note's claim that the pastoral version is 'load-bearing ... in character_analyses §7' could not be confirmed (grep for 'compassion'/'suppression are the same' in npc_character_analyses_v30.md returned nothing), but that affects only the recommendation, not the finding.

#### AUD-NPC2-264 — npc_foils_v30_infill states Almud Conviction as 'Order/Reason' (superseded label, unannotated) and conflicts with roster
- **Unit:** Convictions taxonomy integrity | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon §11 D2 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The Ruler Diamond foil prose gives Almud's Conviction as 'Order/Reason' — 'Reason' is superseded and unannotated, and this also conflicts with the migration roster's Virtue/Authority primary for Almud.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md L65 'Conviction: Order/Reason. Resonant Style: Consequence. ... Ethics: Governance pragmatism with ethical doubt.'; conflicts with conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 Almud Virtue 0.45/Authority 0.30 (L43-54).
- **Fix:** Update foil prose to the canonical Almud vector (Virtue/Authority per PP-685) or at minimum remap Reason->Scholastic/Authority; descriptive prose, so light-touch alignment plus a pointer to the roster suffices.

#### AUD-NPC2-265 — No Conviction, no Beliefs, and no canonical TS value exist for Virke anywhere
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** NPC Behavior System required fields (Conviction Taxonomy §1.2; §7.10 stat table) + character_canon Beliefs | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Virke is a named, TTRPG-Full roster NPC but has no Conviction value, no Beliefs/voice-anchor, and no Thread Sensitivity entry in any canon doc. The behavior system requires these for named NPCs (stance triangle, decision derivation, contest targeting). Because §2.12 and §8.8 were struck along with Niflhel, she fell out of the stat table without being re-added as an independent named NPC.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §7.10 NPC Stat Values table L1184-L1198 — Virke absent (no TS, no Certainty); 'Dalla Virke' does not appear anywhere in npc_behavior_v30.md (only struck-Niflhel references); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md — no Virke entry (only L188 struck-Niflhel mode ref), despite L190 'Per-NPC sheets ... carry 1-3 Beliefs'; designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md S10 L125-133 has Ethics only, no stat block / Conviction / Beliefs.
- **Fix:** Add Virke to npc_behavior_v30.md §7.10 with canonical TS and Certainty (TS almost certainly <30 — non-practitioner broker), add 1-3 first-person Beliefs to character_canon_v30.md Part B, and state her Conviction per §1.2. Without these she cannot be run in the contest/decision systems.
- **Verifier note:** The TS half of the claim is FALSE. npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L55 states verbatim: 'Virke has no TS (TS 0) and no concept of Thread metaphysics.' So a canonical TS value (0) does exist in a v30 design doc — 'no canonical TS value exist for Virke anywhere' is not reproduced. The rest is true: Virke is absent from the §7.10 NPC Stat Values table (verified L1184-1198 — not listed), has no Conviction value, and has no first-person Beliefs/voice-anchor in character_canon_v30.md (verified — the only Niflhel mention is L188 'replaces struck Niflhel intelligence-broker mode', not a Virke entry; L190 confirms per-NPC sheets carry 1-3 Beliefs). 'Dalla Virke' indeed does not appear anywhere in npc_behavior_v30.md (grep: zero matches — note even the struck refs say 'Niflhel', not 'Virke', so the finding's parenthetical 'only struck-Niflhel references' is slightly imprecise). Also, §7.10 is a selective table — Strand, Feldhaus, Almstedt, Brandt, Solberg, Laskaris are all absent too — so 'fell out of the stat table' is not uniquely a Virke regression. Real but overstated gap (TS exists; Conviction + Beliefs + stat-table row missing for a non-practitioner broker who cannot be a Thread-Contest target). Downgrade to LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-266 — No Certainty value for Virke in the canonical stat table
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §7.10 (NPC Certainty table) + character_canon_v30 §7.4 (Certainty 0-5) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** A named NPC carries a Certainty value (with band label); the canonical §7.10 TS/Certainty table omits Virke entirely, and no Certainty appears in character_canon. Certainty gates Conviction-crisis/Scar movement (character_canon §7.4, §8.1 'Certainty reaches 0 -> Conviction permanently altered'), so its absence leaves her transformation path undefined.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §7.10 L1184-1198 (13 NPCs listed; Virke absent); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md §7.4 L281+ (Certainty 0-5 schema) and §8.1 L322 (Certainty->Conviction trigger) — no Virke value.
- **Fix:** Add Virke to §7.10 with a canonical TS (almost certainly <30 — non-practitioner broker; arc_narrative_analysis L164 explicitly states 'Virke TS 0') and a Certainty value with rationale.
- **Verifier note:** The narrow claim is true: §7.10 (L1184-1198) omits Virke entirely, and character_canon carries no Certainty for her (0 Virke hits). §7.4 (L281) defines Certainty 0-5 and §8.1 (L322) confirms 'Certainty reaches 0 -> Conviction permanently altered,' so the value does gate her transformation path. However, the finding's own fix premise is partly wrong and inflates the gap: it says to add 'a canonical TS (almost certainly <30 ...)' as if TS were unknown, but TS IS already authored — npc_roster_v30_infill.md L55 states 'Virke has no TS (TS 0)' and arc_narrative_analysis.md L164 says 'Virke TS 0' (the prior audit's verifier flagged this same overclaim at AUD-NPC2-099 L931). So the only genuinely-missing items are (a) a Certainty value and (b) propagation of her existing TS 0 + a Certainty into the §7.10 table — the same systemic table-propagation gap, on a single scalar field, for a Tier-2 NPC whose transformation arc is not even individually season-tracked at full Tier-1 depth. Downgraded MEDIUM->LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-267 — No Beliefs authored for Virke, and a natural arc Belief->Scar conversion is left unwired
- **Unit:** Dalla Virke (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §6.1 (Beliefs as 1-3 quoted voice anchors) + §9.1 Tier-2 floor; Belief->Scar mechanic §6.2 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Named/Tier-2 NPCs must carry 1-3 first-person Beliefs (the voice anchors the prose-writer consumes); Virke has none. Her arc supplies an obvious Belief and its Scar conversion — 'I know what I'm selling' is explicitly called 'her brand', and the Thread-touched revelation is that she didn't know — a textbook Belief->Conviction-Scar trigger — but no Belief is stated and no Scar conversion is wired.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md L190 ('Per-NPC sheets in Part B carry 1-3 Beliefs as first-person quoted strings'), §6.2 L195-198 (Belief->Scar triple gate), §9.1 L356 (Tier-2 floor: Beliefs may be implicit but required); latent Belief in designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_10_18.md L195 ("'I know what I'm selling' is her brand, and she didn't know"); no Beliefs for Virke in any doc.
- **Fix:** Author 2-3 first-person Beliefs (e.g., 'Your word is your network'; 'I know what I'm selling') in her sheet/Part B, and wire the reputation-collapse arc (arcs_10_18 Fire at Red Cliffs) as the Belief->Scar conversion on the 'I know what I'm selling' Belief.
- **Verifier note:** Confirmed that no Beliefs are authored for Virke (no Virke entry in character_canon; analyses body empty) and that the arc supplies an obvious latent Belief — arcs_10_18.md L195 "'I know what I'm selling' is her brand, and she didn't know" — plus the §6.2 Belief-revision triple gate (L194-200) and the Fire at Red Cliffs reputation collapse (L222-227). BUT the finding overstates the floor: it asserts 'Named/Tier-2 NPCs MUST carry 1-3 first-person Beliefs,' yet §9.1 (L356) explicitly says for Tier 2 that 'Beliefs may be implicit in Behavioral AI prose' — so explicit quoted Beliefs are NOT a hard requirement at her tier, and her NETWORK PROTECTOR / 'your word is your network' prose already encodes the implicit Belief. The full quoted-Belief requirement (L355) is the Tier-1 floor; Virke is Tier 2 (roster mode-coverage table L381; AUD-NPC2-099 treats her as supporting). So this is an authoring opportunity (wire a strong latent Belief->Scar arc) rather than a floor violation. Downgraded MEDIUM->LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-268 — Section title calls Laskaris 'Altonian Prince' — a superseded label, and internally self-contradictory with 'Doux'
- **Unit:** Doux Alexios Laskaris | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Naming / canon/03_canonical_timeline.md line 180 (supersession) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The roster section titles label Laskaris 'Doux Alexios Laskaris — Altonian Prince', but the body and every other canonical doc title him a 'doux' (provincial military governor / Duke). The single title line is internally contradictory (a Doux is not a Prince). The canonical timeline explicitly records 'Altonian prince' as the OLD value, superseded by editorial decision to 'Altonian Duke (Doux)'.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:135 ('## 11. Doux Alexios Laskaris — Altonian Prince'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:57 (same title) vs :58 body ('Altonian *doux* (provincial governor-general)'); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_index.md:30,76 ('Altonian Prince'); contradicted by canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:180 ("Timeline chat: 'Altonian prince' -> Altonian Duke (Doux). Session 5 editorial.") and consistent 'Doux' usage in designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md:381, designs/arcs/throughline_resolutions_v30.md:236, designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_41_45.md:350.
- **Fix:** Change the roster header/index/infill titles from 'Altonian Prince' to 'Altonian Doux' (or 'Duke (Doux)') to match the editorially ratified designation and the body text. 'Prince' is superseded per timeline line 180.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced every anchor. npc_roster_v30.md:135 is verbatim '## 11. Doux Alexios Laskaris — Altonian Prince (married to Elske)'; npc_roster_v30_infill.md:57 repeats the title while :58 body says 'Altonian *doux* (provincial governor-general)' and :62 states 'Doux = provincial military governor'; npc_roster_v30_index.md:30 repeats 'Altonian Prince'. canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:180 explicitly logs '| Timeline chat: "Altonian prince" | **Altonian Duke (Doux).** Session 5 editorial.' — i.e. 'Altonian prince' is the superseded OLD value. Cross-docs consistently use Doux (faction_canon_v30.md:381, throughline_resolutions_v30.md:236, arcs_41_45.md:350). The internal contradiction (Doux vs Prince) and use of the superseded label are real and accurately cited. Downgraded from MEDIUM to LOW: confined to section-header/index cosmetic labels; the body text and every mechanical/canon doc already use 'Doux' correctly, so there is no live-canon or mechanical impact — a documentation-consistency defect, not a constraint violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-269 — Flip trigger 'Elske Return attempt fails' relies on a failure branch the Return mechanic does not define
- **Unit:** Doux Alexios Laskaris | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Elske Off-Board Card / Return mechanic (params/bg/npcs_special.md) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Laskaris's flip fires on 'Elske Return attempt fails', but the canonical Elske Return mechanic only specifies a success outcome (IP +5); it defines no 'failure' branch or what constitutes a failed Return for the purpose of arming the flip. The trigger Laskaris depends on is thus partially undefined.
- **Evidence:** Trigger: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:141 and :340 ('Elske Return attempt fails'). The Return mechanic (params/bg/npcs_special.md:26) specifies only 'Return: Elske Loyalty >= 6 + IP < 60 + Crown/Lowenritter unit in T4: Military vs Ob 2. IP +5 on success.' — no failure consequence defined.
- **Fix:** Define the failure branch of the Return Military test (e.g., 'on Failure, Laskaris Flip card is armed') in params/bg/npcs_special.md, or restate Laskaris's trigger in terms the Return mechanic already produces (e.g., a failed Military vs Ob 2 result).
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced both sides. npc_roster_v30.md:141 and :340 both arm 'Laskaris Flip' on 'Elske Return attempt fails.' The Return mechanic at params/bg/npcs_special.md:26 specifies only 'Return: Elske Loyalty >= 6 + IP < 60 + Crown/Lowenritter unit in T4: Military vs Ob 2. IP +5 on success.' — there is no defined failure branch, no failure consequence, and nothing tying a failed Return to arming the flip. The trigger is therefore under-specified as written, exactly as the finding states. CONFIRMED at LOW (severity unchanged). Context note: the gap is at the recoverable end of LOW — a failed 'Military vs Ob 2' is the obvious intended meaning — but since the explicit failure branch genuinely is not written in the Return mechanic, the finding stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-270 — Laskaris has NO Resonant Style — yet he is a diplomat and a Primary NPC in two persuasion-driven arcs
- **Unit:** Doux Alexios Laskaris (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (Resonant Style 4-set: Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity); npc_behavior_v30 §3.3 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Each named NPC carries a Primary (often Secondary) Resonant Style = the argument form that moves them in the Contest/social system (ED-516, one of the 4). Laskaris has none in any doc. This is acute because his entire function is social: PCs 'influence Laskaris's disposition' via a social scene (Influence vs Ob 2) to delay the Tutoring Demand, and he is a Primary NPC in arc 38 and arc 45 (the diplomatic Tutoring-Demand crisis). Without a Resonant Style the Contest against him has no defined vector — the GM cannot know what argument form lands, which is the whole point of the social-contest layer. By contrast his own wife Elske (the foil he is defined against) is explicitly given 'Resonant Style: Evidence'.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:339 ('A successful social scene (Influence vs Ob 2) delays the next Tutoring Demand'); designs/arcs/gm_ref/arcs_36_40.md:171 + arcs_41_45.md:350 (Laskaris a 'Primary NPC'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:90 (Elske: 'Resonant Style: Evidence'); ED-516 (canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl:325); grep of all designs/ for Laskaris + Resonant = 0 hits
- **Fix:** Assign Laskaris a Primary Resonant Style consistent with his oikonomia/protective profile — Consequence (he is moved by concrete outcomes for the people/province he protects) is the natural fit, with Solidarity secondary IF a Knot path to Elske is defined (see Certainty/Beliefs finding). Record it in his npc_behavior §2 stat block (which must first be created — see next finding).
- **Verifier note:** Raw fact reproduced: no resonant_style for Laskaris anywhere (npc_registry.yaml NPC-011 has no resonant_style field; Edeyja NPC-001 does at line 46; grep of designs/ for Laskaris+Resonant = 0). He IS a Primary NPC in arc 38 (arcs_36_40.md:171) and arc 45 (arcs_41_45.md:350) — confirmed. But severity is overstated. (1) Per npc_behavior_v30 §11 (PP-661, lines 1218-1259) Laskaris is a Background-tier roster NPC; Background tier 'have no tracked state' and is not required to carry the full social stat block (Resonant Style/Certainty/Beliefs) — the same architecture that DOWNGRADED AUD-NPC2-132. (2) 'The Contest against him has no defined vector' overstates: his social scene is specified as Influence vs Ob 2 (npc_roster_v30.md:339), a flat Ob that resolves without a Resonant Style; Resonant Style modulates argument form but is not a prerequisite for the Ob-2 check. A genuine authoring nicety for a social NPC, but permitted by the tier architecture and non-blocking — LOW, not HIGH.

#### AUD-NPC2-271 — Unratified ED-400 Conviction change (Order->Precedent) is applied as settled canon in the Arc Map while flagged 'pending approval' in §2.3
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-400 (Order->Precedent, pending approval) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** ED-400 changes Baralta's Primary Conviction from Order (stage6 canonical) to Precedent and is explicitly marked 'User approval required'. But the §5.2 Arc Map and the §5.1 transition substrate already treat Precedent as the settled primary ('Precedent reinforced', Memory+Revealing 'Precedent style') with no pending marker, while the migration roster reflects neither Order nor Precedent (it has Faith). The pending change is thus simultaneously (a) live in some docs, (b) flagged unresolved in others, and (c) absent in the roster — three states for one unratified decision.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:91 ('[EDITORIAL: ED-400 — Changed from Order (stage6 canonical) to Precedent ... User approval required.]'); applied without pending marker at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:609 ('Precedent reinforced') and substrate designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:165 ('Memory + Revealing (Precedent style)'); roster reflects neither, designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:156-159.
- **Fix:** Hold the Precedent value consistently as PENDING (carry the ED-400 'pending approval' marker into §5.1/§5.2 and any substrate that uses 'Precedent style'), or ratify ED-400 and then propagate Precedent uniformly — including correcting the migration roster — so all docs agree on a single primary Conviction state.
- **Verifier note:** The finding's central premise is factually wrong and must be corrected. It asserts 'ED-400 changes Baralta's Primary Conviction from Order to Precedent.' But the actual ED-400 ledger entry (archives/editorials/editorial_ledger_archive_801_1000.yaml:121-128) is entirely about VAYNARD: 'Vaynard as southern Einhir heritage, von Lohengramm parallel... TS from environmental exposure,' decision 'Canonical. Reframes Vaynard characterisation and Baralta-Vaynard foil,' status resolved 2026-04-11, tags [npc, vaynard, varfell...]. It says nothing about any Baralta Order->Precedent conviction change. So the inline tag at npc_behavior_v30.md:91 ('[EDITORIAL: ED-400 — Changed from Order ... to Precedent ... User approval required.]') is a MIS-CITATION, not a real pending ED-400 decision living in 'three states.' The finding's framing — that a genuine pending ED-400 change is half-applied — mischaracterizes the defect (which is actually a wrong ledger ID on a self-disclosed provisional refinement). The underlying 'Precedent applied without a pending marker at :609 and at character_canon_v30.md:165 (Precedent style)' observation has some truth, but the note is self-disclosing ('User approval required' inline) so it never masquerades as ratified canon. Given the false premise about what ED-400 is, plus the transparent provisional flag, this is LOW (a sourcing/traceability defect), not a MEDIUM unratified-canon-applied-as-settled finding.

#### AUD-NPC2-272 — Faction misfiling: Baralta filed under 'Ecclesiastical Faction' with cultural template 'ecclesiastical' instead of Hafenmark / hafenmark_procedural
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon §11 D6 / migration_roster §2.3 / npc_behavior §2.3 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The migration roster places Baralta (Hafenmark sovereign claimant) under §2.3 'Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)' with cultural_label: ecclesiastical, while behavior §2.3 places her in Hafenmark with the Categorical Imperative / hafenmark_procedural frame. character_canon D6 surfaces this but the placement is more conspicuous than D6 states: the §2.4 'Hafenmark Faction' slot where she belongs is occupied only by a placeholder 'Hafenmark Speaker', so the real Hafenmark leader is absent from her own faction section while a stub stands in. The ecclesiastical template also imports a Church-coded conviction distribution that compounds the Faith-primary error above.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:135 (header '### §2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)'), :152-160 (Baralta block, cultural_label: ecclesiastical), :181-183 ('### §2.4 Hafenmark Faction' followed by placeholder 'Hafenmark Speaker'); flagged at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:431 (D6); correct faction/template per designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:87-93; canonical hafenmark template designs/provincial/factions_personal_v30.md:182 ('mercantile-procedural ... Hafenmark').
- **Fix:** Move Baralta's entry from migration_roster §2.3 (Ecclesiastical) to §2.4 (Hafenmark) and set cultural_label: hafenmark_procedural, as D6 already recommends (defer the formal edit to Jordan if D6's 'surface, do not fix' hold is still in force, but the recommendation should be recorded on Baralta's sheet).
- **Verifier note:** The placement is reproduced exactly: conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:135 header '### §2.3 Ecclesiastical Faction (Cathedral)', Baralta block at :152-160 with cultural_label: ecclesiastical, and §2.4 'Hafenmark Faction' at :181-183 occupied only by the placeholder 'Hafenmark Speaker' (who, ironically, gets the correct hafenmark_procedural label at :191). The hafenmark_procedural template (conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:205-214: Order 0.30 / Utility 0.20 / Scholastic 0.15...) is indeed the procedural-civic frame matching npc_behavior_v30.md:87-93. BUT the finding is overstated at MEDIUM because its own evidence concedes character_canon_v30.md:431 (D6) already flags this precise misfile as a [CONFLICT], explicitly recommends correction to hafenmark_procedural, and dispositions it 'Surface, do not fix... defer to Jordan.' This is a known, surfaced, deliberately-deferred governance item, not a hidden defect — and the finding's own fix says to 'defer the formal edit to Jordan if D6's hold is still in force.' A consciously-bracketed deferred edit that the corpus already documents is LOW, not MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-273 — Migration roster derives Baralta's full Conviction vector from a PROVISIONAL theology-only doc — likely root cause of the Faith-primary error
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/npcs/baralta_v30.md (PROVISIONAL theology doc) as roster source | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The migration roster cites 'per designs/npcs/baralta_v30.md' as the source for Baralta's entire Conviction vector, but that doc is a PROVISIONAL extraction covering only her theological position (Faith/communion), not her governance Convictions or self-other orientation. Building a full vector from a theology-only source plausibly produced the Faith-0.50 'selfless devotional' profile that conflicts with the behavior/analysis canon. Surfacing as the mechanism behind the contradictions above; no independent fix required beyond reconciling the vector.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:152 ('#### Baralta (per designs/npcs/baralta_v30.md)'); source doc status designs/npcs/baralta_v30.md:1 and :6 ('Status: PROVISIONAL — pending Jordan editorial review') — content scoped to '21. Baralta's Theological Position' only.
- **Fix:** When reconciling Baralta's Conviction vector, source it from the behavior/analysis canon (governance proceduralism, Precedent-led), using baralta_v30.md only for the Faith/theology facet. Note on the roster that baralta_v30.md is theology-scoped and not authoritative for the full vector.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:152 heads Baralta's block '#### Baralta (per designs/npcs/baralta_v30.md)', and that source doc is explicitly PROVISIONAL and theology-scoped: baralta_v30.md:1 ('[PROVISIONAL: Baralta NPC profile — extracted from ... miraculous_event_v30.md §21 (Baralta's Theological Position)') and :6 ('Status: PROVISIONAL — pending Jordan editorial review'), with content limited to '21. Baralta's Theological Position.' Building a full Conviction vector (Faith 0.50 / Virtue 0.20 / Warden 0.10, prior_labels 'Divine Command / Virtue Ethics') from a theology-only source is a plausible mechanism for the Faith-primary 'selfless devotional' profile that conflicts with the governance canon. The finding is correctly scoped as an explanatory LOW note with no independent fix beyond reconciling the vector; it accurately surfaces a probable root cause. LOW stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-274 — Analyses layer treats 'categorical imperative vs divine command' as Baralta's live operative frameworks
- **Unit:** Duchess Inge Baralta (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / alias_registry | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_character_analyses_v30 §6 and its infill describe Baralta's core tension as 'the faith-politics contradiction (categorical imperative vs divine command)' and frame these as her active operative frameworks ('still present — it is her strength and her vulnerability'). Both 'Categorical Imperative' and 'Divine Command' are registered legacy framework labels superseded by PP-684 §6, used here without any [SUPERSEDED] annotation. This keeps a superseded vocabulary live as her characterizing mechanism and conflicts with the resolved conviction vector (whichever of Finding 1 lands).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L103 ('The faith-politics contradiction (categorical imperative vs divine command) is still present'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md §6/L100-103 (Henry VIII framing); references/alias_registry.yaml L600-606 (Categorical Imperative + Divine Command as legacy → PP-684)
- **Fix:** Recast the descriptive tension in conviction-vector terms (e.g., Faith-primary vs Authority/Order pull, or the Faith/Precedent tension per the resolved value) and annotate any retained framework names as [SUPERSEDED descriptive tag] so the analyses layer no longer presents superseded frameworks as live.
- **Verifier note:** Quoted text is real: npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L103 = 'The faith-politics contradiction (categorical imperative vs divine command) is still present — it is her strength and her vulnerability.' But two pillars are weak. (1) FALSE evidence: 'Divine Command' is NOT in alias_registry.yaml — grep for 'divine' returns zero hits; the registry legacy list (L603-611) is Categorical Imperative, Virtue, Faith, Scholastic, Equity, Honor, Reason, Continuity. Only 'Categorical Imperative' is a registered legacy label. ('Divine Command' appears only as a roster prior_label, not in the cited registry.) (2) Scope: D2 (L427) and the supersession scheme target labels 'mechanically active via Ob modifiers'; this analyses text is descriptive thematic prose (Henry VIII parallel), not a mechanical driver, and the L49 '[SUPERSEDED] marker' rule is scoped to 'per-NPC sheets', not analyses prose. So this is at most a descriptive-vocabulary hygiene nit on one legacy term, with the second term mis-cited. LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-275 — Varfell framework-drift mechanic still accrues a Thread-Knowledge (TK) track via 'active investigation' — residual collector mechanic
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-787; ED-707 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The §7.1 Framework Drift table gives Varfell 'TK +0.5 per season of active investigation, caps at TK 3 without PC interaction' — the only faction whose drift accrues a knowledge track rather than a faction stat. This is the mechanical residue of the struck collector identity (ED-787 'not intelligence-themed'); it also feeds the off-canon Scholar→Awakened arc gating. Separately, 'TK' (Thread Knowledge) is used as a gating track across §2.4, §5.2, §7.1, and §8.5 but has no definition located in params/canon, so the arc depends on an under-specified mechanic.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md line 756 (Varfell drift 'TK +0.5 ... Caps at TK 3'), line 831 ('TK advancement opportunity ... Private Collection'); TK used as gate at lines 115, and infill lines 92/96/129; no TK track definition found in params/canon (grep of params/canon/designs/scene returns only NPC-priority/stance files re-citing it).
- **Fix:** Re-scope or remove the Varfell TK-investigation drift to match the opportunist/military identity (e.g., drift toward Military or territorial posture). If a Thread-Knowledge track is retained for the personal-scale arc, give it a single canonical definition and cite it; otherwise replace TK gates with TS thresholds already defined.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md §7.1 line 756 — Varfell drift is 'TK +0.5 per season of active investigation. Caps at TK 3 without PC interaction', and it is the only faction in the table whose drift accrues a knowledge track rather than a faction stat (Church=CI/Influence, Crown=Certainty, Hafenmark=Influence, RM=Presence, Guilds=Favour, Lowenritter=Military). Line 831 ties TK advancement to 'Private Collection'. Mechanical residue of the struck collector identity, feeding the off-canon Scholar->Awakened gating (§5.2/§8.5/arc_expansion). The undefined-TK sub-claim also reproduces: TK is used as a gate (§2.4 line 115, §7.1, §8.5, infill arc maps) but no canonical TK-track definition exists in params/canon. LOW is appropriate — a residual mechanic + under-specification downstream of the higher-severity identity findings. Stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-276 — Conviction values conflict with the designated PRIMARY source, and Vaynard has no proper roster entry (only a STRUCK edge-case row)
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30.md §11 D1 (migration roster = PRIMARY); conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) §3 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Per character_canon §11 D1, designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30 (PP-685) is the PRIMARY source for Conviction values when docs conflict. The roster has NO 'Duke Magnus Vaynard' migration entry; Vaynard appears only as a §3 edge-case row flagged 'per PP-650 STRUCK ... Conviction profile needs clean redraft', with default mapping Authority + Honor + Identity / lowenritter_military / +0.20. That default (a pragmatic militarist) directly conflicts with npc_behavior's Reason/Autonomy (secular knowledge-collector). character_canon §11 D1 itself flags that npc_behavior §2 stance triangles conflict with the roster and must not be silently regenerated.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:256 (§3 edge case: 'Vaynard (per PP-650 STRUCK) ... Conviction profile needs clean redraft | Authority + Honor + Identity, lowenritter_military background; orientation +0.20'); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:35-37 (§2 lists the 13 migrated named characters — Vaynard not among them); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:426 (§11 D1 — roster is primary when sources conflict); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:42 ('Does not silently regenerate npc_behavior_v30 §2 stance triangles from migration roster (though they conflict — see §11 D1)')
- **Fix:** Author a real PP-685 §2 migration entry for Duke Magnus Vaynard (currently a deferred §3 edge case) and treat it as the source of truth. Propagate the redrafted vector into npc_behavior §2.4, factions_personal_v30, and arc_expansion_v30. Until then, npc_behavior's Reason/Autonomy is non-canonical relative to D1.
- **Verifier note:** The literal sub-facts check out: conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:35-245 (s2) has no Vaynard entry; :256 (s3) is a STRUCK edge-case row defaulting to Authority+Honor+Identity / lowenritter_military / +0.20; character_canon_v30.md:426 (D1) does say the roster is primary on conflict, and :42 flags non-regeneration of behavior s2. BUT the finding's operative conclusion -- that behavior's values are 'non-canonical relative to D1' because the roster's Authority/Honor/Identity governs -- is built on stale authority. references/npc_registry.yaml (dated 2026-05-08, self-declared 'Canonical source of truth for ALL named characters' with hard enforcement, one day AFTER character_canon's 2026-05-07 D1 consolidation) records Jordan's reversal at :1047-1051 ('behavior_v30 authoritative over PP-685 for conviction assignments ... Vaynard -> Equity/Utility (was Authority/Honor/Identity) ... Per audit + Jordan 2026-05-08') and :714 ('PP-685 s3 mapping (Authority/Honor/Identity) superseded per Jordan 2026-05-08'). So the s3 default the finding leans on is explicitly dead, and D1's roster-primacy is overturned for Vaynard. What survives is only the narrow, low-severity bookkeeping point that no clean s2 roster entry was ever back-authored (registry still carries ts/age/stats as [GAP]). Downgraded to LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-277 — Framework label (Utility/consequentialist) contradicts the PRIMARY conviction vector (Authority/Honor/Identity)
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §2.3 (Utility/instrumentalism = Utility Conviction + Self-Other, not a separate framework); conviction vector consistency | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** If the migration-roster PRIMARY profile governs (Authority + Honor + Identity, +0.20 self-other), then a 'Utility-driven Pragmatism / consequentialist' framework label mischaracterises the NPC: PP-684 represents Machiavellian instrumentalism via Utility Conviction + high Self-Other, not via an Authority/Honor/Identity vector. The framework label and the conviction vector point at different value-frames for the same NPC. (If instead the 'Reason/Scholastic' collector conception wins, the framework still should be expressed as Scholastic/Utility weights, not a standalone consequentialist Ob driver.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:113 (Utility-driven Pragmatism framework); designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:256 (PRIMARY profile Authority+Honor+Identity); designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:49,63 (§2.1/§2.3 — instrumentalism = Utility Conviction + Self-Other, not a framework); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:95 ('His consequentialism is not philosophical preference')
- **Fix:** After the conviction conception is settled, ensure the disposition tag matches the vector: an Authority/Honor/Identity militarist should not carry a 'consequentialist/Utility' framework label; a Scholastic collector should express Utility as a Conviction weight. Remove the standalone consequentialist Ob driver either way.
- **Verifier note:** The finding's premise is stale and its headline largely self-refutes once current canon is applied. It asserts the PRIMARY vector is Authority/Honor/Identity (migration-roster s3) and that a 'Utility/consequentialist' framework therefore mischaracterises the NPC. But the actual current vector per references/npc_registry.yaml:710-711 (2026-05-08) is Equity 0.35 / Utility 0.30 -- Utility IS one of his two primary Convictions -- so a 'Utility-driven' label is broadly CONSISTENT with the live vector, not contradictory. conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:49,63 (s2.1/s2.3) confirm Machiavellian instrumentalism is modelled as Utility Conviction + Self-Other (and Vaynard's self_other is -0.40), which the registry vector already satisfies. What remains valid is a narrow, low-severity modelling point that overlaps with finding #4: instrumentalism should be expressed as the Utility Conviction weight rather than as a standalone consequentialist Ob driver. The contradiction framing (vector vs label point at different value-frames) does not hold against current canon. Downgraded from MEDIUM to LOW.

#### AUD-NPC2-278 — Arc transformation (Consumed) dissolves his framework but no explicit Belief->Scar conversion is authored
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Conviction/Piety Scar mechanic (conviction_track_v30); arc Belief->Scar conversion | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard carries 3 Beliefs and his Arc C ('Consumed') collapses his Conviction/normative framework with Certainty -> 0, which is exactly the transformation profile that should convert a Belief into a Conviction/Piety Scar. Unlike Baralta (whose Belief 1 -> Scar is explicitly authored in the same infill), no Vaynard Belief is named as converting to a Scar in any arc. Arc A/B in npc_behavior §5.2 also have empty bodies (flagged elsewhere as [GAP]).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:119-122 (Beliefs 1-3); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:627, designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:100-102 (Arc C: 'Reason -> collapses', 'normative framework' dissolves, 'All styles suppressed') with no Belief->Scar; designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:86 (Baralta Belief 1 -> Scar authored, by contrast); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:429 (§11 D4 — Vaynard Arc A/B empty bodies [GAP])
- **Fix:** Author which of Vaynard's 3 Beliefs converts to a Conviction Scar at Arc B/C transition (e.g., Belief 3 'No faction can be trusted ... Varfell alone' or 'Knowledge without application is ornament') and add it to the arc map, mirroring the Baralta Belief->Scar pattern.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced as a gap. npc_behavior_v30.md:119-122 gives Vaynard 3 Beliefs; :620-627 shows Arc A ('The Scholar') and Arc B ('The Awakened') as title-only empty bodies and Arc C ('Consumed') describing Certainty -> 0 / framework dissolution with NO Belief->Scar conversion; npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:100-102 (Arc C) likewise dissolves the normative framework ('Reason -> collapses', 'All styles suppressed') with no named Belief->Scar. By contrast the same infill authors Baralta's Belief 1 -> Scar explicitly at :86. character_canon_v30.md:429 (D4) independently flags Vaynard Arc A/B as empty-body [GAP]. The Consumed profile is exactly the transformation that should trigger a Belief->Scar per the Piety/Conviction Scar mechanic, and none is authored. Correctly a LOW gap, not a contradiction.

#### AUD-NPC2-279 — Historical anchor spelled inconsistently: 'Reinhard' vs 'Reinhardt' von Lohengramm
- **Unit:** Duke Magnus Vaynard (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** internal consistency (historical anchor spelling) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Vaynard's in-character historical anchor is spelled 'Reinhard von Lohengramm' in character_analyses/foils but 'Reinhardt von Lohengramm' in character_canon's alias example and the supersession register. Minor, but it is a canon-consistency snag on the same load-bearing anchor used to define his identity.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:94 ('Reinhard von Lohengramm'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:411 ('Reinhardt von Lohengramm — cunning in service of pride'); canon/supersession_register.yaml:56 ('Reinhardt von Lohengramm parallel'); canon/placeholder_names.yaml:32 ('Reinhardt von Lohengramm-coded')
- **Fix:** Standardise one spelling of the anchor across all docs (the registers and character_canon use 'Reinhardt'; align character_analyses/foils to match, or vice versa).
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. 'Reinhard von Lohengramm' (no t): npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:94 and npc_foils_v30_infill.md:55,103. 'Reinhardt von Lohengramm' (with t): character_canon_v30.md:411, canon/supersession_register.yaml:56, canon/placeholder_names.yaml:32, plus designs/provincial/victory_v30.md:11, varfell_path_b_v30.md:15, peninsular_strain_v30.md:405, faction_state_authoring_v30.md:191. A genuine spelling split on the load-bearing identity anchor; the registers and character_canon favour 'Reinhardt'. Minor citation slip in the finding: its evidence cites npc_foils_v30.md:26, which actually contains the 'expulsion' line, not the spelling -- the real 'Reinhard' instances are in npc_foils_v30_infill.md:55,103. Substance holds. LOW is correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-280 — Edeyja framed as the setting's "moral anchor" — moral valence on a Coherence/TS axis the canon defines as non-moral
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** P-10 / GD-1 (moral-anchor framing); cross-doc consistency | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Three NPC docs canonize Edeyja as the "moral anchor of the setting" / "moral anchor" / "the standard against which other practitioners measure themselves," treating her high Coherence (9) and TS (75-80) as moral standing. P-10 explicitly forbids treating Coherence as moral standing or using moral-judgment language; drift/commensurability is tridimensional, NOT moral. Edeyja's distinction is competence and commensurability, not virtue. Casting the highest-Coherence practitioner as the moral pole of the setting is exactly the moral-valence-on-an-ontological-axis collapse the foundations warn against (P-04/P-10 family). The dedicated doc itself deliberately avoids this framing ("She is not a political or charismatic figure; she is a practitioner"), so the roster/analyses framing also contradicts the canonical dedicated doc.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:12 ("Edeyja is the moral anchor of the setting"); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:169 (summary table "Key Consequence: Moral anchor"), :226 ("Moral anchor — she will not compromise"); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:16 ("competence without compromise" framed as moral exception); canon/02_canon_constraints.md:19 (P-10 "Does any Coherence-drift mechanic ... treat Coherence as moral standing, use language of moral judgment ... If yes -> FAIL"); contrast designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:9,25
- **Fix:** Re-label Edeyja's narrative function away from "moral anchor." Use a commensurability/competence frame consistent with the dedicated doc and the character_analyses §1 better framing ("competence without compromise" / "fixed point of substrate-perception"). Replace "moral anchor" in npc_roster_v30_infill.md:12, npc_roster_v30.md:169 and :226 with e.g. "competence benchmark" / "uncompromised-purpose anchor." Ensure no doc presents her Coherence/TS as ethical superiority.
- **Verifier note:** The three 'moral anchor' strings are real and live: npc_roster_v30_infill.md:12 ('Edeyja is the moral anchor of the setting'), npc_roster_v30.md:169 (summary table 'Moral anchor') and :226 ('Moral anchor — she will not compromise'), all in docs marked Status: CANONICAL; and edeyja_npc.md:9,25 deliberately avoids this framing ('Not a political actor', 'She is not a political or charismatic figure; she is a practitioner'). So a prose tension with the dedicated doc genuinely exists. But HIGH/canon-FAIL overstates it. P-10's violation test (02_canon_constraints.md:19) is explicitly scoped to a 'Coherence-drift MECHANIC' treating Coherence as moral standing — it is not a blanket ban on the word 'moral' in characterization prose. None of the cited lines tie her Coherence(9)/TS(75-80) to ethical superiority as a mechanic: the roster defines her exemption structurally ('Compromise: None' = no faction/ethics conflict arc-trigger, per the roster's own Design Principle at infill:9), and her 'flaw' is 'she is correct and the world is dying anyway' (infill:15), not virtue-via-Coherence. Critically, the finding's own cited 'violation' at npc_character_analyses_v30.md:16 ('competence without compromise') is in fact the COMPLIANT non-moral framing the finding elsewhere recommends — it does not frame her as a moral exception ('compromise' there is the roster's structural term, not ethics). Net: a real label-hygiene inconsistency between roster and dedicated doc worth a copy-edit, not a HIGH mechanical canon violation.

#### AUD-NPC2-281 — Edeyja's Certainty and TS given inconsistent values inside npc_behavior_v30 (Certainty 0 vs 1; TS 75-80 vs "50+")
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Stat consistency (Certainty, TS) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_behavior_v30 §2.9 stat block lists Certainty = 0 (Accepted) and TS = 75-80, but the same file's §12 certainty/TS table lists Edeyja as TS "50+" and Certainty 1. The dedicated doc and §2.9 agree on TS 75-80; the §12 "50+" is both internally inconsistent and understates the canonical apex value. Certainty 0 vs 1 is a one-step discrepancy on a 0-5 axis within a single document.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:217-218 ("Thread Sensitivity | 75-80"; "Certainty | 0 (Accepted)"); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1197 ("| Edeyja | 50+ | 1 | ... Certainty 1"); designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:21 (TS 75-80)
- **Fix:** Correct npc_behavior_v30:1197 to TS 75-80 and reconcile Certainty to a single value (0 Accepted per §2.9, which matches the dedicated doc's "full Thread acceptance / Solmund is a rendering" characterization).
- **Verifier note:** The substantive discrepancy is real and reproduced: npc_behavior_v30.md:217-218 give TS '75-80' and Certainty '0 (Accepted)', while the same file's NPC stat-value table at line 1197 gives Edeyja TS '50+' and Certainty '1'. edeyja_npc.md:21 and the registry confirm TS 75-80, so the '50+' both understates the apex value and conflicts internally; Certainty 0 vs 1 is a one-step intra-doc discrepancy. Two corrections to the finding, both reducing severity: (a) the finding mislabels the location as '§12 certainty/TS table' — it is actually §7.10 'NPC Stat Values (Thread Sensitivity and Certainty)' at line 1184 (§12 is 'CANON COMPLIANCE' at line 1093; the doc has duplicate §7 headers); the line number 1197 is correct. (b) This is a self-contained stat-table staleness within one doc, not a canon-constraint breach. The '50+' reads as a conservative lower-bound bucket rather than a contradictory hard value, and the authoritative npc_registry.yaml carries the correct 75-80. MEDIUM overstates a single stale internal table row; LOW is appropriate.

#### AUD-NPC2-282 — Edeyja missing from conviction_migration_roster_v30 — no PP-684 13-Conviction vector authored
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 conviction migration coverage | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** conviction_migration_roster_v30 maps every named roster NPC's legacy Conviction labels onto the canonical 13-Conviction weighted vector (primary convictions + weights + cultural template). Edeyja — roster NPC #1 and the "gold standard" dedicated doc — has no entry. Her legacy "Continuity/Reason" labels are therefore never anchored to a canonical vector, leaving the engine-facing personal_convictions undefined for the highest-profile NPC and leaving the cross-doc "Warden vs Continuity" inconsistency (above) unresolvable from a single source of truth.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md (entries for Almud §41, Cesare §56, Lorenzo §71, Maret Uln §104, Lenneth §167, Hafenmark Speaker §183, RM §219, Niflhel §232, plus Varfell entries — no Edeyja entry anywhere in file); designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:18 (Edeyja is NPC #1); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:23 (edeyja_npc "gold standard")
- **Fix:** Add an Edeyja entry to conviction_migration_roster_v30: prior_labels ["Continuity","Reason"]; primary_convictions e.g. {Warden: 0.55-0.65} dominant + {Scholastic}/{Identity} secondary; cultural_label einhir_traditional; self_other orientation; notes anchoring to edeyja_npc.md Conviction: Warden.
- **Verifier note:** The literal sub-claim is true: conviction_migration_roster_v30.md has no Edeyja entry (verified — its §2 maps a fixed set of 13 named characters; Almud, Cesare, Lorenzo, Yrsa, Maret Uln, Solmund, Reichard, Baralta, Lenneth, Hafenmark Speaker, Lowenritter Master, Restoration Spokesperson, Niflhel envoy). But the finding's framing is wrong on two counts. (1) The premise that this roster 'maps every named roster NPC' is false: roster NPCs #5-13 (Torsvald, Brandt, Feldhaus, Almstedt, Strand, Virke, Laskaris, Solberg) are ALSO absent (grep returned zero) — the doc is an explicitly non-exhaustive curated subset with a §4 'For each character not in this roster' process. Edeyja is not singled out. (2) The headline claim 'no PP-684 13-Conviction vector authored' / 'engine-facing personal_convictions undefined' is FALSE: references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-001 (lines 37-43) authors her full vector — primary Warden 0.40 + Precedent 0.20, cultural_label varfell_alpine, self_other -0.30, with migration_notes anchoring legacy 'Continuity'->Warden+Precedent per PP-685 §4 — and the registry's Open Issues line 1025 records '[RESOLVED] CONVICTION MIGRATION: All NPCs with legacy labels mapped to 13-Conviction vectors.' The auditor checked only the migration-roster doc and missed the registry. The 'gap' is filled in the authoritative engine source; at most this is a LOW doc-placement nit (a back-reference from the roster would be tidy).

#### AUD-NPC2-283 — Dedicated doc mislabels P-codes in its own "Canon Compliance" section ("P-02 (Ein Sof = fullness)")
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** Canon-citation correctness (dedicated doc Canon Compliance section) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** edeyja_npc.md's Canon Compliance section cites "P-02 (Ein Sof = fullness)" to justify that the Southernmost damage is rendering failure not absence. But canonical P-02 is "Monstrosity is grounded in the Lacanian Real"; "Ein Sof = infinite positive being/fullness" is foundations §2.1, and the substrate-damage point is actually covered by P-07 (Calamity = rendered-side) and the Ein-Sof-fullness foundation, not P-02. A compliance section that miscites the constraint it claims to satisfy is a defect, especially in the doc held up as the "gold standard."
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md:90 ("P-02 (Ein Sof = fullness)"); canon/02_canon_constraints.md:11 (P-02 = Lacanian Real); canon/00_philosophical_foundations.md:85-89 (§2.1 Ein Sof as Infinite Positive Being / fullness — not P-02); canon/02_canon_constraints.md:16 (P-07 Calamity = rendered-side)
- **Fix:** Relabel the dedicated doc's first compliance bullet from "P-02 (Ein Sof = fullness)" to the correct reference — Foundations §2.1 (Ein Sof fullness) and/or P-07 (rendered-side Calamity). Leave the P-07 and P-13 bullets as-is (those P-numbers are correct).
- **Verifier note:** The miscitation is real and reproduced exactly: edeyja_npc.md:90 reads 'P-02 (Ein Sof = fullness)'. Per 02_canon_constraints.md:11, P-02 is 'Monstrosity is grounded in the Lacanian Real'; the Ein-Sof-as-fullness content is foundations §2.1 (00_philosophical_foundations.md:85-91, 'The Ein Sof as Infinite Positive Being'), and the rendered-side substrate-damage point is P-07 (02_canon_constraints.md:16). So the label attached to the bullet is the wrong P-number. However: the finding's own evidence confirms the very next two bullets (:91 P-07 Calamity = rendered-side; :92 P-13 Forgetting = rendering failure) are correctly numbered, so the section is substantively compliant and the error is one mislabeled reference tag in prose, not a content/compliance failure. The bullet's actual claim (Southernmost damage = rendering failure not absence) is itself canon-correct. A single wrong P-code in a narrative compliance note — even in the 'gold standard' doc — is a LOW-severity citation typo, not MEDIUM.

#### AUD-NPC2-284 — Stale canonical path: roster cites Edeyja's dedicated doc at non-existent designs/ttrpg/edeyja_npc.md
- **Unit:** Edeyja | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** Cross-reference integrity | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** npc_roster_v30.md cites the canonical Edeyja design as "designs/ttrpg/edeyja_npc.md," but that directory does not exist; the file lives at designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md (correctly cited by character_canon_v30 and the npc_behavior cross-reference lists). A broken path to the single canonical source of truth for the #1 NPC.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:20 ("Existing design: designs/ttrpg/edeyja_npc.md (canonical)"); verified no designs/ttrpg/ directory exists; correct path designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md cited at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:23
- **Fix:** Update npc_roster_v30.md:20 path to designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced and independently verified. npc_roster_v30.md:20 reads 'Existing design: designs/ttrpg/edeyja_npc.md (canonical).' I confirmed via ls that C:/Github/ttrpg/designs/ttrpg does not exist, and that the file actually lives at designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md (ls succeeded). The correct path is used elsewhere (character_canon_v30.md:23; npc_registry.yaml:47 'source: designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md'). A genuine broken cross-reference to the #1 NPC's source of truth. LOW is the right severity — a one-line path typo with no mechanical effect, easily fixed.

#### AUD-NPC2-285 — Certainty value is inconsistent across docs (0 vs 1 vs absent) and the dedicated sheet omits it
- **Unit:** Edeyja (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** alias_registry certainty (PP-551, range 0-5); CERTAINTY/BELIEFS taxonomy | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Three different Certainty states exist for Edeyja: §2.9 stat block = 0 (Accepted); the NPC Certainty roster table = 1; the dedicated canonical sheet edeyja_npc.md carries no Certainty value at all. A named, mechanically central NPC must carry a single consistent Certainty. (Note: both 0 and 1 are within the canonical 0-5 band per alias_registry PP-551 - the audit brief's '0-7 band' is itself stale - so the issue is the cross-doc inconsistency, not an out-of-range value.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.9 line 218 ('Certainty | 0 (Accepted)') vs the Certainty roster table line 1197 ('Edeyja | 50+ | 1 | Warden. Certainty 1 - Thread reality is her primary frame.'); dedicated sheet designs/npcs/edeyja_npc.md 'Conviction and Resonant Style' section lines 29-32 (no Certainty); canonical scale references/alias_registry.yaml certainty range '0-5' note PP-551.
