- **Verifier note:** Reproduced and accurately characterized. npc_behavior_v30 §2.5 lines 137-138 annotate 'ED-392 resolved' (TS) and 'ED-393 resolved' (Certainty). In editorial_ledger.jsonl, live ED-392 (line 279) is a Varfell+RM co-victory item (marked _migration_partial), and grep finds NO live ED-393 through ED-398; the Ehrenwall TS/Cert resolutions are ED-523/ED-524 (lines 332-333). The values are nonetheless correct: ED-687 (line 428) and archived AUD-NPC-02 (archive line 1017) both state 'Ehrenwall TS=0/Cert=4 ... Resolves ED-392 through ED-398,' so ED-392/393 trace to the AUD-NPC-02 batch's original numbering. The finding explicitly says 'values are correct, but the citation IDs are stale/ambiguous,' which is exactly right. The same stale-ED-citation pattern is independently confirmed elsewhere (comprehensive audit line 900, Almud's mis-cited 'ED-398'). Correctly scoped as NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-408 — Prior audit logged a generic 'no Conviction/Certainty/TS' gap (downgraded) but did not perform this taxonomy-specific pass
- **Unit:** Halvar Brandt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md AUD-NPC2-112 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** A prior comprehensive audit already flagged that Brandt lacks Conviction/Certainty/TS/Military stat (AUD-NPC2-112, verdict DOWNGRADED). That finding is generic and does NOT cover the taxonomy-specific defects raised here: absence from the PRIMARY migration roster (D1), the unanchored §4-default registry vector, the total absence of a Resonant Style, and the unmarked 'Protective consequentialism' framework tag. Recording the overlap so these are not dismissed as duplicates — they are deeper, and the prior verdict's downgrade should be revisited given the migration-roster-primary-source rule.
- **Evidence:** designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md AUD-NPC2-112 (L186 summary + L1017-1022 detail: 'Brandt has no Conviction, Certainty, TS... DOWNGRADED'; fix proposes Order/Autonomy). Note that proposed fix's 'Autonomy' is itself a legacy label (->Liberty per PP-684), whereas registry NPC-006 uses the corrected taxonomy (Honor/Authority).
- **Fix:** Reconcile the two: adopt the registry's PP-684-correct Honor/Authority vector (not the audit's 'Order/Autonomy'), and treat the four defects above as the actionable remediation of the downgraded AUD-NPC2-112.
- **Verifier note:** Cross-reference verified factually. AUD-NPC2-112 exists at designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md (summary row L186; detail L1017-1022), unit Halvar Brandt, category gap, verdict DOWNGRADED to LOW, covering 'no Conviction, Certainty, TS, or numeric Military stat'. Its proposed fix does suggest 'Order/Autonomy' (L1021), and 'Autonomy' is indeed a legacy label superseded to Liberty (npc_behavior_v30 §1.2 note L30 'Autonomy is renamed Liberty'; stale usage persists in Vaynard §2.4 L112). Registry NPC-006 uses the corrected Honor/Authority (L159-160). So the NOTE's factual content — prior generic gap, downgraded, with a fix that itself uses a legacy label, vs the registry's PP-684-correct vector — is accurate. As a NOTE it asserts no severity beyond recording the overlap; verified and appropriate. (Its editorial suggestion to 'revisit the downgrade' is a recommendation, and on my review the prior LOW downgrade is in fact the better-supported severity.)

#### AUD-NPC2-409 — §7.10 cites ED-398 as the basis for Almud's TS, but ED-398 is the Maret Uln Certainty item
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §7.10 vs editorial ledger (ED-398) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** The §7.10 Stat Values cell for Almud reads 'TS 0 per ED-398', but ED-398 is not an Almud TS decision — ED-398 concerns Maret Uln's Certainty. The (correct) TS-0 value is anchored to a wrong editorial authority, which undermines traceability when reconciling the TS 28/0 contradiction above.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1186 (§7.10 'Almud | 0 | 3 | TS 0 per ED-398.'); the editorial ledger has no Almud-TS ED-398 (ED-398 is unrelated). Cross-ref AUD-NPC2-095 in designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md.
- **Fix:** Replace the ED-398 citation with the correct authority for Almud TS 0 — the canon/03_canonical_timeline.md 2026-04-11 override ('Almud TS 28 -> TS 0') — or the actual editorial item that ratified it. Do not leave the canonical TS cell pointing at the Maret Uln Certainty ED.
- **Verifier note:** The auditor's premise that 'ED-398 is the Maret Uln Certainty item' is only half-true and the citation is defensible. There is a genuine ID collision: editorial_ledger_archive_801_1000.yaml:103-110 defines ED-398 as 'Almud TS revised to 0 ... Discovery Event as rupture not confirmation' (priority P1-BLOCKER, date 2026-04-11, tags [npc, almud, crown, characterisation, thread_sensitivity]) — exactly the override date in the canonical timeline. The source session log archive/session/session_log_archive_part_2.md:451 also attributes 'Almud - TS 0 ... (ED-398).' The competing Maret-Uln ED-398 is a separate P3 gap-list line in npc_behavior_v30.md §10:1070, folded into batch ED-687 (which only resolved Ehrenwall/Torben/Maret). So 'TS 0 per ED-398' at :1186 actually points at a real, canonically-archived Almud-TS-0 decision; the defect is a cross-numbering collision, not a citation pointing at an unrelated item. The finding propped its LOW on 'the citation is wrong, undermining traceability,' which the archive contradicts. Downgraded LOW->NOTE: worth recording the ED-398 collision, but it does not undermine the TS-0 value (which is independently anchored to the timeline override).

#### AUD-NPC2-410 — Reformer/Fortress arc set is correctly framed as non-moral — clean on P-04/P-10
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-04 / P-10 (no moral framing of arc/transformation); npc_behavior_v30 §12 Canon Compliance | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The task flagged the Reformer/Fortress arcs for possible moral-transformation framing. Verification: the arcs are framed as worldview/governance shifts (Certainty decline, Order doubling-down, Conviction/Resonant-Style reconfiguration), not moral fall or corruption, and the doc's §12 explicitly asserts 'No NPC is evil ... Arc emergence produces transformation, not corruption.' No P-04/P-10 violation; surfaced only to record that the dimension was checked and is clean. (Almud also has no Coherence-drift arc, so P-12 knot-strain propagation does not apply — §5.0b correctly excludes him; the only defect there is the stale TS value, captured in finding 1.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:556-571 (§5.2 Arc A Reformer / Arc B Fortress / Arc C Overthrown — worldview/stat framing); :1101 (§12 'No NPC is evil — they are structurally positioned. Arc emergence produces transformation, not corruption'); :528 (§5.0b correctly lists Almud as no-threshold-crossing, no strain propagation).
- **Fix:** No fix required. (If desired, the §12 constraint-ID labels themselves are mis-numbered doc-wide — e.g. 'P-04' content is filed under a 'Foundations §4.1' line and P-02/P-06/P-12 labels are swapped — but that is a doc-level issue, not specific to this NPC.)
- **Verifier note:** Verified the clean status. §5.2:556-571 frames Arc A (Reformer)/B (Fortress)/C (Overthrown) in worldview/governance/stat terms (Certainty decline, Order doubling-down, Conviction/Resonant-Style reconfiguration) with no moral-fall or corruption language. §12:1101 explicitly asserts 'No NPC is "evil" - they are structurally positioned. Arc emergence produces transformation, not corruption.' No P-04/P-10 violation. Also confirmed §5.0b:528 correctly excludes Almud from knot-strain propagation (no threshold crossing), so P-12 does not apply - the only defect there is the stale TS value already captured in finding 1. The finding's side-note that §12 constraint-ID labels are mis-numbered is also accurate (line 1101's P-04 content is filed under a 'Foundations §4.1' label, and 1098 labels Inseparability as 'P-02' which is actually P-01) - a doc-level issue, not NPC-specific. Recorded clean; NOTE correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-411 — 'Almud' is live v30 canon, not a superseded/placeholder name
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** CLAUDE.md naming rule vs canon/03_canonical_timeline.md + references/proper_noun_registry.yaml:119-121 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The audit naming premise lists 'Almud' among superseded/placeholder names to be flagged if used as live canon. That premise is incorrect for this NPC: 'King Almud Almqvist' (alias 'Almud') is the canonical Crown sovereign per the canonical timeline and the proper-noun registry, and is not present in supersession_register.yaml or placeholder_names.yaml. Bare use of 'Almud' is therefore correct, not a naming violation; the only naming defect is the 'Crown Captain' title artifact (separate finding).
- **Evidence:** references/proper_noun_registry.yaml:119-121 ('king_almud_almqvist: canonical: "King Almud Almqvist", aliases: ["Almud"]'), :436; canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:138; absent from canon/supersession_register.yaml and canon/placeholder_names.yaml. Consistent with NOTE AUD-NPC2-140 in designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md.
- **Fix:** No fix required. Do not strike or rename bare 'Almud' usages as if they were placeholder contamination.
- **Verifier note:** Verified. references/proper_noun_registry.yaml:119-121 registers 'king_almud_almqvist: canonical "King Almud Almqvist", aliases ["Almud"]' as live canon, and canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:138 lists him among Key Figures at game start. Grep for 'almud' (case-insensitive) in both canon/supersession_register.yaml and canon/placeholder_names.yaml returned NO matches, confirming he is absent from both registries. Therefore bare 'Almud' is correct live-canon usage, not placeholder contamination, and must not be struck/renamed. The audit naming premise listing 'Almud' as a placeholder is incorrect for this NPC (the only naming defect is the separate 'Crown Captain' artifact). NOTE correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-412 — Framework label 'Virtue' contradicts the live stat block's own conviction primary (Order)
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** ETHICAL FRAMEWORK vs CONVICTION coherence (D2); CONVICTION taxonomy | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Within npc_behavior §2.1, the Ethical Framework is 'Virtue (Crown)' while the Primary Conviction is 'Order' and Secondary is 'Reason' — Virtue is neither. The constraint flags 'an NPC whose framework label contradicts their conviction vector' as a framework violation. (The contradiction dissolves only if the stat block is corrected to the roster's Virtue/Authority vector per the HIGH conviction finding — which is further evidence the stat block, not the roster, is the stale artifact.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.1 lines 50-52 (Conviction Order/Reason vs Framework Virtue); contrast roster vector Virtue/Authority at designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md §2.1 lines 45-48
- **Fix:** Resolve jointly with the conviction-vector fix: once Primary Conviction is set to Virtue (per PP-685), the 'Virtue' disposition tag becomes coherent. If Order is retained, the disposition tag must be re-derived (Order does not map to a Virtue-Ethics disposition).
- **Verifier note:** The textual juxtaposition is real (§2.1 lines 50-52: Conviction Order/Reason alongside Framework Virtue). But this finding is wholly derivative of finding #1 (it dissolves the moment the conviction text is reconciled to the roster's Virtue/Authority, which the registry has already done) and the 'framework label contradicts conviction vector = framework violation' rule it invokes is not a codified constraint — 02_canon_constraints.md contains no such test. It is a paraphrase. Given that the canonical registry already carries Virtue/Authority (making the disposition tag coherent there) and the §2.1 mismatch is the same deferred-prose-propagation already captured in #1, this is a NOTE-level corollary, not an independent MEDIUM finding.

#### AUD-NPC2-413 — Resonant Style dimension is clean — Consequence/Solidarity are both in the 4-set and the Solidarity Knot path exists
- **Unit:** King Almud Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** RESONANT STYLE 4-set (ED-516); Solidarity Knot path (AUD-NPC-01) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** For completeness: Almud's Primary Resonant Style (Consequence) and Secondary (Solidarity) are both within the canonical four (Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity per ED-516), and are consistent across §2.1, the arc map, and character_canon. The Solidarity secondary correctly carries a Knot prerequisite ('Activates only via intimate Knot'), and an in-play Knot-formation procedure exists (AUD-NPC-01 resolved; params/core.md §Knot Formation), so Solidarity is reachable post-character-creation. No violation on the Resonant Style axis itself — flagged only to record it was audited and found clean (the Evidence sub-issue is filed separately above as a P-08/§6.1b note, not a style-set violation).
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.1 lines 53-54; ED-516 at canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl line 325; Knot-formation at params/core.md line 149 (AUD-NPC-01)
- **Fix:** No change required to the style set. (Address the Arc-B 'Authority only' and Arc-C 'all styles suppressed' transitions only insofar as they ride on the conviction/label fixes above.)
- **Verifier note:** Verified as an accurate clean-audit note. npc_behavior_v30 §2.1 lines 53-54 give Primary=Consequence, Secondary=Solidarity ('Activates only via intimate Knot'); editorial_ledger.jsonl line 325 (ED-516) confirms the canonical four styles are Evidence/Consequence/Authority/Solidarity; params/core.md line 149 confirms the AUD-NPC-01 Knot-formation procedure exists (Disposition +5 + TS ≥ 30 + capacity → Spirit pool vs TN 7, Ob 2), making the Solidarity secondary reachable post-creation. No violation on the style axis. Correctly logged as a NOTE recording the axis was audited and found clean. Confirmed.

#### AUD-NPC2-414 — Lenneth's funding network named 'People's Revolution endowment' — appears stale vs canonical 'Restoration Movement'
- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md §2 L85 — 'People's Revolution' vs canonical 'Restoration Movement' | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Lenneth's covert funding network is called the 'People's Revolution endowment' in a live v30 doc. The canonical faction is the Restoration Movement (RM) throughout the roster/behavior docs, and Lenneth is repeatedly described as pro-Restoration / allied with the RM leader. 'People's Revolution' reads as superseded flavor naming.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L85: 'Covert networks: People's Revolution endowment, sea-republic archive channel.' Registry NPC-032 L450 describes Lenneth as 'Pro-Restoration … allied with Yrsa Vossen' (RM leader). designs/audit/2026-06-11-canon-flatten/canon_flatten_examination.md L121 flags 'hidden funding network ("Revolution" naming stale — ED-061 Restoration).' CAVEAT: the cited supersession authority does not verify — canon/editorial_ledger.jsonl L61 shows ED-061 is about projectile weapon categories, not a Revolution→Restoration rename — so the rename authority needs confirmation before applying.
- **Fix:** Confirm the correct supersession authority for Revolution→Restoration (the cited ED-061 is unrelated), then rename 'People's Revolution endowment' to the canonical Restoration-Movement framing in npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md §2 (and any sibling roster reference).
- **Verifier note:** The text reproduces: npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md L85 'Covert networks: People's Revolution endowment, sea-republic archive channel.' Lenneth is described pro-Restoration / allied with Yrsa Vossen (RM leader) at registry NPC-032 L448, L450 and infill L86. BUT the claimed supersession authority does NOT exist: editorial_ledger.jsonl L61 shows ED-061 is 'Projectile weapon categories' (2 categories Piercing/Blunt, PP-188), entirely unrelated to any Revolution->Restoration rename. The finding itself flags this caveat. Without a verified rename authority, 'People's Revolution' cannot be asserted to be a superseded/forbidden term — 'Revolution' is not in placeholder_names/supersession scope per the cited (wrong) authority, and 'People's Revolution endowment' as the proper name of a covert funding vehicle is not self-evidently the same referent as the 'Restoration Movement' faction. This is an unverified naming-consistency suspicion contingent on locating a real authority first; reduced from LOW to NOTE (flag for confirmation, not an actionable canon defect as cited).

#### AUD-NPC2-415 — Prior audit logged the Lenneth conviction contradiction but DOWNGRADED it; registry's 2026-05-08 adjudication supersedes that downgrade
- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md AUD-NPC2-046 vs npc_registry.yaml resolved-issues log | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** A prior comprehensive audit (2026-06-22) recorded the foils/migration-roster Lenneth conviction conflict as AUD-NPC2-046 and a verifier marked it DOWNGRADED, framing it as 'two contradictory canonical Lenneth characters.' That downgrade understates the issue: the npc_registry resolved-issues log (dated 2026-05-08, predating the audit) already established there is ONE Lenneth (Crown) and that the ecclesiastical profile is a misattribution requiring source correction. The contradiction is therefore a known, directed-to-fix defect, not a soft coherence quibble — surfacing so the downgrade is not treated as closure.
- **Evidence:** designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md L120 + L550 (AUD-NPC2-046, 'contradiction', MEDIUM, 'DOWNGRADED', citing npc_foils_v30 L79 + npc_character_analyses §2 vs conviction_migration_roster §2.3). Superseding adjudication: designs/npcs/npc_registry.yaml L1036-1040 ('[RESOLVED] LENNETH … MISATTRIBUTION — needs correction in PP-685 … Per Jordan 2026-05-08') and NPC-032 note L452. No fix required for this audit beyond executing the HIGH findings above; recorded to prevent the prior downgrade being read as resolution.
- **Fix:** No new mechanical change beyond the HIGH-severity corrections above. When closing those, reference AUD-NPC2-046 and the registry's 2026-05-08 adjudication so the prior DOWNGRADED status is reconciled to RESOLVED.
- **Verifier note:** The procedural facts reproduce: 2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md L120 + L549 record AUD-NPC2-046 (contradiction, MEDIUM, DOWNGRADED); registry resolved log L1036-1040 (Jordan 2026-05-08) establishes one Lenneth (Crown) with the ecclesiastical profile a misattribution. So the underlying misfile is a known directed-to-fix defect — that part is accurate and is already captured by finding 1. However, the finding's framing that the registry adjudication 'supersedes' the audit's DOWNGRADE and that the downgrade 'understates the issue' is itself overstated: the audit's verifier note (L554) already reached the SAME conclusion as the registry — it expressly says the migration-roster entry 'is plainly intended as the same Lenneth, not a distinct NPC ... a single-character conviction-VALUE conflict' and that 'the foils do not themselves propagate a contradiction — they are under-specified.' The DOWNGRADE to MEDIUM (from a 'two contradictory characters' HIGH framing) was the CORRECT call and is consistent with, not contradicted by, the 2026-05-08 adjudication; both agree it is a single mislabel needing source correction. This is a bookkeeping/reconciliation note with no independent mechanical content beyond finding 1; NOTE severity is appropriate, and no 'closure' error is actually demonstrated.

#### AUD-NPC2-416 — If Lenneth's Resonant Style is set to Solidarity, a Knot path must be defined
- **Unit:** Lenneth Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** ED-516 (Solidarity Knot path) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Not currently a violation because no style is assigned, but flagged forward: Lenneth's default arc operates through a long-standing intimate relationship (she persuades Almud across years within the royal household), which is a natural Solidarity-style reading. If an author resolves her Primary Resonant Style to Solidarity, ED-516 requires an active Knot with the persuading party; absent a defined Knot path that would itself become a violation.
- **Evidence:** Relationship basis at designs/npcs/npc_foils_v30_infill.md:30 ('Lenneth has been making her case within the royal household for years'); Solidarity-requires-Knot rule referenced in designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md (Knot/corroboration gating, e.g. :137)
- **Fix:** When assigning her Resonant Style, prefer Evidence/Consequence (no Knot prerequisite); if Solidarity is chosen, define the Knot path (with whom, and how PCs establish/leverage it).
- **Verifier note:** Correctly self-scoped as a forward-looking conditional, not a current violation. Verified the underlying facts: npc_foils_v30_infill.md:30 establishes the long-standing intimate-household relationship ('Lenneth has been making her case within the royal household for years'), and the Solidarity-requires-active-Knot rule is canonical (character_canon_v30.md:168 in the Resonant Style table: Solidarity '**requires active Knot with the NPC**'; corroboration/Knot gating in social_contest_v30.md:137). Because no Style is assigned (per finding #6), this is inert today; it only becomes a constraint if an author resolves her Primary Style to Solidarity. Accurate as a NOTE; the recommendation to prefer Evidence/Consequence (no Knot prerequisite) is sound.

#### AUD-NPC2-417 — Relational graph asserts Cesare<->Lorenzo 'brother-rivalry is canonical' and 'authored', but upstream docs state no kin/rivalry relation and mark Lorenzo provisional
- **Unit:** Lorenzo | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_relational_graph_v30 §4.5/§5.1 vs conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 + character_canon_v30 §11 D7 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The relational graph lists 'Lorenzo <-> Cesare alternative-succession-pair' under 'Authored: scenario init may specify canonical rivalries' (§4.5) and states 'Brother-rivalry is canonical (Cesare <-> Lorenzo arc context)' (§5.1). Upstream, the migration roster §2.1 lists Cesare and Lorenzo only as independent Crown succession alternates with conviction profiles and states NO kinship or rivalry relation between them, and character_canon §11 D7 marks their status 'succession_alternate (per sim §1.2) rather than authored-active.' Asserting a kin+rivalry edge as 'canonical/authored' for a pair the source docs treat as provisional sim-continuity (and never establish as brothers) is an overclaim. This was already verifier-graded NOTE in the standing audit; recorded here at the same tier for consistency.
- **Evidence:** Overclaim: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:302 ('Authored: ... Lorenzo <-> Cesare alternative-succession-pair') and :365 ('Kinship | Rivalry | YES | Brother-rivalry is canonical (Cesare <-> Lorenzo arc context)'). Upstream: designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84 (no kin/rivalry stated) and :56-69 (Cesare, likewise); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:432 (status succession_alternate ... rather than authored-active). Prior verdict: designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:1260-1263 (NOTE, confirmed).
- **Fix:** Soften the graph's wording from 'is canonical/authored' to 'illustrative example (succession_alternate per sim §1.2; relation not yet authored)', or actually author the kinship+rivalry edge in the registry/migration roster so the 'canonical' claim is backed.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced exactly as filed, at the same NOTE tier. npc_relational_graph_v30.md:302 reads '**Authored:** scenario init may specify canonical rivalries (Cesare ↔ Almud succession; Lorenzo ↔ Cesare alternative-succession-pair)' and :365 reads 'Kinship | Rivalry | YES | Brother-rivalry is canonical (Cesare ↔ Lorenzo arc context).' Upstream, conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84 lists Lorenzo (and :56-69 Cesare) only as independent Crown succession alternates 'per sim §1.2' with NO kinship or rivalry relation stated between them, and character_canon_v30.md:432 (D7) marks status 'succession_alternate (per sim §1.2) rather than authored-active.' The graph thus asserts a kin+rivalry edge as canonical/authored for a pair the sources never establish as brothers and treat as provisional sim-continuity. I tried to refute it but could not: no upstream doc establishes the kinship anywhere. The standing audit graded the identical issue NOTE (AUD-NPC2-146, designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:1258-1263, 'Confirmed at NOTE'). It is an illustrative-exemplar phrasing issue in a PROVISIONAL doc whose §12 B2 already defers edge authoring, so NOTE is correct — not higher.

#### AUD-NPC2-418 — Lorenzo's entire profile derives from a simulation-evaluation artifact, not a ratified character source
- **Unit:** Lorenzo | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_migration_roster_v30 §2.1 notes ('per sim §1.2'); character_canon_v30 §11 D7 + Part B status | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Every Lorenzo value (Virtue 0.35 / Scholastic 0.30 / Authority 0.10, self_other -0.20, valorian_court) is justified 'per sim §1.2'. That citation does not resolve to a ratified design/character doc — the only place the baseline 'Virtue:0.5, Scholastic:0.4, Authority:0.1' originates is the architecture-session simulation-evaluation artifact 05_PP-686_simulation_evaluation.md §1.2, where Lorenzo was a test successor ('principled scholar'). The character_canon entry that would canonicalize him (Part B NPC sheet) is explicitly 'pending Q1 scope decision'. So Lorenzo is provisional sim-continuity resting on an evaluation artifact, not authored canon. Surfacing as provenance, not a violation.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:81-83 ('Per sim §1.2 (Virtue:0.5, Scholastic:0.4, Authority:0.1 baseline).'); source artifact designs/audit/2026-04-30-architecture-session/05_PP-686_simulation_evaluation.md:59-67 (§1.2 Succession Dynamics; 'Lorenzo (Virtue 0.5, Scholastic 0.4, Authority 0.1) — principled scholar'); Part B pending: designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:2 and :439.
- **Fix:** No fix required while provisional. On Part B authoring, re-anchor the 'per sim §1.2' citation to a real character source (or fold the sim baseline into the registry entry) so the provenance chain does not dead-end at an evaluation artifact.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced; provenance, not a violation, and correctly filed NOTE. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:81-83 justifies Lorenzo's values 'Per sim §1.2 (Virtue:0.5, Scholastic:0.4, Authority:0.1 baseline).' I traced the citation: designs/audit/2026-04-30-architecture-session/05_PP-686_simulation_evaluation.md:59-67 is the sole origin — §1.2 Succession Dynamics, 'Lorenzo (Virtue 0.5, Scholastic 0.4, Authority 0.1) — principled scholar,' a test successor in a sim battery, not a ratified character. The canonicalizing entry is 'pending Q1 scope decision' (character_canon_v30.md:2 and :439). So the provenance chain dead-ends at an architecture-session evaluation artifact — accurately described. One precision correction to the finding's framing: the host conviction_migration_roster_v30.md is itself a CANONICAL-but-PROVISIONAL Class A doc (header lines 1-9), so Lorenzo is 'provisional canon resting on a sim artifact' rather than wholly non-canon; this does not change the disposition. No constraint is breached and the finding requests no fix while provisional. Correct as NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-419 — Migration-rationale and inspiration coherence: Authority 0.10 is off-mapping, and 'Lorenzo' (Medici) is cast as brother to 'Cesare' (Borgia)
- **Unit:** Lorenzo | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** conviction_taxonomy_v30 §6 mapping table (line 279) + §4.1; npc inspiration framing (character_canon §10.3) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Two intentional-but-novel choices worth surfacing. (1) Mapping: Lorenzo's prior_labels are ['Virtue Ethics','Epistemic Reason']; the taxonomy's own legacy map sends Epistemic Reason -> 'Scholastic + (situational; some Utility)', and Virtue Ethics -> Virtue. The third primary Authority 0.10 is produced by neither label — it comes only from the sim baseline override. The result is mechanically clean (primary sum 0.35+0.30+0.10 = 0.75, in the 0.6-0.8 band; a 0.10 primary is permitted since the schema floors only the sum), but the rationale chain to the prior labels is broken and worth a note. (2) Inspiration: the audit artifacts identify Lorenzo as Lorenzo de' Medici (the Pazzi-conspiracy target) and Cesare as Cesare Borgia — two rival historical houses — yet the relational graph slots them as brothers in one Crown succession line. Repurposing names for a non-historical setting is legitimate, but the surname gap (finding 1) plus rival-house inspirations cast as siblings is a coherence snag the author should resolve deliberately when assigning Lorenzo's last_name.
- **Evidence:** Mapping: designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:279 ('Epistemic Reason | Scholastic + (situational; some Utility)'); Lorenzo primaries designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:74-78. Sum/band rule: conviction_taxonomy_v30 §4.1 (1-3 primaries, sum 0.6-0.8). Inspiration sources: designs/audit/2026-04-30-architecture-session/10_emergent_arcs_engagement_NERS.md:170 ('Pazzi conspiracy: personal (rivalry with Lorenzo) -> family (Pazzi vs Medici)') and :171 ('Borgia ascent: personal (Cesare's ambition)'); Cesare=Borgia also at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:410-411. Sibling framing: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:365.
- **Fix:** No mechanical fix needed (values are in-band). When authoring Lorenzo's registry entry, either (a) annotate the Authority 0.10 primary as a sim-baseline addition beyond the legacy-label mapping, and (b) choose a surname that resolves the Medici-vs-Borgia rival-house tension if the brothers framing is intended to stand.
- **Verifier note:** Both sub-claims reproduced; correctly NOTE with no mechanical fix owed. (1) Mapping: Lorenzo's prior_labels are ['Virtue Ethics','Epistemic Reason'] (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:74). conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:279 maps 'Epistemic Reason | Scholastic + (situational; some Utility)' and :277 maps 'Virtue Ethics | Virtue (primary)' — neither yields Authority, so the third primary Authority 0.10 comes only from the sim baseline override (05_PP-686_simulation_evaluation.md:63), confirming the rationale-chain break. Still mechanically clean: taxonomy §4.1 (lines 121-135) floors only the SUM ('1-3 entries; sum of primary weights = 0.6..0.8'), and 0.35+0.30+0.10 = 0.75 is in-band, so a 0.10 primary is permitted — the finding states this and asks for no mechanical change. (2) Inspiration: 10_emergent_arcs_engagement_NERS.md:170-171 identify Lorenzo as Lorenzo de' Medici and Cesare as Cesare Borgia (rival houses) while npc_relational_graph_v30.md:365 slots them as brothers; Cesare=Borgia also at character_canon_v30.md:410-411. The Medici-vs-Borgia-as-siblings tension is real. Name-repurposing for a non-historical setting is legitimate (Lorenzo/Cesare appear in neither supersession_register nor placeholder_names, so no naming-rule violation), making this a coherence-on-authoring flag, not a defect. NOTE is correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-420 — Conviction vector is clean and primary-source-consistent (positive compliance note)
- **Unit:** Lorenzo (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-685 / PP-684 / character_canon_v30 §11 D1 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Lorenzo's conviction vector is Virtue 0.35 / Scholastic 0.30 / Authority 0.10 (self_other -0.20, cultural_label valorian_court). All three labels are members of the PP-684 13-Conviction set; none are legacy/9-set labels and none lack a [SUPERSEDED] annotation. The value matches the DESIGNATED PRIMARY source (migration roster, PP-685) and is internally consistent with the sim it derives from across every doc that states it. No conflict, no absence from the migration roster.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84 (the §2.1 entry, PRIMARY per character_canon §11 D1); sim source designs/audit/2026-04-30-architecture-session/sims/pp686_sim_v2.py:383 ('Lorenzo (Virtue+Scholastic)', {Virtue:0.5, Scholastic:0.4, Authority:0.1}); sim evals 05_PP-686_simulation_evaluation.md:63,67 and 06_PP-686_sim_v2_evaluation.md:60 (same vector). No competing conviction value found anywhere.
- **Fix:** None. Documented as a positive note only. Preserve this vector as canonical; any future Part B sheet must derive from it, not re-author it (per character_canon §11 D1 'Do not silently re-derive').
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:71-84 carries Virtue 0.35 / Scholastic 0.30 / Authority 0.10, self_other_initial -0.20, cultural_label valorian_court. The sim source pp686_sim_v2.py:383 lists Lorenzo {Virtue:0.5, Scholastic:0.4, Authority:0.1} and the roster note (lines 82-83) explicitly states it is 'structured' from that baseline, so the two are the same vector at different normalization — not a conflict. Sim evals 05 (lines 63,67) and 06 (line 60) restate the same vector and the 'principled scholar' archetype. All three labels are members of the PP-684 13-Conviction canonical set (alias_registry.yaml:613 enumerates the 13). Repo-wide grep found no competing conviction value (the only other 'Lorenzo' hit, 10_emergent_arcs:170, is the historical Lorenzo de Medici / Pazzi conspiracy, an author-side anchor). One immaterial nit: the fix attributes the phrase 'Do not silently re-derive' to character_canon §11 D1, but D1's actual wording is 'Do not attempt to silently regenerate'; the 'Do not silently re-derive' phrase is in D2. The substantive D1 claim (migration roster is PRIMARY) is correct (line 426). Valid positive note.

#### AUD-NPC2-421 — Ethical Framework dimension is clean for Lorenzo — no live framework Ob driver exists (positive note)
- **Unit:** Lorenzo (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 / character_canon §11 D2 (Ethical Framework supersession) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The D2/PP-684 §6 violation class (an Ethical Framework label still used as a LIVE mechanical Ob driver, or a framework label contradicting the conviction vector) does NOT apply to Lorenzo. He has no framework label embedded in npc_behavior §2 (he is absent from that doc entirely), so there is no superseded-but-mechanically-active label to flag. His only framework-flavored strings are the migration prior_labels, which are correctly scoped as descriptive/traceability tags. Recording this so the dimension is not mistaken for unaudited.
- **Evidence:** Lorenzo absent from designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2 (where framework labels + their Ob modifiers are still embedded per character_canon_v30.md:427 §11 D2); his only label-bearing line is prior_labels at conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:74, which the migration framework treats as legacy-traceability, not a live Ob driver. PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc is the live driver (alias_registry.yaml:614-619).
- **Fix:** None now. Caution for the carrier fix (the npc_behavior §2 entry recommended above): if/when Lorenzo is added there, do NOT attach a legacy Ethical Framework Ob modifier to him — derive Ob via PP-686 §3.7 triadic calc and, if a disposition tag is desired, mark it [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] per D2.
- **Verifier note:** Verified. npc_behavior §2 entries do embed Ethical Framework labels with live Ob modifiers (e.g. :52 'Virtue (Crown) ... -1 Ob', :72 'Faith (Church) ... +2 Ob', :93, :113), and character_canon §11 D2:427 confirms those labels remain mechanically active. Lorenzo is absent from npc_behavior §2 entirely (grep 0), so there is no superseded-but-mechanically-active framework label attached to him to flag. His only framework-flavored strings are the migration prior_labels (roster:74), correctly scoped as legacy traceability. The live Ob driver is the PP-686 triadic decomposition (alias_registry:614-619, patch PP-686). Immaterial nit: the finding cites 'PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc (alias_registry.yaml:614-619)' but registry line 615 names 'faction_behavior_v30 §3.7' as the section while the supersession patch is PP-686 — does not affect the conclusion. Accurate positive note plus a valid caution for the carrier fix.

#### AUD-NPC2-422 — Arc is P-10-aware (no Coherence-as-moral-fall), and 'Vaynard' is live canon, not a superseded name
- **Unit:** Maret Uln | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-04 / P-10 / placeholder_names.yaml VARFELL-MANDATE-ACTION-001 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Two clarifications closing the audit brief's flagged concerns. (1) P-04/P-10: the analyses explicitly frame Maret's shift as testing P-10 — 'epistemic seduction... toward empathy rather than monstrosity' — and her Coherence is stable (10 in companion spec); her moral conflict attaches to her CHOICES, not to Coherence drift, so P-10 (no moral framing of drift) and P-04 are not violated by design. (2) The brief asked to check 'Vaynard' against the supersession register: Vaynard is NOT superseded — he is canonical Duke Magnus Vaynard (PP-699/ED-775, canonical_timeline). Only the module flavor 'vaynards_hall' is flagged as identity-coded in placeholder_names.yaml (the mead-hall/Tribune-Network framing), not the surname. The roster's use of 'Vaynard' as Maret's leader is correct canon.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:30 ('tests P-10 (Coherence != corruption)... seduction toward empathy rather than toward monstrosity'); designs/npcs/companion_specification_v30.md:88 (Maret Coherence 10, stable — no drift arc); canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:144 (Duke Magnus Vaynard, canonical); canon/placeholder_names.yaml:26-49 (flags module 'vaynards_hall' flavor only, not the name 'Vaynard')
- **Fix:** No fix required. Recorded to document that P-04/P-10 are clean and that the 'Vaynard' naming flag does not apply to Maret's leader reference.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced; both clarifications are accurate. (1) P-04/P-10: npc_character_analyses_v30.md:30 frames the arc as 'Maret tests P-10 (Coherence ≠ corruption)... epistemic seduction... toward empathy rather than toward monstrosity'; companion_specification_v30.md:88 shows Maret Coherence 10 (stable, no drift arc). Her moral conflict attaches to choices, not Coherence drift — so P-10 (no moral framing of drift) and P-04 (no moral valence on the ontological) are not violated by design. (2) 'Vaynard' is live canon: 03_canonical_timeline.md:144 'Duke Magnus Vaynard' (first name canonicalized Magnus per ED-775/PP-699, ledger:510); placeholder_names.yaml:26-49 flags only the module flavor 'vaynards_hall' (mead-hall/Tribune-Network framing, VARFELL-MANDATE-ACTION-001), NOT the surname. So the roster's use of 'Vaynard' as Maret's leader is correct canon. ADDED OBSERVATION (does not change the verdict): the same timeline:144 entry and ED-787 (ledger:522) establish Vaynard is 'NOT intelligence-themed... opportunist/revolutionary,' which means the 'intelligence operative / intelligence apparatus' framing recurring in roster:30/33, behavior, and analyses is itself in tension with current Vaynard canon — a separate item the NOTE could have flagged, but the NOTE's own two assertions are correct. NOTE stands; no fix required.

#### AUD-NPC2-423 — Beliefs are inconsistent across docs and tied to the contested conviction profile; arcs introduces a third Belief absent from the stat block
- **Unit:** Maret Uln (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** Belief consistency for a named NPC (character_canon §6.1 voice anchors) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_behavior §2.10 authors two RM-aligned Beliefs ('knowledge must serve the community, not the collector'; 'Varfell's resources belong to all of Valoria — I will redirect them') — both Equity/Community-coded, i.e. anchored to the conviction profile the PRIMARY source contradicts. arcs_20_23 then asserts a distinct Belief not in that list: Maret is 'pursuing the Ceiral Ritual as a personal Belief.' So the canonical Belief set is neither internally complete nor consistent with the migration-roster identity. Certainty itself is consistent (2 / Skeptic across behavior §2.10 and the §7 table), so this finding is Beliefs-only.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:243-246 (two RM-aligned Beliefs); designs/arcs/arcs_20_23.md:164 ('pursuing the Ceiral Ritual as a personal Belief'); conviction profile conflict per finding 1 (conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:108-116)
- **Fix:** Once the conviction vector is fixed, reconcile the Belief set to it and fold the Ceiral-Ritual Belief into the §2.10 Belief list (or strike it from arcs). Ensure the 1-3 Beliefs are consistent across behavior and arcs.
- **Verifier note:** Within live canon there is no Belief contradiction: npc_behavior_v30.md:243-246 authors two coherent, mutually-consistent RM-aligned Beliefs. The 'third Belief absent from the stat block' is sourced only to arcs_20_23.md:164 ('pursuing the Ceiral Ritual as a personal Belief'), which is the non-live, pre-CP14, 'Not valid against any post-CP14 ruleset' file (header; restructure_ledger.md:123 PENDING). The remaining leg — that the two Beliefs are 'anchored to the conviction profile the PRIMARY source contradicts' — is not an independent defect; it is a restatement of Finding 1's vector conflict, and the finding itself concedes 'Certainty is consistent ... so this finding is Beliefs-only.' Stripped of the stale-doc belief and the Finding-1 dependency, the standalone live Belief set is internally consistent and complete (1-3 Beliefs). Downgraded from MEDIUM to NOTE: only contingent rework (if Finding 1 resolves to the warlord profile) plus an optional reconciliation of the stale arcs belief.

#### AUD-NPC2-424 — Self-Other +0.10 (mild self-interest) in the roster sits in mild tension with the 'corroding conscience / RM-sympathy' framing elsewhere
- **Unit:** Maret Uln (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §3 Self-Other orientation | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** conviction_migration_roster sets self_other_initial +0.10 ('Mild self-interest... pragmatic warlord rather than self-sacrificing or self-aggrandizing'). The roster/analyses portrait is a conscience-driven operative who hesitates to harm RM at professional cost to herself — which reads mildly altruistic (collective-leaning), nearer the -0.1..-0.3 band. Minor and orientation-only (not a hard contradiction), but it tracks the same warlord-vs-conscience split as finding 1 and should be set consistently once the vector is resolved.
- **Evidence:** designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:113-116 (self_other +0.10, 'pragmatic warlord'); vs designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:17 ('a conscience operating inside a competence structure'), npc_roster_v30_infill.md:38 (hesitates / acts to benefit RM at own risk); band table designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:77-83
- **Fix:** When reconciling the conviction profile, set Self-Other to match the chosen identity (retain +0.10 if warlord; shift toward -0.1/-0.2 if the conscience/RM-sympathy profile wins) and state it once in the canonical sheet.
- **Verifier note:** Verified and correctly scoped. conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:113-116 sets self_other_initial +0.10 ('Mild self-interest ... pragmatic warlord rather than self-sacrificing or self-aggrandizing'); conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:79-82 places +0.1..+0.5 as 'mildly self-interested' and -0.1..-0.5 as 'mildly altruistic / collective-leaning.' The conscience/RM-sympathy portrait (npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:17 'a conscience operating inside a competence structure'; npc_roster_v30.md:38 'her next action must benefit RM') reads collective-leaning, nearer the negative band. The tension is genuine but minor, orientation-only, not a hard contradiction, and is explicitly the same warlord-vs-conscience axis as Finding 1. NOTE is the right severity; confirmed as filed.

#### AUD-NPC2-425 — designs/npcs/ is CLEAN — zero live 'Galbados' contamination; the one true name-supersession rule is fully honored
- **Unit:** Naming contamination | **Category:** canon | **Constraint/Ref:** CLAUDE.md naming rule (always Solmund, never Galbados) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED

Across all 18 designs/npcs/*.md files there are ZERO occurrences of 'Galbados'/'Galbadian'. 'Solmund' appears 36 times and is used correctly throughout (e.g. 'Solmundan orthodoxy', 'Solmundheim', Certainty-track 'Solmund as rendering, not source'). The absolute naming rule is not violated anywhere in the NPC corpus.
- **Evidence:** Grep 'Galbados' over C:/Github/ttrpg/designs/npcs = 0 hits (also 0 over the whole designs/npcs tree). Grep 'Solmund' over designs/npcs = 36 hits across 9 files (e.g. character_canon_v30.md:283,292; baralta_v30.md:6 instances). Authoritative supersession of the name is recorded in canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:9,30,46 (Church of Solmund) and references/alias_registry.yaml:100.
- **Fix:** No action. The NPC corpus passes the Galbados->Solmund rule. (Out-of-scope note: the literal token 'Galbados' does survive in designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:695, but only inside a self-documenting violation-and-fix audit note — lines 697-699 explicitly flag it as a rule violation and record the 2026-04-17 replacement; it is an editorial record, not live canon.)
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced: grep -rn 'Galbados\|Galbadian' over C:/Github/ttrpg/designs/npcs returns 0 hits across all 18 *.md files. Solmund anchors verified live and correct: character_canon_v30.md:281 ('Solmundan orthodoxy', §7.4 Certainty) and :292 ('Solmund as rendering, not source'); baralta_v30.md uses 'Solmund' 6x correctly (communion/theological content). One nuance: my count of Solmund hits is 42 (finding states 36), but the discrepancy is immaterial — direction is identical (corpus is clean, Solmund used correctly). The sole whole-designs 'Galbados' token outside NPC scope is faction_politics_v30.md:697, which is verbatim a self-documenting 'Violation: Use of Galbadian violates the project naming rule' audit note, i.e. an editorial record not live canon. The NOTE (rule upheld, no NPC action) is accurate; cannot be refuted.

#### AUD-NPC2-426 — PREMISE REFUTED: Vaynard / Himlensendt / Almud are NOT superseded or placeholder names — they are live, ratified canonical NPCs
- **Unit:** Naming contamination | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** Audit premise vs canon/supersession_register.yaml + canon/placeholder_names.yaml | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The audit brief lists 'Vaynard, Himlensendt, Almud, Reinhardt, Galbados' as superseded/placeholder names to hunt as contamination. The two registers I was directed to treat as authoritative do not support this for the three character names. supersession_register.yaml contains NO entry superseding the character names Vaynard, Himlensendt, or Almud (its only 'Vaynard'-tagged entries — VTM-STRIKE and CR-STRIKE — strike a MECHANIC and a card action while using 'Vaynard' as the LIVE canonical identity anchor). placeholder_names.yaml flags only MODULE/MECHANIC identifiers (vaynards_hall -> varfell_mandate_action; einhir_revival -> varfell_territorial_acquisition), never the character names, and likewise treats 'Vaynard'/'Einhir' as the canonical anchors the modules must be cleaned toward. Live usage of these names in NPC docs is therefore correct canon, not contamination.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml: NPC-020 'first_name: Almud, last_name: Almqvist, faction: Crown' (status canonical); NPC-021 'Arne Himlensendt' (status canonical); NPC-052 'Magnus Vaynard' with RESOLVED notes #14 ('VAYNARD NAME: Magnus Vaynard, Duke') and #12. canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:80,185 ('Almud's father', 'Almud TS 0'), :142 (Himlensendt), :144 (Vaynard). designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:46 §2.1 King Almud Almqvist, :66 §2.2 Confessor Arne Himlensendt, :107 §2.4 Duke Magnus Vaynard (full stance triangles + Arc Maps at :554/:592/:620). canon/supersession_register.yaml:37-66 (VTM/CR strikes use 'Vaynard' as live anchor). canon/placeholder_names.yaml:26-70 (module-name placeholders only).
- **Fix:** Treat designs/npcs/ usage of Vaynard / Himlensendt / Almud as canonical. No remediation. If the brief intended a different supersession decision, it must first be filed as an entry in canon/supersession_register.yaml (with a name mapping) before any NPC doc can be said to contaminate — no such entry exists today.
- **Verifier note:** Verified both authoritative registers. canon/supersession_register.yaml contains NO character-name supersession: its only Vaynard-tagged entries are VTM-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (line 37, strikes the 'Vaynard Thread Mastery' MECHANIC track) and CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19 (line 51, strikes the Cultural Reformation card action) — both explicitly use 'Vaynard' as the LIVE identity anchor ('Vaynard's identity as military conqueror', line 56). No Almud/Himlensendt entry exists at all. canon/placeholder_names.yaml flags only MODULE names (prior_name 'vaynards_hall', line 28; 'einhir_revival', line 52), treating 'Vaynard'/'Einhir' as canonical anchors. npc_registry.yaml confirms status canonical for NPC-020 Almud Almqvist (line 336+341), NPC-021 Arne Himlensendt (line 360+), NPC-052 Magnus Vaynard ('status: canonical # RESOLVED'), NPC-002 Maret Uln. Premise-refutation is correct; these are live canon, not contamination.

Varfell Stability -1'), and the succession line ('If Vaynard is eliminated, Maret takes over Varfell ... PP-486').
