#### AUD-NPC2-428 — 'Reinhardt' is a real-world historical-parallel inspiration tag, never a Valoria character — legitimate editorial reference
- **Unit:** Naming contamination | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** Inspiration field (npc_character_analyses historical-parallel) vs audit hit-list 'Reinhardt' | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** 'Reinhardt' was on the contamination hit-list. In the entire designs/npcs/ corpus the bare token 'Reinhardt' resolves to exactly one occurrence, and it is the author-side inspiration anchor for Vaynard, always written as 'Reinhardt von Lohengramm' (the Legend of the Galactic Heroes figure). This is the sanctioned historical_parallel facet of the NPC schema (character_canon_v30 §10.3), i.e. a legitimate editorial reference, explicitly distinguished from in-world naming. (Earlier combined-regex line counts overcounted because Reinhardt co-occurred on lines with Almud/Vaynard; an isolated 'Reinhardt' grep returns 1.)
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:411 ('Vaynard -> "Reinhardt von Lohengramm — cunning in service of pride"') is the sole standalone-'Reinhardt' hit in designs/npcs (isolated grep). Schema sanction: character_canon_v30.md:399-416 (Inspiration = historical_parallel + in_character_aspiration; 'NPC Inspirations are author-given'). The archetype is recorded as canonical in references/npc_registry.yaml NPC-052 conviction_notes/notes ('Reinhardt von Lohengramm archetype per Jordan').
- **Fix:** No action. This is the intended use of the inspiration field, not a live-canon name.
- **Verifier note:** Isolated grep -rniE '\breinhardt\b' over designs/npcs returns exactly ONE hit: character_canon_v30.md:411, 'Vaynard -> "Reinhardt von Lohengramm — cunning in service of pride"'. This sits inside the §10.3 Inspiration schema (lines 399-416), which defines Inspiration as two facets (historical_parallel + in_character_aspiration) and states verbatim 'NPC Inspirations are author-given' — explicitly the author-side analytic anchor, distinguished from in-world naming and from player-side PC Inspirations. The earlier-overcount caveat (Reinhardt co-occurring with Almud/Vaynard on combined regex lines) is plausible and does not affect the single isolated hit. This is the sanctioned use of the inspiration field, not a live-canon Valoria name. Cannot refute.

#### AUD-NPC2-429 — Arc design correctly avoids moral-transformation framing
- **Unit:** Peder Almstedt | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-04 / P-10 (no moral framing of arc/transformation) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Almstedt is explicitly written as a non-arcing constant ("doesn't arc in the dramatic sense... a constant — like Edeyja, but institutional"), with the player-facing question framed as "will the players decide the system he protects is worth preserving?" rather than any moral fall/redemption of the NPC. This is clean against P-04/P-10 (no moral valence imposed as an ontological/transformational axis) and against the P-12 concern (no transformation arc, so no missing tridimensional knot-strain propagation is owed). Surfacing as a positive note, no fix required.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:91 ("Almstedt doesn't arc in the dramatic sense. He is a constant... The arc question is not 'will Almstedt change?' but 'will the players decide the system he protects is worth preserving?'"); reinforced by designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:43 (CONSERVATIVE framed as "a values conflict, not a personality defect").
- **Fix:** No change required. If anything, ensure future edits preserve this framing rather than retrofitting a redemption/corruption arc.
- **Verifier note:** Independently reproduced as a positive note. npc_character_analyses_v30.md:91 frames him as a non-arcing constant ('Almstedt doesn't arc in the dramatic sense. He is a constant... The arc question is not "will Almstedt change?" but "will the players decide the system he protects is worth preserving?"'), and npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:43 frames CONSERVATIVE as 'a values conflict, not a personality defect.' Clean against P-04/P-10 (no moral valence imposed as an ontological/transformational axis) and moots the P-12 tridimensional-knot-strain concern (no transformation arc). Accurate; no fix required. NOTE stands.

#### AUD-NPC2-430 — Cultural template contradicts faction placement (valorian_court vs hafenmark_procedural)
- **Unit:** Peder Almstedt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §5 cultural templates / PP-685 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_registry.yaml assigns cultural_label 'valorian_court' (consistent with faction 'Crown (Ministry)'), but faction_politics_v30 §1.2c seats Almstedt in the Hafenmark Inner Circle as Senior Parliamentary Chair. The Hafenmark template in PP-685 is 'hafenmark_procedural' (used for the Hafenmark Speaker and Feldhaus's Hafenmark role). A bureaucrat whose entire characterization is proceduralism is given the courtly template, not the procedural one — and the template choice feeds the distributed cultural-background weights of the conviction vector. This rides on the unresolved Crown-vs-Hafenmark faction conflict (prior AUD-NPC2-012) but is a distinct personality-substrate error.
- **Evidence:** references/npc_registry.yaml:195 (faction 'Crown (Ministry)'), :208 (cultural_label 'valorian_court'); designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:186 (Hafenmark Inner Circle, 'Senior Parliamentary Chair'); template precedent designs/personal/conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:191 (Hafenmark Speaker -> hafenmark_procedural)
- **Fix:** Resolve faction first; if Almstedt is Hafenmark (per faction_politics, the authoritative faction doc), set cultural_label to hafenmark_procedural. If he stays Crown/Ministry, justify valorian_court and correct faction_politics §1.2c's Hafenmark seating. Do not leave the template keyed to a faction the authoritative faction doc contradicts.
- **Verifier note:** Cited values accurate: npc_registry.yaml:195 faction 'Crown (Ministry)', :208 cultural_label 'valorian_court'; faction_politics_v30.md:186 seats him as Hafenmark Inner Circle Senior Parliamentary Chair; conviction_migration_roster_v30.md:191 uses hafenmark_procedural for the Hafenmark Speaker. But the 'distinct personality-substrate error' framing is wrong: the registry is internally CONSISTENT (Crown faction -> valorian_court, exactly as Almud/Cesare/Lorenzo at roster lines 48/63/79). valorian_court only looks wrong because of the unresolved faction question, already tracked as AUD-NPC2-012 (CONFIRMED HIGH at 2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:86). The cultural_label is strictly downstream: resolve faction to Crown and valorian_court is correct; resolve to Hafenmark and it follows automatically. Not independently actionable, not a separate contradiction — a derived consequence of an already-filed finding. Double-counting. Downgraded to NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-431 — 'Ethics: Procedural' framework label carries no [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] annotation and is absent from the alias registry
- **Unit:** Peder Almstedt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** PP-684 §6 -> PP-686 §3.7 / character_canon_v30 §11 D2 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** npc_roster_v30 / npc_character_analyses_v30 state 'Ethics: Procedural — the process is sacred' with no [SUPERSEDED -> mechanical role replaced by PP-686 §3.7 triadic Ob calc] note required by D2 for framework labels embedded in source docs. Unlike Deontological/Consequentialist/Rawlsian/Categorical-Imperative/oikonomia/Stewardship (all carried in references/alias_registry.yaml), 'Procedural' is not even registered as a superseded ethical-tradition label, so it reads as a live disposition driver with no supersession trail. The label does NOT contradict the conviction vector (Procedural aligns cleanly with Order/Precedent), so this is annotation/traceability hygiene rather than a contradiction.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:46 + designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:41 ('Ethics: Procedural'); D2 rule at designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:427; alias_registry superseded-label set at references/alias_registry.yaml:600-615 (lists Reason/Continuity/Equity/Honor etc. — 'Procedural' absent)
- **Fix:** Either tag the roster/analyses 'Procedural' label with '[SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7; descriptive disposition only]' per D2, or add 'Procedural' to alias_registry.yaml as a descriptive disposition tag mapping to Order/Precedent so the supersession trail exists.
- **Verifier note:** Anchors check out: npc_roster_v30_infill.md:46 / npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:41 carry 'Ethics: Procedural' with no supersession note, and alias_registry.yaml philosophical_tradition_legacy (lines 600-613) lists Categorical Imperative/Virtue/Faith/Scholastic/Equity/Honor/Reason/Continuity but not 'Procedural.' But the D2 obligation is mis-applied. D2 at character_canon_v30.md:427 is explicitly scoped to framework labels 'embedded in npc_behavior §2 and mechanically active via Ob modifiers' — it requires the [SUPERSEDED] tag where a label still drives engine Ob. Almstedt has NO npc_behavior §2 entry (the §2 roster runs §2.1-§2.17, no Almstedt), so 'Procedural' is not engine-active for him and D2's tagging duty is not triggered. Separately, 'Procedural' is arguably a plain descriptor rather than a philosophical *tradition* (unlike Deontological/Consequentialist/Rawlsian), so its absence from the tradition-legacy list is not clearly an omission. The finding itself rates this SOFT and calls it hygiene; given the D2-scope mismatch and the non-engine nature of the label, NOTE is the right level. Downgraded to NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-432 — Almstedt's Evidence style is documentary (ontical), so no per-NPC P-08 hole — but his TS is [GAP] so the gate is unverifiable
- **Unit:** Peder Almstedt (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 / Resonant Style Evidence TS gate | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** faction_politics characterizes Almstedt's Evidence as documentary ('whose style is documentary', the founding-terms archivist), i.e. ontical records/precedent rather than Thread-level evidence — so the P-08 'Thread-level evidence is inert vs TS<30' concern does not bite for him the way it does for sensitives. However, his stat block lists TS as [GAP], so the TS>=30 Stirring gate cannot actually be evaluated, and the doc-wide Evidence-gate defect (gate keyed to TS 0 rather than TS<30, prior AUD-NPC2-006) would apply to him if a player ever tried Thread-level Evidence against him. Flagged as a watch-item, not a violation.
- **Evidence:** designs/provincial/faction_politics_v30.md:151 (Almstedt 'whose style is documentary'), :186 (Evidence); TS undefined at references/npc_registry.yaml:201 (ts: null [GAP]); P-08 gate phrasing concern tracked at designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md (AUD-NPC2-006)
- **Fix:** When Almstedt's stat block is completed, set TS explicitly (a TS-0 documentary bureaucrat is the evident intent) and confirm any Evidence targeting against him is restricted to ontical evidence, so the documentary-Evidence characterization is mechanically enforced.
- **Verifier note:** All three claims independently verified and the finding is correctly self-limiting. (a) faction_politics_v30.md:151 characterizes his style as documentary ('taught by Almstedt, whose style is documentary') and :186 sets Resonant Style Evidence — so his Evidence is ontical records/precedent, and the P-08 'Thread-level evidence inert vs TS<30' concern does not bite for him. (b) TS undefined: npc_registry.yaml:201 'ts: null  # [GAP]', so the TS>=30 Stirring gate cannot be evaluated. (c) The doc-wide gate-phrasing defect is real and separately tracked: AUD-NPC2-006 CONFIRMED HIGH at 2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:80 ('gate keyed to TS 0, not TS<30'). The finding explicitly flags this as a watch-item, not a per-NPC violation — the right call; no P-08 breach is attributable to Almstedt himself. NOTE is exactly correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-433 — Brief's premises (Conviction 'Continuity'; Evidence style 'taught by Almstedt') do not match the actual design
- **Unit:** Prince Torben Almqvist | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** Audit-brief premises vs canon (Conviction / Resonant Style) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Recording for transparency, since the brief flagged these for verification: Torben does NOT have Conviction 'Continuity' — that label belongs to Edeyja (§2.9) and Haelgrund (§2.5), and 'Continuity' is itself a deprecated/composite legacy tag, not a canonical Conviction. Torben's Convictions are emergent (ED-618) with a default of Order and Secondary Liberty(/Autonomy). His Resonant Styles are Solidarity (primary) + Authority (secondary), NOT 'Evidence', and there is no 'Evidence taught by Almstedt' linkage anywhere for Torben. The Evidence-style P-08 gate is correctly implemented for TS-0 NPCs (Thread-level evidence is epistemically inert), so even a hypothetical Evidence approach to Torben would be canon-compliant. No fix required; flagged so the brief's misdirection is not propagated.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:196-197 (Torben Solidarity primary / Authority secondary); :192,:199 (emergent Conviction, Order default); 'Continuity' belongs to :212 (Edeyja) and :303 (Haelgrund), and is a deprecated/composite tag per designs/personal/conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:283; P-08 Evidence gate at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:113 (§6.1b).
- **Fix:** No change to Torben. If anything, this confirms the design is clean on Resonant Style / P-08 for this NPC.
- **Verifier note:** Operative content verified. Torben does NOT have Conviction 'Continuity': his Convictions are emergent with Order default (npc_behavior_v30.md:192,:199) and Secondary Autonomy/Liberty (:194); 'Continuity' belongs to Edeyja (:212) and Haelgrund (:303) and is itself a deprecated/composite legacy tag (conviction_taxonomy_v30.md:283 'Continuity (legacy tag) | composite — see PP-685'; also :270). Torben's Resonant Styles are Solidarity primary + Authority secondary (:196-197), NOT 'Evidence', and there is no 'Evidence taught by Almstedt' linkage for Torben anywhere. One imprecision in the brief's supporting detail: it says the P-08 Evidence gate is 'correctly implemented' citing §6.1b at :113, but :113 is Vaynard's Ethical Framework and the §6.1b header (at :684) is actually an EMPTY stub; the live P-08 inertness text resides at character_canon_v30.md:170 ('Thread-level evidence is invalid as Evidence targeting vs TS 0 NPCs (P-08 compliance)'). That imprecision does not affect the NOTE's conclusion ('no change to Torben'), which is correct. Confirmed as a transparency NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-434 — Empty Beliefs block and no Belief->Scar path for the heir (by-design but unflagged in stat block)
- **Unit:** Prince Torben Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** gap | **Constraint/Ref:** Named-NPC Beliefs / Belief->Scar (ED-609; D3) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Torben's 'Beliefs:' block is empty in the stat block, and the analyses doc states Beliefs are undefined at game start and 'form through play.' This is intentional (Torben is the deliberately malleable contested asset), but the stat block carries no [GAP/by-design] marker and there is no specified Belief->Scar conversion path for any arc Belief he later acquires, even though arcs elsewhere convert Beliefs to Scars. For a Tier-1 named NPC this leaves the transformation mechanic unspecified.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.8 lines 201-202 (empty 'Beliefs:'); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md §2.8 line 33 ('Undefined at game start... form through play. First Belief generated after first meaningful... engagement') and line 34 ('Stance Triangle is largely blank'); tracked as ED-609 (arc_expansion_v30.md line 758)
- **Fix:** Add an explicit '[by-design: blank, emerges via ED-618 window; first Belief after first meaningful engagement]' note to the §2.8 Beliefs block, and state that emergent Beliefs follow the standard Belief->Scar conversion (conviction_track_v1 Scar mechanic) once formed, so the transformation path is defined.
- **Verifier note:** Empty Beliefs verified: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.8 lines 201-203 show 'Beliefs:' with no entries; the by-design intent is tracked (ED-609 at arc_expansion line 758 'Torben Beliefs and Conviction emergence mechanic — formal specification required'; npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md §3 frames him as the test-case contested asset). But the claim 'no specified Belief->Scar conversion path' is incorrect: the generic conversion is fully specified and applies to every NPC including Torben — npc_behavior_v30 §6.2 ('Old Belief -> permanent Scar. New Belief forms.'), character_canon §6.2 line 200, and npc_stance_triangles line 66. Once Torben forms an emergent Belief, the standard mechanic governs it; nothing Torben-specific is missing. (Note the finding's evidence also miscites npc_character_analyses_v30.md §2.8 line 33-34 for the 'form through play / Stance Triangle largely blank' quotes — those lines in that SKELETON file are the Maret/Yrsa sections; the Torben analysis is at line 200+, and the malleability prose is in the infill.) Reduced to NOTE: a one-line [by-design] annotation is a reasonable nicety, but the transformation mechanic is not unspecified.

#### AUD-NPC2-435 — Solidarity-primary NPC with a Knot prerequisite that is hard to satisfy (TS>=30 branch) and no NPC-specific Knot path
- **Unit:** Prince Torben Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** Resonant Style — Solidarity Knot prerequisite (npc_behavior §6.3; social_contest §6.3) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Torben's Primary Resonant Style is Solidarity, but the canonical Solidarity targeting prerequisite is 'active Knot + Disposition +5 + either PC or NPC TS >= 30 + Knot capacity.' Torben is TS 0, so the TS>=30 limb can only be met by the PC being a practitioner — a steep bar for the supposedly easiest-to-reach NPC in the roster. His §2.8 entry asserts Solidarity is his main lever ('anyone who treats him as a person gains enormous leverage') but does not point to or carve out a Knot-formation path for a TS-0 prince, leaving the primary lever gated behind a practitioner requirement.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.8 lines 196-198 (Solidarity primary, TS 0); Solidarity prerequisite at designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §6.3 line 702 and designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md §6.3 line 702 (Disposition +5 + PC or NPC TS >= 30)
- **Fix:** Either note in §2.8 that Torben's Solidarity is reached via Disposition+5 + PC-TS>=30 (and is otherwise Authority-gated), or add a low-TS Knot-formation carve-out for court-raised non-sensitive NPCs so a Solidarity-primary heir isn't gated behind a practitioner PC.
- **Verifier note:** The underlying design observation is real and reproducible: npc_behavior_v30.md §2.8 lines 196-198 make Solidarity Torben's primary lever at TS 0, and the Solidarity prerequisite at npc_behavior_v30.md §6.3 line 702 requires 'Disposition +5 + either PC or NPC TS >= 30 + Knot capacity', so for a TS-0 prince the TS limb is satisfiable only via a practitioner PC. That tension is genuine. But the citation is partly wrong and weakens the finding: there is NO parallel prerequisite in designs/scene/social_contest_v30.md §6.3 line 702 as cited — that file is only 624 lines and contains no 'Solidarity' text at all (grep = 0 matches), so the 'social_contest §6.3' anchor is spurious. The mechanic lives only in npc_behavior. Also the generic arc machine already provides a low-bar Solidarity path: character_canon §8.1 (line 320) and npc_behavior §5.1 fire 'PC Knot >= Intimate -> Solidarity activates', and NPC Knot formation (npc_stance_triangles lines 58-63 / ED-522) is Bonds-check-based with no TS gate. So a TS-0 prince is not strictly locked out. Correctly SOFT/NOTE design observation, not a defect; the finding already self-rates SOFT, and I confirm at NOTE given the broken cross-reference.

#### AUD-NPC2-436 — Arc text invests in TS-0 Torben 'with Evidence RS' though Evidence is not his Resonant Style
- **Unit:** Prince Torben Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** Resonant Style match (P-08 ontical-evidence gate; Torben profile) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** An Almud Arc-A trigger references the player having 'invested in Torben with Evidence RS.' Torben's Resonant Styles are Solidarity (primary) / Authority (secondary) — Evidence is not in his profile, so Evidence-style targeting would be wrong-style. It is not a P-08 violation per se (ontical/political evidence is permitted vs TS-0 NPCs), but pairing 'Evidence RS' with a Solidarity/Authority NPC is a Resonant-Style mismatch that could be read as treating Thread/Evidence as a valid vector against a TS-0 prince.
- **Evidence:** designs/arcs/arc_expansion_v30.md line 69 ('...player has invested in Torben with Evidence RS'); Torben RS profile designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §2.8 lines 196-197 (Solidarity/Authority); P-08 ontical-evidence gate designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md §6.1b lines 112-114
- **Fix:** Reword arc_expansion_v30.md line 69 to use Torben's actual styles (Solidarity via Knot, or Authority) — e.g. 'player has built Disposition with Torben (Solidarity/Authority RS)' — or, if the intent is the documentary evidence he carries back, state it as ontical evidence influencing Almud rather than an 'Evidence RS' investment in Torben.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: arc_expansion_v30.md line 69 conditions Almud Arc A on 'Torben Loyalty <= 2 to Crown AND player has invested in Torben with Evidence RS', while Torben's Resonant Styles are Solidarity (primary) / Authority (secondary) per npc_behavior_v30.md §2.8 lines 196-197. So pairing 'Evidence RS' with a Solidarity/Authority NPC is a Resonant-Style label mismatch. The finding correctly characterizes this as NOT a P-08 violation (ontical/documentary evidence is permitted against TS-0 NPCs; the infill §6.1b lines 112-114 gate only Thread-level evidence), i.e. it is a wording/style-label imprecision rather than a canon-rule breach. The likely intent is the Thread-credible testimony Torben carries back from Altonia (cf. line 69's 'returns from Altonia with Thread-credible testimony' and npc_character_analyses §3 line 204 Altonian tutoring), which is documentary/ontical influence on Almud, not an 'Evidence RS investment' in Torben. Confirmed as an editorial mismatch worth a reword; NOTE severity is correct.

#### AUD-NPC2-437 — GD-1 clean; Resonant Style usage valid; Laskaris-side gaps already tracked
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** GD-1; npc_behavior_v30_infill §1.3; prior audit AUD-NPC2-132/133 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Surfacing what is intentionally fine so it is not re-flagged: (1) The Elske-gated Diplomatic Repulsion / succession mechanics feed Regency Establishment and IP defense, which advance toward Peninsular Sovereignty rather than constituting an alternate win path, so GD-1 is not violated. (2) 'Resonant Style: Evidence' (npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90) correctly uses the canonical Resonant Style taxonomy. (3) The Laskaris behavior-system absence and the undefined Return-failure branch were already raised in the 2026-06-22 audit and downgraded to NOTE; the findings above are distinct (Conviction cluster, Schoenland/Altonia, Loyalty start, threshold collision) and not duplicates.
- **Evidence:** GD-1 frame: designs/provincial/victory_v30.md:467-477 (Regency under sovereignty), :606 (IP defense). Resonant Style taxonomy: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:14,18-19. Prior audit: designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:206-207 (AUD-NPC2-132, AUD-NPC2-133, both DOWNGRADED to NOTE).
- **Fix:** No fix required; informational to scope this audit's new findings against the existing audit-of-record.
- **Verifier note:** Verified each scoping claim. (1) GD-1: victory_v30.md:461-477 frames Regency Establishment as a transitional handoff under Peninsular Sovereignty, explicitly 'not a Lowenritter victory in the traditional sense' (:465), so the Elske-gated succession/IP-defense mechanics advance toward the sole victory condition rather than creating an alternate path — GD-1 clean. (2) Resonant Style taxonomy exists at npc_behavior_v30_infill.md:14,18-19, so 'Resonant Style: Evidence' (infill:90) uses the canonical vocabulary correctly. (3) Prior audit AUD-NPC2-132 and AUD-NPC2-133 confirmed at designs/audit/2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md:206-207, both DOWNGRADED to NOTE, covering the Laskaris behavior-system absence and the undefined 'Return attempt fails' branch — distinct from the four new findings here (Conviction cluster, Schoenland/Altonia label, Loyalty start, threshold collision). Accurate informational scoping; no fix required.

#### AUD-NPC2-438 — Primary style Evidence on a TS 0 NPC with no stated TS>=30 / Thread-vs-mundane gate
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** mechanical | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 (Thread-level evidence epistemically inert vs TS<30) | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** Elske is TS 0 (TS<30, sub-Stirring) per the Certainty table, and her Primary Resonant Style is Evidence. Under P-08, Thread-level evidence is inert against a TS<30 NPC unless TS crosses 30. The docs route persuasion through 'concrete evidence' which reads as mundane (her brother's situation) and is therefore fine — but neither the analyses sheet nor arcs_20_23 states the gate, so an Evidence argument carrying Thread-level content (which becomes live if she ever develops TS — see narrative_scenario_chains Thread-development branch) would be wrongly treated as a valid vector. The gate is unstated for a named NPC whose own arc contemplates Thread development.
- **Evidence:** TS 0: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:1193. Evidence primary: designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:90; designs/arcs/arcs_20_23.md:35. Thread-development branch that would raise the stakes: designs/arcs/narrative_scenario_chains.md:514 ('Elske's TS is not established') and :902
- **Fix:** Add an explicit note to Elske's Resonant Style entry: 'Evidence = mundane/situational evidence only; Thread-level Evidence is inert until TS>=30 (P-08).' Tie the gate to the existing TS-development branch so a future TS gain unlocks Thread-Evidence cleanly.
- **Verifier note:** Facts check out: TS 0 (npc_behavior_v30.md:1193), Evidence primary (analyses_infill:90), TS-not-established branch (narrative_scenario_chains.md:514). But there is no live violation. P-08 makes Thread-level evidence globally inert against any TS<30 NPC; the finding itself concedes the current Evidence usage is mundane/situational ('her brother's situation') and 'is therefore fine'. It asks only for a clarifying per-NPC note to pre-empt a hypothetical future TS gain. That is documentation hardening, not a defect — the gate already holds engine-wide without per-NPC restatement. MEDIUM is unwarranted for a no-live-violation forward-looking note -> NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-439 — Ethical Framework dimension is clean (no framework label attached to Elske)
- **Unit:** Princess Elske Almqvist (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** character_canon_v30 §11 D2 / PP-686 §3.7 (Ethical Framework) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** No Deontological/Consequentialist/Virtue/etc. ethical-framework label is attached to Elske in any doc, so there is no live-mechanical-Ob-driver violation and no framework-vs-conviction contradiction on this axis. Noted only to record the dimension was audited and found clean (contrast her spouse Laskaris, who carries 'Ethics: Oikonomia' at npc_roster_v30_infill.md:61 — out of scope here).
- **Evidence:** No 'Ethic'/'Framework'/'Deontolog'/'Consequential' token co-occurs with Elske in any of the audited docs (grep across npc_*, victory_v30, arcs_*, complete_systems_reference). Spouse-only framework (out of scope): designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30_infill.md:61.
- **Fix:** None required for the framework axis. If a disposition tag is later desired, add it only as a descriptive tag with the [SUPERSEDED -> PP-686 §3.7] marker, never as a live Ob driver.
- **Verifier note:** Verified. No Deontological/Consequentialist/Virtue/Oikonomia or other ethical-framework token attaches to Elske in any audited doc; my Elske-scoped greps across npc_*, victory_v30, arcs_*, complete_systems_reference never surfaced one. The spouse-only 'Ethics: Oikonomia' is correctly attributed to Laskaris (npc_roster_v30_infill.md:61) and is out of scope. Accurate 'audited and clean' record entry; no action required. NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-440 — Naming-rule conflict: Vaynard/Himlensendt/Almud are flagged as placeholders to exclude, but the registers don't list them and the timeline uses them as live canon
- **Unit:** Relational-graph integrity | **Category:** naming | **Constraint/Ref:** CLAUDE.md naming rule vs supersession_register.yaml / placeholder_names.yaml / canonical_timeline | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** The audit's naming constraint states Vaynard, Himlensendt, Almud, Reinhardt are superseded/placeholder names 'tracked in supersession_register.yaml and placeholder_names.yaml' that must NOT appear as live canon in v30 docs. Verified against the actual files: (1) supersession_register.yaml mentions Vaynard only to AFFIRM his identity ('Reinhardt von Lohengramm-coded military conqueror per CR-STRIKE') — it supersedes a mechanic (VTM/Cultural Reformation), not the name; it never lists Vaynard, Himlensendt, or Almud as superseded names. (2) placeholder_names.yaml contains no entry for any of these character names (only 'Reinhardt' appears, as a descriptor of Vaynard's canonical anchor). (3) The canonical timeline uses Almud Almqvist, Confessor Arne Himlensendt, Duchess Inge Baralta, and Duke Magnus Vaynard as live canon. So the graph's use of Almud/Vaynard/Baralta is consistent with canon, and these are NOT register-tracked placeholders. Reporting the instruction/canon mismatch rather than flagging canonical names as violations. ('Galbados'→'Solmund' is the only clearly live naming rule and the graph complies — it uses Solmund.)
- **Evidence:** Instruction: audit naming-rule block + CLAUDE.md. Registers: canon/supersession_register.yaml:37-66 (VTM/CR strikes; affirms Vaynard identity), grep of full file shows no Almud/Himlensendt name-supersession; canon/placeholder_names.yaml (no Almud/Himlensendt/Vaynard entries; 'Reinhardt' only as anchor descriptor). Canon usage: canon/03_canonical_timeline.md:138,142,143,144.
- **Fix:** Reconcile the naming directive with the registers before treating Vaynard/Himlensendt/Almud as forbidden. If they are genuinely to be retired, add explicit entries to supersession_register.yaml/placeholder_names.yaml and propagate to the timeline; otherwise update the directive. Independent of that, the graph still must fix 'Cardinal Reichard' (separate finding) since that name is wrong under either reading.
- **Verifier note:** Verified against the actual files. (1) canon/supersession_register.yaml contains NO name-supersession for Vaynard, Himlensendt, or Almud — the only Vaynard mention (CR-STRIKE-2026-04-19, lines 51-66) AFFIRMS his identity ('Incompatible with Vaynard's identity as military conqueror (Reinhardt von Lohengramm parallel)') and supersedes MECHANICS (VTM track, Cultural Reformation), not the name. (2) canon/placeholder_names.yaml contains only mechanic/module placeholders (varfell_mandate_action, altonian_reinforcements, etc.) — no character-name entries for Vaynard/Himlensendt/Almud; 'Reinhardt' appears only as a flavor-descriptor of Vaynard's canonical anchor (line 31). (3) canon/03_canonical_timeline.md uses 'Almud Almqvist' (:138), 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt' (:142), 'Duchess Inge Baralta' (:143), 'Duke Magnus Vaynard' (:144) as live game-start canon. So the audit naming-block's premise that these are 'tracked in supersession_register.yaml and placeholder_names.yaml' placeholders is factually inaccurate; the graph's use of Almud/Vaynard/Baralta/Himlensendt is canon-consistent. The Solmund-not-Galbados rule is the live naming rule and the graph complies (uses Solmund). Correctly filed as an instruction/canon mismatch NOTE, not a violation. Minor refinement: the rename history that exists (PP-665 Maret Vossen→Yrsa Vossen) is tracked in canon/patch_register_index.md:172, not in either register named — reinforcing the finding's point that the two named registers do not carry these names.

#### AUD-NPC2-441 — Upstream geography file has inconsistent settlement-ID assignments that §6 silently inherits
- **Unit:** Relational-graph integrity | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** valoria_geography_v30.yaml (upstream of this unit) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Context bearing on the §6 worked examples (not the graph's own defect): valoria_geography_v30.yaml maps settlement IDs two incompatible ways. Its detailed registry sets S-031=Oastad, S-033=Askeheim Ruins, S-035/S-036=Schoenland City/Harbor; but its PP-726 settlement_adjacency notes and province blocks treat S-031/32/33=Sigurdshelm, S-036=Himmelenger, S-037=Schoenland, and the province→settlement lists disagree again (T8 Gransol={S-015,S-016,S-017}; T9 Himmelenger={S-023,S-024,S-025}). The graph's §6.1/§6.5 follow the adjacency-block reading (S-031 Sigurdshelm, S-036 Himmelenger), so the graph is internally self-consistent with one of the two systems — but any recompute of §6 hop distances will be unreliable until the geography file is reconciled.
- **Evidence:** valoria_geography_v30.yaml:488-535 (registry: S-031 Oastad, S-035/36 Schoenland) vs :602-661 (adjacency notes: S-031 'intra-Sigurdshelm', S-036 Himmelenger, S-037 Schoenland) vs :121,134 (T8/T9 province→settlement lists). Graph follows the adjacency reading: designs/npcs/npc_relational_graph_v30.md:411-415.
- **Fix:** Out of scope for the relational graph, but file a geography-doc fix so a single canonical settlement-ID map exists; the graph's §6 cannot be verified until then. Add a note in graph §6 pinning which ID system it assumes.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced three mutually incompatible settlement-ID systems in valoria_geography_v30.yaml. (a) Detailed settlement registry (lines 480-536): S-031=Oastad, S-033=Askeheim Ruins, S-035=Schoenland City, S-036=Schoenland Harbor. (b) PP-726 settlement_adjacency block (lines 586-661): S-031/032/033 are 'intra-Sigurdshelm' (:627-629), S-036=Himmelenger (:638,646,649,652), S-037=Schoenland (:636 'Valorsplatz↔Schoenland'). (c) Province→settlement lists (lines 100-239): T8 Gransol=[S-015,S-016,S-017] (:121), T9 Himmelenger=[S-023,S-024,S-025] (:134), T13 Oastad=[S-031,S-032] (:186), T16 Schoenland=[S-035,S-036] (:225). These directly contradict each other (e.g., S-036 is simultaneously Schoenland Harbor, Himmelenger, and a Schoenland member; S-023-025 are both Himmelenger's settlements and 'intra-Halvarshelm' in the adjacency block). The relational graph §6.1/§6.5 (npc_relational_graph_v30.md:411-415,472-476) follows the adjacency-block reading (S-036=Himmelenger, S-031=Sigurdshelm), so it is internally self-consistent with ONE of the systems, but any recompute of §6 hop distances is unreliable until the geography file is reconciled. Out of scope for the relational graph itself; correctly filed as upstream context NOTE bearing on the §6 worked examples (and on the BFS finding above).

#### AUD-NPC2-442 — Superseded character names appear as LIVE canon in the audited RS sections (Almud, Himlensendt, Vaynard)
- **Unit:** Resonant-styles taxonomy & P-08 gating | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** CLAUDE.md naming rule / supersession_register.yaml | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Outside the strict RS scope but encountered throughout the Resonant-Style entries: the canonical npc_behavior_v30 §2 stance triangles and arc_expansion headers use placeholder/superseded names tracked in canon/placeholder_names.yaml / supersession_register.yaml — King Almud Almqvist (§2.1), Confessor Arne Himlensendt (§2.2), Duke Magnus Vaynard (§2.4). The project naming rule (CLAUDE.md: 'always Solmund, never Galbados') and the supersession registers require these not to appear as live v30 canon without a [SUPERSEDED->] annotation. They appear here unannotated as the live NPC identities the RS assignments attach to.
- **Evidence:** C:\Github\ttrpg\designs\npcs\npc_behavior_v30.md line 46 (§2.1 'King Almud Almqvist'), line 66 (§2.2 'Confessor Arne Himlensendt'), line 107 (§2.4 'Duke Magnus Vaynard'); registers at C:\Github\ttrpg\canon\placeholder_names.yaml and C:\Github\ttrpg\canon\supersession_register.yaml
- **Fix:** Out of RS-audit scope to resolve here — route to the naming/supersession pass: confirm the live v30 names and either replace these or add [SUPERSEDED->live name] annotations so the RS entries attach to canonical identities.

#### AUD-NPC2-443 — Solberg arc is correctly framed as structural (non-moral) and he carries no Thread-level mechanics
- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-04 / P-10 / P-08 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Surfacing as a clean-pass note (no fix). Solberg's transformation/arc is explicitly framed as a structural incompatibility between personal desire and strategic need ('whether those things are structurally incompatible'), not as a moral fall or redemption, satisfying P-04/P-10. He is a mundane TS<30 commercial actor with no Coherence/drift/Thread-level evidence attached, so P-08/P-12 do not engage. No GD-1 alternate-victory language attaches to him. The design is canon-clean on these dimensions.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30.md:127 (arc framed structurally, not morally); designs/npcs/npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:59-61 (motivation = homesickness, 'each individual decision is defensible'); no TS/Coherence value or Thread mechanic appears for Solberg in any grepped file.
- **Fix:** None — documented as a positive compliance note only.
- **Verifier note:** Independently verified as a clean-pass note. npc_character_analyses_v30.md:127 frames the arc structurally ('whether those things are structurally incompatible'), no moral-fall/redemption language, satisfying P-04/P-10. npc_character_analyses_v30_infill.md:59-61 confirms motivation = homesickness and 'Each individual decision is defensible'. Solberg is absent from the NPC TS/Certainty table (npc_behavior_v30.md:1184-1199), and a repo-wide grep for Solberg + TS/Thread Sensitivity/Coherence returned no value or Thread mechanic attached to him (only tooling data files, an arc note that he is never a primary arc driver, an 'Solberg recall risk' arc mention, the prior audit's own output, and a canon-flatten line tagging him merely 'homesick'). As a TS<30 mundane commercial actor, P-08/P-12 do not engage; no GD-1 alternate-victory language attaches. Positive compliance note, no fix; confirmed at NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-444 — Adjacent (out-of-layer) note: the only behavior-doc mechanical hook for Solberg — the §7.7 Recall trigger — is broken and already filed
- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** contradiction | **Constraint/Ref:** npc_behavior_v30 §7.7 vs npc_roster_v30 §12 / npc_character_analyses_v30 §12 (cross-ref to AUD-NPC2-056) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Outside the strict ethics/resonance layer but flagged because it is the sole behavior-doc mechanical hook touching Solberg: the §7.7 Schoenland Priority Tree fires 'Recall Solberg' off conditions belonging to other NPCs/clocks (Supply Chain Exposure = Feldhaus/Virke; Parish Revolt = Prudence Cardinal; PI = Hafenmark), whereas every personality/arc doc states he is recalled when his stability bias is DISCOVERED (Intel vs Ob 3) or via Schoenland central reassessment. This is a faction-mechanics contradiction (not a conviction/style/certainty issue) and is already tracked as AUD-NPC2-056 (CONFIRMED); restated here only so the personality layer's 'recall = bias-discovery' logic is reconciled with the mechanical trigger when fixed.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md §7.7 line 1167; contradicts npc_roster_v30_infill.md line 68 and npc_character_analyses_v30.md line 127. Existing finding: 2026-06-22-npc-comprehensive-audit.md AUD-NPC2-056 (lines 619-623).
- **Fix:** Per AUD-NPC2-056: re-author the §7.7 step-3 trigger to fire on Solberg's actual driver (faction succeeds Intel vs Ob 3 exposing the stability pattern, OR Schoenland central reassessment); remove the borrowed Feldhaus/Cardinal/Hafenmark conditions. No separate action needed if AUD-NPC2-056 is resolved.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced. npc_behavior_v30 §7.7 line 1167 fires 'Recall Solberg' off Supply Chain Exposure + Parish Revolt + PI >= 8 — conditions belonging to other subsystems (Supply Chain Exposure = Feldhaus/Virke card; Parish Revolt = Prudence Cardinal card; PI = Hafenmark clock, line 1177). Every arc/personality doc states recall = bias DISCOVERED (Intel vs Ob 3) or Schoenland central reassessment (analyses line 127, roster_infill line 68). This faction-mechanics contradiction is already tracked as AUD-NPC2-056 (CONFIRMED, lines 619-624), which the finding correctly cites; it is appropriately flagged here as an out-of-ethics-layer NOTE only to reconcile the 'recall = bias-discovery' logic when the existing finding is fixed. No double-counting concern; correctly de-rated to NOTE. Separately observed (does not affect this or any verdict): the registry line 305 says the homesickness is 'discoverable via Read Ob 2' whereas analyses line 127 says 'Intel action vs Ob 3' — a discovery-mechanic discrepancy not raised by any of the eight findings.

- **Unit:** Rikard Solberg (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-04 / P-08 / P-10 | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED

Because he has no Evidence Resonant Style assigned and no Thread-level evidence is routed at him, the P-08 'Thread-Evidence inert vs TS<30, needs TS>=30 gate' failure mode does not arise. This is the correct handling.

- **Fix:** None — preserve the structural, non-Thread framing as-is.
- **Verifier note:** Verified as an accurate positive-compliance note. Registry NPC-012 has ts/coherence/stats null (lines 294-296). Structural, explicitly non-moral framing confirmed: analyses_infill lines 60-61 ('Each individual decision is defensible. The cumulative pattern is not') and analyses line 127 ('whether those things are structurally incompatible'). Because no Evidence Resonant Style is assigned and no Thread-level evidence is routed at him, the P-08 Thread-Evidence-inert-vs-TS<30 failure mode does not arise. Prior audit AUD-NPC2-147 (lines 1265-1270) independently CONFIRMED the same clean-pass on P-04/P-08/P-10. Correctly a NOTE with no fix.

#### AUD-NPC2-446 — Dimension (a) drift/recovery caps and dimension (b) Ob/dice stacking are essentially clean — with two clarifications
- **Unit:** Stat-scale & mechanics sweep | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** Stat-bound clause for Framework Drift (npc_behavior_v30 §7.1, line 748) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** Sweep dimensions (a) and (b) found no live violations in designs/npcs/, recorded here so reviewers do not mis-flag two literal phrasings. (a) Framework Drift explicitly states it is 'subject to stat ceiling (7) and stat floor (1)' (§7.1, line 748); Self-Other drift is bounded [-1,+1] with κ=0.03 (character_canon §4, lines 153-156); Coherence floors at 0 with a defined Conversion state; no per-NPC drift/recovery formula in these docs pushes a stat past 7/1 without a cap. (b) Resonant-Style dice stacking is hard-capped at +5D with pool-min 1D (§6.5, line 739). The one 'no per-season cap' phrasing — §6.4.3 Wrong-Style stacking — is NOT actually unbounded: it caps at the ±2/season Domain-Action stat cap at Accounting, so 3 nominal +1s resolve to +2 net.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:748 (drift stat-bound); :739 (§6.5 '+5D ... Pool minimum 1D'); :733 (§6.4.3 'no per-season cap ... the ±2/season Stat Cap ... does apply'); designs/npcs/character_canon_v30.md:153-156 (Self-Other bounded, κ=0.03).
- **Fix:** No change required for (a)/(b). Optionally add a one-line cross-reference at §6.4.3 making the net-bound explicit at the point of the 'no per-season cap' phrase to prevent future false-positive audit flags.
- **Verifier note:** Verified the note is accurate, not a defect. npc_behavior_v30.md:748 states Framework Drift 'is subject to stat ceiling (7) and stat floor (1)'; :739 (§6.5) caps Resonant-Style/positional dice at '+5D' with 'Pool minimum 1D'; :733 (§6.4.3) confirms the 'no per-season cap on Wrong-Style-derived Stability gains' phrase is bounded by the '±2/season Stat Cap ... does apply ... cap at +2 net'; character_canon_v30.md:153–156 was cited for Self-Other bounding (κ=0.03). The (a)/(b) dimensions are clean as described; this entry correctly forestalls false positives. Retained as NOTE.

#### AUD-NPC2-447 — TS stated as 12 in most docs but 15 in the registry — affects the P-08 Evidence gate framing
- **Unit:** Sæmund Haelgrund (ethics/resonance) | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** TS value consistency (load-bearing for the P-08 Resonant-Style gate) | **Verifier verdict:** CONFIRMED
- **Issue:** TS is outside the strict personality-mechanics scope, but it gates the Evidence Resonant Style (RS-2) and the Certainty rationale, so the conflict matters here. The roster, character analyses, behavior §7.10, faction_canon, and all arc docs say TS 12; npc_registry.yaml (and npc_registry.sql) say TS 15 ('per Jordan'). Both are <30, so the P-08 inertness conclusion is unchanged, but the inconsistency should be resolved before his Resonant-Style/Certainty block is finalised so the gate and rationale cite one number.
- **Evidence:** designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md L63 (TS 12); designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md L1195 (12 hidden); designs/provincial/faction_canon_v30.md L542 (TS 12); references/npc_registry.yaml L107 (ts: 15 'per Jordan') and references/npc_registry.sql L130 (15).
- **Fix:** Reconcile to a single TS (the registry's 'per Jordan: 15' is the most recent authority; if adopted, propagate 15 everywhere) and ensure the Evidence-gate note and Certainty rationale reference it.
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced: npc_roster_v30.md L63 (TS 12), npc_behavior_v30.md L1195 ('12 (hidden)'), faction_canon_v30.md L542 ('TS 12 unrecognized') all say 12; npc_registry.yaml L107 ('ts: 15  # per Jordan') and npc_registry.sql L130 (value '15', note 'TS 15... per Jordan') say 15. Both are <30 so the P-08 inertness conclusion is unchanged, as the finding correctly concedes. Genuine numeric inconsistency worth reconciling before any Resonant-Style/Certainty gate cites a number. NOTE severity is correct — TS is outside the strict personality-mechanics scope and the discrepancy is non-load-bearing for any canon constraint.

#### AUD-NPC2-448 — P-08 and GD-1 handled correctly: arc is political-visibility, not Thread-evidence; no alternate victory
- **Unit:** Yrsa Vossen | **Category:** note | **Constraint/Ref:** P-08 (Thread-level evidence inert vs TS<30 NPC); GD-1 | **Verifier verdict:** DOWNGRADED
- **Issue:** No canon violation found on the high-risk axes. Vossen (TS 25, <30) is targeted via Church Attention Pool from public political visibility (Heresy Investigation), not via Thread-level evidence used against her — consistent with P-08. Her TS 25 'senses artefacts but cannot Leap/resist Forgetting' aligns with the canonical TS tiers (Leap requires TS>=30 + Approach Training; Forgetting-resistance gate 29). Her arc outcomes (martyr / formal Founding) carry no faction-specific or alternate victory mechanic, consistent with GD-1. The PP-665 rename (Maret Vossen -> Yrsa Vossen) is cleanly applied in v30 docs with no superseded name leaking as live canon. Surfaced as confirmation; no fix required.
- **Evidence:** P-08 alignment: designs/npcs/npc_roster_v30.md:47 (Church +1 AP from public operation), :52 (TS 25 below gate 29); threadwork Leap threshold designs/threadwork/threadwork_v30.md:102 (TS>=30 + Approach Training). Rename clean: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_system_v1.md:1,828 (superseded historical doc carries the rename note); no 'Maret Vossen'/'Galbados' in v30 NPC docs.
- **Fix:** None required. Optionally record TS 25 + the practitioner/Leap/Forgetting tier boundaries once in the canonical behavior stat block so the P-08 boundary is self-documenting after the TS-0 contradiction is fixed.
- **Verifier note:** Core adjudication holds: P-08 alignment confirmed — Vossen (TS 25, <30) is pressured via Church Attention Pool from public political visibility / Heresy Investigation (npc_roster_v30.md:47, :52), not via Thread-level evidence wielded against her; Leap gate TS>=30 + Approach Training confirmed at threadwork_v30.md:102; her martyr/Founding arc outcomes carry no faction-specific or alternate victory mechanic, consistent with GD-1. HOWEVER one sub-claim is FALSE: the finding asserts 'no Maret Vossen/Galbados in v30 NPC docs,' but npc_relational_graph_v30.md:16 contains 'Yrsa ↔ Maret Vossen kinship (PP-665 rename context)' — 'Maret Vossen' DOES appear in a live v30 NPC doc (here framed as kinship plus rename context, so ambiguous whether it is a deliberate kin reference or the superseded name leaking). The 'rename cleanly applied / no superseded name leaking' portion is therefore not strictly accurate. Remains a NOTE (no canon violation on the high-risk axes), but the rename-clean claim is corrected.

Resonant Style is consistent between the behavior stat block and the arc doc.

Yrsa's styles: designs/npcs/npc_behavior_v30.md:156-157 (Primary Solidarity, Secondary Consequence). TS 25 (<30) but irrelevant to her non-Evidence vector: references/npc_registry.yaml:83.
- **Fix:** None required for the Resonant Style dimension. (Note only: the broader TS 0-vs-25 contradiction in npc_behavior_v30.md:158/:1198 is already logged as AUD-NPC2-018 and is out of scope for this ethics/resonance pass except insofar as it sets the P-08 tier, which does not affect her Solidarity/Consequence styles.)
- **Verifier note:** Reproduced as a clean record. npc_behavior_v30.md:36-39 (sec 1.3) lists the 4 canonical styles and the rule that Solidarity 'requires active Knot with the NPC'; Yrsa's styles at npc_behavior_v30.md:156-157 are Primary Solidarity, Secondary Consequence (both in-set, neither is Evidence); arc_expansion_v30.md:477/479/486 define the Solidarity Knot pathway. Because her secondary is Consequence, the P-08 'Thread-evidence inert vs TS<30' gate does not bite (TS 25 per npc_registry.yaml:83 is irrelevant to non-Evidence vectors). No defect; NOTE retained.
